The pay-or-no-pay decision is made entirely through the Pay type field on each form, which drives the Leave Status marker on the auto-created Timekeeping rows (see 4.3.3 and 4.3.5). HR can still query record history for reporting ("how many sick days has Maria taken this year") without needing a stored balance.

**Common record shape (shared across all forms).** Each request record carries, at minimum:

- A **form number** entered manually by HR from the paper form (e.g., "OB-2026-001"). The system does not auto-generate.
- The **date filed** (from the paper form).
- The **employee or employees** the request covers — selected from the Employee Management database; position and other details auto-populate from the employee record.
- The **approvers** named on the paper form — recorded for audit; selected from the employee list where possible.
- A **scanned upload** of the signed hard copy form.
- A **status** — typically "Approved," since HR only enters fully-signed forms. A "Pending" state may be useful if HR records a form before all signatures are collected.
- **Per-form fields** specific to each request type (see each form's subsection).

**Hard-copy file upload — required for every HR Request record.** _Confirmed for all four HR Request forms._ Every HR Request record stored in the system **must include an uploaded image or PDF of the signed hard-copy form**. This is not optional — the system should not allow a record to be saved as Approved/Recorded without the upload. The upload is the system's audit-trail evidence that the paper workflow actually completed, and is the source of truth for the actual signatures (the system records _who_ the signers were; the upload preserves _that_ they signed).

- **Supported formats:** PDF (preferred), JPG, PNG.
- **Multiple files per record:** allowed — e.g., front and back of a long form, multi-page forms, or supplementary attachments (such as proof/evidence files for an after-the-fact Request for Leave; see Section 4.3.3).
- **Visibility:** the uploaded file is part of the record and can be viewed or downloaded from the record's detail view.
- **Required-not-optional rule:** applies uniformly across OB / Travel, OT, Request for Leave, and LOA Without Pay.

_(To confirm with HR / the company owner: maximum file size per upload, retention period for uploaded scans, and any minimum resolution / quality requirements for scanned documents.)_

**Notes for design.** The four forms share the common record shape above and the file-upload mechanism just described. They differ in their per-form fields and in their cross-module effects (e.g., Request for Leave and LOA Without Pay auto-create Timekeeping rows; OB and OT don't). Each form is specified in its own subsection (4.3.1, 4.3.2, 4.3.3, 4.3.5).

#### 4.3.1 Official Business / Travel Form

**Purpose.** To record approved Official Business or Travel events — work performed by employees **away from the usual workplace** (project site visits, client meetings, travel for the company). The day still counts as a working day; the employee is not absent.

**Workflow model.** Paper-first (see 4.3 above). The OB form is filled out and signed sequentially on paper by:

1. **Requester** (the employee or the person filing on behalf of the team)
2. **Approving Officer / Department Head**
3. **Admin and Finance**
4. **HR (acknowledges)**

All four signatures are required and the sequence is strictly enforced **on paper**. Once the form is fully signed, HR enters it into the system and uploads a scanned copy. The system does **not** run the approval workflow.

**Form structure.** _Confirmed from the JCE OB / Travel Form._ Each OB record consists of **form-level fields** (one set per form, shared across all employees on that form) and **a list of employees** (each row is one employee covered by the OB).

_Form-level fields_

- **Form number** — entered manually by HR (e.g., "OB-2026-001"). Sourced from the paper form.
- **Date filed** — when the paper form was filed.
- **Reasons for leaving** — the reason for the OB / Travel.
- **Project Name** — dropdown sourced from the **project list** (same source as Timekeeping's Working Project field — eventually from the Project Management module).
- **Sales Order #** — dropdown sourced from the **Sales Orders list** maintained by the BDD department (Section 9.3). The dropdown applies the same visibility rule as the Timekeeping Working Project dropdown — Sales Orders with Project Status of Ongoing Project, On Hold, or Project Completed, and not Archived. The OB / Travel record stores the chosen Sales Order's number (e.g., `26-05-378`) and links back to the underlying Sales Order record.
- **Destination** — where the employees are traveling to.
- **Date & Time of Departure** — when the team leaves for the OB.
- **Date & Time of Return** — when the team is expected back.
- **Signers — four discrete fields**, mirroring the four signature blocks on the paper form. The system records each signer's name (and optionally their role / department) for audit, and the actual signature lives on the uploaded scan.
  - **Requester** — _Confirmed: always one of the employees being sent on the OB._ The Requester field is a dropdown **limited to the employees listed in this form's employee list** (not the full database). The Requester signs the paper form as the person initiating the OB request on behalf of the team.
  - **Approving Officer / Dept. Head** — first approval signer. Selected from the full employee database (typically the relevant department head).
  - **Admin and Finance** — second approval signer. Selected from the full employee database.
  - **HR Acknowledger** — final signature, acknowledging receipt. Selected from the full employee database.
- **Scanned upload** — _(system addition; not on the paper form)_ the signed hard copy form (image or PDF).
- **Status** — _(system addition; not on the paper form)_ "Approved" (default, since HR enters fully-signed forms) or "Pending" (if HR records the form before all signatures are collected).

_Employee list (one row per employee)_

- **Employee** — selected from the Employee Management database. **Name, position, and other employee details auto-populate** from the employee record; HR does not retype them.

> **UX simplification confirmed.** Project Name, Sales Order #, Destination, and Reasons for Leaving are **form-level fields shared across all rows** — they are not entered per employee. In practice, one OB form covers a team going to the same place on the same project for the same reason, so per-row fields would just duplicate data. The list of employees in the table is the only per-row content.

**Relationship to Timekeeping.** The OB record and the Timekeeping rows for those days are **kept separate** — the system does _not_ auto-create Timekeeping entries from OB records. The timekeeper enters Timekeeping rows for OB days the same way they enter any other day, choosing the Working Project that matches the OB. The OB record sits alongside as the documented authorization for the off-site work; cross-referencing the two (e.g., showing "this day is covered by OB-2026-001") can be a search/lookup view rather than an automatic data flow.

**Cross-module integration with the project list / BDD.**

- **Project Name** comes from the same project list used by Timekeeping. When the Project Management module is scoped, that list becomes its source of truth.
- **Sales Order #** comes from the **BDD department** (see open item — BDD is a department mentioned for the first time here; whether it is a sixth department or a sub-area of an existing one needs confirmation, and its module will need its own scoping).

**Open items for OB / Travel:**

- **"Pending" status use.** Confirm whether HR ever enters an OB before all signatures are collected on paper (in which case a Pending status is needed), or whether HR strictly only enters fully-signed forms (Approved-only).
- **Notification policy.** Confirm whether the system should send any notifications when an OB is recorded — for example, an email to the employees, the Approving Officer, or HR — or whether the paper-first model means no system notifications are needed.
- **Sales Order # source.** Depends on the BDD department being scoped (see Section 9). Interim: a free-text field for the order reference is acceptable.

#### 4.3.2 Overtime (OT) Form

**Purpose.** To record approved Overtime events — employees working hours beyond their regular schedule. JCE's OT process is **pre-approval by default** (the paper form is filled out and signed before the OT happens), with a recognized exception for **individual employee assignments** where the form may be filed after the fact.

**Workflow model.** Paper-first (see 4.3). The OT form is filled out on paper and signed sequentially by:

1. **Requester** (one of the employees being requested for OT)
2. **Department Head**
3. **Timekeeper** ("Noted by" — acknowledges receipt for timekeeping/payroll purposes)
4. **HR Head** ("Approved by" — final approval)

All four signatures are required on paper. Once fully signed, HR enters the form's data into the system and uploads a scanned copy. The system does _not_ run the approval workflow — signatures happen on paper.

**Form structure.** _Confirmed from the JCE Overtime Form._ Each OT record consists of **form-level fields** (one set per form, shared across all employees on that form) and **a list of employees** (each row is one employee on the OT). Per Bryan: per-row fields on the paper form (Actual Time, Reason, Remarks) are **mostly the same across the team** in practice, so they are captured at the form level in the system, matching the OB pattern.

_Form-level fields_

- **Form number** — entered manually by HR (e.g., "OT FORM NO. 2026-001"). Sourced from the paper form.
- **Section** — _single-select_ from: Servicing · Shop/Office · Project site. _(To confirm: short description of what each option represents — e.g., is "Servicing" a client-servicing OT, "Shop/Office" in-house, "Project site" on-site work? The choice may matter for cost classification.)_
- **Project** — dropdown sourced from the project list (same source as Timekeeping's Working Project field).
- **Date prepared** — when the paper form was filed.
- **Date of overtime** — _the single date on which the OT was performed_. One OT form covers one date.
- **Overtime Request — From / To** — the _planned_ time range (e.g., 6:00 PM – 10:00 PM). Filled in when the form is prepared.
- **Actual Time — From / To** — the _actual_ time range the team worked. Captured after the OT happens. May differ from the requested time.
- **Date received by timekeeper** — when the signed paper form reaches the timekeeper. Often the same day HR enters the form into the system.
- **Reason for rendering overtime** — the reason for the OT (mostly shared across the team — collapsed to form-level).
- **Remarks** — additional notes (mostly shared — collapsed to form-level).
- **OT Type** — _Pre-approved_ (the default; form filed and signed before the OT happens) or _After-the-fact_ (allowed for individual employee assignments where pre-approval wasn't feasible). The system records which path each OT followed.
- **Signers — four discrete fields**, mirroring the four signature blocks on the paper form. The system records each signer's name; the actual signatures live on the uploaded scan.
  - **Requester** — dropdown **limited to the employees listed in this form's employee list** (matching the OB convention).
  - **Department Head** — selected from the full employee database.
  - **Timekeeper (Noted by)** — selected from the full employee database.
  - **HR Head (Approved by)** — selected from the full employee database.
- **Scanned upload** — _(system addition; not on the paper form)_ the signed hard copy form (image or PDF).
- **Status** — _(system addition; not on the paper form)_ "Approved" (default, since HR enters fully-signed forms) or "Pending" (if HR records the form before all signatures are collected).

_Employee list (one row per employee on the OT)_

- **Employee** — selected from the Employee Management database. **Name, position, and other employee details auto-populate** from the employee record.
- **Per-row signature on paper** — each employee signs their own row on the paper form, acknowledging they were part of the OT. The system records who is on the form; the actual signatures are captured in the scan.

> **UX simplification confirmed.** Actual Time, Reason for Rendering Overtime, and Remarks are **form-level shared fields**, not per-row, because in JCE's practice the whole team on one OT form does the same OT for the same reason. The per-row content is just the employee list (with the signature captured on the scan). If a particular OT genuinely has per-employee variation, it should be filed as separate OT forms.

**Relationship to Timekeeping.** _Confirmed._ The OT record and the Timekeeping rows for that day are **kept separate** — the system does _not_ auto-create Timekeeping entries from approved OT. The timekeeper still enters OT hours into Timekeeping the usual way (Day Type's Overtime sub-bucket). The OT form sits alongside as the documented authorization; the Timekeeper's "Noted by" signature on the paper form is what bridges the two on the paper side.

**Open items for OT Form:**

- **Section options — short descriptions.** Confirm what each radio option represents (Servicing / Shop/Office / Project site) for clarity and for any cost classification it may drive.
- **"Pending" status use.** Same question as OB: confirm whether HR ever enters an OT before all signatures are collected, or strictly only fully-signed.
- **Notification policy.** Confirm whether the system should send any notifications when an OT is recorded, or whether the paper-first model means no system notifications.

#### 4.3.3 Request for Leave

**Purpose.** To record approved leave requests for employees taking leave from work. This is the form JCE uses for **Leave With Pay** (and also supports a Without Pay checkbox option, though most Without Pay leaves go through the dedicated LOA Without Pay form in Section 4.3.5). When checked With Pay, the system marks those Timekeeping days as paid leave so Payroll pays for them. JCE does not track leave entitlements per employee, so the record is captured for audit and HR reporting only — no balance is deducted.

> _Note on naming:_ Bryan's original verbal list of HR Request types included both "Request for Leave" and "Leave of Absence With Pay" as separate entries. Bryan confirmed these are the **same form** — this Request for Leave form (RFL) is what was being called "Leave with Pay" in the project's early framing. The two entries have been consolidated.

**Workflow model.** Paper-first (see 4.3). The paper form is filled out by the employee and signed sequentially by:

1. **Employee** (Requester)
2. **Department Head / Supervisor** ("Approved by")
3. **HR Head** ("Checked by")
4. **President / Vice President** ("Noted by")

All four signatures are required on paper. The signature chain reaches **higher** than the other HR Request forms — up to the President level — reflecting the greater operational impact of paid leave. Once fully signed, HR enters the form data into the system and uploads a scanned copy.

> _Important UX note._ The paper template has specific names printed (Fortunato B. Januto as HR Head; Jimwel C. Capillo / Evelyn B. Capillo as President / VP). The **system picks names from the employee database**, not these hard-coded template names — so when the people in those roles change, no template reprint is required for the system's data to stay current.

**Pre-approval default with emergency exception.** _Confirmed._ The form's own note states the request must be filed before the intended leave, except in emergencies where leave is enjoyed in advance and proof/evidence must be submitted to HR.

- **Default: Pre-approved** — the form is filed and signed before the leave is taken.
- **Exception: After-the-fact** — when leave is taken before filing (emergency cases), the system records the request retroactively. **Proof/evidence** (e.g., medical documentation for sick leave, supporting documents for family emergencies) must be attached so HR can determine the validity of the reason.

The system stores this as a Request Type field (Pre-approved / After-the-fact), matching the OT form's pattern.

**Form structure.** _Confirmed from the JCE Request for Leave form (RFL-26-158)._ This is a **single-employee form** — one record per employee per leave event.

_Record fields_

- **Form number** — entered manually by HR (e.g., "RFL-26-158"). Sourced from the paper form; sequential numbering by year (RFL-YY-XXX format).
- **Date filed** — when the paper form was filed.
- **Employee** — selected from the Employee Management database. The following auto-populate from the employee record:
  - **Name**
  - **Position**
  - **Department** (Dept.)
  - **Length of Service** — derived from Date Hired
- **Type of Leave** — single-select from: Vacation Leave · Sick Leave · Others. When "Others" is selected, a **"Others Specify"** free-text field captures the specific type (e.g., Paternity Leave, Maternity Leave, Bereavement Leave). _Per Bryan: Paternity and Maternity leaves are filed through this "Others Specify" pathway on this form, rather than as dedicated dropdown options._
- **Pay type** — single-select: **With Pay** (the default use case for this form) · **Without Pay**. Drives both Leave Balance behavior (With Pay deducts from balance; Without Pay does not) and Timekeeping marker (see Cross-module integration below).
- **Applied Period** — From / To (date range).
- **Leave Category (No. of Days)** — count of leave days. _(To confirm: whether the system auto-calculates from From/To working days, or accepts the manual value entered on the paper form.)_
- **Request Type** _(system field — captures the workflow pattern)_:
  - **Pre-approved** (default) — filed before the leave is taken.
  - **After-the-fact** — filed retroactively for emergency cases. Requires a proof/evidence attachment.
- **Signers — four discrete fields**, mirroring the four signature blocks on the paper form. The system records each signer's name from the employee database; the actual signatures live on the uploaded scan.
  - **Employee's Signature (Requester)** — auto-populated from the Employee field (same person).
  - **Approved by (Department Head / Supervisor)** — selected from the employee database.
  - **Checked by (HR Head)** — selected from the employee database.
  - **Noted by (President / Vice President)** — selected from the employee database.
- **Proof / Evidence attachment** — _(system addition; required only for After-the-fact requests)_ file upload for medical certificates or supporting documents.
- **Scanned upload** — _(system addition)_ the signed hard copy form (image or PDF).
- **Status** — _(system addition)_ "Recorded" (default, since HR enters fully-signed forms).

**Cross-module integration with Timekeeping.** _Confirmed — same auto-create pattern as LOA Without Pay (Section 4.3.5), with the Leave Status driven by the form's Pay type._ When a Request for Leave record is saved, the system **automatically creates Timekeeping rows** for each **working day** in the From → To range. Each auto-created row carries:

- **Date** = the leave day.
- **Day Type** = the calendar's classification of that day (typically Regular Day for weekdays).
- **Time In / Time Out** = empty.
- **Manhours Distribution** = all zeros.
- **Leave Status**:
  - **"Leave With Pay"** if the Pay type is **With Pay** → Payroll pays those days (using the employee's base daily rate; the exact pay calculation is a Payroll module decision).
  - **"Leave Without Pay"** if the Pay type is **Without Pay** → Payroll does not pay.
- **Source Request for Leave reference** — the form's RFL number (e.g., "RFL-26-158") for audit and re-sync.

The auto-created rows appear in the **timekeeper's verification batch** (Section 4.2) alongside manually entered rows and rows from approved LOA Without Pay records.

**Leave-balance behavior — record-only, no entitlement tracking.** _Confirmed._ JCE does **not** maintain leave entitlements per employee — there are no annual allotments for Vacation, Sick, Paternity, or Maternity leave that the system needs to enforce. The Request for Leave record is therefore captured for **audit and HR reporting only**, with no balance to deduct against. The downstream pay decision is made entirely through the Pay type field on the form: With Pay → Payroll pays via the Timekeeping "Leave With Pay" marker; Without Pay → Payroll does not pay. HR can still query the record history any time it needs to answer "how many sick days has Maria taken this year" — the data is there in the records, even without a stored balance.

**Open items for Request for Leave:**

- **Reason field.** The paper form has no explicit Reason field beyond "Others Specify" for non-standard leave types. _(To confirm with HR: whether the system should add a Reason / Notes field for additional context, or whether the existing form structure is sufficient.)_
- **Leave Category (No. of Days) calculation.** Confirm whether the system auto-calculates from From/To (counting working days only, excluding Sundays) or accepts the manual value from the paper form as-is.
- **Proof / Evidence for After-the-fact.** Confirm accepted file types (PDF, image), maximum size, and whether multiple files can be attached per request.
- **Reconciliation with LOA Without Pay form.** Since this form also supports Without Pay, confirm with HR under what circumstances they use this form's Without Pay option versus the dedicated LOA Without Pay form (Section 4.3.5).

---

#### 4.3.5 Leave of Absence Form — Without Pay

**Purpose.** To record approved Leave of Absence (LOA) events where the employee will be **absent without pay** for the days requested. Unlike paid leave, no leave balance is deducted; the days simply produce no pay for the employee. The system records the LOA event for HR audit and reporting, and signals to Timekeeping/Payroll that those days are unpaid.

**Workflow model.** Paper-first (see 4.3). The LOA paper form is filled out by the employee and signed sequentially by:

1. **Requester** (the employee themselves — LOA is a single-employee form, so the Requester is always the named employee)
2. **Section Head** ("Approved by")
3. **Plant Operation Head** ("Noted by")
4. **HR** ("Acknowledged by")

All four signatures are required on paper. Once fully signed, HR enters the form's data into the system and uploads a scanned copy. The system does _not_ run the approval workflow — signatures happen on paper. _Per Bryan: the system just records the four signers for the audit trail; no in-system approval state machine is needed because the paper is already approved by the time HR enters it._

**Form structure.** _Confirmed from the JCE Leave of Absence Form (Without Pay version)._ LOA is a **single-employee form** — one record per employee per LOA event.

_Record fields_

- **Internal ID** — _(system-generated; the paper form has no sequential form number)_. The system creates an internal identifier (e.g., "LOA-WP-2026-001") for each LOA Without Pay record so HR and Payroll can reference it.
- **Date filed** — when the paper form was filed (matches the "Date" field at the top of the paper form).
- **Employee** — selected from the Employee Management database. The Position field auto-populates from the employee record.
- **Date of Absence** — From / To (date range).
- **No. of Days** — _system-calculated from From/To, defaulting to working days only (typically Mon–Sat, excluding Sunday rest days)_. Can be overridden manually if needed. _(To confirm: whether No. of Days should count calendar days, working days, or follow the paper form's number exactly.)_
- **Leave Type** — _single-select_ from: Vacation Leave · Sick Leave · Paternity Leave · Maternity Leave · Others. All five are usable for Without Pay (e.g., an employee filing Vacation Leave when their paid balance is depleted, or any of the above when filed as unpaid for whatever reason).
- **Reason** — free-text justification.
- **Signers — four discrete fields**, mirroring the four signature blocks on the paper form. The system records each signer's name; the actual signatures live on the uploaded scan.
  - **Requester** — the employee on the LOA (auto-populated from the Employee field; not a separate dropdown since the LOA covers a single employee).
  - **Approved by (Section Head)** — selected from the full employee database.
  - **Noted by (Plant Operation Head)** — selected from the full employee database.
  - **Acknowledged by (HR)** — selected from the full employee database.
- **Scanned upload** — _(system addition; not on the paper form)_ the signed hard copy form (image or PDF).
- **Status** — _(system addition)_ "Recorded" (default, since HR enters fully-signed forms) or "Pending" (rare; only if HR records before all signatures are collected).

**Cross-module integration with Timekeeping.** _Confirmed — distinct from OB and OT._ When an LOA Without Pay record is saved, the system **automatically creates Timekeeping rows** for each **working day** in the From → To range. Each auto-created row carries:

- **Date** = the leave day.
- **Day Type** = the calendar's classification of that day (typically Regular Day for weekdays).
- **Time In / Time Out** = empty (employee did not work).
- **Manhours Distribution** = all zeros (no hours to count).
- **Leave Status** = **"Leave Without Pay"** (a new row-level marker; see the data-model note below).
- **Source LOA reference** — the internal ID of the LOA Without Pay record that created this row, so the linkage is auditable and the row can be re-synced if the LOA is later edited.

These auto-created rows appear in the **timekeeper's verification batch** at the end of the pay period (Section 4.2), so they're visible in the timekeeper's review and travel with the rest of the period's data to Payroll. The timekeeper does not need to enter these manually.

When Payroll consumes a verified batch, rows with **Leave Status = "Leave Without Pay"** produce **no pay** for those days (no Regular Hours to multiply; no premium math).

_Data-model note._ The exact representation of "Leave Without Pay" on a Timekeeping row needs a small design decision: either (a) add a new optional **Leave Status** field on the Timekeeping row (preferred — keeps Day Type as the calendar classification and adds leave status as a separate concern), or (b) extend the Day Type dropdown with leave values. Option (a) is recommended and is assumed in this draft.

**Leave balance.** _Confirmed._ LOA Without Pay does **not** deduct from any paid leave balance. The record is kept for HR audit and reporting purposes only.

**Open items for LOA Without Pay:**

- **Timekeeping data-model representation of "Leave Without Pay"** — confirm the row-level Leave Status field approach (recommended) versus extending the Day Type list.
- **No. of Days definition** — confirm whether the count is calendar days, working days (excluding Sundays/rest days), or whatever the paper form's number says (with the system auto-calculating as a suggestion only).
- **Annual cap** — confirm whether there's an annual maximum on Leave Without Pay days per employee (and if so, where it's tracked).
- **"Pending" status use.** Same question as OB/OT: confirm whether HR ever enters before all signatures are collected.
- **LOA With Pay (Section 4.3.4) form** — Bryan confirmed it's a separate paper template; obtaining a copy will let us scope 4.3.4 in parallel, with the structural difference being: (i) it deducts from the leave balance, and (ii) it marks Timekeeping days as "Leave With Pay" so payroll _does_ pay for those days.

### 4.4 Finalization Decisions (v1.64)

The HR module's remaining "to confirm" items are resolved here (Bryan, finalization 2026-06-03). Field-meaning items only HR can define are listed as **client-input required** at the end.

**Self-service & request workflow:**

1. **Employee self-service portal (v1).** Employees log in to **submit their own HR requests** and **view their own** payslip, leave records, and timekeeping, scoped strictly to their own records (RBAC §3.7).
2. **Request workflow = digital intake + offline approval.** An employee submits a request digitally → it enters **Pending** → approval signatures are collected **on paper** (wet-signed, the confirmed signer chains per form) → HR uploads the scanned signed copy and the status moves to **Approved/Recorded**. This **supersedes** the earlier "HR keys the paper form" intake step; the offline-approval principle is unchanged.
3. **Pending status** is supported on all request forms (submitted but not yet fully signed), alongside Approved/Recorded.
4. **Notifications** fire on key events: approver notified on submission, employee notified on approval, HR on record (per FR-XC-style alerts).
5. **LOA With Pay (4.3.4) — kept as a separate form.** To be scoped from its own template _(client-input)_; structurally it mirrors Request for Leave (4.3.3) but sets the Timekeeping **"Leave With Pay"** marker. No balance deduction (entitlements not tracked, see #14).

**Employee record & identifiers:**

6. **Canonical identifier = system-assigned Employee Number.** `S/N` is a display/list index; `BIO Nos` is the link to the biometric/timekeeping device. All time entry keys off the Employee Number.
7. **Place of Assignment = picklist from Projects / Sales Orders**, with a free-text fallback for non-project assignments (Main Office, Workshop).
8. **Employment status values:** Probationary · Regular (Active) · On Leave · Suspended · Resigned · Terminated.
9. **Contract-expiry alert:** an on-screen flag on the employee record/list **plus** a notification to HR when a contractual employee crosses the 6-months-remaining mark.

**Calculations & policies:**

10. **Leave / LOA "No. of Days" = auto-calculated working days** (From–To, excluding Sundays), shown as an editable default.
11. **OB-driven Timekeeping rows** use the **employee's normal shift hours** per OB day; **Sundays are skipped** (no auto-row).
12. **Timekeeping batch re-open:** the **timekeeper may re-open** a verified batch with an **audited reason**; every batch records its verifier + verification timestamp.
13. **Attachment policy:** PDF / JPG / PNG, up to ~10 MB per file, multiple files per request, retained for the life of the record.
14. **Leave entitlements — recording-only** (confirmed): no per-employee balances are enforced; pay-or-no-pay is driven by each form's Pay-type marker into Timekeeping.

**Client-input required** (HR to define before build):

- Field meanings: **Insurance** (yes/no vs provider/coverage), **Vaccinated** (yes/no vs detail), **Expiration Date** (which document it tracks), the **Section** option descriptions (Servicing / Shop-Office / Project site), and the **Travel Time** definition.
- The **PIS form layout** (for add/edit screen design; non-blocking).
- The **LOA With Pay (4.3.4)** paper template.
- Any **RA 10173** obligations beyond standard practice (consent capture, data-subject requests, retention periods).

### 4.5 Roles & Permissions

The HR module's roles and their detailed permissions are defined in the consolidated system-wide RBAC matrix (Section 3). In summary:

- **HR Head** — manages employee records and timekeeping oversight, and **approves/records** HR requests (offline-signed, status tracked); full HR-module access. **Compensation fields are masked** — salary, allowances, and payslip amounts are visible only to the Payroll Officer and Owner (§3.7). _(The HR Head replaces the earlier "HR Staff" role; JCE's HR function is led by the HR Head, who is also the HR approver named on the request forms in §4.3.)_
- **Timekeeper** — creates/edits timekeeping entries and verifies pay-period batches; reads the Sales Orders / project list to pick the Working Project.
- **Employee (Self-Service)** — submits own HR requests and views own payslip, leave records, and timekeeping — own records only.
- **Owner** — full access. **Admin** — user/role and reference-data configuration.

### 4.6 Audit Log (Department-Scoped)

Consistent with the other department modules (Purchasing §7.10, Warehouse §10.13, BDD §9.6), the HR module maintains a department-scoped, append-only audit log:

- Every state-changing action is recorded with **actor, timestamp, affected record, and before/after values**: employee-record create / edit / archive, timekeeping entry edits, batch **verify** and **re-open** (with reason), and HR-request status changes (submitted → approved/recorded, void).
- Entries are **immutable** — not editable or deletable through the application.
- **Retention** follows the system-wide policy: time-bounded with archival (NFR-AUDIT-02).
- **Access** follows RBAC: the HR Head sees the full HR log and Owner/Admin have full visibility; employees do not have audit-log access.

---

## 5. Accounting Department Module _(finalized v1.66 — pending JCE source docs)_

This section covers how the Accounting department uses the dashboard. Accounting is the **downstream consumer** of much of what already lives in HR (Timekeeping, HR Requests) and BDD (Sales Orders, Suppliers) — when an HR pay period closes, Accounting pays employees; when a project finishes, Accounting bills the client; when a supplier delivers, Accounting cuts the check. The module is structured around eight working areas plus the standard roles & permissions and pending-information subsections.

### 5.1 Module Overview

The Accounting module covers eight sub-tabs:

- **Payroll** (5.2) — pays employees from Timekeeping's verified batches, respecting Pay Period Type (Daily / Weekly / Monthly), HR Requests' Leave Status markers, and JCE's multiplicative DOLE pay-rate matrix (Section 5.2.1).
- **Sales** (5.3) — the revenue side: invoicing clients for awarded Sales Orders, tracking billed-vs-unbilled work.
- **Collections** (5.4) — receivables follow-through: which client invoices are outstanding, paid, overdue.
- **Payable Voucher** (5.5) — supplier and vendor payment authorizations; references the Suppliers entity owned by Purchasing 7.9 and triggered by Purchasing's RFP 7.7.
- **Disbursement** (5.6) — outgoing payments (operational expenses, supplier payments, payroll disbursement). Closes the upstream chain `Purchasing PO 7.5 → RFP 7.7 → PV 5.5 → Disbursement 5.6`.
- **Journal Voucher** (5.7) — manual accounting journal entries for adjustments, reclassifications, and entries that don't fit the other tabs.
- **Project Costing** (5.8) _(deferred)_ — labor cost from Timekeeping + materials from Purchasing module (Section 7) + other costs, all aggregated against the Sales Order they belong to. Tells Accounting which projects are profitable.
- **Reporting** (5.9) — financial statements, cash position, aging reports, project profitability dashboards.

**Cross-module hooks already implied by earlier sections:**

- **HR Timekeeping (4.2)** → Payroll consumes verified batches at cutoff time. Pay Period Type (Section 4.1's Salary Rate Category) determines which batches go where. The multiplicative DOLE pay-rate matrix (Section 5.2.1) is what Payroll actually applies, looking up the appropriate cell per hour based on Day Type, Night Differential, and Overtime status.
- **HR Requests (4.3)** → Payroll respects the Leave Status marker on each Timekeeping row: Leave With Pay → pay the day; Leave Without Pay → don't pay the day.
- **BDD Sales Order (9.3)** → Sales invoices clients for Sales Orders; Project Costing aggregates against the Sales Order No.
- **BDD Suppliers (9.5)** → Payable Voucher records who is being paid; Disbursement executes payments.
- **BDD Offers (9.4)** → may inform expected revenue once an Offer is awarded but before it's invoiced.

These hooks should each be detailed in the relevant Accounting sub-tab as it's scoped.

> **Finalization note (v1.62).** The open-item lists scattered through §5.2–§5.9 below were resolved in the consolidated **§5.12 Finalization Decisions**; items still needing a JCE source document are flagged there as _client-input required_. Inline 'Confirm.' markers below are superseded by §5.12.

### 5.2 Payroll _(scoping in progress — Settings sub-tab fully scoped)_

**Purpose.** Compute and pay employees their compensation for each pay period, based on the verified Timekeeping batches and the Pay Period Type for each employee. The Payroll module is organized as a parent screen with sub-tabs; the first sub-tab fully specified is **Settings** (5.2.1), the central configuration store for all rates, contribution schedules, policy thresholds, and scheduling rules used during payroll computation.

> ✅ **Composition model resolved.** The Pay Rate Multipliers matrix in Section 5.2.1 below uses **multiplicative composition** matching the standard DOLE Handbook on Workers' Statutory Monetary Benefits (e.g., Rest day OT = 130% × 1.3 = 169%, Regular Holiday on Rest Day = 200% × 1.3 = 260%). This was confirmed in v1.39 from the official DOLE Handbook. Section 4.2's "JCE's multiplier composition rule" subsection has been updated accordingly to multiplicative, superseding an earlier v1.11 record of additive composition.

#### 5.2.1 Settings sub-tab

**Purpose.** The Settings sub-tab is the **central configuration and reference-data store for the Accounting module**. It holds two kinds of content: (a) **Payroll-engine configuration** — every rate, government-mandated table, policy threshold, and scheduling rule used during payroll computation, externalized so that **no value is hard-coded in the application logic**; the payroll engine reads the active configuration at the moment of computation. (b) **Cross-cutting accounting reference data** — the **Chart of Accounts** (Section 8 below, added v1.51) used by every double-entry posting across the Accounting module (Sales 5.3, Collections 5.4, Payable Voucher 5.5, Disbursement 5.6, Journal Voucher 5.7, and Reporting 5.9), and later additions like ATC withholding-tax codes and JCE's own bank-account list. All configuration items and reference entries are **versioned by effectivity date** where applicable, so that payroll runs and accounting reports for past periods continue to reproduce figures that were correct at the time. _Architectural note: Settings currently sits structurally inside the Payroll sub-tab (5.2.1) for historical reasons but is conceptually module-wide. If Settings continues to grow with cross-cutting reference data, a future revision may promote it to a top-level Accounting sub-tab — the requirement codes (SET-XXX) and content layout would carry forward unchanged._

**SET-001** — The system shall store all payroll configuration parameters in the Settings sub-tab and read them at computation time rather than embedding them in application code.

**SET-002** — The system shall associate each rate table and contribution schedule with an effectivity date (and optional end date), and shall apply the version that was in effect for the payroll period being computed.

**SET-003** — Access to view and edit the Settings sub-tab shall be restricted to authorized roles (e.g., Accounting Head / Administrator). Edits shall be logged with the editing user and timestamp.

##### 1. Pay Rate Multipliers

This section defines the percentage multipliers applied to an employee's base daily or hourly rate according to the type of day worked, whether the work fell on a night shift, and whether it was rendered as overtime. The base pay value originates from the Timekeeping module (days/hours worked); the engine multiplies that base by the applicable percentage drawn from this table.

Night-shift figures represent the regular rate multiplied by 1.1 (10% night-shift differential). Overtime figures represent the regular rate multiplied by 1.25 on ordinary days and 1.3 on all other day types.

**SET-010** — The system shall maintain an editable matrix of pay multipliers keyed by day type, with separate columns for the regular rate, the night-shift rate, and the overtime rate.

**SET-011** — The system shall apply the appropriate multiplier to the base pay based on the day type and work conditions recorded in Timekeeping.

| Day type                            | Regular % | Night shift % | Overtime % |
| ----------------------------------- | --------- | ------------- | ---------- |
| Ordinary day                        | 100       | 110           | 125        |
| Rest day                            | 130       | 143           | 169        |
| Special (non-working) day           | 130       | 143           | 169        |
| Special day, on rest day            | 150       | 165           | 195        |
| Double special (non-working) day    | 150       | 165           | 195        |
| Double special day, on rest day     | 195       | 214.5         | 253.5      |
| Regular holiday                     | 200       | 220           | 260        |
| Regular holiday, on rest day        | 260       | 286           | 338        |
| Double regular holiday              | 300       | 330           | 390        |
| Double regular holiday, on rest day | 390       | 429           | 507        |

##### 2. Government Mandatory Contributions

This section stores the contribution schedules for the three statutory programs — SSS, PhilHealth, and Pag-IBIG (HDMF). Each schedule is versioned by effectivity date. The engine computes the employee share for each program based on the employee's compensation and subtracts it from gross pay before income tax is calculated.

**SET-020** — The system shall store the SSS, PhilHealth, and Pag-IBIG contribution schedules, each versioned by effectivity date and traceable to its source circular.

**SET-021** — The system shall compute each employee's contribution share according to the applicable schedule and the employee's monthly compensation.

**SET-022** — The system shall deduct mandatory contributions from gross pay before computing withholding tax (i.e., contributions reduce taxable income).

**SSS** _(effective January 2025, per SSS Circular No. 2024-006):_ Total contribution rate of 15%, split into a 4.5% employee share and a 9.5% employer share (plus Employees' Compensation and Mandatory Provident Fund components carried by the employer). The Monthly Salary Credit (MSC) ranges from a minimum of ₱5,000 to a maximum of ₱35,000. The full bracket table (45 compensation ranges) shall be stored and used to look up the exact contribution for each salary range.

**SET-023** — The system shall determine the employee's SSS contribution by locating the compensation range that contains the employee's monthly salary and applying the corresponding employee-share amount from the stored bracket table.

**PhilHealth** _(effective 2024–2025, per PhilHealth Circular No. 2019-0009):_ Premium rate of 5%, shared equally between employer and employee (2.5% each). The income floor is ₱10,000 and the ceiling is ₱100,000, producing a monthly premium between ₱500 and ₱5,000.

**SET-024** — The system shall compute the PhilHealth premium as 5% of monthly basic salary bounded by the ₱10,000 floor and ₱100,000 ceiling, and shall apply 50% of that premium as the employee share.

**Pag-IBIG (HDMF)** _(effective February 2024, employed member):_ Employee rate of 1% for monthly compensation of ₱1,000 to ₱1,500 and 2% above ₱1,500; employer rate of 2%. Both shares are computed on a Maximum Fund Salary of ₱10,000, capping each share at ₱200 per month.

**SET-025** — The system shall compute the Pag-IBIG employee share at 1% of compensation for the ₱1,000–₱1,500 band and 2% above ₱1,500, applied to compensation capped at the ₱10,000 Maximum Fund Salary, such that the employee and employer shares each do not exceed ₱200 per month.

##### 3. Withholding Tax (TRAIN Law)

This section stores the graduated income-tax table used to compute withholding tax on compensation. Tax is computed on **taxable income**, defined as gross pay less the employee's mandatory contributions. The table below is the **monthly** table; the frequency at which it is applied is configurable.

**SET-030** — The system shall store the TRAIN-law graduated withholding-tax table and compute withholding tax on taxable income (gross pay net of mandatory contributions).

**SET-031** — The system shall allow selection of the tax-table frequency to match the employee's pay frequency. _(Open item — see Open Items below.)_

| Monthly taxable income | Tax due                                       |
| ---------------------- | --------------------------------------------- |
| ₱20,832 and below      | 0% (no tax)                                   |
| ₱20,833 to ₱33,332     | 15% of the excess over ₱20,833                |
| ₱33,333 to ₱58,332     | ₱1,875 + 20% of the excess over ₱33,333       |
| ₱58,333 to ₱166,666    | ₱6,875 + 25% of the excess over ₱58,333       |
| ₱166,667 to ₱666,666   | ₱33,958.33 + 30% of the excess over ₱166,667  |
| ₱666,667 and over      | ₱183,958.33 + 35% of the excess over ₱666,667 |

##### 4. Tardiness / Lates Policy

This section defines how late arrivals are converted into a time deduction, based on the company's policy. The official workday start is 8:00. The employee's recorded clock-in time from Timekeeping is matched against the windows below to determine the amount of time deducted from pay.

**SET-040** — The system shall maintain an editable mapping of clock-in time windows to corresponding time deductions.

**SET-041** — The system shall determine each employee's daily tardiness deduction by matching the recorded clock-in time from Timekeeping against the configured windows.

| Clock-in window | Time deducted |
| --------------- | ------------- |
| 8:00 – 8:10     | None          |
| 8:11 – 8:15     | ½ hour        |
| 8:16 – 8:30     | 1 hour        |
| 8:31 – 9:00     | 1½ hours      |
| 9:01 – 10:00    | 2 hours       |
| 10:01 – 12:00   | Half day      |

##### 5. Deduction Calendar

This section defines which mandatory contributions and which loan amortizations are deducted on each cutoff, and varies by pay frequency. It ensures that a given contribution or loan payment is taken once per month on the correct cutoff rather than on every payroll run.

**SET-050** — The system shall maintain a configurable deduction calendar that assigns each mandatory contribution and loan type to a specific cutoff, separately for each pay frequency.

**SET-051** — During a payroll run, the system shall include only those deductions assigned to the current cutoff under the applicable pay frequency.

_For semi-monthly and daily pay frequencies:_

| Cutoff | Deductions taken                                         |
| ------ | -------------------------------------------------------- |
| 15th   | Pag-IBIG contribution, PhilHealth contribution, SSS loan |
| 30th   | SSS contribution, Pag-IBIG loan                          |

_For the weekly pay frequency:_

| Week   | Deductions taken                        |
| ------ | --------------------------------------- |
| Week 1 | Pag-IBIG contribution and Pag-IBIG loan |
| Week 2 | PhilHealth contribution                 |
| Week 3 | SSS contribution                        |
| Week 4 | SSS loan                                |

##### 6. Cut-Off Periods

This section defines the calendar date ranges that delimit each cutoff. Timekeeping aggregates attendance within these ranges before the data is passed to the payroll engine.

**SET-060** — The system shall store the date ranges defining each cutoff so that Timekeeping can aggregate attendance to the correct payroll period.

| Frequency / cutoff    | Date range |
| --------------------- | ---------- |
| Daily — 15th cutoff   | 21 to 05   |
| Daily — 30th cutoff   | 06 to 20   |
| Monthly — 15th cutoff | 23 to 07   |
| Monthly — 30th cutoff | 08 to 22   |

_Note: these date ranges match the Pay Period Types specified in Section 4.2's "Pay periods, verification, and hand-off to payroll" subsection (Daily 6–20 / 21–5; Monthly 8–22 / 23–7) — only the labeling differs (this table identifies each cutoff by its pay date — the 15th or 30th of the month — rather than by the date range alone). The weekly cutoff (Sunday–Saturday) from Section 4.2 is implicit in the Deduction Calendar's week-numbered table above._

##### 7. Statutory Leave Benefits

This section stores the **DOLE-mandated statutory leave benefits** that the Payroll engine must recognize when an employee is on leave. _Per the DOLE Handbook on Workers' Statutory Monetary Benefits._ Each benefit has its own eligibility rules, duration, and payment source — and not every benefit is paid by the employer (Maternity Leave is paid through SSS, not employer payroll). The Settings sub-tab stores these so payroll computation can apply the correct treatment when a Leave With Pay record (Section 4.3.3) references a statutory leave type.

**SET-070** — The system shall store the DOLE statutory leave benefit definitions, each with eligibility conditions, duration, and payment source.

**SET-071** — The system shall apply the correct payment treatment when a Timekeeping row's Leave Status references a statutory leave: employer pays for employer-paid statutory leaves (Service Incentive Leave, Paternity Leave); the day is recorded as leave but employer pay is zero for SSS-paid statutory leaves (Maternity Leave) — the employee receives the benefit directly from SSS.

**SET-072** — Service Incentive Leave eligibility (one year of service) shall be computed automatically from the employee record's Date Hired (Section 4.1's Years of Service field).

| Type of Leave                     | Conditions of Entitlement                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                               | Duration of Paid Leave                                                                                                                                                                      | Payment Source                                                                                      |
| --------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Service Incentive Leave** (SIL) | Employee has rendered **at least one year of service**.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                 | **5 days**                                                                                                                                                                                  | Employer                                                                                            |
| **Maternity Leave**               | (1) Female employee has **at least three monthly SSS contributions** in the 12-month period immediately preceding the semester of childbirth, miscarriage, or emergency termination of pregnancy; **and** (2) Employee has **notified her employer** about her pregnancy and the probable date of childbirth.                                                                                           | **105 days** for live childbirth · **+15 additional days** (total 120) if the female employee is a qualified solo parent · **60 days** if miscarriage or emergency termination of pregnancy | **SSS** _(employer pays zero for these days; employee receives the SSS maternity benefit directly)_ |
| **Paternity Leave**               | (1) Married male employee at the time of the delivery of his child; (2) Cohabiting with his spouse at the time that the latter gives birth or suffers a miscarriage; (3) Has applied for paternity leave with his employer within a reasonable period from the expected date of delivery (or within a period provided by company rules / CBA, provided that prior application for leave shall be made). | **7 days**                                                                                                                                                                                  | Employer                                                                                            |

_Note: this table captures the three statutory leaves shown in the DOLE Handbook excerpt provided. Additional DOLE statutory benefits (e.g., Solo Parent Leave under R.A. 8972, Special Leave for Women under R.A. 9710 / Magna Carta of Women, leave under R.A. 9262 / Anti-VAWC Act) are not included here yet; they can be added to this table when JCE confirms the full set of statutory benefits the system should recognize._

_Connection to HR Requests (Section 4.3.3):_ the Request for Leave form's "Type of Leave" includes Vacation / Sick / Others (with "Others Specify" capturing Paternity / Maternity in the current spec). When statutory leaves are selected, the Payroll engine looks up the configured rules above to determine duration eligibility (e.g., does the employee have one year of service for SIL?), payment source (employer vs SSS), and the maximum days allowed for that benefit.

##### 8. Chart of Accounts

**Purpose.** The Chart of Accounts (CoA) is JCE's General Ledger account list — the canonical set of bookkeeping accounts used by every Dr/Cr posting across the Accounting module. Every Payable Voucher (5.5) debit account, every Collection (5.4) bank/CWT debit, every Sales (5.3) revenue credit, every Journal Voucher (5.7) line, and every Reporting (5.9) financial-statement aggregation reads from this list. Without a maintained CoA the rest of Accounting can't post double-entry transactions; with it, every transaction is a structured Dr/Cr against named accounts and the trial balance, income statement, and balance sheet derive automatically.

**Account-code structure.** JCE uses a **5-digit numeric coding scheme** organized by account type, with the leading digit indicating the major category:

| Range         | Category                               | Examples                                                                            |
| ------------- | -------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `10000–19999` | **Assets**                             | Cash, Receivables, Inventory, Fixed Assets, Accumulated Depreciation (contra-asset) |
| `20000–29999` | **Liabilities**                        | Accounts Payable, Tax Payables, Statutory Payables, Loans Payable, VAT Payables     |
| `30000–39999` | **Equity**                             | Capital Stock, Retained Earnings                                                    |
| `40000–49999` | **Revenue**                            | Sales (VAT / Zero-Rated / Exclusive), Other Income                                  |
| `50000–59999` | **Cost of Sales & Operating Expenses** | Direct Materials, Direct Labor, all overhead expense lines                          |

Within each range there are sub-ranges (e.g., `10000–10099` is Cash specifically; `11000–11099` is Receivables and Current Assets; `12000–12099` is Fixed Assets). The numbering is sparse and does not need to be contiguous — JCE can leave gaps for future additions without renumbering existing accounts.

**SET-080** — The system shall maintain a Chart of Accounts as a master reference list. Each account has the following fields:

| Field               | Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| ------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `account_code`      | 5-digit numeric (e.g., `10001`). Globally unique. Manually assigned by Accounting at creation.                                                                                                                                                           |
| `account_name`      | Display name (e.g., `Cash-On-Hand`). Free text.                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| `account_type`      | Enum: `Asset` · `Liability` · `Equity` · `Revenue` · `Expense`. Derived from the account_code's leading digit (with override allowed).                                                                                                                   |
| `account_subtype`   | Free-text or enum (e.g., `Cash`, `Receivable`, `Fixed Asset`, `Contra-Asset`, `Current Liability`, `Statutory Payable`, `Tax Payable`, `Equity`, `Operating Revenue`, `Cost of Sales`, `Operating Expense`).                                             |
| `normal_balance`    | `Debit` or `Credit` — drives validation and reporting sign conventions. (Assets and Expenses are normally Debit; Liabilities, Equity, Revenue are normally Credit; contra-accounts like Accumulated Depreciation are Credit despite being under Assets.) |
| `is_contra_account` | Boolean. `true` for Accumulated Depreciation accounts (12054–12060) and Sales Discounts (40004).                                                                                                                                                         |
| `parent_code`       | Optional — for sub-account hierarchies if JCE ever uses them (none observed in current list).                                                                                                                                                            |
| `is_active`         | Boolean — inactive accounts cannot receive new postings but remain visible for historical reports.                                                                                                                                                       |
| `notes`             | Optional free text — e.g., to record the BIR alphanumeric tax code that typically posts to this account, or the bank account number for cash accounts.                                                                                                   |

**SET-081** — The system shall prevent deletion of any account that has at least one journal posting against it. Inactivation is allowed; deletion is not.

**SET-082** — The system shall validate that every double-entry posting (from Payable Voucher 5.5, Collection 5.4, Sales 5.3, Journal Voucher 5.7) references an active account from this list. Free-text account names are not accepted.

**SET-083** — Account codes shall be globally unique. Account names may repeat (rare, but the system uses the code, not the name, as the primary key).

**The full chart — current JCE accounts.** Captured from the artifact provided in v1.51 (4 photos of the printed Chart of Accounts document). Marked `(typo)` where the printed source has spelling errors that should be cleaned up before data entry. **Re-verified v1.63** against a fresh 4-photo copy (Bryan, 2026-06-03): all **129 account codes (10001–50055) confirmed present and correct — 0 discrepancies.** Accounts referenced in transactions but absent from this chart (BDO, Security Bank `852-5`, Land Bank `580` Workshop, `ACCOMMODATION EXP.`, `PROJECT OVERHEAD`) are confirmed genuinely missing — not a lost page — and are candidates to be added (see open items and §5.12).

**ASSETS — Cash and Cash Equivalents (10001–10012)**

| Code    | Account Name                          | Notes                                                                                                                             |
| ------- | ------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `10001` | Cash-On-Hand                          |                                                                                                                                   |
| `10002` | Petty Cash Fund                       |                                                                                                                                   |
| `10003` | Cash-In-Bank-LAND BANK-067-4          | The `067-4` here likely matches the `with forms 067-4` annotation on the 2026 Collections register (5.4 open item #10) — confirm. |
| `10004` | Cash-In-Bank-LAND BANK-029-1          |                                                                                                                                   |
| `10005` | Cash-In-Bank-LAND BANK-032-1          |                                                                                                                                   |
| `10006` | Cash-In-Bank-LAND BANK-031-3          |                                                                                                                                   |
| `10007` | Cash-In-Bank-LAND BANK-Dollar (Zorro) | USD-denominated. Confirm currency-conversion handling.                                                                            |
| `10008` | Cash-In-Bank-LAND BANK-Dollar (Soleq) | USD-denominated.                                                                                                                  |
| `10009` | Cash-In-Bank-BPI                      |                                                                                                                                   |
| `10010` | Cash-In-Bank-Security Bank Peso       |                                                                                                                                   |
| `10011` | Cash-In-Bank-Security Bank-Dollar     | USD-denominated.                                                                                                                  |
| `10012` | Cash-In-Bank-Robinsons Bank           |                                                                                                                                   |

#### Cross-module hooks closing prior data models

Adding the CoA closes loops the SRS opened in earlier versions:

- **Sales 5.3 DEBIT side** maps to: `11022 Account Receivable - Trade` (the main AR), `11023 Retention Receivable` (the 10% holdback), `20019 DP on ongoing projects` (when a down payment is being recouped, this gets Dr'd to reduce the prior credit).
- **Sales 5.3 CREDIT side** maps to: `40001 Sales-Vat`, `40002 Sales-Zero rated`, `40003 Sales-Exclusive` (likely the VAT-exempt line — clarify), `20016 VAT Output` (the 12% Output VAT credit).
- **Collections 5.4 closing entry** maps to: `Dr 10003-10012` (whichever bank received the deposit), `Dr 11032 Tax withheld by Customers` (the 2% CWT), `Cr 11022 Account Receivable - Trade` (the AR reduction).
- **Payable Voucher 5.5 standard entry** maps to: `Dr 50001 Direct Materials / 50005 Materials & Supplies / 50032 Meals / etc.` (the Sundry Account — the expense or asset being charged), `Cr 10003-10012` (the bank cash being paid out), `Cr 20006 Expanded Withholding Tax Payable` (if EWT applies).
- **Payable Voucher 5.5 advance / down-payment entry** maps to: `Dr 11034 Advances - Suppliers` (the 50% down-payment case, like CV-0902), then later when delivery happens the advance gets transferred to expense.
- **Payable Voucher 5.5 recoupment entry** (the `(1,700.68)` parens on the PR worksheet) maps to: `Cr 11038 Advances Employees` (reducing the advance balance the employee owes JCE).
- **Payroll 5.2.3 standard entry** maps to: `Dr 50046 Salaries and wages` (gross), `Dr 50012 Allowances` (non-taxable allowance), `Dr 50022-50024 Employer SSS/PhilHealth/Pag-IBIG Contributions` (employer share), `Cr 20007 EE Income Tax Withheld Payable`, `Cr 20008-20012 EE Statutory Payables` (employee share + loan payables), `Cr 10003-10012` (the bank account funding the net pay).

Every cross-module hook the SRS previously described in terms of conceptual fields (`trade_receivable`, `creditable_tax`, `down_payment`, `retention`, `vat`) now has a **concrete GL account code** behind it. This is the unlock that lets 5.7 Journal Voucher and 5.9 Reporting (the Trial Balance, Income Statement, Balance Sheet) be designed.

#### Open items for the Chart of Accounts

1. ✅ **BDO bank account — VERIFIED ABSENT (v1.63).** The complete 4-page chart was re-verified against a clean copy (2026-06-03): all 129 accounts confirmed and there is **no BDO Cash-In-Bank account** (banks are Land Bank ×6, BPI, Security Bank ×2, Robinsons). It is **not a missing page.** Since BDO (`…4061`) funds real disbursements (5.5/5.6), the recommendation is to **add a new `Cash-In-Bank-BDO` account** (next free asset code, e.g., `10013`). _Final confirm with Accounting before data entry._
2. ✅ **`40003 Sales-Exclusive` — RESOLVED: VAT-Exempt (Non-VAT) sales.** This is the Non-VAT revenue line (revenue from VAT-exempt transactions / non-VAT customers), matching the Sales §5.3 assumption.
3. ✅ **`50001 Direct Materials` vs `50005 Materials & Supplies` — RESOLVED.** `50001` is **project-attributed** materials that roll up to a project's Cost of Sales; `50005` is **overhead** materials/consumables (office and shop supplies). Drives Project Costing (5.8).
4. ✅ **`20001 Accounts Payable - Trade` vs `20004 Voucher's Payable`** — **RESOLVED in v1.52.** The Disbursement register (5.6) confirms the two-step posting pattern: PV authorization posts `Dr Expense / Cr 20004 Voucher's Payable`, then Disbursement execution posts `Dr 20004 / Cr Cash-In-Bank` — i.e., **20004 is the intermediate clearing account** between authorized-but-unpaid and paid. **20001** remains for the longer cycle: a supplier invoice received but not yet authorized for payment (booked when the invoice arrives, settled when the PV is cut and 20001 transfers into 20004 → eventually Cash). See Section 5.6 for the full journal-entry pattern.
5. ✅ **`11023 Retention Receivable - Trade (Foreign)` — RESOLVED: keep Foreign, add a Local account.** `11023` remains the **Foreign** retention receivable; a separate **Local** Retention Receivable - Trade account is to be added (code to be assigned by Accounting — see client-input list).
6. ✅ **`50048 SOP Expense` — RESOLVED: kept as-is.** The account label is retained; its meaning follows JCE's existing usage (no reclassification).
7. ✅ **Typos — RESOLVED: clean up at data entry.** Corrections: `11032 Tax withheld`, `20006 Withholding`, `20007 Withheld`, `50051 Taxes`.
8. ✅ **Numeric gaps — RESOLVED: reserved headroom.** The gaps (11001–11020, 12001–12044, etc.) are treated as **reserved ranges** for future accounts and documented for the development team.
9. ✅ **Sub-account hierarchy — RESOLVED: account × project drill-down.** Every posting can be tagged to a project, giving a 2-D **account × project** view (no per-project sub-accounts needed). Enables Project Costing (5.8).
10. ✅ **Versioning — RESOLVED: effectivity-dated.** CoA entries opt into the Settings effectivity-date versioning (SET-002), so prior-period reports reproduce the names/structure correct at the time.

##### Open Items for 5.2.1 Settings

The following require a decision before this section is finalized:

- **Tax-table frequency (SET-031).** The TRAIN table above is the monthly table, but employees are paid on daily, weekly, and semi-monthly frequencies. The BIR publishes separate daily, weekly, semi-monthly, and monthly withholding tables. The system must either store all four tables and apply the one matching each employee's pay frequency, or annualize taxable income and derive the period tax from the monthly/annual table. This decision affects the Settings data model and must be resolved.

- ✅ **Composition model reconciled (v1.39):** The DOLE Handbook on Workers' Statutory Monetary Benefits, provided in v1.39, confirms **multiplicative composition** as the authoritative model. Section 4.2's earlier "additive" record (from v1.11) has been revised to match. The Pay Rate Multipliers matrix above is the canonical source; Section 4.2 references it.

#### 5.2.2 Loans sub-tab

**Purpose.** To manage employee loans of every type — government salary/calamity loans, employer-granted company loans, and short-term cash advances — and to feed the Payroll engine the right deduction amount per employee per cutoff. The Loans tab is where the loan records _live_; the Payroll engine _consumes_ them at each run; the Payslip line items and the Payable Voucher / Disbursement records _reference_ them.

**The shared loan object.** Every loan, regardless of type, shares the same core record structure. This lets the Payroll engine treat all loans uniformly at compute time; the _type_ governs only who can create and edit the loan and how its schedule is defined.

_Core fields (all loan types)_

- **Employee** — the borrower; selected from the employee database (Section 4.1).
- **Type** — single-select: **Government Loan · Company Loan · Cash Advance**.
- **Sub-type** — selected from a per-type list (e.g., for Government: _SSS Salary Loan, SSS Calamity Loan, Pag-IBIG Salary Loan, Pag-IBIG Calamity Loan_; for Company: free text or a Company-defined list; for Cash Advance: typically just "Cash Advance").
- **Reference Number** — the loan's external or internal identifier (SSS loan no., Pag-IBIG loan no., or an internal CA/loan no. for company-side loans).
- **Principal** — total amount borrowed, in PHP.
- **Date Granted** — when the loan was approved / issued.
- **Terms of Payment** — the amortization plan (detailed below).
- **Deduction Schedule** — which cutoff(s) the deduction comes out on.
- **Running Balance** — outstanding amount, decremented each deduction. Recomputed from the ledger at any time (the ledger is authoritative).
- **Status** — single-select: **Active · Fully Paid · On Hold · Restructured · Cancelled**.
- **Ledger** — append-only history of every deduction posted against the loan (one entry per cutoff, plus entries for adjustments, holds, restructures, and manual overrides). Each entry records: timestamp, user, payroll run ID, amount, resulting balance, and an optional note. _(Same event-stream pattern as Offers and Quotation — the ledger is the audit trail by construction.)_

**The Payroll engine's view of a loan is uniform.** At each run, the engine asks: _for this employee, this cutoff, which loans are scheduled to deduct, and how much?_ The Type only matters at the _Loans tab_ boundary (who can create/edit, what fields are required). The Payroll engine doesn't branch on Type — it just reads the schedule and the amortization from the loan record.

**How the three types differ.**

_Government Loans (owned by HR)._ SSS and Pag-IBIG salary/calamity loans. Deduction timing is **not free-form** — it's fixed by the Deduction Calendar already in Settings (Section 5.2.1 Section 5):

- **SSS loan**: deducted on the **15th** cutoff (semi-monthly/daily) or **Week 4** (weekly).
- **Pag-IBIG loan**: deducted on the **30th** cutoff (semi-monthly/daily) or **Week 1** (weekly).

The official per-period amortization comes from the government's own schedule, so **HR enters the principal, term, and per-period amount as given by SSS/Pag-IBIG** — the system trusts what HR enters (it doesn't independently validate against published SSS/Pag-IBIG tables in v1; that could be added later).

_Company Loan (owned by Accounting)._ Employer-granted, payable to JCE. Schedule is **employer-defined** — this is where the spreadsheet legend's "every cut-off" vs "15th/30th different" distinction lives. **Accounting sets the start cutoff, the frequency (every cutoff or once monthly), and the amortization** when the loan is created.

_Cash Advance (owned by HR)._ Typically short-term and interest-free; recovered over the next one or few cutoffs. Functionally similar to a short-term Company Loan but operationally distinct (different owner, different workflow). _(Open decision below: keep as a distinct Type, or model as a Company Loan variant.)_

**Terms-of-payment engine — entry modes.** A loan's amortization can be entered two ways, and the system derives the rest:

1. **By term count** — enter principal and number of installments; system computes per-period amount = principal ÷ term.
2. **By fixed amortization** — enter principal and a fixed amount per cutoff; system computes how many installments it takes (the last installment may be smaller — see Final Installment Trueing below).

Either way, the engine stores: **principal, per-period amortization, number of installments, start cutoff, and schedule**.

_Two essential engine behaviors:_

- **Final installment trueing.** The last deduction equals the **remaining balance**, not the standard amortization, so the loan never over-collects. Example: a ₱5,000 loan at ₱480/cutoff runs as 10 deductions of ₱480 plus a final deduction of ₱200 — not 11 deductions of ₱480 totalling ₱5,280.
- **Auto-stop.** When the Running Balance hits zero, the loan flips to **Fully Paid** and stops appearing in future payroll runs automatically. No manual close-out step required.

**Critical behaviors and edge cases.**

- **Multiple concurrent loans.** An employee can carry an SSS loan + Pag-IBIG loan + Company loan + Cash Advance simultaneously. At each payroll run, the engine sums all scheduled deductions for the cutoff. _(See open decision below on deduction priority when net pay is tight.)_
- **Insufficient net pay.** If the sum of scheduled loan amortizations exceeds the employee's available net pay (after mandatory contributions and withholding tax), the engine needs a policy. _(See open decision below — this is the single most important rule to lock in.)_
- **Re-run protection.** A deduction must post against a loan **only once per cutoff**, tied to an approved payroll run. Recomputing or re-opening a run does **not** double-charge the balance. The ledger is keyed on (loan + payroll run + cutoff) to enforce uniqueness.
- **Adjustments are logged.** Putting a loan **on hold** (e.g., employee on extended leave), **restructuring** terms, recording an **extra/early payment**, or any **manual override** writes a new ledger entry with the user, timestamp, and a reason. The ledger never loses history.

**How the Loans tab plugs into the rest of the Payroll module.**

- **Settings (5.2.1)** supplies the Deduction Calendar that governs Government loan timing.
- **The Loans tab (this section)** supplies the per-loan amount and balance.
- **The Payroll engine** pulls both at compute time and applies the deductions to the employee's net pay.
- **The Payslip** (to be detailed) shows each loan deduction as a line item with the resulting Running Balance.
- **The Ledger** gives Accounting and HR a full audit trail of every deduction, hold, restructure, and override.

**Confirmed behaviors (resolving the four decisions flagged in v1.40):**

1. **Insufficient net pay = Skip.** _Confirmed._ When total scheduled loan amortizations for a cutoff exceed the employee's available net pay (after mandatory contributions and tax), the Payroll engine **skips all loan deductions for that employee that cutoff**. No balance is decremented; no shortfall is carried forward; no partial deduction is taken. Skipped loans appear in the payroll review with a "skipped: insufficient net pay" indicator so the payroll officer sees the situation. _(See Decision 4 below for the manual override.)_

2. **Cash Advance is a distinct Type.** _Confirmed._ Cash Advance is its own loan Type, separate from Company Loan. **HR owns CAs**; they have their own sub-type list and their own reporting view. (Operationally, a CA stays in HR's hands rather than appearing in Accounting's Company Loans dashboard.)

3. **All Company Loans and Cash Advances are zero-interest.** _Confirmed._ Company Loans carry **no interest** — the Principal is the total to repay. The system does not store or compute interest fields for Company Loans or Cash Advances. _(Government loans already have their interest baked into the official amortization HR enters, so that case is handled at the data-entry level.)_

4. **Deduction priority — payroll officer reviews.** _Confirmed: no automated priority order is hard-coded._ When the engine produces skipped loans (per Decision 1) or when the payroll officer wants to manually push through some loans during the run review, **the payroll officer chooses which loans to deduct on a case-by-case basis**. The system surfaces the skip alongside the loan list, the officer selects which to override, and each manual deduction writes a ledger entry (user, timestamp, amount, optional reason note) using the same audit pattern as any other adjustment. The system may _suggest_ a default review order in the UI (e.g., government loans first, since SSS and Pag-IBIG deductions have legal weight as the employer acts as collection agent), but the engine itself does not enforce a priority and never auto-deducts past the skip rule — every push-through is a human decision with a logged reason.

**Why the skip + officer review combination works.** The skip rule is the safe default: it never leaves an employee with negative net pay, never silently carries a shortfall the employee will be surprised by next cutoff, and never partially collects in a way that's hard to explain. The payroll officer's review is the escape valve: when a skip happens, a human can decide whether to push through (e.g., the SSS loan must collect this cutoff per the official schedule) or accept the skip (e.g., the employee is in a hardship situation and the company loan can wait). The ledger captures every override with a reason, so the audit trail explains both _what_ was done and _why_.

#### 5.2.3 Payroll Summary sub-tab

**Purpose.** The Payroll Summary is the workspace where a payroll batch is built from Timekeeping data, reviewed, and carried through sign-off before release. It supports the **Daily, Weekly, and Monthly** pay frequencies; it can be generated either as a **consolidated register** (all workers, with a per-project charging breakdown at the foot) or as a **per-project register** (one Service Order) from the same underlying data; and it enforces the **Prepared → Checked → Verified → Approved → Paid** sign-off lifecycle. Its approved output is the **single source** for payslips, the alpha list, and the project charging report.
