**PS-001** — The system shall organize payroll into **batches**, where one batch is defined by a pay frequency, a payroll period, and a register scope, and shall hold **one summary line per employee** in that batch.

**PS-002** — The system shall support three pay frequencies (Daily, Weekly, Monthly) and shall render the register column set appropriate to the selected frequency.

**PS-003** — The system shall generate either a **Consolidated** register (all workers, with a project charging breakdown) or a **Per-Project** register (one Service Order), both derived from the same computed data.

##### Data model overview

The Payroll Summary is composed of **five record types**: the **Payroll Batch** (header), the **Payroll Summary Line** (one per employee), the **Daily Earnings Detail** (the per-day manhours build-up, where project charging is recorded), the **Charging Allocation** (labor cost rolled up per project), and the **Workflow Log** (the sign-off trail). The field dictionaries follow.

##### 1. Payroll Batch (header record)

| Field                         | Type                                                      | Source      | Description                                                                                                               |
| ----------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------- | ----------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `batch_id`                    | Integer (PK)                                              | System      | Unique batch identifier.                                                                                                  |
| `pay_frequency`               | Enum {Daily, Weekly, Monthly}                             | User        | Frequency for this batch. Drives column set and deduction calendar.                                                       |
| `period_start`                | Date                                                      | User        | First date of the payroll period (e.g., 2026-05-17).                                                                      |
| `period_end`                  | Date                                                      | User        | Last date of the period (e.g., 2026-05-23).                                                                               |
| `cutoff_label`                | String                                                    | Settings    | Cutoff this batch maps to (15th / 30th, or week number) — selects which contributions apply via the deduction calendar.   |
| `register_scope`              | Enum {Consolidated, Per-Project}                          | User        | Whether this batch is the consolidated register or a single-project register.                                             |
| `project_so_no`               | String (FK, nullable)                                     | User        | Service Order / project code (e.g., GARCIA 3 — SO# 25-10-346). Required when scope = Per-Project; null when Consolidated. |
| `section`                     | String                                                    | Master data | Section/group (e.g., Workshop).                                                                                           |
| `pay_method`                  | Enum {Cash}                                               | Fixed       | Disbursement method. Cash for now.                                                                                        |
| `status`                      | Enum {Draft, Prepared, Checked, Verified, Approved, Paid} | Workflow    | Current batch status; controls editability.                                                                               |
| `total_workers`               | Integer                                                   | Computed    | Count of summary lines (e.g., 13).                                                                                        |
| `total_gross`                 | Decimal(12,2)                                             | Computed    | Sum of line gross pay. Control total.                                                                                     |
| `total_deductions`            | Decimal(12,2)                                             | Computed    | Sum of line total deductions. Control total.                                                                              |
| `total_net`                   | Decimal(12,2)                                             | Computed    | Sum of line net pay. Control total.                                                                                       |
| `prepared_by` / `prepared_at` | String / DateTime                                         | Workflow    | User and timestamp at "Prepared."                                                                                         |
| `checked_by` / `checked_at`   | String / DateTime                                         | Workflow    | User and timestamp at "Checked."                                                                                          |
| `verified_by` / `verified_at` | String / DateTime                                         | Workflow    | User and timestamp at "Verified" (internal check stage for now).                                                          |
| `approved_by` / `approved_at` | String / DateTime                                         | Workflow    | User and timestamp at "Approved."                                                                                         |
| `created_at` / `updated_at`   | DateTime                                                  | System      | Audit timestamps.                                                                                                         |

**PS-010** — The system shall require `project_so_no` when `register_scope` is `Per-Project` and shall leave it null when `Consolidated`.

**PS-011** — The system shall recompute `total_workers`, `total_gross`, `total_deductions`, and `total_net` whenever any summary line changes, while the batch is editable.

##### 2. Payroll Summary Line (one per employee)

| Field                               | Type          | Source                           | Description                                                                                                       |
| ----------------------------------- | ------------- | -------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `line_id`                           | Integer (PK)  | System                           | Unique line identifier.                                                                                           |
| `batch_id`                          | Integer (FK)  | System                           | Owning batch.                                                                                                     |
| `line_no`                           | Integer       | System                           | Display sequence (the "No." / "L/N" column).                                                                      |
| `employee_id`                       | Integer (FK)  | Master data                      | Employee reference.                                                                                               |
| `employee_name`                     | String        | Master data                      | Display name.                                                                                                     |
| `rate_per_day`                      | Decimal(12,2) | Master data                      | Daily rate (e.g., 695.00); monthly basic for monthly-paid staff.                                                  |
| **— Earnings —**                    |               |                                  |                                                                                                                   |
| `basic_pay`                         | Decimal(12,2) | Computed (Daily Earnings Detail) | Regular-day basic, rolled up from per-day amounts.                                                                |
| `overtime_pay`                      | Decimal(12,2) | Computed                         | Sum of OT amounts across the period.                                                                              |
| `holiday_pay`                       | Decimal(12,2) | Computed                         | Sum of regular/special holiday premiums.                                                                          |
| `night_diff_pay`                    | Decimal(12,2) | Computed                         | Sum of night-differential amounts.                                                                                |
| `allowance`                         | Decimal(12,2) | Master data / entry              | Meal allowance. Non-taxable; excluded from contribution and tax base.                                             |
| `other_adjustments`                 | Decimal(12,2) | Entry                            | Other earnings/adjustments ("OTHERS / ADJUSTMENTS"). Each adjustment flagged taxable or non-taxable.              |
| `total_earnings`                    | Decimal(12,2) | Computed                         | `basic + overtime + holiday + night_diff + allowance + other_adjustments`.                                        |
| **— Attendance deductions —**       |               |                                  |                                                                                                                   |
| `absences_days`                     | Decimal(6,2)  | Timekeeping                      | Count of absence days.                                                                                            |
| `absences_amount`                   | Decimal(12,2) | Computed                         | Peso value of absences.                                                                                           |
| `lates_undertime_min`               | Decimal(8,2)  | Timekeeping                      | Minutes/hours of lates + undertime (per tardiness policy in Settings).                                            |
| `lates_undertime_amount`            | Decimal(12,2) | Computed                         | Peso value of lates/undertime.                                                                                    |
| `gross_pay`                         | Decimal(12,2) | Computed                         | `total_earnings − absences_amount − lates_undertime_amount`. (Matches "GROSS PAY" on legacy sheets.)              |
| **— Statutory & loan deductions —** |               |                                  |                                                                                                                   |
| `sss_contribution`                  | Decimal(12,2) | Settings + calendar              | Employee SSS share, if scheduled this cutoff.                                                                     |
| `philhealth_contribution`           | Decimal(12,2) | Settings + calendar              | Employee PhilHealth share, if scheduled.                                                                          |
| `pagibig_contribution`              | Decimal(12,2) | Settings + calendar              | Employee Pag-IBIG share, if scheduled.                                                                            |
| `withholding_tax`                   | Decimal(12,2) | Settings (TRAIN)                 | Income tax withheld ("ITW"). See SET-031 tax-frequency open item.                                                 |
| `sss_loan`                          | Decimal(12,2) | Loans tab                        | SSS loan amortization, if scheduled.                                                                              |
| `pagibig_loan`                      | Decimal(12,2) | Loans tab                        | Pag-IBIG loan amortization, if scheduled.                                                                         |
| `company_loan`                      | Decimal(12,2) | Loans tab                        | Company loan amortization.                                                                                        |
| `cash_advance`                      | Decimal(12,2) | Loans tab                        | Regular cash advance recovery.                                                                                    |
| `other_deductions`                  | Decimal(12,2) | Entry                            | Any other deduction.                                                                                              |
| `total_deductions`                  | Decimal(12,2) | Computed                         | `absences + lates/undertime + all contributions + withholding_tax + all loans + cash_advance + other_deductions`. |
| **— Result —**                      |               |                                  |                                                                                                                   |
| `net_pay`                           | Decimal(12,2) | Computed                         | `total_earnings − total_deductions`. ("NET PAYROLL".)                                                             |

**PS-020** — The system shall populate `basic_pay`, `overtime_pay`, `holiday_pay`, and `night_diff_pay` by rolling up the Daily Earnings Detail records for the employee within the batch period.

**PS-021** — The system shall treat `allowance` (meal allowance) as **non-taxable**: it is included in `total_earnings` and `net_pay` but **excluded from the contribution base and from taxable income**.

**PS-022** — The system shall populate `sss_contribution`, `philhealth_contribution`, `pagibig_contribution`, `sss_loan`, `pagibig_loan`, and `cash_advance` **only when the deduction calendar (Settings 5.2.1 Section 5) assigns them to the current cutoff**; otherwise the field is zero/blank.

##### 3. Daily Earnings Detail (per employee, per day — the build-up)

This is the manhours distribution from the Personnel Payroll Information sheet. **Project charging is recorded here**, one entry per worked day, which is how a worker's pay splits across projects.

| Field               | Type          | Source      | Description                                                                                                                |
| ------------------- | ------------- | ----------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `detail_id`         | Integer (PK)  | System      | Unique identifier.                                                                                                         |
| `line_id`           | Integer (FK)  | System      | Owning summary line (employee + batch).                                                                                    |
| `work_date`         | Date          | Timekeeping | The day worked (e.g., 2026-05-20).                                                                                         |
| `charge_project_so` | String (FK)   | Timekeeping | Project / SO# the day's labor is charged to (e.g., NORECO II / SIKATUNA).                                                  |
| `day_type`          | Enum          | Timekeeping | Day classification (Regular Day, Rest Day, Special Holiday, Regular Holiday, etc.) — selects the multiplier from Settings. |
| `reg_hours`         | Decimal(6,2)  | Timekeeping | Regular hours worked that day.                                                                                             |
| `ot_hours`          | Decimal(6,2)  | Timekeeping | Overtime hours that day.                                                                                                   |
| `night_diff_hours`  | Decimal(6,2)  | Timekeeping | Night-shift hours that day.                                                                                                |
| `applied_rate`      | Decimal(12,2) | Master data | Rate per day/hour used.                                                                                                    |
| `reg_multiplier`    | Decimal(6,4)  | Settings    | Regular multiplier for the day type (e.g., 1.00, 1.30).                                                                    |
| `ot_multiplier`     | Decimal(6,4)  | Settings    | OT multiplier (e.g., 1.25, 1.69).                                                                                          |
| `basic_amount`      | Decimal(12,2) | Computed    | Regular-hours pay for the day.                                                                                             |
| `overtime_amount`   | Decimal(12,2) | Computed    | OT-hours pay for the day.                                                                                                  |
| `night_diff_amount` | Decimal(12,2) | Computed    | Night-differential pay for the day.                                                                                        |
| `day_total`         | Decimal(12,2) | Computed    | `basic_amount + overtime_amount + night_diff_amount`.                                                                      |

**PS-030** — The system shall record one Daily Earnings Detail entry per employee per worked day, tagged with the project / SO# the labor is charged to, and shall compute each amount as `hours × applied_rate × the applicable multiplier from Settings`.

**PS-031** — When an employee works across multiple projects within one period, the system shall allocate that employee's labor cost to each project according to the actual manhours recorded per day, and shall present the employee's full pay in the consolidated register.

##### 4. Charging Allocation (derived, per employee per project)

| Field              | Type          | Source                | Description                                |
| ------------------ | ------------- | --------------------- | ------------------------------------------ |
| `alloc_id`         | Integer (PK)  | System                | Unique identifier.                         |
| `batch_id`         | Integer (FK)  | System                | Owning batch.                              |
| `line_id`          | Integer (FK)  | System                | Employee summary line.                     |
| `project_so`       | String (FK)   | Daily Earnings Detail | Project / SO# charged.                     |
| `allocated_hours`  | Decimal(8,2)  | Computed              | Sum of manhours charged to this project.   |
| `allocated_amount` | Decimal(12,2) | Computed              | Sum of labor cost charged to this project. |

**PS-040** — The system shall aggregate Daily Earnings Detail by project to produce the **charging breakdown** (the per-project totals shown at the foot of the consolidated register, e.g., Garcia 3, Greenbelt, Luelco-Bacnotan, Metrodragon, Noreco II, Sikatuna, Workshop).

##### 5. Workflow Log (sign-off trail)

| Field                       | Type                   | Source   | Description                              |
| --------------------------- | ---------------------- | -------- | ---------------------------------------- |
| `log_id`                    | Integer (PK)           | System   | Unique identifier.                       |
| `batch_id`                  | Integer (FK)           | System   | Owning batch.                            |
| `from_status` / `to_status` | Enum                   | Workflow | Status transition.                       |
| `action`                    | Enum {Advance, Revert} | Workflow | Direction of the transition.             |
| `acted_by`                  | String                 | System   | User performing the action.              |
| `acted_at`                  | DateTime               | System   | Timestamp.                               |
| `remarks`                   | String                 | Entry    | Optional note (e.g., reason for revert). |

**PS-050** — The system shall advance a batch through **Draft → Prepared → Checked → Verified → Approved → Paid**, recording the user and timestamp at each stage.

**PS-051** — The system shall **lock all summary lines from editing once a batch reaches Approved**; re-opening shall require a logged status revert with remarks.

##### Computation order (authoritative)

For each employee in a batch the system computes in this sequence:

1. **Roll up Daily Earnings Detail** → `basic_pay`, `overtime_pay`, `holiday_pay`, `night_diff_pay`.
2. **`total_earnings`** = `basic + overtime + holiday + night_diff + allowance + other_adjustments`.
3. **Apply attendance deductions:** `gross_pay` = `total_earnings − absences_amount − lates_undertime_amount`.
4. **Determine contribution base** = regular basic compensation, excluding non-taxable allowance; compute SSS, PhilHealth, Pag-IBIG employee shares from Settings — but **include only those scheduled for this cutoff**.
5. **`taxable_income`** = (taxable earnings after attendance deductions) − mandatory employee contributions. _(Meal allowance excluded.)_
6. **`withholding_tax`** = TRAIN table applied to `taxable_income`.
7. **Add loan and cash-advance amortizations scheduled for this cutoff** (from the Loans tab, Section 5.2.2 — subject to the skip + officer review rule when net pay is insufficient).
8. **`total_deductions`** = attendance deductions + contributions + withholding_tax + loans + cash_advance + other_deductions.
9. **`net_pay`** = `total_earnings − total_deductions`.

##### Print and PDF export

**PS-060** — The system shall provide a **print-friendly view** of the Payroll Summary that preserves the legacy register layout (control header at the top, summary lines in the middle, totals and per-project charging breakdown at the foot) and is suitable both for review during the workflow and for permanent filing post-Approval.

**PS-061** — Both the **Consolidated** register (all workers with the per-project charging breakdown at the foot) and the **Per-Project** register (one Service Order) shall be printable, rendered with the column set appropriate to the batch's `pay_frequency` (Daily / Weekly / Monthly) and `register_scope`.

**PS-062** — The system shall support **PDF export** of the Payroll Summary in addition to direct printing, so a batch can be archived, emailed, or shared without going to paper. PDF export shall preserve the same layout as the printed view.

**PS-063** — The printed register shall include, at minimum: the batch header (Payroll Period Covered, Pay Frequency, Cutoff label, Register Scope, Project SO# where applicable, Section, Status), every summary line in `line_no` order, all column totals (Total Workers, Total Gross, Total Deductions, Total Net), the per-project charging breakdown at the foot (when scope = Consolidated), and the four workflow-stage signature lines (Prepared by / Checked by / Verified by / Approved by — auto-populated with the name and timestamp of the user at each stage).

**PS-064** — Recommended paper layout for the printed register: **Landscape orientation** on **Legal (8.5″ × 14″) or Long bond (8.5″ × 13″)** paper, since the legacy register sheets are wide and the multi-column layout needs the horizontal room. The system shall auto-fit columns to the chosen page width so the entire row prints on a single line; long employee names may wrap within their column.

##### Open item carried forward

- **SET-031 — tax-table frequency.** The TRAIN table in Settings (Section 5.2.1 Section 3) is the monthly table; employees are paid Daily, Weekly, or Semi-monthly. The BIR publishes separate tables for each frequency. The system must either store all four BIR tables and apply the one matching each employee's pay frequency, or annualize taxable income and derive the period tax from the monthly/annual table. This decision directly affects `withholding_tax` in step 6 of the computation order above and must be resolved before the engine is finalized.

#### 5.2.4 Payslip sub-tab

**Purpose.** The per-employee output of an **Approved** Payroll Batch (Section 5.2.3). The Payslip is a **derived view**, not a separate data store — every value on it comes from the Batch header, the employee's Summary Line, the Daily Earnings Detail rolled up by pay-rate category, and the Loans tab's running balances. Its layout matches the legacy JCE payslip exactly, so the visual experience is familiar to employees and to the payroll team.

**PSL-001** — The system shall generate one Payslip per employee per Approved Payroll Batch. Payslips shall not be generated for batches whose status is not Approved or Paid.

**PSL-002** — Each Payslip shall display **all multiplier rows even when the hours value is zero**, so the employee can see at a glance that those categories were considered (not omitted) for the period.

**PSL-003** — The Payslip's Balances section shall show **outstanding loan balances as of after this period's deductions have been applied** — i.e., the post-payslip running balance from the Loans ledger (Section 5.2.2).

**PSL-004** — Payslip values shall be **read-only**. Corrections require reverting the batch to an editable status, adjusting the Summary Line or Daily Earnings Detail, re-advancing through the workflow, and regenerating the Payslip — the same audit trail as any other batch change (Section 5.2.3 PS-050 / PS-051).

##### Header

| Field                  | Source                                | Example                                             |
| ---------------------- | ------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------- |
| Company Name           | Constant                              | JC ELECTROFIELDS POWER SYSTEMS INC.                 |
| Payroll Period Covered | Batch `period_start` and `period_end` | MAY 06 – 20, 2026                                   |
| Employee Name          | Employee record (top-right and main)  | DELA CRUZ, JUAN MIGUEL A.                           |
| Office / Department    | Employee record (department)          | Head Office – FINANCE AND ADMINISTRATIVE DEPARTMENT |
| Daily Rate             | Employee record (`rate_per_day`)      | 695.00                                              |
| Rate / Hour            | Computed: `Daily Rate ÷ 8`            | 86.88                                               |

##### Earnings — Basic Pay block

Each row shows the pay-rate category, its multiplier from Settings (5.2.1 Section 1), the hours worked under that category (rolled up from Daily Earnings Detail), and the resulting PHP amount.

| Pay-rate row                     | Multiplier        | Hours  | Computation            | Amount (example) |
| -------------------------------- | ----------------- | ------ | ---------------------- | ---------------- |
| Total Work Hours _(Regular Day)_ | 1.00              | 104.00 | hours × Rate/Hr × 1.00 | 9,035.00         |
| Sunday _(Rest Day)_              | 1.30              | —      | —                      | —                |
| Special Holiday                  | 1.30              | —      | —                      | —                |
| Regular Holiday                  | 2.00              | —      | —                      | —                |
| Rest Day – Spc Hol               | 1.50              | —      | —                      | —                |
| Rest Day – Reg Hol               | 2.60              | —      | —                      | —                |
| Double Holiday                   | 3.00              | —      | —                      | —                |
| R.Day – Double Hol               | 3.90              | —      | —                      | —                |
| Travel Hours                     | 1.00              | —      | —                      | —                |
| Night Diff'l                     | (computed × 1.10) | —      | —                      | —                |
| **TOTAL BASIC**                  |                   |        |                        | **9,035.00**     |

##### Earnings — Overtime Pay block

| Pay-rate row              | Multiplier        | Hours | Amount (example) |
| ------------------------- | ----------------- | ----- | ---------------- |
| Regular OT                | 1.25              | 4.00  | 434.38           |
| Sunday OT _(Rest Day OT)_ | 1.69              | —     | —                |
| Special Holiday OT        | 1.69              | —     | —                |
| Regular Holiday OT        | 2.60              | —     | —                |
| Rest Day – Spc Hol OT     | 1.95              | —     | —                |
| Rest Day – Reg Hol OT     | 2.99              | —     | —                |
| Double Holiday OT         | 3.90              | —     | —                |
| R.Day – Double Hol OT     | 5.07              | —     | —                |
| Night Diff'l OT           | (computed × 1.10) | —     | —                |
| **TOTAL OVERTIME**        |                   |       | **434.38**       |

##### Earnings — Allowances block

| Item                  | Source                                                 | Amount (example) |
| --------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------ | ---------------- |
| Allowance _(general)_ | Summary Line `allowance` portion                       | 1,000.00         |
| Project Allowance     | Summary Line allowance breakdown                       | —                |
| Meal Allowance        | Summary Line allowance breakdown                       | —                |
| Others                | Summary Line `other_adjustments` (non-taxable portion) | —                |

**TOTAL EARNINGS** = Total Basic + Total Overtime + Allowances. _(Example: 9,035.00 + 434.38 + 1,000.00 = 10,469.38.)_

##### Deductions — Mandatory Contribution block

| Item                   | Source                                 | Amount (example)                              |
| ---------------------- | -------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------- |
| SSS                    | Summary Line `sss_contribution`        | 900.00                                        |
| PhilHealth             | Summary Line `philhealth_contribution` | — _(zero this cutoff per Deduction Calendar)_ |
| Pag-IBIG               | Summary Line `pagibig_contribution`    | — _(zero this cutoff per Deduction Calendar)_ |
| **TOTAL CONTRIBUTION** |                                        | **900.00**                                    |

##### Deductions — Loan Payment block

> _Note on the legacy heading._ "Loan Payment" on the printed payslip groups several deduction types together for display familiarity, but the underlying data model (Section 5.2.3) keeps each one as a distinct field. The grouping below is a **display** convention, not a data structure.

| Item                    | Source                                                                    | Amount (example) |
| ----------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------- |
| SSS Loan                | Summary Line `sss_loan`                                                   | —                |
| Pag-IBIG Loan           | Summary Line `pagibig_loan`                                               | 641.91           |
| W/Holding Tax           | Summary Line `withholding_tax`                                            | —                |
| Others                  | Summary Line `other_deductions`                                           | 2,741.23         |
| Cash Advances           | Summary Line `cash_advance` (regular CA recovery)                         | —                |
| C.A 1st & 3rd Week      | Summary Line `cash_advance` (week-1 & week-3 CA recovery, weekly cutoffs) | —                |
| Unreturned Changed      | Summary Line `other_deductions` (specific JCE deduction line)             | —                |
| **TOTAL LOAN PAYMENTS** |                                                                           | **3,383.14**     |

**TOTAL DEDUCTIONS** = Total Contribution + Total Loan Payments. _(Example: 900.00 + 3,383.14 = 4,283.14.)_

##### Net Pay

**NET PAY** = Total Earnings − Total Deductions. _(Example: 10,469.38 − 4,283.14 = 6,186.24.)_

##### Balances (outstanding loan balances after this period)

| Loan type     | Source                                                     | Amount (example) |
| ------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------- |
| SSS Loan      | Loans ledger running balance after this period's deduction | 36,938.42        |
| Pag-IBIG Loan | Loans ledger running balance                               | 6,419.10         |
| Cash Advances | Loans ledger running balance                               | 8,223.60         |

##### Signature block

| Field       | Description                                                                            |
| ----------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Received By | Employee's printed name (auto-populated); employee signs to acknowledge receipt of pay |
| Date        | Blank line for employee to write the date of receipt                                   |

##### Two anomalies surfaced by the actual payslip — please confirm

1. **Travel Hours reappeared in Basic Pay.** The legacy payslip has a "Travel Hours" line in the Basic Pay block with multiplier 1.00. However, **Travel Time was removed from the Manhours Distribution in v1.9** ("I want to remove the travel time"). Either (a) the legacy payslip template still carries the line but it's always zero and the field can stay only as a display row, or (b) Travel Hours has been reinstated and Timekeeping (Section 4.2) needs to bring back the Travel Time bucket. **Please confirm.**

2. **Rest Day – Reg Hol OT multiplier on the payslip is 2.99.** Per the DOLE multiplicative matrix (Section 5.2.1 Section 1 and Section 4.2), this combination should be **3.38** (2.60 × 1.3). The payslip's 2.99 doesn't match either the DOLE multiplicative or a clean additive formula. Possible explanations: (a) a legacy typo on the printed template that has gone unnoticed because the row is usually zero hours, or (b) a JCE-specific policy that diverges from DOLE on this particular combination. **Please confirm so the Settings matrix and the payslip render the same number.**

##### Computation linkage

The Payslip's values map directly onto Section 5.2.3 fields and Section 5.2.1 multipliers:

- **Basic Pay rows**: each row sums Daily Earnings Detail entries where the row's day-type matches, multiplied by the row's multiplier × Rate/Hr.
- **Overtime Pay rows**: same logic against `ot_hours` and the OT multiplier.
- **Allowances**: from the Summary Line allowance fields (split into general / project / meal as displayed).
- **Total Earnings**: equals Summary Line `total_earnings`.
- **Mandatory Contribution rows**: from Summary Line `sss_contribution`, `philhealth_contribution`, `pagibig_contribution`.
- **Loan Payment rows**: from Summary Line `sss_loan`, `pagibig_loan`, `withholding_tax`, `other_deductions`, `cash_advance` (split into the regular CA and the week-1&3 CA per JCE's display convention).
- **Total Deductions**: equals Summary Line `total_deductions`.
- **Net Pay**: equals Summary Line `net_pay`.
- **Balances**: queried live from the Loans ledger (Section 5.2.2) at the moment the Payslip is rendered, reflecting the running balance after this period's deductions are posted.

##### Print and PDF export

**PSL-005** — The system shall provide a **print-friendly view** of the Payslip that preserves the legacy layout exactly — header (company, period, employee, dept, Daily Rate, Rate/Hr), Earnings section with the three blocks (Basic Pay, Overtime Pay, Allowances) and Total Earnings, Deductions section with the two blocks (Mandatory Contribution, Loan Payment) and Total Deductions, Net Pay, Balances section, and the Received-By / Date signature block at the foot.

**PSL-006** — Each Payslip shall print on **its own page**. The system shall **not** split a Payslip across two pages; if a Payslip would otherwise overflow a page, the system shall scale the layout to fit rather than break it.

**PSL-007** — The system shall support **PDF export** of an individual Payslip and **bulk PDF export** of all Approved Payslips for a batch as a **single multi-page PDF** — one Payslip per page, in `line_no` order from the parent Summary — so a payroll officer can hand off the entire pay run for printing in one operation.

**PSL-008** — Bulk printing shall allow the payroll officer to print **all Approved Payslips for a batch** in one action, with each Payslip starting on a new page. Payslips for batches not in Approved or Paid status shall not be available for bulk print (mirrors PSL-001).

**PSL-009** — Recommended paper layout for printed Payslips: **Portrait orientation** on **Letter (8.5″ × 11″) or A4** paper — the Payslip's two-column Earnings/Deductions layout fits cleanly on a standard portrait page. The system shall preserve margins so the printed Payslip can be inserted into a payslip envelope or folded for distribution without losing content.

**PSL-010** — Print and PDF output shall be available for the employee's record as well as the payroll officer's — when an employee needs a re-issue of a past Payslip (e.g., for a loan application), HR shall be able to reprint or re-export any past Approved Payslip without modifying the underlying batch.

---

### 5.3 Sales _(preliminary scope — open items below)_

**Purpose.** Track revenue events — every **billing statement** JCE issues to a client for work performed against an awarded Sales Order. The Sales tab is the canonical record of _what was billed, to whom, for which project, at what amount, and how the amount is classified for VAT_. It feeds Collections (5.4) for tracking which billings have been paid, and it feeds Project Costing (5.8) and Reporting (5.9) as the revenue side of project profitability.

**Source document.** _Received._ JCE's **"BILLING STATEMENT SUMMARY"** spreadsheet (for the year 2025). The legacy sheet is a flat register with one row per billing statement issued, organized by Date, with separate DEBIT and CREDIT amount columns reflecting the underlying accounting entries.

**The DEBIT / CREDIT accounting structure.** Each billing statement records a journal entry in the books — when an invoice is issued, the accounting entry is roughly: **debit Trade Receivable** (the amount the client now owes JCE) and **credit Sales/Revenue** (split into VATable Sales, Zero-Rated Sales, or VAT-Exempt Sales depending on the client and the transaction) plus **credit Output VAT** (the 12% VAT JCE owes the BIR on VATable sales). The spreadsheet's column layout mirrors this — DEBIT columns on the left (Trade Receivable, Down Payment, Retention) and CREDIT columns on the right (VAT Sales, VAT, Zero Rated, VAT-Exempt). The system stores both sides of each statement so the books balance automatically when statements roll into Journal Voucher (5.7) and Reporting (5.9).

#### Record fields — Billing Statement

> _Unified data model, two input forms._ Per Bryan's v1.47 decision (Option B), the Sales sub-tab presents **two separate input entry points** — "Issue Service Invoice" and "Issue Statement of Account" — each opening its own focused screen matching the corresponding paper form. Both produce records in **the same Billing Statement table** distinguished by a `document_type` field. The legacy 2025 register-style summary, Collections (5.4), Project Costing (5.8), and Reporting (5.9) all see one unified list of billing statements regardless of which form produced them.

_Identification_

- **`document_type`** — single-select: **Service Invoice** · **Statement of Account**. Set at creation by which entry point the user used (not user-editable after issue). Drives which print template renders, which validation rules apply at Issue time, and the form's labels on the input screen.
- **Date** — the date the billing statement was issued (e.g., 15-Jul-25).
- **Document Number** — for Service Invoice: the SI No. from the pre-printed BIR booklet (e.g., `0406`). For Statement of Account: the internally assigned SOA No. _(Open question carried forward: SI numbering is manual from booklet; SOA numbering — system-sequential or manual — still to confirm.)_
- **OR / CR Reference** — Official Receipt or Credit Memo reference. In the legacy data: prefixes like `AR-`, `CR-`, or a plain receipt code (e.g., `AR-33`, `CR-1563`, or just `R`). _(Open question: confirm exactly what this column tracks — receipt-side reference linked back when the client pays, or initial billing-side reference, or both at different times.)_

_Client and project_

- **Company Name (Client)** — the client being billed (e.g., `3M MF AND KW CORP.`, `ANHUI JCE CONSORTIUM`, `ASIAN GREENENERGY CORP.`). Currently free text (same convention as Sales Order / Offers / Quotation). _(See open question on promoting to a Clients entity.)_
- **Sales Order No. (SO#)** — link to the Sales Order in BDD (Section 9.3) the billing is against. Format `YY-MM-XXX` matching the system primary key. The legacy data also has occasional sub-letter suffixes (e.g., `25-05-309A`) — _(open question: what does the letter suffix mean in the SO# context, and does it always match an existing Sales Order record exactly?)_
- **Client TIN** — the client's Tax Identification Number (e.g., `640-947-734-000`, or `N/A` when not yet known). _(Open question: store on a Clients master record rather than per-statement, since it doesn't change per billing.)_

_Particulars (what the billing is for)_

- **Particulars** — the description of the billing event. Legacy values seen: `1ST BILLING`, `1 LOT`, `FINAL BILLING`, `50% DOWN PAYMENT`. _(Open question: should this be a controlled dropdown — e.g., `Down Payment` / `Progress Billing (Nth)` / `Final Billing` / `Retention` / `Adjustment` — or stay free text?)_

_DEBIT side (what the client owes / what we record on the balance sheet asset side)_

| Field              | Source | Description                                                                                                                       |
| ------------------ | ------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `trade_receivable` | Entry  | Total amount the client owes JCE for this billing. The principal AR figure.                                                       |
| `down_payment`     | Entry  | When the statement specifically records a down-payment receipt (e.g., 50% DP), the DP portion of the trade receivable.            |
| `retention`        | Entry  | When the statement carries a retention amount (a portion of payment held back per contract, released on completion / acceptance). |

_CREDIT side (the revenue and tax classification)_

| Field        | Source               | Description                                                                                                                                                       |
| ------------ | -------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `vat_sales`  | Entry                | The taxable-sale portion of the billing (the amount on which 12% VAT is computed).                                                                                |
| `vat`        | Computed (typically) | The 12% Output VAT amount, normally `vat_sales × 0.12`. _(Open question: auto-compute from `vat_sales`, or entered manually?)_                                    |
| `zero_rated` | Entry                | The portion of the billing classified as Zero-Rated for VAT (e.g., export-equivalent transactions, sales to certain client categories). VAT-applicable but at 0%. |
| `vat_exempt` | Entry                | The portion of the billing classified as VAT-Exempt (transactions outside the VAT system per BIR rules).                                                          |

_Status and lifecycle_

- **Status** — proposed values: **Issued · Partially Paid · Paid · Cancelled · Credited (Credit Memo issued)**. _(Open question: confirm the lifecycle and how partial vs full payment is tracked here vs in Collections 5.4.)_

_System-managed_

- **Created By** / **Created Date** — at issuance.
- **Updated By** / **Updated Date** — on any edit.
- **Audit trail** — change history (cross-cuts with the Audit Logs pattern; for accounting records this matters for compliance).

#### Examples from the legacy data

| Date      | Client                  | SO#        | Particulars      | Trade Receivable | VAT-Exempt    | Zero Rated    | Notes                                       |
| --------- | ----------------------- | ---------- | ---------------- | ---------------- | ------------- | ------------- | ------------------------------------------- |
| 15-Jul-25 | 3M MF AND KW CORP.      | 25-02-274  | 1ST BILLING      | 2,676,165.00     | −2,676,165.00 | —             | VAT-Exempt sale                             |
| 07-Nov-25 | 3M MF AND KW CORP.      | 25-02-274  | 1 LOT            | 1,464,425.00     | (assumed)     | —             | Same SO, second billing                     |
| 02-Jan-25 | ANHUI JCE CONSORTIUM    | 24-08-238  | FINAL BILLING    | 2,457,000.00     | —             | −2,457,000.00 | Zero-Rated (consortium / export-equivalent) |
| 09-Jul-25 | ASIAN GREENENERGY CORP. | 25-05-309A | 50% DOWN PAYMENT | 47,082.00        | —             | −47,082.00    | Zero-Rated, DP receipt                      |

_Reading the numbers:_ the DEBIT (Trade Receivable, positive) and CREDIT (VAT-Exempt or Zero Rated, negative — showing as credit balance) on each row are equal-and-opposite, which is the underlying journal entry's debit-credit balance. The system will store both sides explicitly per statement.

#### Adding a Billing Statement — two input entry points

JCE issues **two physically distinct billing documents** depending on the VAT classification of the sale. _Per Bryan's v1.47 decision (Option B),_ the Sales sub-tab presents these as **two separate input entry points** — not a mode-switch on a single screen, and not separate sub-areas in the navigation either. The two are:

- **"Issue Service Invoice"** — opens an input screen matching the BIR-pre-printed VAT invoice template.
- **"Issue Statement of Account"** — opens an input screen matching the SOA template.

Each screen has its own validation rules, its own print preview, and its own button on the Sales sub-tab. The user picks which document they're issuing by clicking the right button — there is no in-form picker that morphs the screen. Underneath, both write to the same Billing Statement table with `document_type` set accordingly (Section 5.3 → Record fields), so the consolidated register, Collections, Project Costing, and Reporting all see one unified list.

> _Assumption on which to verify with JCE:_ a single billing event is **strictly one or the other** — JCE does not issue both a Service Invoice and a Statement of Account for the same transaction. _(If JCE does occasionally pair the two, the model needs a small adjustment — see open item.)_

The two screens are documented below, each matching its legacy paper form so users transcribing from a paper booklet have one-to-one field correspondence.

##### Entry point 1 — Issue Service Invoice (for VATable Sales)

The Service Invoice is the **BIR-compliant VAT invoice**, issued from a pre-printed, sequentially numbered booklet produced by a BIR-accredited printer (the legacy booklet in evidence is at No. 0406). The user enters the next available number from the physical booklet — the system records what was issued rather than auto-assigning, so the system's numbers track the paper booklet exactly.

**Header fields (one per statement)**

| Field                   | Notes                                                                                                                                                            |
| ----------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Service Invoice No.** | Manually entered from the pre-printed booklet (e.g., `0406`).                                                                                                    |
| **Date**                | Defaults to today; editable.                                                                                                                                     |
| **Customer**            | Selected from the Clients list _(open item — see #8 in 5.3 open items)_; auto-fills Address and TIN.                                                             |
| **Address**             | Auto-filled from the Customer record; editable.                                                                                                                  |
| **TIN**                 | Auto-filled from the Customer record; editable.                                                                                                                  |
| **Terms**               | Free text (e.g., `Net 30`, `COD`, `7-day terms`).                                                                                                                |
| **OSCA / PWD ID #**     | Only filled when the buyer claims a Senior Citizen / Person With Disability discount. _(Rarely applies for JCE's corporate clientele; included for compliance.)_ |
| **SO #**                | _(Added by the system on top of the paper layout.)_ Link to the Sales Order in BDD (9.3) the billing is against.                                                 |

**Line items (multiple rows, dynamically add/remove)**

| Column          | Notes                                                                                                                        |
| --------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Qty**         | Quantity of the item or service.                                                                                             |
| **Unit**        | Unit of measure (`lot`, `set`, `pc`, `hr`, etc.).                                                                            |
| **Description** | What's being billed (e.g., `Supply, delivery, installation testing & commissioning of 10 MVA 67/13.2 KV Power Transformer`). |
| **Unit Price**  | Price per unit in PHP.                                                                                                       |
| **Amount**      | _Computed:_ `Qty × Unit Price`.                                                                                              |

**Totals block (auto-computed from the line items + classification splits)**

| Row                         | Computation                                                                                                                     |
| --------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| VATable Sales               | Sum of line amounts classified as VATable.                                                                                      |
| VAT-Exempt Sales            | Sum of line amounts classified as VAT-Exempt.                                                                                   |
| Zero-Rated Sales            | Sum of line amounts classified as Zero-Rated.                                                                                   |
| VAT Amount                  | `VATable Sales × 0.12` _(open item — see #3 in 5.3 open items: auto-compute vs manual)_.                                        |
| Total Sales (VAT Inclusive) | `VATable Sales + VAT-Exempt Sales + Zero-Rated Sales + VAT Amount`.                                                             |
| Less: VAT                   | Same as VAT Amount above.                                                                                                       |
| Amount Net of VAT           | `Total Sales (VAT Inclusive) − VAT Amount`.                                                                                     |
| Less: SC/PWD Discount       | `Amount Net of VAT × 0.20` when an OSCA/PWD ID is on file; otherwise zero.                                                      |
| Amount Due                  | `Amount Net of VAT − SC/PWD Discount`.                                                                                          |
| Add: VAT                    | VAT amount restored on the discounted base (Philippine SC/PWD rules — discount is on the net amount, VAT applies on the gross). |
| **TOTAL AMOUNT DUE**        | `Amount Due + Add VAT`.                                                                                                         |

**Signature block**

- **Authorized Representative** — selected from the employee database (e.g., the Billing & Collection Officer or Accounting Head); name auto-fills on the printed Service Invoice for the physical signature.

##### Entry point 2 — Issue Statement of Account (for Non-VAT Sales)

The Statement of Account (SOA) is the **simpler non-VAT billing document** — internally numbered, no VAT computation, two-signature approval block reflecting JCE's internal sign-off discipline. _Per Bryan's v1.48 confirmation,_ the SOA input supports two body modes — a **Billing Breakdown** for contract-based progress billings (the common case at JCE's typical contract scale) and a **simple line items** mode for one-shot SOAs that aren't part of a progress-billing structure. The mode is determined automatically by whether the linked Sales Order has its progress-billing percentages (Down Payment %, Retention %, DP Recoupment %) configured.

**Header fields**

| Field         | Notes                                                                                                                                                          |
| ------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **SOA No.**   | _(Open item — see #1 below: system-sequential or manual.)_                                                                                                     |
| **Date**      | Defaults to today; editable.                                                                                                                                   |
| **Billed To** | Selected from the Clients list; auto-fills Address.                                                                                                            |
| **Address**   | Auto-filled from the Customer record; editable.                                                                                                                |
| **SO #**      | Link to the Sales Order in BDD (9.3) the billing is against. When the SO carries progress-billing percentages, the Billing Breakdown mode loads automatically. |

**Body mode A — Billing Breakdown (when the linked SO has progress-billing percentages)**

_Per Bryan's v1.48 sample SOA (Bohol Solar Sikatuna 8th Billing), the Particulars area is a structured progress-billing computation, not free-form text._ The input captures the structured values; the print template renders them in the legacy two-column layout (label/amount rows) within the Particulars cell of the SOA.

_Header section of the breakdown (free text)_

- **Project header text** — single-line free text shown above the breakdown table on the printed SOA (e.g., `This is to bill you for the following: BOHOL SOLAR 44.993MWP SIKATUNA SOLAR POWER PLANT PROJECT`). Defaults to the linked SO's Project Name; editable.

_Context section (auto-populated, read-only, always rendered on print per Bryan's v1.48 confirmation)_

| Field                   | Source                                                       | Example                                                                            |
| ----------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Contract Price**      | SO's Contract Amount (9.3)                                   | `365,000,000.00`                                                                   |
| **Down Payment**        | SO's `Contract Amount × Down Payment %` (9.3)                | `73,000,000.00` (20% of Contract Price)                                            |
| **Prior billings list** | All prior SOAs issued on this SO# (label, percentage, gross) | `6th Billing (6.4662…%)  23,621,967.50`<br>`7th Billing (9.3395…%)  35,261,194.24` |

_This billing — current billing computation (editable inputs)_

| Field                     | Default / Source                                                                                                                                                           | Notes                                                                                                 |
| ------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Billing label**         | Auto-suggested as the next sequential ("8th Billing") based on the count of prior SOAs on this SO#; manually editable per Bryan.                                           | User can override to handle exceptional naming.                                                       |
| **Billing % of Contract** | Manually entered                                                                                                                                                           | The percentage of the Contract Price being billed this round (e.g., `5.4705…%`).                      |
| **Gross amount**          | Computed: `Contract Amount × Billing %`                                                                                                                                    | Editable — entering a Gross amount back-computes the Billing %. The two fields are linked.            |
| **Retention %**           | Default from SO's Retention % (9.3); editable per billing                                                                                                                  | e.g., `10%`                                                                                           |
| **Retention amount**      | Computed: `Gross × Retention %`                                                                                                                                            | e.g., `1,998,351.38`                                                                                  |
| **DP Recoupment %**       | Default from SO's DP Recoupment % (9.3); editable per billing. Suppressed when the cumulative DP recouped already equals the SO's Down Payment Amount (full DP recovered). | e.g., `20%`                                                                                           |
| **DP Recoupment amount**  | Computed: `Gross × DP Recoupment %`                                                                                                                                        | e.g., `3,996,702.76`                                                                                  |
| **Net Amount (Total)**    | Computed: `Gross − Retention − Recoupment`                                                                                                                                 | The figure the client owes in cash for this billing — drives Trade Receivable on the legacy register. |

_Mapping to the legacy DEBIT register columns (Section 5.3 record schema)_

- **trade_receivable** = Net Amount
- **retention** = Retention amount
- **down_payment** = DP Recoupment amount _(reduces the Down Payment liability balance — what the legacy register's Down Payment column tracks)_

_Print rendering of the Billing Breakdown._ The structured values render in the Particulars cell of the printed SOA as a two-column block:

```
This is to bill you for the following:

BOHOL SOLAR 44.993MWP SIKATUNA SOLAR POWER PLANT PROJECT

  Contract Price                          365,000,000.00
  20% Down Payment                         73,000,000.00
  6th Billing (6.4662…%)                   23,621,967.50
  7th Billing (9.3395…%)                   35,261,194.24
  8th Billing (5.4705…%)                   18,983,513.82
  Less: 10% Retention                       1,998,351.38
  Less: 20% DP: Recoupment                  3,996,702.76
  Total Amount                             13,988,459.67
```

The right-side columns of the printed SOA — **Qty** `1 Lot`, **Unit Cost** `₱ <Net Amount>`, **Total Amount (in PHP)** `₱ <Net Amount>` — are populated automatically from the Net Amount above.

**Body mode B — Simple line items (when the linked SO has no progress-billing structure)**

For SOs that aren't progress-billed (one-shot service work, fixed deliverables), the SOA falls back to the legacy line-items table without the breakdown computation.

| Column                    | Notes                               |
| ------------------------- | ----------------------------------- |
| **Item**                  | Item code or short identifier.      |
| **Particulars**           | Description of what's being billed. |
| **Qty**                   | Quantity.                           |
| **Unit Cost**             | Cost per unit in PHP.               |
| **Total Amount (in PHP)** | Computed: `Qty × Unit Cost`.        |

**Total and amount-in-words**

- **Final Amount** — Mode A: the Net Amount from the Billing Breakdown. Mode B: the sum of line `Total Amount` values. PHP.
- **Amount in Words** — _(open item — see #2 below: auto-generated or manual)_ the Final Amount spelled out (e.g., `***Thirteen Million Nine Hundred Eighty Eight Thousand Four Hundred Fifty Nine & 67/100 Pesos Only***`), styled with the legacy asterisk delimiters.

**Signature block — two signatories**

- **Authorized by** — Billing and Collection Officer; selected from the employee database. _(Legacy paper shows DANILO G. BALWIT as the current incumbent.)_
- **Approved by** — Accounting Head; selected from the employee database. _(Legacy paper shows GRACE BRANDEZ as the current incumbent.)_

**Note on Service Invoice progress-billing.** _Open per Bryan's v1.48 reply ("no example for VAT sales order")._ The Service Invoice currently retains the simple line-items + VAT-classification model documented above. If JCE later issues progress billings on VAT-able contract work using the same Contract → Gross → Retention → DP Recoupment pattern, the Billing Breakdown sub-form can extend to the SI side with the addition of a per-line VAT classification picker on the Gross amount. Awaiting a sample to confirm the structure.

##### UX considerations — shared across both input entry points

The two paper forms are physical artifacts with no defaults, no validation, and no math help. The digital input screens should remove the friction those papers create while staying visually familiar — the goal is "looks like the paper, works like a modern form." The UX behaviors below apply to both entry points (Issue Service Invoice and Issue Statement of Account) unless noted otherwise.

1. **Two entry-point buttons on the Sales sub-tab.** A toolbar at the top of the Sales register shows **`+ Issue Service Invoice`** and **`+ Issue Statement of Account`** as side-by-side actions. The user picks which document they're issuing by clicking the right button. No in-form picker, no morphing screen.
2. **Smart prefill on SO# selection.** Picking the Sales Order auto-fills Customer (Billed To), Address, TIN (Service Invoice only — the SOA does not display a TIN field), and suggests a Particulars value based on the SO's Description (editable). User confirms or overrides; the rest of the form populates around that anchor.
3. **Live computation.** Every total updates as the user types into the line items or (on the Service Invoice) the per-line VAT classification picks. VAT, Net of VAT, SC/PWD Discount, and Total Amount Due recompute on each keystroke or blur.
4. **Per-line VAT classification on Service Invoice only.** Each Service Invoice line item carries a small classification picker (VATable · VAT-Exempt · Zero-Rated) that defaults to the client's typical classification but can be overridden per line — so a mixed-classification Service Invoice is possible. The Totals block aggregates accordingly. _(The SOA has no VAT classification — it's all non-VAT by definition.)_
5. **Dynamic line items.** Add row / delete row buttons in the line-items table; rows reorderable. Empty rows at the end are ignored on save / print.
6. **Save Draft vs Issue.** Both screens support a two-stage save: **Save Draft** (the statement is held in `Draft` status, editable, not yet posted to the books) and **Issue** (the statement is finalized, posted to Trade Receivable, locked from substantive edits if the strict no-edit pattern is adopted — see open item #6 in 5.3).
7. **Live print preview.** A "Preview" pane on the side shows the statement rendered as it will print on paper — the Service Invoice template for the SI screen, the SOA template for the SOA screen. The preview reflects every keystroke; users see what they're about to issue.
8. **Required-field validation at Issue.** Each entry point has its own required-field rules — Service Invoice requires Customer, Date, SI No. (from booklet), TIN, SO#, at least one line item with a non-zero amount; SOA requires Billed To, Date, SOA No., SO#, at least one line item. Save Draft is forgiving — drafts can have missing fields.
9. **Duplicate from previous.** From any past statement, a "Duplicate" action creates a new draft of the **same document type** — i.e., duplicating an SI creates a draft SI, duplicating an SOA creates a draft SOA. Prefilled with everything except the document number and the date.
10. **Print right after Issue.** Issuing a statement opens the print dialog directly (with the option to defer) — matches the paper workflow where issuance and printing are the same act.
11. **One-page rule.** Both forms should fit on one printed page even with the maximum sensible number of line items (paper booklets have ~25 ruled lines). If line items exceed the page, the system warns and offers to split into multiple statements rather than continuing onto a second page (the paper booklet doesn't have a "continued on next page" pattern; a second-page statement is a _separate_ SI/SOA).
12. **Numbering aids.** For Service Invoice, the system shows the **last SI number issued** as a hint when the user enters the new number, catching out-of-sequence entries (e.g., user enters `0408` when the last was `0405` and they should be entering `0406`). For SOA, the system suggests the next sequential SOA number based on past records (assuming SOA numbering is system-sequential — pending open item).

##### Open items for the Billing input form — please confirm

1. **SOA numbering.** Should SOA No. be **system-assigned sequentially** (e.g., `SOA-2026-001`, `SOA-2026-002`) like the Sales Order No. convention, **manually entered** from JCE's internal SOA log, or **prefilled with a suggested next number** that the user can override?

2. **Amount-in-words on SOA.** The legacy form has the amount written out manually. Should the system **auto-generate** the amount in words from the Final Amount (e.g., `***Two Hundred Eighty Six Thousand Six Hundred Forty Only***`) using PHP-locale number-to-words conversion, or should it be **manually typed**? Auto-generation is friendlier but the system has to handle decimals and currency phrasing correctly.

3. **Zero-Rated sales — which form?** A Zero-Rated sale is still a VAT-able transaction (just at 0%) for BIR purposes. Does JCE issue Service Invoices for Zero-Rated sales (the BIR-standard answer), or have they been using Statement of Account for those historically? The legacy 2025 register data shows several Zero-Rated billings (ANHUI JCE CONSORTIUM, ASIAN GREENENERGY) — but doesn't tell us which physical form was issued. _(This shapes which entry point a user clicks for Zero-Rated work.)_

4. **Signatories from the employee database.** The SOA names two specific people — Danilo G. Balwit (Billing & Collection Officer) and Grace Brandez (Accounting Head). Should the system **select these by role from the employee database** (so when the role changes hands, the form updates automatically), or **let the user pick the signatory each time** from a list?

5. **Per-line vs per-statement VAT classification.** The Service Invoice's totals block has separate lines for VATable / VAT-Exempt / Zero-Rated Sales — implying a single Service Invoice can carry **mixed-classification line items**. Confirm that a single Service Invoice in practice ever mixes classifications (e.g., some lines VATable, some VAT-Exempt), or whether each Service Invoice in practice carries a single uniform classification.

6. **OSCA / PWD discount workflow.** When an OSCA/PWD ID is provided, the standard PH rule is 20% off the Net of VAT, with VAT then re-applied on the discounted base. Confirm JCE applies the standard rule (system can fully auto-compute) or has its own variant.

7. **Multiple SO#s per statement.** Can a single Service Invoice or SOA cover line items from **multiple Sales Orders**, or is it strictly one statement per SO? The legacy register data shows one SO# per row, suggesting one-to-one — confirm.

8. **Paired SI + SOA for same transaction.** _Verify the Option B assumption:_ JCE issues either a Service Invoice or a Statement of Account for a given billing event — never both — so the data model only needs `document_type` as a single field per statement. Confirm.

#### Cross-module integrations

- **BDD Sales Order (Section 9.3)** ← Sales. Every billing statement links to a Sales Order by SO#. When a project is awarded and a Sales Order exists, Sales records billings against it. Multiple billings per SO are common (down payment, progress billings, final billing, retention release). _(Open question: does the system validate that total billings against a Sales Order do not exceed the Sales Order's Contract Amount?)_
- **Collections (Section 5.4)** ← Sales. Each issued billing creates a receivable that Collections tracks until paid. The OR/CR reference field is the bridge — once the client pays, an Official Receipt is recorded and the statement's status moves toward Paid.
- **BDD Offer (Section 9.4)** ← indirectly. Bryan's Offer model has a COC Date and Receipt event in its append-only event stream. Final billings here should logically follow COC issuance on the source Offer; the link is via the Sales Order, not a direct Offer reference.
- **Journal Voucher (Section 5.7)** ← Sales. Each statement is effectively a system-generated journal entry; the Journal Voucher tab will reflect the aggregate of issued statements per period.
- **Reporting (Section 5.9)** ← Sales. Revenue rolls up here for project profitability (5.8) and for the formal financial reports.

#### Print and PDF export

**SAL-001** — The system shall produce a printable **Billing Statement** for each row — the formal invoice document JCE issues to the client — with the company letterhead, the statement details (date, SI number, particulars, SO# reference, client TIN), the DEBIT-side amounts presented as the billed total with VAT breakdown, and a signature/acceptance block.

**SAL-002** — The system shall produce a printable **Billing Statement Summary register** for any date range, scoped to a single client, a single Sales Order, or all clients — matching the layout of the legacy 2025 summary spreadsheet so the printed register is recognizable to Accounting.

**SAL-003** — Both outputs shall support **PDF export** in addition to direct printing.

#### Open items — please confirm

1. **Service Invoice Number format and generation.** Is the SI number sequential and system-assigned (e.g., next available number from a series), manually typed from a pre-printed booklet of physical invoices, or some hybrid?

2. **OR/CR column meaning.** What exactly does this column track in the legacy data — the Official Receipt number from the client's eventual payment, the Credit Memo number when one is issued, or both at different stages of the same row?

3. **VAT auto-compute.** Should the system auto-compute the 12% Output VAT from `vat_sales` (= `vat_sales × 0.12`), or is `vat` always manually entered to allow for non-standard cases?

4. **Particulars vocabulary.** Should the Particulars field be a controlled dropdown (Down Payment · 1st Progress Billing · 2nd Progress Billing · ... · Final Billing · Retention · Adjustment), or stay free text? The legacy data is consistent enough that a dropdown would tighten data quality without losing flexibility.

5. **Status lifecycle.** Proposed states: Issued · Partially Paid · Paid · Cancelled · Credited. Confirm — and confirm how partial payment is tracked (incrementally on the statement, or as a sequence of OR entries on the Collections side that the statement just references).

6. **Strict no-edit pattern (like Offers)?** Billing statements are formal tax/accounting documents — once issued and given to a client, the _substantive_ terms (amount, VAT classification, SO# linkage) should arguably be immutable, with corrections going through Credit Memos rather than edits. Apply the same immutable-record + event-stream pattern as Offers (9.4) and Quotation (9.5)? Or normal edit pattern with audit log?

7. **Contract-amount validation against the linked Sales Order.** Should the system warn (or block) if the cumulative billings against a Sales Order exceed the Sales Order's `contract_amount`? This catches overruns and double-billing.

8. **Clients as a maintained entity.** With Sales now joining Quotation in needing client information (name, TIN, possibly billing address), the case for a maintained Clients entity (currently free text per the Sales Order decision in v1.27) grows. Promote? Or stay free text?

9. **Sales Order sub-letter suffix.** Some legacy SO# entries have a letter suffix (e.g., `25-05-309A`). What does the letter mean in the SO# context — a variant of the parent SO, a sub-project, or something else? And should the Sales tab enforce that the SO# matches an existing Sales Order record?

### 5.4 Collections _(preliminary scope — open items below)_

**Purpose.** Track every payment JCE receives from a client against a previously issued billing statement (Section 5.3). Collections records _what was paid, by whom, for which billing, on which date, into which bank_ — and produces the printable receipt the client gets as proof of payment. It feeds Sales (5.3) by reducing each billing statement's outstanding balance, drives the aging report in Reporting (5.9), and feeds Project Costing (5.8) for the cash-collected side of project profitability.

**Source documents.** _Received._ Three artifacts inform this section:

- **Collection Receipt (VAT)** — BIR-pre-printed sequentially numbered booklet (example No. **1703**), the official receipt JCE issues to a client for a VAT-able collection. The form's small print is BIR-mandated; importantly, it carries the legal note **"THIS DOCUMENT IS NOT VALID FOR CLAIMING INPUT TAXES"** — meaning the CR documents the _receipt_ of payment, not the _underlying sale_ (the Service Invoice did that). The booklet is registered with the BIR (ATP date, BIR Permit OCN) and printed by an accredited printer.
- **Acknowledgement Receipt (Non-VAT)** — JCE's internal receipt for non-VAT collections, with a two-copy carbonless layout (one for the client, one for files). Lighter-weight than the BIR CR; uses internal sequential numbering (`AR NO.`) and explicitly carries the Sales Order No. and the SOA No. being paid.
- **Collections register spreadsheet** (2026, "with forms" / column note `067-4`) — the flat per-row register that aggregates every collection issued, regardless of which physical form was used. Columns: Date, CR No., SI No., Client, TIN, SO#, Particular, **Trade Receivables** (the gross amount being collected — note that on the Sales side this was a DEBIT to AR; on the Collections side it's a CREDIT reducing the same AR), **Creditable Tax** (the Creditable / Expanded Withholding Tax the client withheld, typically 2% on services), and **Banks** (the net cash actually deposited).

**The double-entry that Collections closes.** When Sales issued a billing (Section 5.3), the entry was roughly: _Dr Trade Receivable, Cr Sales/Revenue (split by VAT classification)_. When Collections records a payment, the offsetting entry is: _Dr Banks (net cash), Dr Creditable Tax (the withheld CWT — claimable against income tax), Cr Trade Receivable (reducing the AR)_. Trade Receivables that went up in Sales come down in Collections; when fully paid, the original billing statement's outstanding balance is zero.

#### Record fields — Collection

> _Per Bryan's v1.49 confirmation,_ the Collections sub-tab uses the same **Option B pattern as Sales (5.3)**: two separate input entry points (one per physical receipt form), both writing to a unified Collection table with a `document_type` field. The legacy 2026 register, Sales' outstanding-balance tracking, Reporting's aging, and Project Costing all see one consolidated list.

_Identification_

- **`document_type`** — single-select: **Collection Receipt** · **Acknowledgement Receipt**. Set at creation by which entry point the user used; not editable after issue.
- **Document Number** — for Collection Receipt: the CR No. from the pre-printed BIR booklet (e.g., `1703`). For Acknowledgement Receipt: the internally assigned AR No.
- **Date** — the date payment was received.

_Client and source billing_

- **Client (Received from)** — selected from the Clients list; auto-fills TIN and Address.
- **Client TIN** — auto-filled; editable.
- **Address** — auto-filled; editable.
- **Sales Order No. (SO#)** — link to the Sales Order in BDD (Section 9.3) the payment is against.
- **Billing Statement reference** — link to the source billing statement in Sales (Section 5.3) — either a Service Invoice No. or an SOA No. depending on what was billed. _Confirms which receivable the collection closes against._
- **Particulars** — free-text description of what the payment is for (e.g., `Payment for Variation Order No. 01 for SO#25-02-274 (Supply and Construction of 13.2 KV Primary Metered Substation/Testing and Installation of Fuse Link)`). On the CR, this is the "In partial/full payment for" field; on the AR, it's the "Payment for" paragraph.

_Amounts_

- **`trade_receivables`** — the gross amount being collected, in PHP. Reduces the outstanding balance on the linked billing statement.
