- **`creditable_tax`** — the Creditable / Expanded Withholding Tax (CWT/EWT) the client withheld at source. Typically **2%** for services (per BIR rules); JCE claims this as a credit against its annual income tax. _(Open question — see below: auto-compute as 2% × gross or manual entry.)_
- **`banks`** — the net cash actually deposited into JCE's bank account: `trade_receivables − creditable_tax`. _(In the legacy register, this is highlighted in yellow on the row — emphasizing it as the figure reconciled against bank statements.)_

_Payment method (Acknowledgement Receipt specifically)_

- **Cash Payment** — amount paid in cash (when applicable).
- **Bank & Check No.** — when payment is by check, the issuing bank and the check number.
- **Check Date** — the date on the check.

_(The Collection Receipt's printed format doesn't separate these — it just states the sum received — but the underlying record stores them so all collections share one schema.)_

_Authorization_

- **Received by** _(Acknowledgement Receipt)_ — signed by the JCE staff member who received the payment; printed name selected from the employee database.
- **Cashier / Authorized Signature** _(Collection Receipt)_ — same idea, signed by the Cashier or authorized signatory; printed name from employee database.

_System-managed_

- **Status** — _proposed:_ **Issued** · **Voided / Cancelled**. _(Open question — see below: confirm lifecycle.)_
- **Created By** / **Created Date** — at issuance.
- **Updated By** / **Updated Date** — on any edit.
- **Audit trail** — change history.

#### Adding a Collection — two input entry points

The Sales sub-tab toolbar shows **`+ Issue Collection Receipt`** and **`+ Issue Acknowledgement Receipt`** as side-by-side actions on the Collections register. The user picks the right one based on whether the original billing was a Service Invoice (VAT — Collection Receipt) or a Statement of Account (Non-VAT — Acknowledgement Receipt). _(Open question — see below: confirm this is always the trigger.)_

##### Entry point 1 — Issue Collection Receipt (for VAT collections)

The Collection Receipt is the **BIR-compliant VAT receipt**, issued from a pre-printed booklet. The user enters the CR No. from the physical booklet; the system records what was issued.

**Header fields**

| Field              | Notes                                                             |
| ------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **CR No.**         | Manually entered from the pre-printed BIR booklet (e.g., `1703`). |
| **Date**           | Defaults to today; editable.                                      |
| **Received from**  | Selected from Clients list; auto-fills TIN and Address.           |
| **with TIN**       | Auto-filled from the Client record; editable.                     |
| **and address at** | Auto-filled; editable.                                            |
| **SO #**           | Link to the Sales Order in BDD (9.3).                             |
| **Source SI No.**  | Link to the Service Invoice being paid (Section 5.3).             |

**Amount fields**

| Field                           | Notes                                                                                                 |
| ------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **the sum of pesos** (words)    | _Open item — see below:_ auto-generated or manual.                                                    |
| **(P\_\_\_)** (figures)         | The `trade_receivables` amount in PHP.                                                                |
| **In partial/full payment for** | Free-text Particulars describing the underlying transaction (e.g., `Final billing for SO#25-08-238`). |

**Withholding tax**

| Field              | Notes                                                                                                                                       |
| ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Creditable Tax** | _(Not on the physical CR but stored in the record)_ the CWT amount. Auto-compute defaults: `trade_receivables × 2%` for services; editable. |
| **Banks (Net)**    | Auto-computed: `trade_receivables − creditable_tax`.                                                                                        |

**Signature**

| Field                                   | Notes                            |
| --------------------------------------- | -------------------------------- |
| **BY (Cashier / Authorized Signature)** | Selected from employee database. |

##### Entry point 2 — Issue Acknowledgement Receipt (for Non-VAT collections)

The Acknowledgement Receipt is the **internal non-VAT receipt**. Numbered internally; carries explicit links to the SOA and SO; printed as a two-copy layout (client + file).

**Header fields**

| Field       | Notes                                                      |
| ----------- | ---------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Date**    | Defaults to today; editable.                               |
| **AR No.**  | _(Open item — see below: system-sequential or manual.)_    |
| **SOA No.** | Link to the Statement of Account being paid (Section 5.3). |
| **SO #**    | Link to the Sales Order in BDD (9.3).                      |

**Body**

| Field                     | Notes                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| ------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **Received payment from** | Client name; selected from Clients list (auto-fills downstream fields).                                                                                                                    |
| **the amount of**         | Both _amount in words_ (e.g., `Five Hundred Fifty Six Thousand Sixty Pesos Only`) and _amount in figures_ (`556,060.00`). The `trade_receivables` field of the underlying record.          |
| **Payment for**           | Free-text Particulars (e.g., `PAYMENT FOR VARIATION ORDER NO. 01 FOR SO#25-02-274 (Supply and Construction of 13.2 KV Primary Metered Substation/Testing and Installation of Fuse Link)`). |

**Payment details**

| Field                | Notes                                                                                               |
| -------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Cash Payment**     | Amount paid in cash.                                                                                |
| **Bank & Check No.** | When by check.                                                                                      |
| **Check Date**       | The date on the check.                                                                              |
| **Creditable Tax**   | _(Not on the physical AR but stored in the record)_ the CWT amount. Auto-compute default; editable. |
| **Banks (Net)**      | Auto-computed.                                                                                      |

**Signature**

| Field           | Notes                                                                      |
| --------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Received by** | Signature over printed name; printed name selected from employee database. |

##### UX considerations — shared across both Collection entry points

The two paper forms have no defaults, no validation, no auto-math, no balance tracking. The digital input screens should remove the friction while staying visually familiar — same approach as the Sales side (5.3).

1. **Two entry-point buttons on the Collections register.** A toolbar at the top of the Collections list shows `+ Issue Collection Receipt` and `+ Issue Acknowledgement Receipt` as side-by-side actions. No mode switcher.
2. **Smart prefill on Billing Statement selection.** Picking the source billing (Service Invoice for CR, SOA for AR) auto-fills Client, TIN, Address, SO#, and suggests a Particulars value from the billing's description (editable). The user confirms or overrides; everything else builds from that anchor.
3. **Outstanding balance display.** When a billing statement is selected, the system shows the **remaining unpaid balance** alongside the input — so the user can see at a glance whether this is a partial or full payment. The trade_receivables input defaults to the remaining balance (full payment is the common case); editable for partials.
4. **Live computation.** `Banks = trade_receivables − creditable_tax`. Creditable Tax auto-computes from the gross when a default rate (e.g., 2%) is in effect; editable per receipt.
5. **Save Draft vs Issue.** Two-stage save: **Save Draft** holds the receipt in editable state, not yet posted to the books. **Issue** finalizes — records the payment against the source billing statement, updates the outstanding balance, locks the receipt from substantive edits.
6. **Live print preview.** A "Preview" pane shows the receipt rendered as it will print on paper — Collection Receipt template for the CR screen, Acknowledgement Receipt template (two-copy layout) for the AR screen.
7. **Required-field validation at Issue.** CR requires Client, Date, CR No. (from booklet), source SI No., amount > 0, signatory. AR requires Client, Date, AR No., SOA No., SO#, amount > 0, signatory.
8. **Print right after Issue.** Same pattern as Sales — issuing opens the print dialog directly (defer is an option).
9. **Partial payment workflow.** When the user enters a trade*receivables less than the source billing's outstanding balance, the system flags it as a partial payment, leaves the balance open, and lets a subsequent Collection close the remaining balance. *(Open question — see below: confirm partials are tracked this way.)\_
10. **Numbering aids.** For Collection Receipt, the system shows the **last CR number issued** as a hint to catch out-of-sequence entries. For Acknowledgement Receipt, the system suggests the next sequential AR number based on past records (assuming AR numbering is system-sequential — pending open item).
11. **Two-copy print layout for AR.** The Acknowledgement Receipt prints as **two identical copies on one page** (matching the legacy form), so the user can separate after issuance — one for the client, one for the file.

#### Cross-module integrations

- **Sales (Section 5.3)** ← Collections. Every collection writes a reduction against a specific billing statement's outstanding balance. The system tracks per-statement: cumulative collected, remaining outstanding, status (Issued · Partially Paid · Paid). When the cumulative collected reaches the billing's gross amount, the statement moves to `Paid`.
- **Sales Order (Section 9.3)** ← Collections. Each collection also rolls up to the project's cumulative cash collected (across all billings), feeding the project-status views and the Project Costing tab (5.8).
- **Journal Voucher (Section 5.7)** ← Collections. Each issued collection produces a journal entry (Dr Banks + Dr Creditable Tax / Cr Trade Receivable) that the Journal Voucher tab aggregates per period.
- **Reporting (Section 5.9)** ← Collections. The aging report shows outstanding balances bucketed by days outstanding (0–30 / 31–60 / 61–90 / 90+); the collection efficiency report shows ratio of collected vs billed per period.

#### Print and PDF export

**COL-001** — The system shall produce a printable **Collection Receipt** matching the BIR-pre-printed booklet layout — including the mandatory note `THIS DOCUMENT IS NOT VALID FOR CLAIMING INPUT TAXES` and the BIR ATP / Permit / accredited-printer block in the footer.

**COL-002** — The system shall produce a printable **Acknowledgement Receipt** in the **two-copy layout** (two identical copies stacked vertically on one page) matching the legacy form, with the company letterhead, the receipt body, payment details, and Received-by signature block.

**COL-003** — The system shall produce a printable **Collections register** for any date range, scoped to a single client, a single Sales Order, or all clients — matching the layout of the legacy 2026 register spreadsheet so the printed register is recognizable to Accounting.

**COL-004** — All three outputs shall support **PDF export** in addition to direct printing.

#### Open items — please confirm

1. **Entry point driver.** Is the CR vs AR choice **always driven by the source billing's VAT status** (Service Invoice → CR; SOA → AR), or can JCE issue an AR against a Service Invoice (or vice versa) in unusual cases?

2. **CR vs AR numbering.** CR No. comes from the BIR pre-printed booklet (manually entered, like the Service Invoice). Should AR No. be **system-sequential** (e.g., `AR-2026-001`), **prefilled with a suggested next number** that the user can override, or **manually entered** from a separate internal AR log JCE keeps?

3. **Creditable Tax auto-compute.** The standard PH rate for services is **2%** of gross. Should the system auto-compute `creditable_tax = trade_receivables × 2%` by default (editable per receipt for non-standard cases), or always manual? If auto, should the default rate live in a Settings field for tunability?

4. **Sum-of-pesos in words on CR.** Auto-generate from the figures (`P 556,060.00` → `Five Hundred Fifty Six Thousand Sixty Pesos Only`) or manually typed? Same question already open for SOA's Amount in Words (open item #2 in Sales) — likely the same answer applies here.

5. **Partial payments.** Confirm the system tracks partial payments — a single billing statement can have multiple Collection records summing to the full amount, with the outstanding balance decremented on each. Aging reports work off the _outstanding_ balance, not the original gross.

6. **Multiple billings per receipt.** Can a single Collection Receipt or AR record a payment that covers **multiple billing statements** in one transaction (e.g., the client cuts one check for two SOAs), or is it strictly one-to-one (one receipt per billing being paid)? The legacy register shows one row per (CR No., SI No.) pair — suggesting one-to-one — but worth confirming.

7. **Strict no-edit pattern.** Collection Receipts especially are formal BIR documents. Once issued and given to a client, the substantive amounts should arguably be immutable, with corrections going through voiding and re-issuing. Apply the same immutable-record + event-stream pattern as Offers (9.4) / Quotation (9.5), or normal edit with audit log?

8. **Status lifecycle.** Proposed: **Issued · Voided / Cancelled**. Confirm — and confirm how a voided receipt interacts with the source billing's outstanding balance (does voiding restore the balance, and is the void itself a separate event in the audit trail?).

9. **Banks linkage and reconciliation.** The Excel register has a `Banks` column with a value highlighted in yellow (`657,253.33` in the example) — implying it's reconciled against an actual bank deposit. Should the Collection record carry a `bank_account` field (which JCE bank account received the deposit), and does the system support **bank reconciliation** (matching collection records to bank statement entries)? Out of scope for v1?

10. **The "with forms" / `067-4` annotation.** The 2026 Collections register header carries an annotation `with forms 067-4`. What is this — a batch reference, a deposit slip number, a CR booklet series number, or something else?

11. **Authorized signatories.** Both forms name a Cashier or Receiver. Should the system **lock the signatory by role** (Cashier role from the employee database, auto-fills the current incumbent) or **let the user pick the signatory each time**?

### 5.5 Payable Voucher _(preliminary scope — v1.50)_

**Purpose.** The Payable Voucher sub-tab is where JCE Accounting **authorizes and records every outgoing payment** — to suppliers (most common), to employees (advances, reimbursements, meal/travel expense), and to other third parties. It is the cash-disbursement side of the ledger, mirroring how Section 5.3 Sales / 5.4 Collections sits on the receivable side. Each Payable Voucher carries the **double-entry journal that debits the expense or asset account (the "Sundry Account") and credits Cash in Bank**, and once authorized produces a real bank transfer / check / cash payment whose execution reference is captured back on the voucher itself.

> **Naming note.** Bryan's artifact shows the printed form titled both "Payable Voucher" (handwritten at the top of the page) and "**BANK PAYMENT VOUCHER**" (printed title). The Transaction Number on the same form uses a **`CV-XXXX`** prefix (the example is `CV-0902`, with the trailing 4 digits matching the PR Ref. No.), which most naturally reads as **Cheque Voucher** or **Cash Voucher**. The three terms appear to refer to the same document. The SRS uses "Payable Voucher" for the sub-tab and `CV-XXXX` for the document number; the printed form's title bar can read "Bank Payment Voucher" exactly like the legacy form. **Confirm terminology with Bryan.**

#### Source artifacts

**Artifact 1 — Bank Payment Voucher CV-0902** (filled paper voucher, ₱39,000 to D.I.Y Builder's Depot Inc, dated 26-Feb-26):

- **Header block**: JCE company name and address; printed title "BANK PAYMENT VOUCHER"; Year (`2026`); Transaction Number (`CV-0902`).
- **Supplier block**: Name of Supplier (`D.I.Y BUILDER'S DEPOT INC`), Business Address (`0963 J.A CLARIN ST DAO 6300 TAGBILARAN CITY, BOHOL`), Supplier's TIN No. (`009-360-407-000`), Date (`26-Feb-26`), Project tag highlighted (`SIKATUNA`), Sales Order # (`SO#: 25-05-306`).
- **Body — Payment Particulars table**: columns `Date`, `PR REF. No.` (`0902` — same trailing digits as the CV number), `Payment Particulars` (free-text block reading `For payment of the following: / 50% DOWNPAYMENT / SUPPLIES & MATERIALS / RFP-PUR-26020188 — Request for Payment / PO NO: 26020056 — PO number / 2 PCS 10MM STEEL PLATE 4FT X 8FT 30MM THK — Item`), `Amount (PHP)` (`39,000.00`).
- **Total block**: `PHP 39,000.00` and amount-in-words (`...nd Pesos Only`, truncated in photo).
- **Journal block — Ledger Account Name | Debit | Credit**: a debit line (account name truncated, likely "PROJECT EXPENSE" or "SUPPLIES & MATERIALS") `39,000.00 Dr`, a credit line "CASH IN BANK" `39,000.00 Cr`, totals `39,000.00 = 39,000.00` (the double-entry balances on the voucher itself).
- **Authorization block**: handwritten BDO account number (`BDO - 007-818-008-107`) and the supplier's account name (`DIY Builders Depot Inc.`), and four signature slots — one preparer (partially visible, ending in `…anitan`, possibly _Canitan_), **EULYN B. CAPILLO — VP-Finance**, **JIMWEL C. CAPILLO — President**, and a final "Payment received by — Signature over printed — Date:" line at the far right (signed by the payee after the funds land).
- **Bank execution evidence** (mobile banking overlay): BDO Send Money confirmation, `PHP 39,000.00`, `To: DIY Builders Depot Inc / 007818008107`, `From: SA W/ ATM W/O PASBK-INDIVIDUAL …4061`, `Created on: Feb 26, 2026 04:15 PM`, `Reference no.: PC-NDBMOB-20260226-76725862`. This is the proof of execution; the system needs a place to store the reference number alongside the voucher.

**Artifact 2 — Worksheet - Payment Request** (Excel register, the preceding stage):

Columns observed: `JCE-PR` (internal Payment Request number, e.g. `0418`, `0419`), `Date` (`29-Jan-26`), `Suppliers' Name` (notably also includes **employees** — e.g. `JAYSON B. TORRES`, `JOCEN CANITAN`), `Supplier's TIN`, `Supplier's Address`, `Invoice no.`, `Project` (`NPSI BACOLOD`, `NEW BUILDING`), `RFP NO.` (Accounting-side reference, `ACCTG-0093`, `ACCTG-0094`), **`Sundry Account`** (the GL chart-of-accounts line being charged — e.g. `Advances Employees`, `Meals Expense`), `Amount` (note: some values shown in parentheses `(1,700.68)` indicating negative / reversal / credit), `Description` (e.g. `PAYROLL PERIOD COVERED: JAN. 18 - 24, 2026 -`), `ATC` (the BIR Alphanumeric Tax Code identifying the type of withholding tax applied — e.g. `WC158` for goods, `WC160` for services), `SO NO.` (Sales Order linkage, `25-01-259`).

**Artifact 3 — BDO Send Money mobile banking confirmation** (overlay on the photo): the bank-side transaction reference (`PC-NDBMOB-20260226-76725862`) that proves the funds left JCE's bank account.

#### Inferred workflow

Reading the three artifacts together, JCE runs payments through a multi-step process — each step adds reference numbers that show up on the next:

```
[Field/Project request]
       │   produces an internal Request for Payment number
       ▼
RFP-PUR-26020188   (Request for Payment)
       │
       ▼
[Purchasing issues a PO if applicable]
       │
       ▼
PO 26020056   (Purchase Order — future Section 7 Purchasing)
       │
       ▼
[Accounting consolidates into Payment Request worksheet]
       │
       ▼
JCE-PR 0902 / ACCTG-XXXX   (Payment Request — the Excel register row)
       │
       ▼
[Accounting prepares the Payable Voucher and routes for sign-off]
       │
       ▼
CV-0902   (Payable Voucher — Prepared → VP-Finance → President)
       │
       ▼
[Bank transfer / check / cash issued]
       │
       ▼
PC-NDBMOB-20260226-76725862   (Disbursement — bank reference, recorded back on the CV)
       │
       ▼
[Payee signs "Payment received by" on the CV → record is closed]
```

The Payable Voucher (CV) is the **legal authorization document** in the middle; the Payment Request (PR) is the **internal queue / batch worksheet** that feeds it; the Disbursement reference is the **proof of execution** that closes it. Section 5.6 Disbursement will be where the actual bank reference, payment date, and reconciliation status live; 5.5 Payable Voucher is the authorization document that points at the Disbursement. **Confirm this RFP → PR → CV → Disbursement flow with Bryan.**

#### Data model — Payable Voucher record

The unified record schema each Payable Voucher carries:

| Field                                                            | Source / Notes                                                                                                                                                                       |
| ---------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **Identification**                                               |                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| `cv_number`                                                      | `CV-XXXX` — system-assigned sequential per year (suggested by system, manually overridable to match a pre-printed booklet if JCE uses one). Globally unique within the year.         |
| `year`                                                           | Auto from current year at create time.                                                                                                                                               |
| `cv_date`                                                        | Date the voucher is prepared.                                                                                                                                                        |
| **Payee**                                                        |                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| `payee_type`                                                     | `Supplier` · `Employee` · `Other`. Drives which lookup feeds the payee_id. (See open item — the Excel worksheet shows both suppliers and employees as payees.)                       |
| `payee_id`                                                       | FK to **Suppliers (Purchasing 7.9)** when `payee_type = Supplier`, or FK to **Employees (4.1)** when `payee_type = Employee`.                                                        |
| `payee_name`                                                     | Auto-populated from payee_id (display only; snapshot for the voucher print).                                                                                                         |
| `payee_address`                                                  | Auto-populated from payee_id.                                                                                                                                                        |
| `payee_tin`                                                      | Auto-populated from payee_id.                                                                                                                                                        |
| **Project linkage**                                              |                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| `sales_order_no`                                                 | FK to BDD 9.3 Sales Order. The `SO#` on the voucher.                                                                                                                                 |
| `project_tag`                                                    | Optional short label (e.g., `SIKATUNA`) — likely derived from the Sales Order's Project Name; **confirm** whether this is a separate field or auto-derived.                          |
| **Reference numbers** (the workflow trail)                       |                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| `pr_ref_no`                                                      | The Payment Request number (`0902` on the example — system links the CV to the upstream PR record).                                                                                  |
| `rfp_no`                                                         | The Request for Payment reference (`RFP-PUR-26020188` on the example) — captured as free text or FK if RFPs become a system-managed entity.                                          |
| `po_no`                                                          | Purchase Order number (`26020056` on the example) — **FK to the future Purchasing module (Section 7)**. Free text until Purchasing is scoped.                                        |
| `invoice_no`                                                     | The supplier's invoice number being paid (column on the PR worksheet).                                                                                                               |
| **Payment particulars**                                          |                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| `payment_particulars`                                            | Free-text or structured block describing what's being paid (e.g., "50% DOWNPAYMENT / SUPPLIES & MATERIALS / 2 PCS 10MM STEEL PLATE…").                                               |
| `line_items`                                                     | Optional table of line items (Description, Qty, Unit Price, Amount) — see open item on multiple items per voucher.                                                                   |
| **Amounts**                                                      |                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| `gross_amount`                                                   | The base amount before withholding (PHP).                                                                                                                                            |
| `withholding_tax_amount`                                         | Computed from `atc_code` × `gross_amount` × applicable rate. May be zero.                                                                                                            |
| `net_amount`                                                     | `gross_amount − withholding_tax_amount` — the amount actually paid to the payee.                                                                                                     |
| `amount_in_words`                                                | Auto-generated from `net_amount` (see open item on auto-generate vs typed).                                                                                                          |
| **Tax classification (BIR)**                                     |                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| `atc_code`                                                       | BIR Alphanumeric Tax Code identifying the withholding type (`WC158` = goods 1%, `WC160` = services 2%, etc.). Drives the withholding tax computation.                                |
| **Journal entry (double-entry)**                                 |                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| `debit_account`                                                  | The Sundry Account being charged — FK to **Chart of Accounts** (a new system-wide master entity; see open item).                                                                     |
| `debit_amount`                                                   | Usually `gross_amount` (or `net_amount` if withholding is journalled separately).                                                                                                    |
| `credit_account`                                                 | Cash in Bank — FK to Chart of Accounts.                                                                                                                                              |
| `credit_amount`                                                  | The cash outflow.                                                                                                                                                                    |
| `withholding_credit_account`                                     | When applicable — `Withholding Tax Payable` GL account.                                                                                                                              |
| `withholding_credit_amount`                                      | The withheld portion, sitting as a payable to BIR.                                                                                                                                   |
| **Authorization (sign-off)**                                     |                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| `prepared_by`                                                    | Accounting clerk who drafted the voucher (Employees 4.1).                                                                                                                            |
| `prepared_at`                                                    | Timestamp.                                                                                                                                                                           |
| `vp_finance_approver`                                            | Eulyn B. Capillo (or whoever holds the VP-Finance role) — Employees 4.1.                                                                                                             |
| `vp_finance_approved_at`                                         | Timestamp.                                                                                                                                                                           |
| `president_approver`                                             | Jimwel C. Capillo (or whoever holds the President role) — Employees 4.1.                                                                                                             |
| `president_approved_at`                                          | Timestamp.                                                                                                                                                                           |
| `payment_received_by_name`                                       | Payee representative who signs on receipt. Free text — they sign the paper voucher with the funds.                                                                                   |
| **Execution evidence**                                           |                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| `payment_method`                                                 | `Bank Transfer` · `Check` · `Cash`.                                                                                                                                                  |
| `payment_bank`                                                   | Which JCE bank account funded the payment (e.g., `BDO …4061`).                                                                                                                       |
| `recipient_bank`                                                 | Payee's receiving bank (e.g., `BDO`).                                                                                                                                                |
| `recipient_account_number`                                       | Payee's bank account number (e.g., `007-818-008-107`).                                                                                                                               |
| `recipient_account_name`                                         | Payee's bank account name.                                                                                                                                                           |
| `check_no` / `check_date`                                        | If `payment_method = Check`.                                                                                                                                                         |
| `bank_reference_no`                                              | The Disbursement reference (e.g., `PC-NDBMOB-20260226-76725862`) — captured manually after the bank transfer is executed.                                                            |
| `payment_executed_at`                                            | Actual timestamp the funds left JCE's account.                                                                                                                                       |
| **Status and system-managed**                                    |                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| `status`                                                         | `Draft` · `Pending VP-Finance` · `Pending President` · `Approved` · `Paid` · `Voided`.                                                                                               |
| `attachments[]`                                                  | Uploaded supporting documents (the printed RFP, supplier invoice, delivery receipt, PO copy, bank confirmation screenshot — the "E ATTACHED" / "FILE ATTACHED" line on the voucher). |
| `created_by`, `created_at`, `last_updated_by`, `last_updated_at` | Standard audit.                                                                                                                                                                      |

#### Cross-module integrations

- **Suppliers entity (Purchasing 7.9)** — payee lookup when `payee_type = Supplier`. The PV inherits supplier-detail auto-fill from the same record Purchasing uses. May need a **bank-accounts sub-entity** under each Supplier (a supplier can be paid to one of several accounts; see open item — recommended in Purchasing 7.13 as well).
- **Purchase Order (Purchasing 7.5)** — hard FK when paying a Supplier (the upstream PO authorizes the spend). Free-text fallback when paying an Employee or Other payee.
- **Request For Payment (Purchasing 7.7)** — the upstream document that triggers PV creation. The PV inherits the RFP's payee details, line items, and **computed EWT withholding** (the PV does not re-compute; it carries forward what 7.7.5 calculated).
- **Employees (4.1)** — payee lookup when `payee_type = Employee` (advances, meals, travel reimbursements); also the sign-off lookup (Prepared by, VP-Finance, President).
- **Sales Order (BDD 9.3)** — project linkage for every voucher (`sales_order_no`).
- **Future Purchasing module (Section 7)** — `po_no` becomes an FK once Purchasing is scoped. The Request for Payment (RFP) likely originates there.
- **Disbursement (5.6)** — the actual cash outflow tracking. Each Approved Payable Voucher produces one Disbursement record; the bank execution reference lives there.
- **Journal Voucher (5.7)** — every Payable Voucher's Dr/Cr automatically posts a Journal entry. Confirm whether vouchers create JVs implicitly (cleaner) or whether the Accountant manually creates a parallel JV (legacy practice — needs confirmation).
- **NEW — Chart of Accounts entity.** The `Sundry Account` column on the PR worksheet and the Ledger Account Name lines on the voucher both reference a GL chart of accounts (examples: `Advances Employees`, `Meals Expense`, `Cash in Bank`). The system needs a maintained list of GL accounts with account code, name, type (Asset / Liability / Equity / Revenue / Expense), and active flag. This is foundational — it's used by 5.5 Payable Voucher, 5.4 Collections, 5.7 Journal Voucher, and 5.9 Reporting. **Confirm with Bryan whether JCE has a documented chart of accounts.**
- **NEW — ATC code reference table.** The Alphanumeric Tax Codes on the PR worksheet drive automatic withholding-tax computation. Either a hard-coded enum (the BIR list rarely changes) or a maintained list in Settings (5.2.1). Common codes JCE will use: `WC158` (goods, 1%), `WC160` (services, 2%), `WC157` (rentals, 5%), `WC110/120` (professional fees, 5%/10%). **Confirm scope.**
- **Project Costing (5.8)** — vouchers tagged to a Sales Order become non-labor cost rows in that project's cost roll-up (parallel to Payroll's labor cost contribution via Timekeeping).

#### Per-entry-point input fields — `+ New Payable Voucher`

| Field                                                                    | Behavior                                                                                                                                                                  |
| ------------------------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `cv_number`                                                              | System suggests next sequential `CV-XXXX`; manually overridable; uniqueness validated at Save.                                                                            |
| `cv_date`                                                                | Defaults to today; editable.                                                                                                                                              |
| `payee_type`                                                             | Radio: `Supplier` / `Employee` / `Other`. Drives the next field.                                                                                                          |
| `payee`                                                                  | Lookup against Suppliers or Employees based on `payee_type`. Auto-populates name/address/TIN.                                                                             |
| `sales_order_no`                                                         | Lookup against BDD 9.3 Sales Order. Auto-populates `project_tag` from the SO's Project Name.                                                                              |
| `rfp_no`                                                                 | Free text (will become FK when Purchasing/RFP is scoped).                                                                                                                 |
| `po_no`                                                                  | Free text (will become FK when Purchasing is scoped).                                                                                                                     |
| `invoice_no`                                                             | Free text.                                                                                                                                                                |
| `payment_particulars`                                                    | Multi-line text — first line typically the high-level description (e.g., "50% DOWNPAYMENT"), subsequent lines the supplier reference numbers and item detail.             |
| `line_items` _(optional)_                                                | If structured items are wanted, table with Description / Qty / Unit / Unit Price / Amount; the table total feeds `gross_amount`. Otherwise `gross_amount` typed directly. |
| `gross_amount`                                                           | PHP.                                                                                                                                                                      |
| `atc_code`                                                               | Dropdown from ATC reference table. Defaults to the payee's usual ATC if Suppliers carries a `default_atc_code` field. Triggers withholding-tax auto-compute.              |
| `withholding_tax_amount`                                                 | Auto-computed (`gross × rate`); manually overridable for non-standard cases.                                                                                              |
| `net_amount`                                                             | Auto-computed (`gross − withholding`); read-only.                                                                                                                         |
| `amount_in_words`                                                        | Auto-generated from `net_amount` (or `gross_amount` — see open item on which amount the words quote).                                                                     |
| `debit_account`                                                          | Dropdown from Chart of Accounts. Defaults from the linked PO / RFP if those carry an account suggestion.                                                                  |
| `payment_method`                                                         | Radio: `Bank Transfer` / `Check` / `Cash`.                                                                                                                                |
| `payment_bank`                                                           | Dropdown from JCE's bank accounts list (a new sub-entity to maintain in Settings).                                                                                        |
| `recipient_bank` / `recipient_account_number` / `recipient_account_name` | If `payment_method = Bank Transfer`; defaults from the Supplier's primary bank account if maintained.                                                                     |
| `check_no` / `check_date`                                                | If `payment_method = Check`.                                                                                                                                              |
| `attachments`                                                            | Multi-file upload — supplier invoice, RFP scan, PO scan, etc.                                                                                                             |

After Issue, the voucher moves to `Pending VP-Finance`; once both VP-Finance and President have authorized, the voucher is `Approved`. The bank execution then happens (in or out of the system depending on banking integration — see open item), and Accounting comes back to fill the `bank_reference_no` and `payment_executed_at` to flip status to `Paid`. The payee signs the printed voucher and that signed copy is the final attachment.

#### UX considerations

1. **Two-step entry — Draft, then Issue.** The voucher can be saved as Draft (Accounting still building it), and only `Issue` triggers the sign-off routing. Draft vouchers are editable; Issued/Approved are not (see strict no-edit open item).
2. **Withholding-tax preview.** As soon as `atc_code` and `gross_amount` are filled, the system shows the withholding split inline (`Gross 39,000 − WHT (2%) 780 = Net 38,220`). For the example voucher (DIY Builders), if the supplier is a goods vendor, `WC158` (1%) would apply.
3. **Sundry Account suggestion.** When the user picks a Supplier with a `default_debit_account` (e.g., DIY Builders defaults to "Supplies & Materials") or an Employee with a payee-type-based default (advances → "Advances Employees", meals → "Meals Expense"), the GL account pre-fills. Editable.
4. **Outstanding PO / RFP balance display.** When the user enters a `po_no` (once Purchasing exists), the system shows the PO's contract amount, prior payments against it, and remaining balance — so Accounting can see at a glance that this 50% Downpayment leaves 50% still to bill, and the next voucher against this PO won't double-pay.
5. **Sign-off routing.** Sequential: Prepared by → VP-Finance → President. Each approver gets a notification (or a dashboard tile) of "Vouchers awaiting your approval"; each click-to-approve is logged with timestamp. Mirrors the four-stage Payroll Summary sign-off (5.2.3).
6. **Bank execution capture.** After President approval, Accounting executes the bank transfer (in the BDO app or by check) and pastes the bank reference back into the voucher to flip status to `Paid`. This is a manual step unless we integrate with banking APIs (out of scope for v1 — see open item).
7. **"Payment received by" — paper-only.** The payee's signature is captured on the printed voucher when they sign for the funds; the system holds a scanned upload of the signed voucher as proof. The system does not try to capture a digital signature from the payee.
8. **Print layout matches legacy form** (COL-001 / SAL-001 / PSL-005 pattern). The printed Payable Voucher is the document JCE files; print fidelity matters.
9. **Multi-row PR worksheet view.** The Excel worksheet shows multiple Payment Request rows per period; the system's "Payment Requests" list view should be the same shape — filterable by date range, project, payee, status. Queued PRs can be batched into vouchers (one voucher per row, or several rows consolidated into one voucher to the same supplier).
10. **Negative amounts on the PR worksheet** (`(1,700.68)` in parentheses) likely represent **deductions / recoupments** against an employee's pay rather than outflows — e.g., a prior advance is being recovered from this period's payroll. The system should support negative-amount line items distinctly from voids. **Confirm with Bryan.**
11. **Void / Cancel workflow.** A voided Approved voucher needs an explicit reversal entry; a cancelled Draft voucher just disappears. Mirror Collections (5.4) and Sales (5.3) on void semantics.

#### COL-style requirements

- **PV-001 — Print Payable Voucher.** The system shall print a Payable Voucher matching the legacy "Bank Payment Voucher" form layout — header (company, CV number, year, date), supplier block, payment particulars body, total and amount-in-words, journal block (debit/credit), authorization block with three sign-off slots plus "Payment received by," and an attachments-attached annotation.
- **PV-002 — Print Payment Request worksheet.** The system shall produce a printable / exportable Payment Request register matching the legacy Excel format — JCE-PR, Date, Payee, TIN, Address, Invoice no., Project, RFP NO., Sundry Account, Amount, Description, ATC, SO NO.
- **PV-003 — PDF export.** Both the individual Payable Voucher and the Payment Request worksheet shall be exportable as PDF. Bulk export of all vouchers in a period produces one multi-page PDF.
- **PV-004 — Audit trail.** The system shall log every status change (Draft → Pending → Approved → Paid → Voided) with user and timestamp, append-only — mirroring the strict-no-edit pattern from Offers (9.4), Quotation (9.5), and the proposed pattern for Sales (5.3) and Collections (5.4).

#### Open items for Bryan's confirmation

1. **Terminology.** Is the sub-tab called **"Payable Voucher"**, **"Bank Payment Voucher"**, or **"Cheque Voucher" (CV)** internally? The handwritten title says Payable Voucher, the printed title says BANK PAYMENT VOUCHER, and the number prefix is CV-. Lock the official term for screen labels and reports.
2. **Workflow steps.** Confirm the inferred flow: **RFP → JCE-PR → CV → Disbursement**. Is RFP the field/project request, JCE-PR the consolidated Accounting queue, CV the authorization document, and Disbursement the bank execution? Or is JCE-PR the same thing as the CV (the trailing 4 digits do match — `JCE-PR 0902` and `CV-0902`)?
3. ✅ **RFP vs JCE-PR vs ACCTG-XXXX** — **RESOLVED in v1.56 by Purchasing 7.7.** The three prefixes denote three distinct workflow stages: `RFP-PUR-#####` is the **Purchasing-side** Request For Payment (owned by Section 7.7, format flagged for confirmation — see PV 5.5 open item below); `ACCTG-####` is **Accounting's** internal payment-request reference; `JCE-PR` is the **Accounting consolidated worksheet** number. The full workflow is: Purchasing emits RFP from an Approved PO → Accounting receives and creates the PV (5.5) referencing the RFP → Disbursement (5.6) executes payment.
4. ✅ **Chart of Accounts** — **RESOLVED in v1.51.** Bryan provided the full printed Chart of Accounts (4-page artifact, ~129 accounts in the 10001–50055 numeric range). Captured as **Section 8 of 5.2.1 Settings** with full account list, code-range structure (Assets 10000s / Liabilities 20000s / Equity 30000s / Revenue 40000s / Expenses 50000s), per-account schema (code, name, type, subtype, normal balance, contra flag, parent, active, notes), and cross-module hooks closing prior data models (Sales 5.3, Collections 5.4, PV 5.5, Payroll 5.2.3). Ten residual chart-level open items captured at the bottom of the CoA section (BDO missing, 40003 Exclusive vs Exempt, 50001 vs 50005, 20001 vs 20004, etc.). The Payable Voucher's Sundry Account dropdown now reads from this list.
5. ✅ **ATC withholding-tax handling** — **RESOLVED in v1.56 by Purchasing 7.7.5 (FR-RFP-04, FR-RFP-06).** The RFP itself computes EWT by payment classification (Goods 1%, Services 2%, Rentals 5%, Professional Fee 5%/10%); the rates are admin-configurable. The PV **inherits the computed withholding from the RFP** rather than re-computing — so by the time a voucher arrives in Accounting, the EWT split is already determined. ATC code mapping to BIR form numbers is Phase 2 work (FR-BIR-03).
6. ✅ **PO linkage** — **RESOLVED in v1.56 by Purchasing 7.5.** For voucher records where `payee_type = Supplier` and an RFP exists (FR-RFP-02 requires the RFP to come from an Approved PO), the PV's `po_no` is a **hard FK** to a real PO record in 7.5. For voucher records where `payee_type = Employee` (advances, meals, reimbursements) or `payee_type = Other` (one-off payments), the `po_no` remains optional / free-text since no PO is required.
7. **Approval signatories.** The example shows three sign-off slots plus a "Payment received by." Is the routing **always Prepared by → VP-Finance → President**, or does it vary by amount threshold (e.g., small vouchers stop at VP-Finance, large ones require President)? Mirror or differ from Payroll Summary's four-stage Prepared/Checked/Verified/Approved (5.2.3)?
8. **Multiple line items per voucher.** The example shows one item (2 PCS Steel Plate). Can a single voucher cover multiple items / multiple POs / multiple suppliers in a batch run (the way the PR worksheet has multiple rows)? Or is it strictly one voucher = one supplier = one logical payment (possibly multi-line within that single payment)?
9. ✅ **Supplier bank accounts** — **RESOLVED in v1.58** via Purchasing 7.15.2. `SupplierBankAccount` is now a 1..N sub-entity under Supplier (Bank Name FK to JCE Banks lookup, Account Number, Account Name, Currency, SWIFT, IBAN, Primary flag, Active flag, effective_from/to) with fraud-control workflow on bank changes. The PV input form sources recipient bank/account details from this entity, filtered by payment currency. **Employee bank accounts for salary disbursements** — separate workflow; recommend mirroring the SupplierBankAccount pattern on the Employee entity (4.1) when Payroll's actual disbursement flow is built out.
10. **Payment methods.** Confirm the three methods (Bank Transfer / Check / Cash). Any others — wire transfer, PayPal, GCash for petty cash?
11. **Banking integration vs manual capture.** v1 assumption is that the bank execution happens outside the system (in BDO Online / BDO mobile / by check) and Accounting manually pastes the bank reference number back into the voucher. Confirm — or is there appetite for a future BDO API integration that auto-stamps the reference once the transfer is executed?
12. **Negative amounts in the PR worksheet.** The `(1,700.68)` for `Advances Employees` likely represents a recoupment / deduction (employee's prior advance being recovered from current-period pay). Is the negative-amount payment request a real concept JCE uses on the same worksheet as positive payments, or is it a Payroll-side adjustment that just happens to share this list? Drives whether Payable Vouchers can themselves be negative or whether negatives stay in the Payroll module.
13. **Payee duality — Supplier vs Employee vs Other.** The PR worksheet has employees as "Suppliers' Name" — clearly the column name is loose. Confirm that the Payable Voucher must support all three payee types (Supplier, Employee, Other), each with the appropriate lookup. Employee payments (advances, meals, travel reimbursements) are common; "Other" is for one-off payees not in either master list.
14. ✅ **Voucher-vs-Disbursement data model** — **RESOLVED in v1.52.** Bryan provided the Bank Disbursement Excel register, which shows the **journal-entry pattern** that settles the question: each Disbursement row is a `Dr Voucher's Payable / Cr Cash-In-Bank` entry that clears the credit posted by the upstream Payable Voucher (which was `Dr Expense/Asset / Cr Voucher's Payable`). The two records are **separate but linked**: a CV carries one Disbursement when paid, and `20004 Voucher's Payable` is the intermediate clearing account between authorization and cash. See Section 5.6 for full scope.

### 5.6 Disbursement _(preliminary scope — v1.52)_

**Purpose.** The Disbursement sub-tab is the **bank-side execution layer**. Where 5.5 Payable Voucher records the _authorization_ (the Approved CV with three sign-offs), 5.6 Disbursement records the _actual payment leaving JCE's bank account_ — the check that was cut, the bank transfer that was initiated, the cash that was handed over. Every Approved Payable Voucher produces one Disbursement record when it's paid; the Disbursement carries the bank reference (check number or bank-transfer ID), the bank account that funded the payment, and the date the funds actually left JCE's books. Without this layer, the company knows what it _authorized_ but not what it _actually paid_ — and the bank reconciliation (matching JCE's records against the BDO / Land Bank statement) has nothing to match against.

#### Source artifact

**Bank Disbursement register Excel** (titled "JC ELECTROFIELDS POWER SYSTEM, INC. / BANK DISBURSEMENT / ALL PROJECT") — a wide spreadsheet where each row is one disbursement and columns include:

| Column                       | Example values                                                                                                                          | Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| ---------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `DATE`                       | `01-Apr-26`                                                                                                                             | The disbursement date (= when JCE recorded the cash outflow).                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| `CV NO`                      | `1564`, `1565`, `1566`, `1567`, `1568`                                                                                                  | The Payable Voucher being executed. Note the **shorter format** (`1564`, 4 digits) compared to the printed voucher's `CV-0902` format — likely the same sequential counter, just rendered without the `CV-` prefix on the register.                         |
| `PAYEE`                      | `RIZA MAY BATUIGAS`, `E. DACPANO ENGINEERING AND ENGINE REBU(ILDING)`, `JIRRO CAPILLO`, `JIMWEL CAPILLO`, `NELDA MENDOZA/JOCEN CANITAN` | Mix of employees (Riza, Jirro, Jimwel, Nelda/Jocen) and suppliers (E. Dacpano Engineering). Confirms the payee-duality model from 5.5. Note the **joint payee** "NELDA MENDOZA/JOCEN CANITAN" — two employees on one disbursement.                          |
| `DESCRIPTION`                | `PCF REFLENISHMENT NO. 74 - MARCH 30-31` `(typo: Replenishment)`, `1 PC PIN W/THREAD FOR FABRICATION & 4 P…`, `CASH ADVANCE` × 3        | Free text; derived from the source CV's payment particulars.                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| `CHECK DATE`                 | `01-Apr-26`                                                                                                                             | The date written on the check.                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| `CHECK NO`                   | `000019370`, `000019370`, `000019371`, `000019371`, `0000658947`                                                                        | **Important — one check covers multiple CVs**: Check `000019370` covers CV 1564 (₱15,657) + CV 1565 (₱9,035.71); Check `000019371` covers CV 1566 (₱10,000) + CV 1567 (₱75,000); Check `0000658947` covers only CV 1568 (₱50,000).                          |
| `BANK ACCOUNT`               | `067-4`, `067-4`, `067-4`, `067-4`, `852-5`                                                                                             | The bank account funding the payment — matches the suffix on the Cash-In-Bank chart entries (`10003 Cash-In-Bank-LAND BANK-067-4`). The `852-5` account is a Security Bank account not yet captured in the visible CoA — flagged below.                     |
| `Voucher's Payable`          | `15,657.00`, `9,035.71`, `10,000.00`, `75,000.00`, `50,000.00`                                                                          | The **DEBIT side** of the disbursement journal entry. Clears the credit posted by the upstream Payable Voucher. Maps to `20004 Voucher's Payable`.                                                                                                          |
| `Workshop Account/580…067-4` | `(15,657.00)`, `(9,035.71)`, `(10,000.00)`, `(75,000.00)`, _(empty for row 10)_                                                         | A bank-specific CREDIT column — the cash outflow from this specific account. Negative because it's an outflow from the bank's perspective. The `580` prefix suggests a Land Bank sub-account labeled "Workshop" — likely an account not in the visible CoA. |
| `Non-Vat Account 029-1`      | _(empty in visible rows)_                                                                                                               | Land Bank `029-1` (matches `10004 Cash-In-Bank-LAND BANK-029-1`). The "Non-Vat Account" label suggests this bank is JCE's account for Non-VAT receivables (the SOA collections flow from 5.4).                                                              |
| `Security Bank 852-5`        | _(empty)_, _(empty)_, _(empty)_, _(empty)_, `(50,000.00)`                                                                               | The Security Bank cash outflow for CV 1568.                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| `DOLLAR ACCT`                | _(empty visible)_                                                                                                                       | The USD bank accounts (`10007/10008/10011`).                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| `Vou…` (truncated)           | —                                                                                                                                       | Likely a continuation or summary column.                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |

The pivoted column structure (one column per bank account, plus a "Voucher's Payable" debit column) is a **report view** — internally each row is a single Dr/Cr pair, displayed across columns so a quick visual scan tells you "this disbursement came out of bank 067-4 for ₱15,657, clearing 15,657 in Voucher's Payable." The Excel column total for each bank column gives the period's cash outflow from that bank — a natural input to bank reconciliation.

#### The two-step posting model (resolves PV open item #14)

This artifact settles the architectural question from 5.5 about whether Payable Voucher and Disbursement are one record or two:

```
Step 1 — Payable Voucher authorized (5.5)
  Dr  Expense / Asset (Sundry Account, e.g., 50001 Direct Materials)
  Cr      Voucher's Payable (20004)

Step 2 — Disbursement executed (5.6)
  Dr  Voucher's Payable (20004)              ← clears the prior credit
  Cr      Cash-In-Bank (e.g., 10003 LAND BANK-067-4)
```

`20004 Voucher's Payable` is the **clearing account** — a transit account that holds the obligation between authorization and payment. After both entries post, Voucher's Payable nets to zero for that CV, the expense / asset is recognized, and the bank balance is reduced. **PV and Disbursement are two linked records, not one.**

This also clarifies how the chart's `20001 Accounts Payable - Trade` vs `20004 Voucher's Payable` distinction plays out: 20001 holds long-cycle supplier obligations (invoice received but not yet authorized for payment); 20004 holds short-cycle authorized-but-not-paid obligations. Both ultimately settle the same way (Dr Payable / Cr Cash), but 20004 is the one the system uses for the PV-to-Disbursement transit.

#### Data model — Disbursement record

| Field                                           | Source / Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| ----------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Identification**                              |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| `disbursement_id`                               | System-assigned UUID; the natural-key view uses `cv_no` since it's 1:1 with PV.                                                                                                                                                           |
| `disbursement_date`                             | The date the bank was charged (typically = `check_date` for checks, or the bank-transfer execution date).                                                                                                                                 |
| **Source voucher linkage**                      |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| `cv_no`                                         | **FK to 5.5 Payable Voucher.** Each Disbursement references exactly one Approved CV. Derived display fields (payee, description, gross_amount, net_amount) come from the CV; the Disbursement does not duplicate them in its own columns. |
| **Payment instrument**                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| `payment_method`                                | Enum: `Check` · `Bank Transfer` · `Cash`. Drives which of the next fields are required.                                                                                                                                                   |
| `check_no`                                      | When `payment_method = Check`. Globally unique (within the JCE bank account that issued it).                                                                                                                                              |
| `check_date`                                    | When `payment_method = Check`. The date written on the check; may differ from `disbursement_date` if the check is post-dated.                                                                                                             |
| `bank_reference_no`                             | When `payment_method = Bank Transfer`. Like the BDO mobile reference `PC-NDBMOB-20260226-76725862` from the 5.5 artifact.                                                                                                                 |
| `cash_release_form`                             | When `payment_method = Cash`. Reference to the petty-cash release slip if one exists.                                                                                                                                                     |
| **Bank account funding the payment**            |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| `bank_account`                                  | **FK to Chart of Accounts** — one of the `10001–10012` Cash-In-Bank accounts. Drives the credit-side journal post.                                                                                                                        |
| **Journal entry** (computed; not user-editable) |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| `dr_account`                                    | Always `20004 Voucher's Payable`.                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| `dr_amount`                                     | Net amount paid (matches the gross_amount on the CV less withholding tax — i.e., the cash that actually leaves the bank).                                                                                                                 |
| `cr_account`                                    | `bank_account` (e.g., `10003`).                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| `cr_amount`                                     | Same as `dr_amount`.                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| **Status**                                      |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| `status`                                        | `Recorded` (paid, normal) · `Voided` (the bank execution was reversed, e.g., stopped check) · `Reissued` (a voided disbursement was re-cut against a new check).                                                                          |
| **Audit**                                       |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| `recorded_by`, `recorded_at`                    | Who entered the bank execution and when.                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| `voided_by`, `voided_at`, `void_reason`         | If voided.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| `attachments[]`                                 | Bank confirmation screenshot (the BDO Send Money image from 5.5), check stub photo, or scanned check copy.                                                                                                                                |

#### The Check entity — implicit but useful

One Check Number can fund multiple CVs (`000019370` paid both CV 1564 and CV 1565). Two model choices:

**Option A — Check as a separate entity** (relational, clean):

- A **Check** has its own record (check_no, check_date, bank_account, total_amount, payee_on_check, status).
- A Check has 1..N child Disbursements (one per CV it pays).
- The Disbursement record stores `check_id` (FK to Check) instead of `check_no` directly.

**Option B — Check_no as a denormalized field on Disbursement** (current Excel pattern):

- Each Disbursement carries its own `check_no`.
- Multi-CV checks are recognized by `GROUP BY check_no`.
- The system computes "what did Check 000019370 pay for" by aggregating.

Option A is cleaner for void-the-whole-check workflows and matches how the bank itself thinks (one check = one debit on the bank statement). Option B is closer to the legacy Excel. **Recommend Option A**; confirm with Bryan.

#### Cross-module integrations

- **Payable Voucher (5.5)** — every Disbursement points back at one CV. The CV's status flips from `Approved` to `Paid` when its Disbursement is recorded.
- **Chart of Accounts (5.2.1 Section 8)** — `bank_account` is an FK to one of `10001–10012` (Cash-In-Bank entries). `dr_account` is always `20004 Voucher's Payable`.
- **Suppliers (Purchasing 7.9) and Employees (4.1)** — payee derived from the CV; no duplicate lookup at the Disbursement level. For Supplier disbursements, the full upstream chain is **Purchasing PO 7.5 → RFP 7.7 → Accounting PV 5.5 → Disbursement 5.6 → bank execution**.
- **Project Costing (5.8)** — the Disbursement's bank-side cash outflow rolls into per-project cost (via the CV's `sales_order_no`), parallel to how Payroll's labor cost contributes through 5.2.3.
- **Reporting (5.9)** — the Bank Disbursement register _is_ one of the reports. Period totals per bank account are the input to bank reconciliation. A "Disbursements by Project" pivot is the natural cross-project cost view.
- **Future bank-reconciliation feature** — once Disbursements live in the system, matching them against the actual bank statement (uploaded as CSV or fetched via banking API) becomes the next-tier feature. Out of scope for v1; mentioned here so the data model anticipates it.

#### Per-disbursement input fields — `+ Record Disbursement`

The intended flow: an Approved CV from 5.5 is opened, and the user clicks "Record Payment" to enter the bank execution.

| Field                                    | Behavior                                                                                                                                                            |
| ---------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `cv_no`                                  | Pre-filled from the source CV. Read-only.                                                                                                                           |
| `payee`, `description`, `net_amount`     | Read-only display from CV.                                                                                                                                          |
| `disbursement_date`                      | Defaults to today; editable.                                                                                                                                        |
| `payment_method`                         | Radio: Check / Bank Transfer / Cash.                                                                                                                                |
| `check_no` _(if Check)_                  | If using a multi-CV check, the system shows other CVs already attached to this check and warns if the running check total exceeds the typed payee's expected total. |
| `check_date` _(if Check)_                | Defaults to `disbursement_date`.                                                                                                                                    |
| `bank_reference_no` _(if Bank Transfer)_ | Manual paste of the bank app's transfer ID.                                                                                                                         |
| `bank_account`                           | Dropdown from `10001–10012`. Defaults from the CV's `payment_bank` field if pre-filled there.                                                                       |
| `attachments`                            | Upload bank confirmation screenshot, check stub, or signed receipt.                                                                                                 |
| **Confirm and post**                     | On Save: (1) creates Disbursement record; (2) posts Dr 20004 / Cr `bank_account` journal entry; (3) flips source CV's status to `Paid`.                             |

#### UX considerations

1. **Single screen / register view.** The default Disbursement page is the wide register matching the legacy Excel — one row per disbursement, columns for date, CV no, payee, description, check date, check no, bank account, amount. Bank-account pivot columns are a **print/export layout**, not the screen view (too wide to scroll).
2. **Filter by project ("ALL PROJECT" → project picker).** The Excel header says "ALL PROJECT" — the system default; users can filter to a specific Sales Order to see only that project's disbursements. Mirrors how the Payroll Summary 5.2.3 register supports both Consolidated and Per-Project views.
3. **Period filter.** Default to current month; date range filter for ad-hoc views.
4. **Bank-account filter.** Filter to one bank to prep for that bank's reconciliation.
5. **Group by check.** When several rows share a `check_no`, the screen view shows them grouped (visually indented or with a visual rule) so the user sees "this check paid X, Y, Z."
6. **Check duplicate warning.** If the user types a `check_no` that already exists on a different disbursement, the system asks "Is this part of a multi-CV check?" — and either links it (Option A) or accepts the duplicate with a warning (Option B).
7. **Void workflow.** Voiding a Disbursement (because the check bounced, was stopped, or the bank transfer was reversed) posts a reversing journal entry (`Cr 20004 / Dr Cash-In-Bank`) and flips the source CV back to `Approved` status. A new Disbursement can then be cut against the same CV. The void event is audited with reason.
