8. **Bank reconciliation prep.** A read-only "Bank Reconciliation Worksheet" view that groups Disbursements by bank account, totals them for the period, and shows the running cash position. Compares against an uploaded bank statement (CSV) once that feature exists — out of scope for v1.
9. **Print / PDF export.** The wide register format (matching the legacy Excel layout — Date, CV No, Payee, Description, Check Date, Check No, Bank Account, plus the pivoted bank-account columns) prints as a landscape-Legal report. Period totals at the bottom.
10. **"ALL PROJECT" report.** The unfiltered register, with grand totals per bank account. Matches the artifact's title.

#### DISB-XXX requirements

- **DISB-001** — Every Disbursement record shall reference exactly one Approved Payable Voucher (1:1).
- **DISB-002** — Recording a Disbursement shall post the journal entry `Dr 20004 Voucher's Payable / Cr Cash-In-Bank (bank_account)` and flip the source CV's status from `Approved` to `Paid`.
- **DISB-003** — One `check_no` may be shared across multiple Disbursements (multi-CV checks). The system shall recognize multi-CV checks and allow grouped operations (e.g., void all Disbursements on a stopped check in one action).
- **DISB-004** — Voiding a Disbursement shall post a reversing journal entry, flip the source CV's status back to `Approved`, and log the void with user, timestamp, and reason. Voided Disbursements remain visible for audit.
- **DISB-005** — The Disbursement register shall be printable / PDF-exportable in the legacy bank-pivot layout (one column per bank account, plus a Voucher's Payable column). Recommended layout: Landscape on Legal or Long bond paper.
- **DISB-006** — The register shall support filtering by project (Sales Order), bank account, payment method, and date range; printed output reflects the active filters.
- **DISB-007** — All status changes (Recorded → Voided → Reissued) shall be append-only and audited.

#### Open items for Bryan's confirmation

1. **Check entity model (Option A vs Option B).** Recommend Option A — Check as a separate entity with 1..N Disbursements. Cleaner for void-the-whole-check workflows. Confirm.
2. **CV number format reconciliation.** The 5.5 voucher artifact showed `CV-0902` (4 digits with `CV-` prefix). The 5.6 register shows `1564`, `1565`, etc. (4 digits, no prefix). Are these the same counter — i.e., `CV-0902` and `0902` refer to the same voucher — and is the `CV-` prefix just a display convention on the printed voucher? Confirm.
3. **`852-5` bank account identification.** The register has rows funded from `852-5` (column header "Security Bank") but the visible CoA pages show only `10010 Cash-In-Bank-Security Bank Peso` without a `852-5` suffix on the account name. Is `852-5` the actual Security Bank account number that should be appended to `10010`'s name (like the Land Bank accounts have suffixes `067-4`, `029-1`, `032-1`, `031-3`)? Or is there a missing CoA page?
4. **`580` Workshop account missing from CoA.** The register has a column "Workshop Account/580…067-4" suggesting a Land Bank Workshop sub-account with prefix `580`. Not visible in the captured CoA. Is this a separate account that needs to be added to the chart, or is it a sub-divisional view of `10003 Cash-In-Bank-LAND BANK-067-4`?
5. **PCF Replenishment workflow.** Row 6 (RIZA MAY BATUIGAS, PCF REPLENISHMENT NO. 74) is the petty-cash replenishment cycle: the petty-cash fund custodian liquidates expenses, and a CV is cut to top up the float back to its standard level. The CV's debit side here is interesting — it's not a single expense account but a **batch of expense accounts** matching the liquidated petty-cash receipts (meals, transport, supplies, etc.). Should the system support multi-line debit posting for replenishment vouchers? Mirror or differ from the standard single-debit voucher model?
6. **Partial payment of a CV.** Can a single CV be paid across two Disbursements (e.g., the supplier was given ₱20K today and ₱19K tomorrow against a ₱39K voucher)? Or is it strictly one CV = one Disbursement? Likely no (the legacy register suggests 1:1), but worth confirming.
7. **Joint payee — "NELDA MENDOZA/JOCEN CANITAN".** Row 10 shows two employees on one disbursement. Should the system support multi-payee disbursements explicitly (a `payees[]` array on the CV), or is the slash-separated string the simplest practical solution? Recommend keeping it simple — the payee shows as the slash-separated string on the printed voucher, and both employees individually receive the audit-trail visibility through the payment description.
8. **Cash disbursements.** The artifact shows only checks; the system also needs to support direct bank transfers (the BDO mobile case from 5.5) and cash. The cash case raises the question of which bank account is debited — likely `10002 Petty Cash Fund` rather than a Cash-In-Bank account. Confirm the cash workflow.
9. **Negative disbursements?** The register doesn't show negatives, but if a bounced check or refund needs to reverse a prior disbursement, the system should support the void/reverse mechanic (DISB-004) rather than negative-amount records. Confirm.
10. **Bank reconciliation scope for v1.** The register clearly anticipates eventual reconciliation against actual bank statements (the per-bank column totals). Is bank reconciliation a v1 feature, a v1.5 add-on, or v2? Drives whether the data model needs a `reconciliation_status` field per disbursement now or later.

### 5.7 Journal Voucher _(preliminary scope — v1.53)_

**Purpose.** The Journal Voucher (JV) sub-tab is JCE's **flexible double-entry workspace** — the place where any transaction that doesn't naturally come through Sales (5.3), Collections (5.4), Payable Voucher (5.5), Disbursement (5.6), or Payroll (5.2.3) gets recorded as a hand-built journal entry. Each JV has N debit lines and N credit lines drawn from the Chart of Accounts (5.2.1 Section 8), and the system enforces `Σ debits = Σ credits` before the JV can be posted. **The most common JV use case at JCE — based on the artifact provided — is the Cash Advance Liquidation**, where a previously-issued cash advance is closed against the actual expenses the employee paid for, with any unused cash returned to the bank. JVs also cover period-end adjustments, reclassifications, depreciation runs, VAT settlement entries, and error corrections.

#### Source artifact

**Journal Voucher JV-0159** (filled paper voucher, dated 7-May-26, ₱25,000 total):

- **Header**: JCE company name and address; printed title "JOURNAL VOUCHER"; Series No. (`JV-0159`); Date (`7-May-26`); Project (`WORKSHOP/QUEZELCO`); SO No. (`25-10-346`).
- **Body — Reference / Description / Debit / Credit table**:

| REFERENCE   | DESCRIPTION              |         DEBIT |        CREDIT |
| ----------- | ------------------------ | ------------: | ------------: |
|             | CASH IN BANK - 067-4     |      3,147.44 |               |
|             | REPRESENTATION EXPENSE   |     10,869.72 |               |
|             | ACCOMMODATION EXP.       |      4,957.84 |               |
|             | TRANSPORTATION EXPENSE   |      3,525.00 |               |
|             | PROJECT OVERHEAD         |      2,500.00 |               |
| `CV - 1635` | CASH ADV. LIQ. CA26-0064 |               |     25,000.00 |
|             | **Php**                  | **25,000.00** | **25,000.00** |

- **Narrative annotations**: `CV - LIQUIDATED`, `MEETING W TWG CHAIRMAN ENGR. CUBELCO FOR BOHERO PROJECT`, `PAYEE: DENOWELL HIDALGO`.
- **Signatures**: **Prepared by Angelita S. Enopia** (left), **Checked by Riza Capillo / Grace Irlandez** (right — two names handwritten, suggesting either checker can sign).

**Excel JV tracker snippet** (single visible row):

| JCE-PR | Date        | Particulars         | Project | RFP NO.    | Sundry Account                       | Amount | Description                                  | SO NO.      |
| ------ | ----------- | ------------------- | ------- | ---------- | ------------------------------------ | -----: | -------------------------------------------- | ----------- |
| `0245` | `18-Jan-25` | `ROWENA V. ADRIANO` | `NPSI`  | `ADM-0098` | `Cash Advance - Liquid…` (truncated) |    `-` | `CASH ADVANCE -CA25-0032 - KICK OFF MEETING` | `25-01-259` |

This shows the JV tracker is shaped similar to the Payment Request worksheet, with `Sundry Account` highlighted in red (likely to flag attention) and the `Amount` left blank or shown as `-` for liquidation rows (because the JV's net effect is zero — it just reclassifies the existing advance balance into expenses).

#### The Cash Advance Liquidation pattern

The JV-0159 artifact is a textbook **Cash Advance Liquidation**. The full lifecycle of a Cash Advance at JCE spans four sub-tabs and is tied together by the **`CA-YY-XXXX` reference number** (e.g., `CA26-0064`):

```
Step 1 — PV issued (5.5)                    [CV-1635, CA26-0064, ₱25,000]
  Dr  11037 Advances for Liquidation       25,000
  Cr      20004 Voucher's Payable               25,000

Step 2 — Disbursement executed (5.6)         [Cash leaves bank 067-4]
  Dr  20004 Voucher's Payable              25,000
  Cr      10003 Cash in Bank - 067-4              25,000

Step 3 — Employee spends the advance         [No system entry; receipts collected on paper]
  - Representation receipts:   ₱10,869.72
  - Accommodation receipts:     ₱4,957.84
  - Transportation receipts:    ₱3,525.00
  - Project overhead receipts:  ₱2,500.00
  - Subtotal spent:            ₱21,852.56
  - Unused cash returned:       ₱3,147.44
  - Total to liquidate:        ₱25,000.00

Step 4 — JV liquidates the advance (5.7)     [JV-0159, references CV-1635]
  Dr  10003 Cash in Bank - 067-4            3,147.44
  Dr  50043 Representations & Entertain    10,869.72
  Dr  [Accommodation Expense]               4,957.84
  Dr  50054 Travel & Transportation         3,525.00
  Dr  [Project Overhead]                    2,500.00
  Cr      11037 Advances for Liquidation         25,000.00
```

After Step 4, the original ₱25,000 sitting as an asset in `11037 Advances for Liquidation` (it was JCE's money temporarily with the employee) is closed: ₱3,147.44 returns to the bank, ₱21,852.56 becomes real expense recognized against the project. The `CA26-0064` reference is the thread that connects the CV, the Disbursement, and the JV — and lets Accounting answer "is CA26-0064 liquidated or still outstanding?" by checking whether a JV with that CA reference exists.

**The Cash Advance is implicitly an entity that spans sub-tabs.** Three modeling choices:

1. **Reference-only** — `ca_no` is just a free-text field on the PV and on the JV; reports group by `ca_no` to derive the lifecycle view. Simplest.
2. **Implicit entity** — the system maintains a derived "Cash Advance Ledger" view that's just a query (SELECT … FROM PV WHERE description LIKE 'CASH ADVANCE%' UNION SELECT … FROM JV WHERE description LIKE 'CASH ADV. LIQ.%'). Read-only.
3. **First-class entity** — a `cash_advance` record with fields (ca_no, issued_to, project_so_no, issued_amount, source_cv_id, status, liquidating_jv_id, outstanding_balance). Created automatically when a Cash Advance PV is issued; updated when the liquidating JV posts. Most explicit; supports an "Outstanding Cash Advances" report directly without query gymnastics.

Recommend **Option 3 (first-class entity)** because it aligns with how JCE thinks about advances (an outstanding-balance concept), gives clean reporting on stale advances (those issued > 30 days ago without liquidation), and integrates naturally with employee-level accountability (sum of advances per employee). Confirm with Bryan.

#### Other JV use cases

Beyond Cash Advance Liquidation, JV is the catch-all for:

- **Period-end adjustments** — accruing salaries earned but not yet paid (Dr 50046 Salaries / Cr 20018 Accrued Salaries Payable), accruing utilities, expense recognition for prepaid items as they consume.
- **Reclassifications** — moving a previously-recorded expense from one GL account to another (e.g., a meal recorded as `50032 Meals Expense` should have been `50043 Representations & Entertainment` because it was a client meeting).
- **Depreciation runs** — monthly entry of `Dr 50018 Depreciation Expense / Cr 12054-12060 Accumulated Depreciation` per asset category.
- **VAT settlement entries** — clearing the period's VAT Input (20015) and VAT Output (20016) into VAT Payable (20017) for BIR remittance.
- **Bad debt write-offs** — writing off uncollectible Trade Receivable (Dr Bad Debt Expense / Cr 11022 Account Receivable - Trade).
- **Year-end closing entries** — closing revenue and expense accounts into Retained Earnings (30003).
- **Error corrections** — reversing a misposted entry and posting it correctly. Always done via a balanced JV, never by editing the original.
- **Inter-bank transfers** — moving cash between two JCE bank accounts (Dr `10004` / Cr `10003`).
- **Loan accruals** — interest expense recognized periodically on outstanding loans.

#### Data model — Journal Voucher record

| Field                                  | Source / Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| -------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Identification**                     |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| `jv_series_no`                         | `JV-XXXX` — system-assigned sequential. Globally unique within the year.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| `jv_date`                              | The transaction date the JV records (the accounting date, not necessarily the entry date).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| **Project linkage (optional)**         |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| `project_so_no`                        | FK to Sales Order. Optional — some JVs are project-tagged (the artifact case: WORKSHOP/QUEZELCO 25-10-346), others are general-overhead (no SO).                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| `project_label`                        | Free-text project label as written on the form (e.g., `WORKSHOP/QUEZELCO`).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| **Payee / context (optional)**         |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| `payee`                                | When the JV concerns an employee or supplier (e.g., a Cash Advance Liquidation cites the original advance recipient). Free text or FK to Employees/Suppliers.                                                                                                                                                                           |
| `description`                          | The "what's being recorded" narrative (e.g., `MEETING W TWG CHAIRMAN ENGR. CUBELCO FOR BOHERO PROJECT`). Multi-line free text.                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| `jv_category`                          | Enum: `Cash Advance Liquidation` · `Period-End Adjustment` · `Reclassification` · `Depreciation Run` · `VAT Settlement` · `Write-Off` · `Closing Entry` · `Error Correction` · `Inter-Bank Transfer` · `Other`. Drives reporting filters and any category-specific validation (e.g., a Cash Advance Liquidation must cite a source CV). |
| **Source-document linkage**            |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| `reference_cv_id`                      | FK to a Payable Voucher (5.5) — populated for Cash Advance Liquidations (the artifact's `CV - 1635`). NULL for other categories.                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| `cash_advance_ref`                     | The `CA-YY-XXXX` advance reference number (e.g., `CA26-0064`).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                          |
| **Journal lines** (1..N rows)          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| `lines[]`                              | Array of `{ reference (optional, free text per line), account_code (FK to CoA), description (free text), debit (decimal, ≥ 0), credit (decimal, ≥ 0) }`. Each line has either a debit or a credit but not both.                                                                                                                         |
| **Computed totals**                    |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| `total_debits`, `total_credits`        | Sum across lines. Must be equal at Save (validation).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| **Workflow / sign-off**                |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| `prepared_by`, `prepared_at`           | The clerk who built the JV.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                             |
| `checked_by`, `checked_at`             | The accounting head who verified.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| `posted_at`                            | When the JV's journal entries become final in the GL. Once posted, the JV is immutable (strict no-edit pattern — see open item).                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| **Status**                             |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| `status`                               | `Draft` · `Pending Check` · `Posted` · `Reversed`.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| **Reversal linkage** (when applicable) |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| `reverses_jv_id`                       | FK to a prior JV that this one reverses (for error corrections). Null if not a reversal.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| `reversed_by_jv_id`                    | FK to a reversing JV created later (if this JV has been reversed).                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| **Attachments**                        |                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                         |
| `attachments[]`                        | Supporting receipts (for Cash Advance Liquidations — the actual receipts the employee submitted), depreciation schedules, BIR forms, etc.                                                                                                                                                                                               |

#### Cross-module integrations

- **Payable Voucher (5.5)** — `reference_cv_id` is the link for Cash Advance Liquidations. The CV's status remains `Paid` after the linked JV is posted; the JV is the _liquidation_ event, not a state on the CV itself.
- **Chart of Accounts (5.2.1 Section 8)** — every journal line references a CoA `account_code`. The visible CoA covers most JV postings but **two visible expense accounts on JV-0159 are not in the captured pages**: `ACCOMMODATION EXP.` and `PROJECT OVERHEAD` — flagged as open items.
- **Cash Advance ledger (proposed Option 3)** — when a Cash Advance Liquidation JV posts, it updates the Cash Advance record's `outstanding_balance = 0` and `status = Liquidated`.
- **Project Costing (5.8)** — every JV line with a project_so_no contributes to that project's cost roll-up. The artifact's ₱21,852.56 of expense lines all roll into project 25-10-346 (Workshop/Quezelco).
- **Reporting (5.9)** — JVs participate in the trial balance and the General Ledger like every other transaction. A "Cash Advance Aging" report becomes natural (outstanding advances grouped by age — 0–30 days, 31–60, 61–90, 90+).
- **Audit log** — every Draft → Pending Check → Posted → Reversed transition is logged with user, timestamp, and reason; matches the pattern used in Offers 9.4 and Quotation 9.5.

#### Per-JV input fields — `+ New Journal Voucher`

| Field                                    | Behavior                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
| ---------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------- | ----------------------------------------------------------- |
| `jv_series_no`                           | Auto-suggested sequential (`JV-XXXX`); manually overridable; uniqueness validated at Save.                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| `jv_date`                                | Defaults to today; editable.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| `jv_category`                            | Dropdown; defaults to `Other`. Selecting `Cash Advance Liquidation` reveals the `reference_cv` lookup and `cash_advance_ref` field, and pre-fills the project_so_no and payee from the source CV.                                                                               |
| `project_so_no`                          | Optional lookup against BDD 9.3 Sales Order.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| `project_label`, `payee`, `description`  | Free text.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                      |
| `reference_cv`                           | Lookup against PV when `jv_category = Cash Advance Liquidation`.                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| `cash_advance_ref`                       | Free text — `CA26-0064` pattern.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| **Lines table**                          | Dynamic table — `+ Add line`, each row has: `reference` (optional), `account_code` (dropdown from active CoA), `description`, `debit` (number), `credit` (number). The system enforces exactly one of debit/credit per line (not both). Running totals at the bottom: `Σ Debits | Σ Credits | Difference`. Difference must be zero to enable Save / Post. |
| `attachments`                            | Multi-file upload — supporting receipts, BIR forms, depreciation schedules.                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| **Save Draft / Submit for Check / Post** | Three-stage workflow. Save Draft is editable. Submit for Check moves to `Pending Check`. Post finalizes — JV becomes immutable.                                                                                                                                                 |

#### UX considerations

1. **Two-stage workflow** (Prepared → Checked → Posted). Simpler than PV's three-stage (Prepared → VP-Finance → President) because JVs are internal accounting adjustments, not external cash outflows. The "Checked by" line on the artifact shows two names (Riza Capillo / Grace Irlandez) — likely either-or, not both. Confirm.
2. **Balance enforcement at every save.** Even Draft JVs should warn if Σ debits ≠ Σ credits, though Drafts can be saved unbalanced (work-in-progress). Posting requires balance = 0.00 exact (no tolerance — accounting requires exact penny-level match).
3. **Line-level account search.** When typing into the `account_code` cell, the dropdown filters by both code prefix (typing `500` shows expense accounts) and name fuzzy match (typing `meal` highlights `50032 Meals Expense`). Speed matters because power users (Angelita/Riza/Grace) will be building dozens of JVs per month.
4. **Cash Advance Liquidation wizard.** When `jv_category = Cash Advance Liquidation`, the form switches to a guided layout: enter source CV → system pre-fills outstanding balance, payee, project; user enters expense breakdown and returned-cash amount; system validates `Σ expenses + returned cash = outstanding balance` automatically. Reduces error rate on the most common JV type.
5. **Depreciation Run automation.** Once monthly depreciation schedules are in Settings (future), the system can generate the monthly depreciation JV automatically — the user just reviews and posts.
6. **Strict no-edit on Posted JVs** (mirroring Offers 9.4, Quotation 9.5, and the proposed Sales/Collections pattern). A Posted JV cannot be edited; corrections happen via a reversing JV that explicitly cites the original (`reverses_jv_id`).
7. **Reversal-as-new-JV pattern.** A "Reverse this JV" action on a Posted JV creates a Draft JV with debits and credits flipped, references back to the original, and pre-fills the description with `REVERSAL OF JV-XXXX:` . User reviews and posts; both JVs remain in the ledger.
8. **Print layout matches the legacy form.** Header (company, JV series no, date, project, SO no), body (Reference / Description / Debit / Credit table), narrative annotation (CV - Liquidated, meeting details, payee), totals row, and the two-signature block. Recommend **Portrait on Letter or Long bond paper**.
9. **Outstanding Cash Advances widget.** A dashboard tile / report showing all Cash Advances (sourced from Cash Advance ledger if Option 3 is adopted) where outstanding_balance > 0, sorted by age. Flags stale advances that should have been liquidated already — a real operational concern at JCE (cash advances tend to accumulate when employees don't promptly submit liquidations).
10. **Project filter, category filter, period filter** on the JV list view. Mirrors the filter set on PV (5.5) and Disbursement (5.6).

#### JV-XXX requirements

- **JV-001** — Every Journal Voucher shall contain at least 1 debit line and 1 credit line. The system shall enforce `Σ debits = Σ credits` exactly (zero tolerance) before allowing Post.
- **JV-002** — Every journal line shall reference an active account from the Chart of Accounts (5.2.1 Section 8). Free-text account names are not accepted.
- **JV-003** — JV series numbers shall be unique within the year. The system suggests the next sequential number on `+ New JV`; manual override is allowed for matching a pre-printed booklet.
- **JV-004** — Once a JV transitions to `Posted` status, its lines, accounts, amounts, and descriptions become immutable. Corrections require a reversing JV that references the original via `reverses_jv_id`.
- **JV-005** — When `jv_category = Cash Advance Liquidation`, the JV shall require: a valid `reference_cv_id`, a `cash_advance_ref` (the CA-YY-XXXX reference), and the journal must include at least one credit line to the advance-source account (e.g., `11037 Advances for Liquidation`) that matches the outstanding balance on that advance.
- **JV-006** — The JV shall be printable matching the legacy form layout (header, line table, totals, narrative annotation, two-signature block). PDF export shall also be available.
- **JV-007** — The system shall maintain an audit log of every JV state transition (Draft → Pending Check → Posted → Reversed) with user, timestamp, and reason; append-only.

#### Open items for Bryan's confirmation

1. **Cash Advance modeling — Option 1 (reference-only) vs Option 2 (derived view) vs Option 3 (first-class entity).** Recommend **Option 3** (first-class `cash_advance` record with outstanding_balance, source_cv, liquidating_jv) — it gives clean reporting on outstanding/stale advances, which seems operationally important at JCE. Confirm.
2. **`ACCOMMODATION EXP.` missing from captured CoA pages.** The JV-0159 debit includes `4,957.84` to an Accommodation Expense account. Not visible in the captured 4-page CoA. Either it's on a page not shown, or it should be added. **Confirm.**
3. **`PROJECT OVERHEAD` missing from captured CoA pages.** The JV-0159 debit includes `2,500.00` to a Project Overhead account. Same question. Possibly a sub-grouping under the 50000-series that wasn't captured. **Confirm.**
4. **Two-stage workflow confirmation.** Artifact shows Prepared → Checked, with the "Checked by" line bearing two names (Riza Capillo / Grace Irlandez). Confirm that JVs follow the two-stage workflow (versus PV's three-stage), and confirm whether Riza and Grace are alternate checkers or co-checkers (both must sign).
5. **JV-XXXX numbering format.** Confirm the format is `JV-XXXX` (4-digit padding, year-reset) matching the artifact's `JV-0159`. And confirm whether the counter is annual (resets January 1) or perpetual.
6. **Cash Advance reference format `CA-YY-XXXX`.** Confirm format and uniqueness rules. The artifacts show `CA25-0032` (2-digit year + 4-digit serial) and `CA26-0064` — looks like 2-digit-year-prefixed 4-digit serial. Where does the CA number get issued — at the PV stage when the advance is cut, or at a separate "issue advance" step?
7. **Strict no-edit on Posted JVs.** Confirm the Posted-is-immutable pattern. Corrections via reversing JV, not by editing the original.
8. **Reversal mechanic — auto-flip vs manual rebuild.** When a user clicks "Reverse this JV" on a Posted JV, should the system auto-generate the reversing JV with debits/credits flipped (then user reviews/posts), or require the user to manually rebuild the inverse entry? Auto-flip is faster but riskier (the user might post a wrong reversal without thinking); manual rebuild forces deliberation. Recommend auto-flip with mandatory review.
9. **`jv_category` enumeration — completeness.** The proposed list (Cash Advance Liquidation, Period-End Adjustment, Reclassification, Depreciation Run, VAT Settlement, Write-Off, Closing Entry, Error Correction, Inter-Bank Transfer, Other) covers what comes to mind. Confirm with Accounting whether additional categories are used routinely.
10. **Returned-cash flow for Cash Advance Liquidation.** The artifact's `3,147.44` Dr to Cash in Bank represents unused cash returned to the bank. In practice this means the employee literally deposited the change back to JCE's account. Confirm the workflow: does Accounting deposit the returned cash separately and reference that deposit on the JV, or is the JV the _only_ record of the deposit? If the latter, the bank reconciliation needs to recognize JV-sourced cash inflows in addition to Collection-sourced ones.
11. **Project Overhead as a category vs an account.** The line "PROJECT OVERHEAD ₱2,500" is suspiciously round — could be a flat per-meeting allowance JCE pays to cover incidental project costs. Is this an actual GL account, or a placeholder category that should be reclassified into a more specific expense (Representation, Office Supplies, Miscellaneous)? **Worth asking.**

### 5.8 Project Costing _(deferred — v1.55)_

**Purpose.** Aggregate all costs incurred against a project (Sales Order) and compare to the contract amount so Accounting can see project profitability. Cross-module: labor cost from **Timekeeping (4.2)** rolled up by Working Project; materials, services, and outside-supplier costs from **Payable Voucher (5.5)** + **Disbursement (5.6)** tagged to the SO#; project-tagged Journal Voucher (5.7) postings (e.g., the Cash Advance Liquidation expenses charged to a project); subcontractor advances from the Suppliers/Subcontractors flow; potentially overhead allocation rules.

**Status — explicitly deferred (per Bryan, v1.55).** Project Costing depends on data emitted by every sub-tab around it and is best scoped after the other modules are stable and the system has live transactional data to roll up against. Surfaced as a Family C operational report in 5.9 Reporting (the "Project Costing summary" line) for the v1 build; the dedicated 5.8 sub-tab with cost categories, allocation rules, and profitability dashboards comes in a later phase.

_To detail when this tab is revisited: cost categories (labor / materials / subcontract / overhead / other), labor-cost allocation method (Timekeeping's per-project even-split from 4.2 is the input), profitability calculation (Gross Margin %, Net Margin %, Cost Overrun flag against Sales Order's Contract Amount from 9.3), per-project dashboard with cost-to-date / billed-to-date / remaining-contract layout, and any overhead allocation rules JCE wants to apply (flat percentage, weighted by direct cost, etc.)._

### 5.9 Reporting _(preliminary scope — v1.54)_

**Purpose.** The Reporting sub-tab is JCE's **financial-statement, statutory, and operational reporting layer**. It consumes the journal-entry stream produced by every other sub-tab — Sales (5.3), Collections (5.4), Payable Voucher (5.5), Disbursement (5.6), Journal Voucher (5.7), and Payroll Summary (5.2.3) — plus the master data in the Chart of Accounts (5.2.1 Section 8), and produces (a) the four **core financial statements** (Trial Balance, Balance Sheet, Income Statement, Cash Flow), (b) **statutory reports** for BIR, SSS, PhilHealth, and Pag-IBIG, and (c) **operational reports** that aggregate transactional data into management views (cash position, AR aging, payroll registers, project profitability, cash-advance aging). The financial statements at JCE are presented in **BIR-aligned formats** — the Income Statement uses BIR Form 1702 Schedule prefixes (Sched I … Sched XLI), the Balance Sheet uses BIR-style Roman-numeral line items (I … XXXV) — because the same statements feed both internal management and the annual tax return.

#### Source artifacts

Bryan provided four artifacts establishing the report inventory and layout:

**Artifact 1 — Trial Balance** (Excel, "TRIAL BALANCE-Chart of Accounts / For the year 2025") — a wide period-comparative grid where each row is one CoA account and each column-pair is one period (Total beginning balance, January activity, January cumulative total, February activity, …). Visible rows include `CASH IN BANK-LANDBANK-0674(WORKSHOP)`, `CASH IN BANK-LANDBANK-0291 (NON-VAT)`, `CASH IN BANK-LANDBANK-DOLLAR (ZORRO)1`, `CASH IN BANK-SECURITY BANK 852-5`, `CASH IN BANK-ATM PAYROLL`, `CASH IN BANK-BPI`, `PETTY CASH FUND`, `CASH ADVANCES-BOSS JCC-with sched`, `CASH ADVANCE-SUPPLIERS & OTHERS`, `CASH ADVANCE-JICAPOWER`, `ACCOUNTS RECEIVABLES-OTHERS`, `BID SECURITY & PERFORMANCE DEPOSIT`, `CONTRACT TRADE RECEIVABLES-ALL PROJECT`, `CONTRACT RETENTION RECEIVABLES`, `CASH ADVANCES-real steel`, `CASH ADVANCE-SALARY EMPLOYEES`, `CASH ADVANCE-LIQUIDATION`, `CASH ADVANCS-SUBCON`, `INVENTORY/ WAREHOUSE STOCK`, `PREPAID EXPENSE` (truncated). Note the period header `36,571,292.74` at the top suggests grand total / prior-year balance carried forward. **Bryan's specific guidance**: "better if we can display all on the charts of accounts" — meaning the Trial Balance must show **every CoA account, even those with zero activity** (the artifact does this — note the empty rows for inactive bank accounts like LANDBANK-0291 (NON-VAT) and CASH IN BANK-ROBINSONS BANK).

**Artifact 2 — Balance Sheet** ("As of December 31, 2025", prepared by GTI) — hierarchical statement organized by Roman numerals I–XXXV:

- **ASSETS**
  - **Current Assets**: I. Cash in Bank · II. Cash on hand - PCF · III. Cash advance-Personal · IV. Cash Advance-Others · VI. Contract Trade Receivables · VII. Contract Retention Receivables · VII. Accounts Receivables-others · IX. Advances liquidation · X. Advances Salary Employees · XI. Advances Subcon · XII. Inventory · XIII. Prepaid Expenses · **Total Current Assets**
  - **Other Assets**: XIV. Security Deposit · XV. Tax witheld by customers · **Total Other Assets**
  - **Property Plant & Equipment**: XVI. Tools & Equipment · XVII. Office Equipment, Furniture & Fixture · XVIII. Transportation & Construction Equipment · Total · Less: XIX. Accumulated Depreciation · **Net Book Value**
  - **TOTAL ASSETS**
- **LIABILITIES AND STOCKHOLDER'S EQUITY**
  - **Current Liabilities**: XX. Accounts Payable - Trade · XXI. Importation Payable · XXII. SSS / Med & HDMF Payable · XXIII. SSS & Pagibig Loan Payable · XXIV. W/tax Payable-Compensation · XXV. W/tax Payable-expanded · XXVI. Voucher Payable · XXVII. DP on ongoing projects · XXVIII. Retention Payable · XXIX. Provision for Income tax payable · XXX. VAT Payable · **Total Current Liabilities**
  - **Non Current liabilities**: XXXI. Mortgage Payable · XXXII. Loans Payable-Security Bank · **Total Non-Current Liabilities**
  - **Stockholders Equity**: XXXIII. Retained earnings Beg · XXXIV. Net Income after tax · XXXV. Retained earnings end · **Total Stockholders Equity**
- **TOTAL LIABILITIES & STOCKHOLDERS' EQUITY**

**Artifact 3 — Income Statement-BIR** ("For the month ended March 31, 2026", prepared by Grace Irlandez) — BIR Form 1702-aligned, organized by Schedule prefixes:

- **SALES**: Sched I — SALES-VATABLE · Sched II — SALES-ZERO-RATED · Sched III — SALES-EXCLUSIVE · **Total SALES**
- **Less Cost of Sales**: Sched IV — Direct Labor · Sched V — Direct Materials · Sched VI — Project Overhead · Sched VII — Importation Charges-Banal · Sched VIII — Importation - Materials · Sched IX — Duties & Taxes · Sched XI — Rental Equipment · **Total Cost**
- **Gross Profit**
- **Less Operating Expense**: Sched XIII — De Minimis and allowances · Sched XIV — Employees Benefits and SIL · Sched XV — Bidding Expense · Sched XVI — Bonus & Incentives · Sched XVII — Christmas Expenses · Sched XVIII — Communication Expense · Sched XIX — Depreciation Expense · Sched XX — Donation & Contribution · Sched XXI — Electricity and Lights · Sched XXII — Freight & Handling · Sched XXIII — Fuel Oil & Lubricants · Sched XXIV — Insurance & Bonds · Sched XXV — Interest & Bank Charges · Sched XXV — Liquidated Damages _(numbering duplicate in source — should be XXVI?)_ · Sched XXVI — Meals Expense · Sched XXVII — Medical Expense · Sched XXVIII — Miscellaneous Expense · Sched XXIX — Office Supplies · Sched XXX — PPE Expense · Sched XXXI — Professional Fee · Sched XXXV — Rental Office · Sched XXXII — Repair & Maintenance · Sched XXXIII — SOP/Representation & Entertainment · Sched XXXIV — Salaries & Wages and 13th month expense · Sched XXXVI — Security Services · Sched XXXVII — SSS/Med & ECC, Philhealth, HDMF Expense · Sched XXXVIII — Taxes, Licenses and Registration Expenses · Sched XXXIX — Transportation and Tollfee Expense · Sched XL — Water Expense · **Total Operating Expense**
- **Net Income / Loss from Operation** · Sched XLI — Add: Other Income · **Net Income before Income tax** · Less: Income tax payable · **Net Income after Income tax**

**Artifact 4 — Statements of Cash Flow** (indirect method, period TBD):

- **CASH FLOWS FROM OPERATING ACTIVITIES**: Income (loss) before income tax → Adjustments (Prior Period adjustment, Depreciation per Note 8) → Operating income before working capital changes → Decrease/(increase) in (Trade and other receivables per Note 5, Costs and estimated earnings in excess of billings, Other current assets) → Increase/(decrease) in (Trade and other Payables per Note 9, Income taxes paid per Note 3) → **Net cash from (used in) operating activities**
- **CASH FLOWS FROM INVESTING ACTIVITIES**: Additions to Property and equipment (Note 8) → **Net cash used in investing activities**
- _(Financing Activities section not visible on the captured page — typically mortgage and equity flows; either on a missed page or JCE simply has no material financing activity for the period; confirm.)_
- **NET INCREASE (DECREASE) IN CASH AND CASH EQUIVALENTS** → Cash and Cash Equivalents at the Beginning of the Year → Cash and Cash Equivalents at End of Year (Notes 3 and 4).

#### Architectural insight — the Reporting Mapping layer

The Trial Balance is **1:1 with the Chart of Accounts** (one row per CoA account). But the Balance Sheet and Income Statement aggregate **many CoA accounts into each named line**. For example:

- BS line **I. Cash in Bank** = SUM of (`10003 Cash-In-Bank-LAND BANK-067-4` + `10004 LAND BANK-029-1` + `10005 LAND BANK-032-1` + `10006 LAND BANK-031-3` + `10009 BPI` + `10010 Security Bank Peso` + `10012 Robinsons Bank` + likely the LANDBANK-0674 Workshop sub-account + ATM Payroll).
- BS line **III. Cash advance-Personal** = some subset of the cash-advance accounts (likely `CASH ADVANCES-BOSS JCC-with sched`).
- BS line **XVII. Office Equipment, Furniture & Fixture** = SUM of (`12046 Office Equipments` + `12047 Computers` + `12049 Office Furnitures & Fixtures` + `12050 Office Installations` + `12053 Other Furniture & Fixtures`).
- BS line **XIX. Accumulated Depreciation** = SUM of (`12054 + 12055 + 12056 + 12057 + 12058 + 12059 + 12060`).
- IS line **Sched XXXIII SOP/Representation & Entertainment** = SUM of (`50048 SOP Expense` + `50043 Representations & Entertainment`).
- IS line **Sched XXXIV Salaries & Wages and 13th month expense** = SUM of (`50046 Salaries and wages` + `50047 13th month Expense`).
- IS line **Sched XXXVII SSS/Med & ECC, Philhealth, HDMF Expense** = SUM of (`50022 Employer's Pag-ibig Contribution` + `50023 Employer's Philhealth Contributions` + `50024 Employer's SSS Contributions`).

So the system needs a **Reporting Mapping** configuration that defines, for each Balance Sheet line and each Income Statement Schedule, which CoA accounts contribute. This mapping is master data — it lives in Settings (5.2.1) alongside the CoA itself. Once defined, regenerating any of the financial statements is just SUM the contributing accounts for the requested period.

**Recommended placement:** Section 9 (new) of Settings (5.2.1), titled **"Financial Statement Mapping"**, with two sub-sections: Balance Sheet Lines (Roman-numeral line I → list of contributing CoA codes) and Income Statement Schedules (Sched prefix → list of contributing CoA codes). Versioned by effectivity date (SET-002) so prior-year statements continue to reproduce their original line structure even after the mapping is updated.

#### The report catalog

Section 5.9 groups its outputs into four families:

**A. Core financial statements** (the four artifacts):

1. **Trial Balance** — every CoA account with beginning balance + per-period activity + running total. Default period range: current fiscal year, month-by-month columns. Adjustable.
2. **Balance Sheet** — as-of-date snapshot with Roman-numeral line items per the mapping. Default as-of-date: today (or last month-end). Comparative view (two as-of dates side by side) supported.
3. **Income Statement (BIR)** — period-totals statement with Sched prefixes per the mapping. Default period: current month. Period-over-period comparison supported.
4. **Statements of Cash Flow** — indirect method, derived from BS comparative + IS + select adjustments per Notes references.

**B. Statutory reports** (per BIR / SSS / PhilHealth / Pag-IBIG):

1. **BIR Form 1601-C** — Monthly Remittance Return of Income Taxes Withheld on Compensation. Sourced from Payroll Summary 5.2.3 `withholding_tax`.
2. **BIR Form 1601-EQ** — Quarterly Remittance Return of Creditable Income Taxes Withheld (Expanded). Sourced from Payable Voucher 5.5 `withholding_tax_amount` (which itself inherits from RFP 7.7.5 EWT computation).
3. **BIR Form 2307** _(Phase 2 — Purchasing FR-BIR-01)_ — Certificate of Creditable Tax Withheld per supplier per payment. Sourced from RFP 7.7.5 EWT data; issued by JCE to the supplier as proof of withholding.
4. **BIR Form 2550M / 2550Q** — Monthly / Quarterly VAT Declaration. Sourced from Sales 5.3 and Payable Voucher 5.5 VAT lines.
5. **BIR Form 1702** — Annual Income Tax Return. Built from the BIR Income Statement format above.
6. **BIR Alphalists** — Alphalist of Employees (1604-CF), Alphalist of Payees (1604-E).
7. **SSS R-3 / R-5** — Monthly employer + employee contribution remittance.
8. **PhilHealth ER2** — Monthly contribution remittance.
9. **Pag-IBIG MCRF** — Monthly contribution remittance form.
10. **SSS / Pag-IBIG Loan Collection** — Monthly loan-payment remittance to each agency. Sourced from Payroll Loans 5.2.2.

**C. Operational reports** (management views):

1. **Bank Disbursement register** (formal report version of the 5.6 list view, with bank-pivot columns).
2. **Sales / Billing Statement register** (formal report version of 5.3's BILLING STATEMENT SUMMARY 2025).
3. **Collections register** (formal report version of 5.4's 2026 Collections register).
4. **Accounts Receivable Aging** — outstanding billing statements (5.3) grouped by 0–30 / 31–60 / 61–90 / 90+ days since issue date.
5. **Accounts Payable Aging** — outstanding Payable Vouchers (5.5) not yet paid, similar aging.
6. **Cash Advance Aging** — outstanding Cash Advances (5.7 first-class entity if Option 3 is adopted) grouped by age since issuance, flagging stale advances.
7. **Cash Position** — current balance per bank account (across `10001-10012`), with last-30-day inflow / outflow trend.
8. **Project Costing summary** — per-project Cost vs Contract Amount with profitability (the 5.8 sub-tab's primary output, surfaced as a report).
9. **Payroll Register** (Consolidated and Per-Project) — the 5.2.3 sub-tab's two register variants, surfaced as reports.
10. **Loan Ledger** — outstanding employee loans (5.2.2) with running balances, suitable for HR + employee inquiry.
11. **Purchasing PO Monitoring Ledger** _(from Purchasing 7.6)_ — the cross-project view of all POs with payment status; surfaces here for Accounting visibility.
12. **Purchasing Spend Analytics** _(from Purchasing FR-X-05)_ — spend by project, supplier, and month; Local vs Import split.
13. **Supplier Spend & Performance** _(from Purchasing 7.9 FR-SUP-05 + Phase 2 FR-LT-04)_ — supplier-level total spend, PO count, on-time delivery %, last order date, reliability score.
14. **Open Purchase Orders** — POs not yet fully delivered/paid, with aging and expected ETA (for imports).

**D. Custom queries / ad-hoc analysis**:

1. **General Ledger drill-down** — pick any CoA account, see every Dr/Cr posted to it in a date range, with source-document linkback (CV / SI / CR / JV).
2. **Project profitability by client** — cross-Sales-Order roll-up.
3. **Cash flow projection** — outstanding Payables (with expected pay dates) + Receivables (with expected collection dates) = projected cash position over the next 30 / 60 / 90 days.

#### Data model — Report record

Reports are largely **derived views** (not persisted records), but the system maintains some metadata:

| Field                                                                          | Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| ------------------------------------------------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Report definition**                                                          |                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| `report_id`                                                                    | System identifier.                                                                                                                                                                                              |
| `report_type`                                                                  | Enum: `Trial Balance` · `Balance Sheet` · `Income Statement` · `Cash Flow` · `BIR 1601-C` · etc. (the full catalog above).                                                                                      |
| `mapping_version`                                                              | FK to the Financial Statement Mapping version effective at the report's as-of-date — ensures historical reports reproduce their original layout.                                                                |
| **Generation parameters** (per run)                                            |                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| `as_of_date` _(BS, CF)_ or `period_start` + `period_end` _(IS, TB, registers)_ | Period controls.                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| `comparative`                                                                  | Boolean — whether to show a prior-period comparison column.                                                                                                                                                     |
| `project_filter`                                                               | Optional — generate the report for one Sales Order only (relevant for project-scoped registers).                                                                                                                |
| `format`                                                                       | `On-screen` · `PDF` · `Excel` · `Print`.                                                                                                                                                                        |
| **Output metadata**                                                            |                                                                                                                                                                                                                 |
| `generated_at`, `generated_by`                                                 | Who ran it and when.                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| `snapshot_file` _(optional)_                                                   | When a generated report is saved as a snapshot (e.g., a finalized year-end Balance Sheet), the PDF/Excel file is preserved as evidence; future regenerations from the live data could differ as later JVs post. |

#### Cross-module integrations

- **Chart of Accounts (5.2.1 Section 8)** — every report draws from CoA balances. The Trial Balance shows every account 1:1 (per Bryan); the BS/IS use the Mapping layer.
- **Financial Statement Mapping (5.2.1 new Section 9)** — defines the BS Roman-numeral line items and IS Schedule prefixes; required for BS, IS, and the BIR statutory reports.
- **All transactional sub-tabs (5.2.3 Payroll, 5.3 Sales, 5.4 Collections, 5.5 PV, 5.6 Disbursement, 5.7 JV)** — feed journal entries into the GL that reports aggregate.
- **Project Costing (5.8)** — its primary output is the per-project profitability report, which surfaces as one of the Operational reports in 5.9.
- **Drill-down to source documents** — clicking any report figure opens the underlying journal entries with linkback to the source CV / SI / CR / JV.

#### Per-report screen — `Generate Report`

| Field                                       | Behavior                                                                                                                                                          |
| ------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `report_type`                               | Dropdown of the report catalog (grouped by family: Core / Statutory / Operational / Custom).                                                                      |
| `as_of_date` or `period_start + period_end` | Date pickers; defaults based on report type (BS → today; IS / TB → current month or current fiscal year).                                                         |
| `comparative`                               | Checkbox — when checked, adds a prior-period column.                                                                                                              |
| `project_filter`                            | Optional dropdown of Sales Orders; applies to project-scoped reports only.                                                                                        |
| `display all CoA accounts (TB)`             | For Trial Balance — checkbox defaulting to **checked** per Bryan's guidance. When checked, every CoA account shows a row even with zero activity.                 |
| **Generate**                                | Renders on-screen first. From the on-screen view, user can Print, Export to PDF, or Export to Excel.                                                              |
| **Snapshot**                                | Optional action — preserves the current rendering as a static file in the report archive, useful for year-end statements that should not change after BIR filing. |

#### UX considerations

1. **Hierarchical line rendering for BS / IS.** Major sections (Current Assets, Other Assets, PPE, etc.) render with visual emphasis (bold header on a tinted band like the artifacts' olive-green callouts), each line item indented underneath, with subtotals (`Total Current Assets`) styled distinctly.
2. **Trial Balance default = show all CoA accounts.** Per Bryan's explicit guidance. Inactive accounts (where `is_active = false` per SET-080) are excluded by default but reappear with an "Include inactive" toggle.
3. **Period selector with quick-picks.** "This month", "Last month", "This quarter", "Last quarter", "This year", "Last year", plus custom date range.
4. **Comparative columns.** BS and IS support comparative views (BS = two as-of dates side by side; IS = two periods side by side). The Trial Balance is inherently comparative (multiple month columns).
5. **Drill-down on any figure.** Click a Balance Sheet line item → see the contributing CoA accounts. Click a CoA account → see every journal entry. Click a journal line → open the source document (CV, SI, CR, JV).
6. **BIR statutory report wizards.** For each BIR / SSS / PhilHealth / Pag-IBIG form, a guided generation wizard that prompts for the reporting period and any agency-specific parameters (e.g., RDO Code, Branch Code), then produces the form in the exact agency-required layout for printing or eFiling.
7. **Snapshot vs live.** Live reports reflect the current GL state — re-running tomorrow may produce different numbers if any JV posts in between. Snapshots preserve the as-rendered file; once a year-end BS is filed with BIR, the snapshot becomes the legal record.
8. **Notes references.** The Cash Flow artifact references Notes 3, 4, 5, 8, 9 — these are the **footnotes to financial statements** that explain the composition of each line (e.g., Note 5 = breakdown of Trade and Other Receivables). The system should generate notes alongside the statements; v1 can render placeholder notes; full automation of notes is a v2 feature.
9. **Print layouts.** BS and IS: Portrait Letter. Trial Balance: Landscape Long bond (it's wide). Operational registers (Bank Disbursement, Collections, Sales register): match the legacy Landscape Legal layouts already specified in their source sub-tabs.
10. **Roles and access.** Reports are read-only views, but some carry sensitive data — the BIR Alphalist of Employees has SSS numbers, the Cash Position report shows aggregate liquidity. Role-based gating (see 5.10) controls who can generate which family.

#### RPT-XXX requirements

- **RPT-001** — The Trial Balance shall display every account in the Chart of Accounts (per Bryan's guidance, `display all on the charts of accounts`), including accounts with zero activity. An "Include inactive" toggle controls whether `is_active = false` accounts also appear.
- **RPT-002** — The Trial Balance shall present beginning balance, per-period activity columns, per-period cumulative totals, and an ending balance — with the period grain (month, quarter, year) user-selectable.
- **RPT-003** — The Balance Sheet shall render in the artifact-2 layout (Current Assets / Other Assets / PPE with Net Book Value / Current Liabilities / Non-Current Liabilities / Stockholders Equity), with each Roman-numeral line item computed by the Financial Statement Mapping configured in Settings (5.2.1 new Section 9).
- **RPT-004** — The Income Statement shall render in the artifact-3 BIR-Form-1702-aligned layout (Sales / Cost of Sales / Gross Profit / Operating Expense / Net Income before tax / Net Income after tax), with each Sched-prefix line item computed by the same Financial Statement Mapping.
- **RPT-005** — The Statement of Cash Flow shall render in the indirect-method layout (Operating Activities, Investing Activities, Financing Activities), derived from a comparative Balance Sheet + the Income Statement + configured non-cash adjustments. Financing Activities section is required even if zero for the period.
- **RPT-006** — The system shall maintain templates for the BIR / SSS / PhilHealth / Pag-IBIG statutory reports listed under family B, each producing output in the agency-required layout for printing or eFiling.
- **RPT-007** — All reports shall be exportable to PDF and Excel; the on-screen rendering is the primary output, exports preserve the layout.
- **RPT-008** — All reports shall support drill-down: report line → contributing CoA accounts → journal entries → source documents (CV / SI / CR / JV).
- **RPT-009** — The system shall allow snapshotting of any generated report — the rendered PDF/Excel file is preserved with `generated_at` and `generated_by` metadata, immutable after creation.
- **RPT-010** — The Financial Statement Mapping (BS lines + IS Schedules → CoA codes) shall be versioned by effectivity date (SET-002 inherited), so prior-period reports continue to reproduce their original layout.
- **RPT-011** — Reports shall respect role-based access (5.10): sensitive reports (Cash Position, BIR Alphalist) restricted to authorized roles only.

#### Open items for Bryan's confirmation

1. **Bryan's second bullet was incomplete.** The message after the Trial Balance instruction ended with `*` — the second bullet was started but not written. **What was the second guidance point on Reporting?** Likely additional report types, frequency rules, recipients, or specific BIR-form requirements; needed to finalize this section.
2. **Financial Statement Mapping placement.** Recommend Section 9 of 5.2.1 Settings (BS line items + IS Schedules → CoA contributing accounts). Confirm placement or propose alternative (could live as a standalone sub-tab within 5.9 Reporting).
3. **BS line numbering anomaly.** The artifact's Current Assets list skips from V (missing — should be between IV and VI) and shows two `VII` (`VII. Contract Retention Receivables` and `VII. Accounts Receivables-others`). Confirm this is a typo in the legacy template versus an intended numbering convention; clean up at mapping-config time.
4. **IS Schedule numbering anomalies.** Sched XXV appears twice (Interest & Bank Charges and Liquidated Damages); Sched XXXV (Rental Office) and Sched XXXVI (Security Services) appear out of order with Sched XXXII (Repair & Maintenance) sandwiched between. Confirm intended numbering or treat as legacy-template errata.
5. **Financing Activities section of Cash Flow.** Not visible on the captured artifact. Confirm whether JCE has material financing activities (mortgage drawdowns/repayments, equity contributions, owner withdrawals) — needed for completeness.
6. **Notes to Financial Statements.** The Cash Flow references Notes 3, 4, 5, 8, 9. Are full footnoted notes a v1 deliverable (which requires significant additional structure — depreciation schedules, lease commitments, contingencies, related-party transactions) or v2? v1 can render placeholder note headers without full automated content.
7. **Trial Balance period grain.** Artifact shows monthly columns within a year. Confirm: monthly is the standard; quarterly and year-end are exception cases?
8. **New CoA accounts visible in the Trial Balance.** The Trial Balance shows accounts that weren't on the captured 4-page CoA pages — `CASH IN BANK-LANDBANK-0674(WORKSHOP)`, `CASH IN BANK-LANDBANK-0291 (NON-VAT)`, `CASH IN BANK-ATM PAYROLL`, `CASH ADVANCES-BOSS JCC-with sched`, `CASH ADVANCES-real steel`, `CASH ADVANCE-SALARY EMPLOYEES`, `CASH ADVANCE-LIQUIDATION` (distinct from `11037 Advances for Liquidation`?), `CONTRACT TRADE RECEIVABLES-ALL PROJECT`, `CONTRACT RETENTION RECEIVABLES`, `BID SECURITY & PERFORMANCE DEPOSIT`. These need to be added to the CoA. **Is there a CoA page or page(s) we haven't captured yet?** This is the cleanest path; otherwise these accounts need to be added one-by-one during system data entry.
9. **`Project Overhead` is confirmed as a real account** by Sched VI on the Income Statement. Closes one of the JV-side open items. Will need to be added to the CoA capture too if not on a missing page.
10. **Customer / employee sub-accounts.** `CASH ADVANCES-BOSS JCC-with sched`, `CASH ADVANCES-real steel`, `CASH IN BANK-ATM PAYROLL` look like **named sub-accounts** (per-customer or per-purpose) under a parent category. Confirm whether JCE's CoA truly has named sub-accounts at this level (which means the CoA structure needs `parent_code` field actively used) or whether these are reporting groupings that should live in the Financial Statement Mapping instead.
11. **Comparative-period defaults.** For BS comparative view (two as-of dates), is the default Current Year-End vs Prior Year-End, or Current Month-End vs Prior Year Same Month-End? For IS, is the default Current Month vs Prior Year Same Month, or Current Month vs Prior Month? Confirm what JCE reads internally.
12. **Snapshot frequency.** Are snapshots auto-generated at month-end / year-end (audit trail by default), or only when a user explicitly clicks "Snapshot this report" (audit trail on demand)? Recommend automatic snapshots at fiscal-period-end for BS/IS/CF, manual elsewhere.

### 5.10 Roles & Permissions

✅ **RESOLVED: Accounting Staff, Accounting Lead / CFO, Payroll Officer, Owner.** **Accounting Staff** do data entry across the sub-tabs; **Accounting Lead / CFO** approve and run reports; **Payroll Officer** is scoped to Payroll (sensitive compensation data); **Owner** has full access. Per-action permissions are consolidated in the system-wide RBAC matrix (Section 3). This yields a **three-axis Accounting split, enforced per screen**: (1) **compensation visibility** — Payroll Officer + Owner only (§3.7); (2) **line / document editing** — any role holding **E** or **F** on ACC (Accounting Staff, Payroll Officer, Accounting Lead / CFO, Owner); (3) **sign-off verbs** — Issue / Approve / Post / Record / Void — restricted to the **F+A+P** holders (Accounting Lead / CFO, Owner), realizing **preparer ≠ approver** segregation of duties. No single permission gate governs all three.

### 5.11 Pending Information

Items to obtain from the Accounting interview when each sub-tab is scoped:

- **Payroll register format / payslip template** (5.2).
- **Invoice template and billing milestones** used today (5.3).
- **Aging bracket definitions and current collections workflow** (5.4).
- **Voucher templates** for Payable, Disbursement, and Journal (5.5–5.7).
- **Project costing categories and overhead allocation rules** (5.8).
- **Report catalog and statutory reports required** (5.9).
- **Chart of accounts** — likely a foundational reference document the module's structure will lean on.

### 5.12 Finalization Decisions (v1.62)

The open items scattered through §5.2–§5.9 are resolved here in one consolidated block (Bryan, finalization sweep 2026-06-03). Items that genuinely require a JCE source document are listed separately as **client-input required** at the end.

**Cross-cutting document behavior** (SOA, Service Invoice, Collection Receipt, Acknowledgement Receipt, Payable/Cash Voucher, Journal Voucher):

1. **Numbering** — system **auto-sequential**, assigned on Issue, gap-free, with an out-of-sequence warning.
2. **Amount-in-words** — **auto-generated** from the figure in PHP words with legacy asterisk styling.
3. **Edit-after-Issue** — issued documents remain **editable with a full audit trail** (not hard-locked). _Consistency note: BDD Offers/Quotation use a strict no-edit pattern; this divergence is intentional per Bryan and recorded for the §3 / consistency review._
4. **Tax computation** — output VAT (12%) and Creditable Withholding Tax are **auto-computed** from Settings rates, editable per line where needed.
5. **Void/cancel** — issued documents are **voided (never deleted)** with reason + timestamp; the number is retained and marked Void.

**Sales (5.3) & Collections (5.4):**

6. **Clients** — promoted to a **maintained entity** (name, address, TIN); documents pick from and auto-fill it.
7. **SI vs SOA** — a billing event is **strictly one or the other**, never both for the same transaction.
8. **Multiple SO#s per statement** — **allowed**.
9. **Partial payments** — **tracked**; a short collection flags the billing Partially Paid and leaves the balance open until closed.
10. **Over-billing guard** — the system **warns** (overridable) when billed-to-date against an SO would exceed its contract amount.
11. **Sub-ledgers** — per-customer AR, per-supplier AP, and per-employee advances maintained beneath the control accounts.
12. **OSCA/PWD discounts** — **out of scope for v1** (B2B billing).

**Payable Voucher (5.5) & Disbursement (5.6):**

13. **Payee model** — `payee_type = Supplier | Employee | Other`.
14. **Voucher lines** — **multiple line items per voucher**, each to its own CoA account.
15. **Payment methods** — **Check, Bank Transfer, Cash, and Petty Cash Fund**.
16. **Checks** — a **reference-only field** on the Cash Voucher (no separate Check entity in v1).
17. **Joint payees** — **supported** (e.g., "NELDA MENDOZA / JOCEN CANITAN").
18. **Petty Cash Fund** — **dedicated replenishment workflow** (float, expense logging, top-up).
19. **Bank reconciliation** — **basic reconciliation in v1**.
20. **Bank execution** — **manual capture in v1** (paste bank reference to mark Paid); banking-API integration is a later phase.

**Journal Voucher (5.7) & Cash Advances:**

21. **Cash Advance** — a **first-class entity** (`CA-YY-XXXX`) with issuance, a **liquidation wizard**, and aging.
22. **JV reversal** — **auto-flip** reversing entry linked to the original.
23. **Depreciation** — automated runs **deferred to Phase 2**; v1 books depreciation via manual JV.

**Payroll (5.2) corrections:**

24. **Pay-rate multipliers** — **multiplicative (DOLE-standard)** is authoritative (e.g., Rest-day OT = 1.30 × 1.30). §4.2 and §5.2.1 already use the multiplicative DOLE matrix (corrected in v1.39); this confirms that model and closes any residual ambiguity from the earlier v1.11 additive note.
25. **Travel hours** — **excluded** from Basic Pay (per §4.2 v1.9); the payslip reoccurrence is treated as legacy.
26. **Rest-Day–Reg-Holiday OT multiplier** — the **Settings matrix governs**; the payslip discrepancy is corrected against it.
27. **Withholding-tax table frequency** — **single fixed frequency** company-wide (SET-031).

**Reporting (5.9):**

28. **Report mode** — **live for a selected period / as-of date**, with a **snapshot/lock at period close**.
29. **Financial statements** — Cash Flow includes **Operating, Investing, and Financing** sections; statements **default to prior-period comparative columns**.
30. **Notes to Financial Statements** — **placeholder note headers in v1**; full automated notes are a v2 feature.
31. **Signatories** — **picked per document** (not auto-pulled from the employee database).

**Client-input required** (cannot be closed without a JCE source document):

- **Chart of Accounts — VERIFIED COMPLETE (v1.63):** all 129 accounts confirmed against a clean copy; zero-rated sales **is** in the chart (`40002 Sales-Zero rated`). The accounts referenced in transactions but **absent** from the chart — **BDO** Cash-In-Bank, Security Bank **`852-5`** (likely the account number for `10010`), Land Bank **`580` Workshop**, **`ACCOMMODATION EXP.`**, and **`PROJECT OVERHEAD`** — are confirmed genuinely missing and are to be **added by Accounting** (BDO → e.g. `10013`; append `852-5` to `10010`; add a Land Bank `580` Workshop cash account, `ACCOMMODATION EXP.`, `PROJECT OVERHEAD`, and a new **Local Retention Receivable - Trade** account — final codes assigned by Accounting). _(Bryan: leave account creation to the accountant.)_
- Meaning of the legacy **"with forms / 067-4"** annotation on the Collections register.
- **BS line / IS schedule numbering anomalies** in the source statements.
- Completeness of the **`jv_category`** enumeration.
- The **SOA / Collection-Receipt form templates** and the **payroll register / payslip template** (for print fidelity).

## 6. Project Management Department Module _(finalized v1.68)_

> **Source.** This section integrates the **"JC Electrofields — Project Management Group (PMG) Module Requirements"** document provided by Bryan on 2026-06-03, derived from review of: `NORECO2 Accomplishment No.1.xlsx` (a per-project weighted accomplishment report plus its NET AMOUNT progress-billing sheet, ≈183 rows) and `JCE-GARCIA 3-0257.xlsx` (the Material Request form, footer code `JCE-F-WMS02`). Requirement codes use the `FR-XXX-NN` convention established in Section 7, with Section-6-unique prefixes (`FR-INT`, `FR-BOQ`, `FR-VO`, `FR-ACC`, `FR-PB`, `FR-XCK`, `FR-PORT`, `FR-TL`, `FR-MR`, `FR-LOG`, `FR-XC`) so the SRS and the source requirements doc stay in 1:1 correspondence and no code collides with Section 7's set.

> **Why PMG matters to the rest of the system.** The Project Management Group is the **authoring owner of the Project and Material Request (MR) entities** that Purchasing (Section 7) and other modules reference as foreign keys. The cross-module dependencies that Section 7 surfaced as "soft FKs awaiting Section 6" (7.3.1, 7.13 #7–#8, 7.15.9) are **resolved here** — Section 6 defines the canonical Project and MR entities those modules link to. Where this section finalizes a decision that an earlier section flagged as pending, the back-reference is called out inline.

### 6.1 Module Overview

The Project Management module is JCE's **project delivery and progress-billing engine**. It owns the lifecycle of a construction/electrical project from contract intake through period-by-period accomplishment reporting and progress billing, and it owns the warehouse stock master and the Material Request (MR) workflow that feeds purchase needs into Purchasing. The module is **computation-heavy on the billing side and inventory-controlled on the materials side**: the PM head keys a single human judgment per line each period (the _This Period %_), and the system derives every downstream figure — weighted accomplishment, grand-total progress, progress-bill amount, capped downpayment recoupment, and held retention — exactly as her spreadsheet does today, but with the running ledgers and integrity guards a spreadsheet cannot enforce.

The module is organized into **six functional areas** plus cross-cutting services, with an approved Phase 2 extension set built on the Phase 1 backbone:

- **Phase 1 functional areas**: Project Intake (import / clone / manual builder) · Bill of Quantities (BOQ) & Weighting · Accomplishment Report → Progress Billing · Project Portfolio · Latest-Update / Activity Timeline · Warehouse / Inventory Master + Material Request workflow — plus cross-cutting roles, attachments, notifications, dashboard, reporting, and currency policy, and a department-scoped Audit Log.
- **Phase 1 integrity refinements** (added during the 2026-06-03 review and committed to v1): Variation Orders (6.5.5), capped cumulative recoupment + held/released retention (6.6.5–6.6.6), the read-only delivery cross-check against Purchasing's import tracker (6.6.9), inventory reservation (now owned by the Warehouse module, Section 10.6), and the stage-sequence guardrail (6.6.2).
- **Phase 2** (scoped, deferred): field photo evidence, BOQ template library, S-curve charting, MR→PO→delivery traceability board, inventory reorder points, multi-warehouse, document-pack export, and budget-vs-actual — see 6.15.

**Cross-module hooks resolved by this section:**

| Open item                                                                           | Resolution                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                        |
