| ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **7.3.1 / 7.13 #7** — Project entity scope (customer SOs vs. internal cost centers) | The **Project** entity defined in 6.3.2 is the unifying parent: `project_type = Customer` (backed by a BDD 9.3 Sales Order) or `Internal Cost Center` (`WORKSHOP`, `JCE STOCK`, etc.). This realizes the structure proposed in 7.15.9. Purchasing's `Project` FK now points here. |
| **7.13 #8** — Material Request entity ownership                                     | **MR No.** is owned by PMG (6.10). Purchasing's `MR No.` soft-FK becomes a hard FK to the `MaterialRequest` records created in 6.10.                                                                                                                                              |
| **§6 placeholder soft-FK note (v1.56)**                                             | The "soft-FK → hard-FK" transition Purchasing described is now executable: PMG owns `Project` and `MaterialRequest`; Purchasing reads them. Legacy rows with no parent still use Purchasing's validated-fallback mode until migrated.                                             |
| **7.8 Stage 2 (MR) / Stage 1 (BOM) ownership**                                      | Stage 2 "MR (Material Request)" of Purchasing's 15-stage import tracker is the same MR No. authored in 6.10. The accomplishment report's PROCURE/DELIVER/INSTALL sub-stages **overlap** that tracker but are **not auto-synced** in v1 — see the read-only cross-check (6.6.9).   |

### 6.2 Scope & Locked Decisions

**Decisions already locked** (not to be re-litigated during build):

1. **Accomplishment % is entered manually, by the PM head only.** She keys the _This Period %_ per line each billing cycle, exactly as in the spreadsheet today. The system performs all downstream math (weight × %, roll-up, grand total, progress billing) but **never auto-derives the % from delivery or Purchasing data.** Accepted trade-off: progress is entered in two places (here and in Purchasing's import tracker). Billing integrity is guarded — without forcing a sync — by the read-only cross-check in 6.6.9.
2. **The warehouse inventory master is built from scratch.** No existing stock master is imported. It is seeded by the warehouse and grows as MRs add new items on the fly; every issue/receipt adjusts stock.
3. **Inventory-first is enforced by the MR form.** The MR form's own columns (`REQUESTED → AVAILABLE STOCK → FOR PURCHASE`) require a stock check before any purchase quantity is produced. Only "For Purchase" lines flow to Purchasing.
4. **The primary new-project path is BOQ import**, because the PM head already authors the contract BOQ in this exact spreadsheet layout. Clone and manual builder are fallbacks.
5. **Import is "mostly consistent, minor drift"** → import requires a column-mapping + row-preview confirmation step before commit. No silent auto-parse.
6. **The Procure/Deliver/Install split is set per line (no default ratio).** It comes from the imported file or is typed by the user; the split varies by item (observed: 30/50/20, 35/52/13, 39/59/2).
7. **First-pass scope covers both the accomplishment portfolio AND the MR/warehouse workflow** together.
8. **Signatures are offline.** Reports and MRs print with signatory blocks for wet-signing; the system tracks status only — mirroring the Purchasing approval model (7.2 #2).

These decisions are confirmed; the SRS treats them as committed.

### 6.3 Data Backbone & Entity Model

#### 6.3.1 Linking keys

Three business keys connect PMG records to each other and to the wider JCE dashboard. **PMG is the authoring owner of `Project` and `MR No.`**; Purchasing and other modules reference them as foreign keys (resolving 7.3.1).

- **Project** — the job (e.g., `NORECO II`, `GARCIA 3`). Created in PMG; carries the contract, BOQ, and accomplishment history. The unifying parent over both customer projects and internal cost centers (6.3.2; realizes 7.15.9).
- **SO No. (Sales Order No.)** — e.g., `25-10-346`. Links a project/MR to its BDD 9.3 Sales Order. May be `N/A` for internal/stock work (`project_type = Internal Cost Center`).
- **MR No. (Material Request No.)** — e.g., `JCE-GARCIA 3-0258`. Created in PMG; referenced by Purchasing POs (7.3.1).

**Integration decision (confirmed by Bryan):** `Project`, `SO No.`, and `MR No.` are **first-class, indexed entities owned/created here** and read by Purchasing and other modules — **not free text**. Selecting a Project anywhere in the dashboard pulls its BOQ, accomplishment status, MRs, POs, and shipments. Where a referenced record does not yet exist (legacy data, modules not yet built), the system shall allow a **validated fallback value** and reconcile on migration — the inverse of the soft-FK mode Purchasing adopted in 7.3.1.

#### 6.3.2 Entities

| Entity                   | Purpose                                                                                                          | Key relationships                                                                                                                |
| ------------------------ | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- |
| **Project**              | Master record of a job; header + billing terms. Unifying parent over customer projects and internal cost centers | 1 Project → 1 BillOfQuantities; → many AccomplishmentPeriods; → many MaterialRequests; → 1 RecoupmentLedger; → 1 RetentionLedger |
| **BillOfQuantities**     | The contract line-item set for a project                                                                         | belongs to 1 Project; → many BoqCategory                                                                                         |
| **BoqCategory**          | A grouping (e.g., General Requirement, Electrical)                                                               | belongs to 1 BOQ; → many BoqLine                                                                                                 |
| **BoqLine**              | One contract line; `line_type = Staged                                                                           | Single`                                                                                                                          | belongs to 1 BoqCategory; Staged → 3 BoqLineStage |
| **BoqLineStage**         | Procure / Deliver / Install slice of a staged line, with its own amount & weight                                 | belongs to 1 BoqLine                                                                                                             |
| **AccomplishmentPeriod** | One progress-billing period (PB1, PB2…); frozen on submit                                                        | belongs to 1 Project; → many PeriodEntry; → 1 ProgressBilling                                                                    |
| **PeriodEntry**          | The _This Period %_ the PM head keyed for one line/stage in one period                                           | belongs to 1 AccomplishmentPeriod + 1 BoqLine/Stage                                                                              |
| **ProgressBilling**      | The NET AMOUNT computation for a period (PB amount, recoupment, retention, net)                                  | belongs to 1 AccomplishmentPeriod                                                                                                |
| **RecoupmentLedger**     | Running tally of DP recouped to date vs. the downpayment cap                                                     | belongs to 1 Project                                                                                                             |
| **RetentionLedger**      | Running tally of retention held to date + release events                                                         | belongs to 1 Project                                                                                                             |
| **VariationOrder**       | A contract change (added/revised/removed BOQ lines) applied mid-project                                          | belongs to 1 Project; → many BoqLine deltas                                                                                      |
| **StockReservation**     | Stock committed to an approved MR line but not yet issued                                                        | belongs to 1 InventoryItem + 1 MrLine                                                                                            |
| **MaterialRequest**      | An MR header (form `JCE-F-WMS02`)                                                                                | belongs to 1 Project; → many MrLine                                                                                              |
| **MrLine**               | One requested item: requested / available / for-purchase qty + canvass                                           | belongs to 1 MaterialRequest; references 1 InventoryItem (nullable for new items)                                                |
| **InventoryItem**        | Warehouse stock master record                                                                                    | 1 Item → many StockMovement; → many MrLine                                                                                       |
| **StockMovement**        | Immutable receipt/issue/adjustment affecting stock-on-hand                                                       | belongs to 1 InventoryItem                                                                                                       |
| **Attachment**           | Uploaded file                                                                                                    | polymorphic — attaches to Project, AccomplishmentPeriod, or MaterialRequest                                                      |
| **AuditLogEntry**        | Immutable record of a state-changing action                                                                      | references actor + affected entity                                                                                               |
| **Unit** _(lookup)_      | Controlled unit list (lot, set, pcs, kg, assy…)                                                                  | referenced by BoqLine, InventoryItem, MrLine                                                                                     |

Phase 2 introduces additional PMG entities (ProgressPhoto, BoqTemplate, DocumentPack) — see 6.15.0. _(The `ReorderRule` and multi-location `Warehouse` entities now belong to the Warehouse module — see Section 10.12.0.)_

**Inventory entities (`InventoryItem`, `StockMovement`, `StockReservation`) are owned by the Warehouse module (Section 10.3)** and appear in the table above only for their relationship to `MrLine`; the MR workflow (§6.10) consumes the Warehouse stock-verification service (Section 10.7).

**The Project header**, realizing 7.15.9, carries: `project_id` (system ID), `project_code` (display — equals the Sales Order No. when `project_type = Customer`, or a parallel label such as `WORKSHOP` / `JCE STOCK` when `Internal Cost Center`), `project_type` (`Customer | Internal Cost Center`), `project_name`, `client / cooperative`, `location`, `sales_order_id` (FK to BDD 9.3 — required for Customer, null for Internal Cost Center), `contract_amount`, `start_date`, `target_date`, `dp_recoupment_pct` (default 15%), `retention_pct` (default 10%), and `status` (`Active / On Hold / Completed / Cancelled`).

#### 6.3.3 Relationship summary

```
Project (1) ──1 BillOfQuantities ──< BoqCategory ──< BoqLine
BoqLine (Staged) (1) ──3 BoqLineStage          [Procure / Deliver / Install]
BoqLine (Single) (1) ──0 BoqLineStage          [single price, no split]
Project (1) ──< AccomplishmentPeriod (PB1, PB2…) ──< PeriodEntry
AccomplishmentPeriod (1) ──1 ProgressBilling
Project (1) ──1 RecoupmentLedger · ──1 RetentionLedger
Project (1) ──< VariationOrder ──< (BoqLine deltas)
Project (1) ──< MaterialRequest ──< MrLine >── InventoryItem
InventoryItem (1) ──< StockMovement
MrLine (1) ──1 StockReservation (when in-stock qty reserved)
{Project, AccomplishmentPeriod, MaterialRequest} (1) ──< Attachment
```

#### 6.3.4 The `line_type` flag drives BOQ behavior

A single `BoqLine.line_type` field (`Staged | Single`) governs how a line is priced, weighted, and progressed. The SRS treats this as the **central branch point** for the accomplishment engine — analogous to the PO `type` flag in 7.3.4:

| Behavior                             | Staged (equipment)                     | Single (lot / service)                               |
| ------------------------------------ | -------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------- |
| **Pricing**                          | 3 amounts: Procure + Deliver + Install | 1 amount                                             |
| **Sub-stages**                       | 3 (P/D/I), each weighted               | none                                                 |
| **% entry per period**               | per stage                              | per line                                             |
| **Stage-sequence guardrail (6.6.2)** | applies (P→D→I order)                  | not applicable                                       |
| **Examples**                         | Power transformer, MVSG, breakers      | Mobilization, engineering design, bonds, LGU permits |

### 6.4 Project Intake (New Project)

Three creation paths. **Import is primary**; clone and manual builder are fallbacks. All three converge on the same `Project` + `BillOfQuantities` structure.

#### 6.4.1 Path A — BOQ import (primary)

The PM head uploads her existing accomplishment/BOQ spreadsheet. Because formats drift slightly between jobs, parsing is a **guided, confirmable flow — never a silent auto-import**:

1. **Upload** — accept `.xlsx`. The system reads the target sheet (default the first/largest; allow sheet selection).
2. **Auto-detect** — best-effort match of source columns (`ITEM`, `DESCRIPTION`, `QTY.`, `UNIT`, `UNIT PRICE`, `TOTAL`) to system fields, and detection of category rows and Procure/Deliver/Install sub-rows.
3. **Confirm header** — pre-fill and let the PM head edit: project title, client/cooperative, location, SO/contract no., contract amount, start + target dates, DP-recoupment %, retention %.
4. **Confirm column mapping** — show each detected source column mapped to a system field via dropdown; flag any low-confidence mapping for explicit confirmation; allow "ignore".
5. **Row preview** — render parsed categories, lines, P/D/I splits, and auto-computed weight %, with a summary (line count, staged count, grand total, opening period).
6. **Commit** — on confirm, create the `Project` + `BOQ`. Nothing persists before this step.

#### 6.4.2 Path B — Clone a past project

Duplicate an existing project's BOQ structure into a new project; the user edits header, quantities, and prices. Intended for repeat job types (e.g., substations). Weights recompute from the edited amounts. The clone is **independent** — edits to the copy do not affect the source.

#### 6.4.3 Path C — Manual guided builder

For small jobs or fixing imported lines: a header form, then add category → add line. Per line the user picks `line_type`; Staged lines require all three (P/D/I) amounts (no default split); Single lines take one amount. Weights recompute live; the user never types a weight.

#### 6.4.4 Requirements

- **FR-INT-01** — Import shall not commit a project until the user passes the mapping + preview confirmation step (6.4.1 steps 4–5). No record persists before commit.
- **FR-INT-02** — A low-confidence column mapping shall block commit until explicitly confirmed or marked "ignore".
- **FR-INT-03** — On import, equipment lines with three sub-rows shall be parsed as `Staged` (with the source P/D/I split preserved); category-level and lot/service lines shall be parsed as `Single`.
- **FR-INT-04** — Clone shall produce an independent BOQ; subsequent edits to the copy shall not mutate the source project.
- **FR-INT-05** — The manual builder shall reject a `Staged` line that is missing any of its three P/D/I amounts, and shall never allow a weight to be typed (weights are derived per 6.5.2).
- **FR-INT-06** — Sheet selection shall be available when the workbook has multiple sheets; the default selection is the first/largest sheet.

#### 6.4.5 Acceptance criteria

- Import never commits a project until the mapping + preview confirmation step is passed.
- A low-confidence column mapping blocks commit until explicitly confirmed or ignored.
- Importing the NORECO2 sample reproduces its grand total (**₱53,277,688**) and per-line weights within rounding tolerance, with equipment lines parsed as `Staged` (correct P/D/I split) and General Requirement lines as `Single`.
- Clone produces an independent BOQ (edits to the copy do not affect the source).
- The manual builder rejects a `Staged` line missing any of the three P/D/I amounts.

### 6.5 Bill of Quantities & Weighting Model

#### 6.5.1 Structure

A BOQ is `Project → Category → Line → (Stage)`. Each line carries: item no., description, qty, unit, unit price, line total. Staged lines additionally carry three sub-amounts (Procure / Deliver / Install).

#### 6.5.2 Weight computation (system-derived, never typed)

- **Line relative weight %** = line total ÷ project grand total.
- **Stage weight %** (staged lines) = stage amount ÷ project grand total. The three stage weights sum to the line weight.
- **Grand total** = Σ all line totals. Recomputed on any line edit.
- The sum of all line weights = **100%** (allow rounding tolerance; display to the precision used in the source, ≈2–10 decimals — see 6.14 #6).

#### 6.5.3 Requirements

- **FR-BOQ-01** — Editing any amount shall recompute that line's weight, all of its stage weights, and the project grand total **atomically**.
- **FR-BOQ-02** — For a staged line, the three stage weights shall always sum to the line weight.
- **FR-BOQ-03** — All line weights shall sum to 100% (± rounding tolerance); the system shall surface any out-of-tolerance variance to the PM head.
- **FR-BOQ-04** — Weights shall be stored at high precision and displayed rounded to the source's precision (6.13 #6 / 6.14 #6).

#### 6.5.4 Acceptance criteria

- Editing any amount recomputes that line's weight, all stage weights, and the grand total atomically.
- Stage weights of a staged line always sum to its line weight.
- All line weights sum to 100% (± rounding tolerance) and the system surfaces any out-of-tolerance variance.

#### 6.5.5 Variation Orders (change orders)

The BOQ is **not frozen after import.** Construction contracts change mid-project — added lines, revised quantities/prices, or removed scope. A `VariationOrder` captures each change as a controlled, audited event so weights and billing stay correct.

**Model.** A `VariationOrder` has a number, date, description, status (`Draft → Approved`), and a set of line deltas: _add line_, _revise line_ (qty / unit price / P-D-I amounts), or _remove line_. On approval, the deltas apply to the live BOQ; the grand total and all line/stage weights recompute. The pre-change BOQ snapshot is retained for audit.

**Reconciliation with already-billed periods (the hard part).** Because weights are share-of-grand-total, changing the grand total retroactively shifts every line's weight, which would alter the % accomplishment of locked periods. Two candidate policies:

- **(A) Prospective / revised-contract — ✅ CONFIRMED (§6.14 #3, finalization sweep).** Locked periods keep their originally-billed peso amounts unchanged; the variation takes effect from the next open period forward, computed against the new grand total. Cumulative % is re-expressed against the revised contract value. _Rationale:_ cleanest for billing integrity — past bills are never restated, which matches how progress billings are issued and accepted in the field. This is the default the build should assume unless the contract requires otherwise.
- **(B) Retrospective restatement** — recompute all periods against the new BOQ and issue an adjustment line for the delta. Use only if the client/contract explicitly requires restated cumulative figures.

**Requirements.**

- **FR-VO-01** — Variation orders shall be role-gated to the PM head and fully audited (who, when, before/after snapshot).
- **FR-VO-02** — Approving a variation order shall recompute the grand total and all line/stage weights and retain the pre-change BOQ snapshot.
- **FR-VO-03** — Under the default policy (A), peso amounts of locked periods shall be unchanged after a variation; the variation shall take effect from the next open period against the revised contract value.
- **FR-VO-04** — The accomplishment report shall display **original contract value, approved variations to date, and revised contract value.**
- **FR-VO-05** — The DP-recoupment cap (6.6.5) and retention basis (6.6.6) shall recompute against the revised contract value under the chosen policy.

**Acceptance criteria.**

- Approving a variation order recomputes the grand total and all weights, and retains a pre-change snapshot.
- Under policy (A), peso amounts of locked periods are unchanged after a variation.
- The report displays original, variation, and revised contract values.

### 6.6 Accomplishment Report

The **core deliverable.** It renders each project in the PM head's existing layout; she enters progress per period; the system computes everything downstream and produces the progress billing.

#### 6.6.1 Period model

Periods are sequential progress billings: **PB1, PB2, PB3…**, each with an "as of" date. Each period tracks, per line/stage: **Previous** (cumulative before this period), **This Period** (the new % the PM head keys), **To Date** (Previous + This Period). A period is a **draft** until submitted, then it **locks (freezes)**. Subsequent periods open from the locked To-Date baseline. Corrections to a locked period require a controlled re-open (audited) or an adjusting entry in the next period — see 6.14 #1 for the recommended mechanism.

#### 6.6.2 Data entry (PM head only) + stage-sequence guardrail

She edits **only** the _This Period %_ per line (Single) or per stage (Staged). All other cells are computed and read-only.

- Single value per cell; validate 0–100% and that To-Date never exceeds 100% per line/stage.
- **Stage-sequence guardrail:** on a Staged line the natural order is Procure → Deliver → Install. The system **warns (soft block, overridable with a recorded reason)** if a later stage progresses while an earlier stage on the same item is below 100% To-Date — e.g., Install advancing while Procure/Deliver are incomplete. Prevents physically impossible progress and improves data quality.
- The report header mirrors the source: title, "as of" date, client/cooperative, project, location, grand-total row.

#### 6.6.3 Computation

For each line/stage in a period:

- `to_date_% = previous_% + this_period_%`
- `this_period_weighted = stage_weight × this_period_%` (the weighted contribution)
- `to_date_weighted = stage_weight × to_date_%`

**Grand Total % accomplishment (to date)** = Σ `to_date_weighted` across all lines/stages. **This-period amount** per line/stage = `stage_amount × this_period_%` (peso value earned this period).

#### 6.6.4 Progress billing (the NET AMOUNT computation)

Per period, derived from the source NET AMOUNT sheet. Reference figures from **NORECO2 PB1** (% accomplishment **11.34%**, contract grand total **₱53,277,688**):

| Step                    | Formula                                                | NORECO2 PB1       |
| ----------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------ | ----------------- |
| **Progress Bill (PBn)** | this-period earned amount = Σ this-period line amounts | ₱6,039,221.60     |
| **Less DP recoupment**  | − recoupment for this period (see 6.6.5)               | − ₱905,883.24     |
| **Less retention**      | − (retention % × PBn), default 10% — held, see 6.6.6   | − ₱603,922.16     |
| **Net Amount (PBn)**    | PBn − recoupment − retention                           | **₱4,529,416.20** |

DP-recoupment % and retention % are **project-level settings** (defaults 15% / 10%, editable per contract). The single-period table above matches the source sheet, but recoupment and retention are modeled as **running ledgers across all periods**, not flat per-period deductions — see 6.6.5–6.6.6.

#### 6.6.5 DP recoupment is capped (cumulative)

The mobilization downpayment is advanced once at project start and recouped progressively until fully recovered, then recoupment stops. A flat 15% × PBn on every period would claw back more than was advanced.

- `downpayment_amount = dp_recoupment_pct × contract_amount` — the cap, recorded in `RecoupmentLedger`. _(For NORECO2: 15% × ₱53,277,688 = ₱7,991,653.20.)_
- Per period: `recoupment_this_period = min(dp_recoupment_pct × PBn, downpayment_remaining)`.
- `downpayment_remaining` decrements each period; once it reaches 0, recoupment is 0 for all later periods.
- The report shows **recouped-to-date** and **remaining downpayment**.

#### 6.6.6 Retention is held, then released

Retention is **withheld and accumulated, not lost**; it is released later (typically on project completion / after the defects-liability period).

- Per period: `retention_this_period = retention_pct × PBn`, added to `RetentionLedger` (retention-held-to-date).
- A **release event** (manual, role-gated, audited) pays out held retention — in full on completion, or in partial tranches (e.g., 50% on completion, 50% after defects-liability) per contract (default recommended in 6.14 #4).
- The system tracks retention held-to-date, release events, and outstanding retention payable; **a release is its own billing line, not a negative deduction inside a progress bill.**

#### 6.6.7 Requirements

- **FR-ACC-01** — Only the PM-head role may create/edit/submit accomplishment periods; all other roles are read-only (see Section 3 and 6.12.1).
- **FR-ACC-02** — Keying _This Period %_ shall recompute To-Date, Grand Total %, and the full NET AMOUNT block live.
- **FR-ACC-03** — To-Date % shall not exceed 100% for any line/stage; the system blocks or warns.
- **FR-ACC-04** — A submitted period is immutable except through an audited re-open (6.14 #1).
- **FR-ACC-05** — Subsequent periods shall open from the prior period's locked To-Date baseline.
- **FR-ACC-06** — The stage-sequence guardrail (6.6.2) shall warn when a later stage progresses ahead of an incomplete earlier stage, and shall record the reason when overridden.
- **FR-ACC-07** — The on-screen and printable report shall match the PM head's existing column layout (faithfulness level per 6.14 #7).
- **FR-PB-01** — `PBn` shall equal Σ of this-period line amounts (`stage_amount × this_period_%`).
- **FR-PB-02** — Recoupment per period shall be `min(dp_recoupment_pct × PBn, downpayment_remaining)`; cumulative DP recouped shall never exceed `dp_recoupment_pct × contract_amount`; once the cap is reached, later periods deduct zero recoupment.
- **FR-PB-03** — Retention held-to-date shall equal Σ(`retention_pct × each PBn`) minus released amounts; a release event reduces outstanding retention and is recorded as a separate billing line, not a deduction inside a progress bill.
- **FR-PB-04** — `Net Amount (PBn) = PBn − recoupment_this_period − retention_this_period`.
- **FR-PB-05** — The report shall display recouped-to-date, remaining downpayment, retention held-to-date, and outstanding retention payable.

#### 6.6.8 Acceptance criteria

- Only the PM head can create/edit/submit accomplishment periods; all other roles are read-only.
- Keying _This Period %_ recomputes To-Date, Grand Total %, and the full NET AMOUNT block live.
- Reproducing NORECO2 PB1 inputs yields **Net Amount ₱4,529,416.20** (within rounding), at **11.34%** accomplishment.
- To-Date % cannot exceed 100% for any line/stage.
- A submitted period is immutable except through an audited re-open.
- Across multiple periods, total DP recouped never exceeds `dp_recoupment_pct × contract_amount`; once the cap is reached, later periods deduct zero recoupment.
- Retention held-to-date equals Σ(retention % × each PBn) minus released amounts; a release event reduces outstanding retention and is recorded separately from progress bills.
- The stage-sequence guardrail warns on out-of-order P/D/I progress and records the override reason.

#### 6.6.9 Delivery cross-check (read-only, no forced sync)

A v1 integrity guard that **does not auto-update the accomplishment %.** The system surfaces a **non-blocking flag** where the PM head's reported stage % diverges from the actual state in Purchasing's 15-stage import tracker (7.8) for the same item/line.

- _Example:_ reported Install = 100% while the import tracker shows the unit at Stage 8 (Production) → flag "**reported ahead of delivery.**"
- **Display only** — she may still submit; the flag is informational and logged.
- **Matching key:** the BOQ line / equipment item linked to its MR → PO → ImportShipment (7.8). Where no link exists, no flag is shown. The exact mapping from a BOQ stage % to an import-tracker stage is finalized in 6.14 #9.

**Requirements.**

- **FR-XCK-01** — When a linked item's reported stage % exceeds what the import-tracker stage (7.8) implies, a read-only flag shall appear on that line.
- **FR-XCK-02** — The flag shall never block submission and shall never alter the entered %.
- **FR-XCK-03** — Where no MR→PO→ImportShipment link exists for a line, no flag is shown.

**Acceptance criteria.**

- When a linked item's reported stage % exceeds what the import-tracker stage implies, a read-only flag appears on that line.
- The flag never blocks submission and never alters the entered %.

### 6.7 Project Portfolio (PM head's view)

A single screen listing all JCE projects so the PM head can see and update any of them.

**Requirements.**

- **FR-PORT-01** — One card/row per project shall show: project name, client, % complete (to date), this-period gain, current period (PBn), billing status, and last-updated timestamp + author.
- **FR-PORT-02** — Click-through shall open the full accomplishment report (6.6) for that project.
- **FR-PORT-03** — The list shall be filterable/sortable by client, % complete, last-updated, and billing status.
- **FR-PORT-04** — Each project shall show a visual progress indicator (ring/bar).
- **FR-PORT-05** — Portfolio % complete shall equal the project's Grand Total % accomplishment to date (6.6.3).

**Acceptance criteria.**

- Every project the PM head owns appears; figures match each project's latest submitted/draft period.
- Portfolio % complete equals the project's Grand Total % accomplishment to date.

### 6.8 Latest-Update / Activity Timeline

A per-project chronological feed that makes "the latest update" presentable.

**Requirements.**

- **FR-TL-01** — The timeline shall aggregate events across modules: last accomplishment period posted, last MR issued, last PO raised (from Purchasing 7.x), last delivery/stock receipt.
- **FR-TL-02** — Each event shall carry type, timestamp, actor, and a short summary, and shall link to the underlying record.
- **FR-TL-03** — The system shall surface a compact "updated X ago by [name] · next milestone" summary usable on the portfolio card (6.7).
- **FR-TL-04** — Events shall be ordered newest-first.

**Acceptance criteria.**

- Posting a period, issuing an MR, or receiving stock creates a timeline event within the project feed.
- Events are ordered newest-first and link to their source record.

### 6.9 Warehouse / Inventory _(promoted to Section 10)_

The warehouse inventory master, stock movements, and reservation logic were **promoted to a standalone Warehouse Department module in v1.60 — see Section 10.** The Warehouse is the authoritative owner of `InventoryItem`, `StockMovement`, and `StockReservation` (Section 10.3). The Material Request workflow below (§6.10) **authors** requests and consumes the Warehouse **stock-verification service** (Section 10.7): it reads Available Stock (= on-hand − reserved), and on approval the Warehouse reserves in-stock quantities and issues them on fulfillment. The locked inventory-first and reservation decisions formerly stated here now live in Section 10.2 / 10.6.

### 6.10 Material Request (MR) Workflow

PMG issues MRs on the **`JCE-F-WMS02`** form. **Inventory-first is enforced** (6.2 #3): every requested line is checked against the warehouse master before any purchase quantity is produced; only the "For Purchase" remainder flows to Purchasing.

#### 6.10.1 Header fields (from `JCE-GARCIA 3-0257.xlsx`)

Title **MATERIAL REQUEST**; Date; **MR No.** (e.g., `JCE-GARCIA 3-0258`); Name of Project (FK to `Project`, 6.3.1); SO No. (FK); Date Required; General Purpose for Request.

#### 6.10.2 Line columns (the inventory-first structure)

| Group               | Columns                                                                      |
| ------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Item**            | Item no., Description, Purpose                                               |
| **Requested**       | Qty, Unit                                                                    |
| **Available Stock** | Qty, Unit — auto-filled = `on_hand − reserved` (Section 10.6) on item select |
| **For Purchase**    | Qty, Unit — computed = `max(0, Requested − Available)`                       |
| **Canvass Sheet**   | Supplier A, Supplier B, Supplier C (quotes)                                  |

#### 6.10.3 Workflow

1. PMG adds a line and selects/creates an item; the system auto-fills **Available Stock** (= on-hand − reserved) from the master.
2. The system computes **For Purchase** = `max(0, Requested − Available)`.
3. On approval: in-stock quantities are **reserved** (`StockReservation`, Section 10.6) and later **issued** (`StockMovement = Issue`) on fulfillment; **For-Purchase lines flow to Purchasing** as a PO candidate, carrying the canvass-sheet quotes.
4. **Signatory blocks are print-only** (offline signing), mirroring the form: Requested by, Noted by, Received by (Purchasing), Approved by (Department Head), plus Verified by Warehouse.

#### 6.10.4 MR numbering

**RESOLVED (6.14 #2): a single global running sequence** across all projects, owned by PMG and never reset — the hard-FK source that Warehouse (§10.16 #6) defers to. The legacy per-project format observed on the forms (`JCE-GARCIA 3-0258`) becomes display-only on migrated rows; the project remains a field on the MR. Footer form code `JCE-F-WMS02`.

#### 6.10.5 Requirements

- **FR-MR-01** — Selecting an existing item shall auto-fill Available Stock from the live master (= on-hand − reserved).
- **FR-MR-02** — For Purchase shall equal `max(0, Requested − Available)` on every line and update if Requested changes.
- **FR-MR-03** — Approving an MR shall reserve in-stock quantities (available decremented) and emit For-Purchase lines to Purchasing with their canvass quotes; on fulfillment the reservation converts to an issue (on-hand decremented).
- **FR-MR-04** — A line with Available ≥ Requested shall produce zero For-Purchase and no Purchasing hand-off.
- **FR-MR-05** — An item created inline on the MR shall be persisted to the inventory master (Section 10, FR-WIM-02).
- **FR-MR-06** — The printable MR shall reproduce the `JCE-F-WMS02` layout including all signatory blocks.

#### 6.10.6 Acceptance criteria

- Selecting an existing item auto-fills Available Stock from the live master.
- For Purchase = `max(0, Requested − Available)` on every line and updates if Requested changes.
- Approving an MR reserves in-stock quantities and emits For-Purchase lines to Purchasing with canvass quotes; on fulfillment the reservation converts to an issue.
- A line with Available ≥ Requested produces zero For-Purchase and no Purchasing hand-off.
- An item created inline on the MR is persisted to the inventory master.
- The printable MR reproduces the `JCE-F-WMS02` layout including all signatory blocks.

### 6.11 Audit Log (Department-Scoped)

Mirroring Purchasing 7.10, the PMG module maintains an immutable, department-scoped audit log of state-changing actions.

- **FR-LOG-01** — The system shall record an immutable `AuditLogEntry` for each state-changing action: project create/edit, BOQ edit, variation-order approval, period submit/re-open, retention release, MR approval, and stock adjustment.
- **FR-LOG-02** — Each entry shall capture actor, timestamp, affected entity, and before/after values where applicable (variation orders and re-opens retain full snapshots, FR-VO-02 / FR-ACC-04).
- **FR-LOG-03** — Audit entries shall be append-only (not editable or deletable through the application). **Retention follows the system-wide policy: time-bounded with archival (NFR-AUDIT-02 / §7.13 #3).**

### 6.12 Cross-Cutting Requirements

#### 6.12.1 Roles & permissions

| Role                | Capability                                                                                                                                    |
| ------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **PM Head**         | Sole create/edit/submit on Projects, BOQ, Variation Orders, and Accomplishment periods; triggers retention releases; full read on everything. |
| **PMG Staff**       | Create/edit Material Requests; read projects/portfolio.                                                                                       |
| **Warehouse**       | Maintain inventory master & stock movements; verify MR stock.                                                                                 |
| **Purchasing**      | Receives For-Purchase lines (read MR / canvass); PO authority per Section 7.                                                                  |
| **Department Head** | Approves MRs (offline signing; system records status).                                                                                        |

The full role × action matrix is produced centrally in Section 3 (RBAC); this table is the PMG input to it.

#### 6.12.2 Attachments / document repository

- **FR-XC-01** — Files may be attached to Projects, Accomplishment periods, and MRs (e.g., signed report scans, MR attachments referenced as "PLEASE SEE ATTACHED" in the sample). The `Attachment` entity is polymorphic (6.3.2).

#### 6.12.3 Notifications & alerts

- **FR-XC-02** — The system shall raise alerts for: new MR awaiting approval; MR approved → Purchasing hand-off; period submitted; To-Date approaching/over 100%; and (Phase 2) low stock against a reorder point (Section 10.12.1).

#### 6.12.4 Dashboard

- **FR-XC-03** — The PMG landing page shall summarize: portfolio status, projects needing a period update, open MRs by status, and recent stock movements.

#### 6.12.5 Reporting & analytics

- **FR-XC-04** — Per-project: accomplishment report (print/export), progress-billing history (PB1…n), and MR register.
- **FR-XC-05** — Roll-up: portfolio % complete, and billed-to-date vs. contract.

#### 6.12.6 Numbers & currency

- **FR-XC-06** — Project amounts are in **PHP**. Weights are stored at high precision and displayed rounded to the source's precision (6.5.2, 6.14 #6). All on-screen monetary and % values are rounded for display.

#### 6.12.7 Non-functional (see the expanded Non-Functional Requirements, Section 12)

- **FR-XC-07** — Period locking shall be atomic and auditable; stock-on-hand shall be transactionally consistent with its movements; import parsing shall be deterministic given a confirmed mapping.

### 6.13 Resolved Decisions

1. **Accomplishment % is manual, PM-head only** — no auto-sync from Purchasing's import tracker in v1. (6.2 #1, FR-ACC-01)
2. **Inventory master is built from scratch**; MRs may add items on the fly. _(Now owned by the Warehouse module — Section 10.2 #1, FR-WIM-02.)_
3. **Inventory-first** is enforced by the MR form's Requested → Available → For-Purchase columns. (6.2 #3, FR-MR-02)
4. **New projects come in primarily by BOQ import** with a mapping/preview confirmation step; clone and manual builder are fallbacks. (6.4, FR-INT-01)
5. **P/D/I split is per-line, no default**; lines are either `Staged` (P/D/I) or `Single`. (6.3.4, FR-INT-05)
6. **Billing math:** `PBn = period-earned amount; less DP recoupment; less retention; = Net Amount` (defaults 15% / 10%, editable per project). Recoupment is **capped cumulatively** (6.6.5) and retention is **held-and-released** (6.6.6) — not flat per-period deductions. (FR-PB-02, FR-PB-03)
7. **Variation orders (6.5.5) are in v1** — the BOQ can change mid-project with audited recompute; reconciliation policy is **(A) prospective / revised-contract** (confirmed, 6.14 #3).
8. **Delivery cross-check (6.6.9) is in v1** — read-only flag vs. Purchasing's import tracker (7.8); no forced sync.
9. **Inventory reservation (now Section 10.6) is in v1** — `available = on-hand − reserved`; MR approval reserves stock. (FR-RSV-01)
10. **Stage-sequence guardrail (6.6.2) is in v1** — warns on out-of-order P/D/I progress (soft block, overridable with reason). (FR-ACC-06)
11. **Signatures (reports, MRs) are offline**; the system tracks status only. (6.2 #8)
12. **First-pass scope = portfolio + accomplishment reports AND warehouse + MR workflow** together. (6.2 #7)
13. **Project entity unification** — `Project` is the unifying parent over customer projects (`project_type = Customer`, SO-backed) and internal cost centers (`Internal Cost Center`). **Resolves 7.13 #7 and realizes 7.15.9**; Purchasing's `Project` FK now points to 6.3.2.
14. **MR entity ownership** — `MaterialRequest` (MR No.) is owned by PMG (6.10). **Resolves 7.13 #8**; Purchasing's `MR No.` soft-FK becomes a hard FK.

### 6.14 Open Questions for SRS Finalization _(all resolved — finalization sweep)_

1. ✅ **Locked-period correction — RESOLVED: both, next-period adjusting entry by default.** Routine corrections post as an adjusting entry in the next period; an audited re-open (full snapshot, FR-ACC-04) is reserved for genuine errors in an already-issued bill.
2. ✅ **MR numbering — RESOLVED: global running sequence.** A single continuous MR number across all projects, owned by PMG and never reset, is the hard-FK source (Warehouse §10.16 #6 defers to it). _Note: this changes the observed per-project form format (`JCE-GARCIA 3-0258`) to a continuous sequence; the project is still a field on the MR. See §6.10.4._
3. ✅ **Variation-order reconciliation — RESOLVED: policy (A) prospective / revised-contract.** Locked periods keep their billed amounts; variations take effect from the next period against the revised contract value (6.5.5).
4. ✅ **Retention release schedule — RESOLVED: per-contract, no system default.** The release schedule (full-on-completion, or tranches such as 50/50 across the defects-liability period) is set per project at contract setup; the system enforces whatever schedule is configured and tracks held vs. released (6.6.6).
5. ✅ **Stock Receipt ↔ PO — RESOLVED (Warehouse §10.16 #2): automatic in v1** via the MRR goods-receipt, superseding the earlier 'manual in v1' recommendation.
6. ✅ **Weight precision — RESOLVED: store at full precision, display rounded** to the source's precision (FR-XC-06); totals compute on full-precision values to avoid rounding drift.
7. ✅ **Printable report fidelity — RESOLVED: byte-faithful to the PM head's template.** The printable accomplishment report reproduces her exact spreadsheet layout (columns, headers, totals, styling). _Implication: the report template is built to match the source sheet precisely and re-versioned if her template changes._
8. ✅ **Multi-project inventory — RESOLVED (Warehouse §10.16 #4): project-scoped balances** with a `JCE STOCK` central cost-centre at Main Office, superseding the earlier shared-master recommendation.
9. ✅ **Delivery cross-check mapping — RESOLVED:** Procure → import stages 1–7, Deliver → stages 8–13, Install → stages 14–15; the read-only flag fires when a line's reported stage % implies completion beyond the tracker's current stage (6.6.9).

### 6.15 Phase 2 — Extended Scope

These features build on the Phase 1 backbone above and are **approved for inclusion as a second phase.** Three items originally considered here — the delivered-vs-reported cross-check, inventory reservation, and the stage-sequence guardrail — were **promoted into v1** (6.6.9, Section 10.6, 6.6.2). What remains below is genuinely deferrable.

#### 6.15.0 New entities introduced in Phase 2

| Entity            | Purpose                                    | Key relationships                                   |
| ----------------- | ------------------------------------------ | --------------------------------------------------- |
| **ProgressPhoto** | Field evidence per BOQ line/stage          | references 1 BoqLine/Stage + 1 AccomplishmentPeriod |
| **BoqTemplate**   | Reusable standard BOQ for repeat job types | → seeds a new BOQ on intake                         |
| **DocumentPack**  | Export bundle per progress billing         | references 1 AccomplishmentPeriod + its Attachments |

#### 6.15.1 Field photo evidence

Site engineers attach progress photos per BOQ line/stage (mobile-friendly capture). The PM head reviews the photos before keying the _This Period %_, so the manual figure has visual backing — strengthening the trust model behind the v1 manual-entry decision (6.2 #1).

#### 6.15.2 BOQ template library

Reusable standard BOQs for repeat job types (e.g., substation classes) — standard categories, lines, and P/D/I splits — so a new project starts from a curated template, not just a clone of a past project. Faster intake and consistent weighting across jobs.

#### 6.15.3 S-curve & progress charting

Planned-vs-actual S-curve per project from period history; portfolio heatmap.

#### 6.15.4 MR → PO → delivery traceability board

End-to-end view linking each For-Purchase MR line (6.10) to its PO (7.5), shipment stage (7.8), and stock receipt (Section 10.5). Closes the loop the v1 cross-check (6.6.9) only flags.

#### 6.15.5 Inventory reorder points & low-stock alerts _(moved)_

Moved to the Warehouse module — see **Section 10.12.1**.

#### 6.15.6 Multi-warehouse / location transfers _(moved)_

Moved to the Warehouse module — see **Section 10.12.2**.

#### 6.15.7 Document pack export

One-click bundle per progress billing (accomplishment report + NET AMOUNT + attachments) as a PDF set.

#### 6.15.8 Project budget vs. actual (PMG side)

Compare BOQ contract value against committed POs and issued materials per project — ties to Purchasing's budget feature (7.14.6) and the unified `Project` key (6.3.2).

---

## 7. Purchasing Department Module _(finalized v1.69)_

> **Source.** This section integrates the **"JC Electrofields — Purchasing Module Requirements"** document provided by Bryan on 2026-06-02, derived from review of: JCE Supplier List New.xlsx (~145 suppliers), IMPORTATION MONITORING 05-26-26.xlsx (~970 rows), the Eslite local PO sample (`PO 26060001`), the Shandong JNDO import PO sample (`PO 2606000IC`), and the JCE Request For Payment Form (`RPF-blank.xlsx`). Requirement codes preserve the source document's `FR-XXX-NN` naming (e.g., `FR-NUM-01`, `FR-PO-04`) so the SRS and the source requirements doc remain in 1:1 correspondence.

### 7.1 Module Overview

The Purchasing module is JCE's **vendor-side procurement engine**. It owns the Purchase Order lifecycle (creation → approval → send → close), the Request For Payment that hands off to Accounting, and the 15-stage import shipment tracker that follows foreign-sourced material from BOM through onsite delivery. The module is **transactional and document-heavy** — every record produces a print-ready PDF that gets wet-signed offline; the system tracks status for workflow visibility while signatures happen on paper. The Purchasing module is also the **authoritative owner of the Supplier master entity** — BDD 9.5 Quotation, which introduced Suppliers as a maintained list, reads from Purchasing's canonical record; the more comprehensive Supplier schema in this section supersedes the earlier BDD reference.

The module is organized into **six functional areas** plus cross-cutting services in Phase 1, with eight approved Phase 2 features built on top of the Phase 1 backbone:

- **Phase 1**: PO Monitoring Ledger · PO Creation (with auto-generated PDF) · Supplier CRUD · Request For Payment (RFP) · Imported-PO Tracker (15-stage engine) · Department-scoped Audit Log · plus cross-cutting roles, attachments, notifications, dashboard, reporting, and FX policy.
- **Phase 2** (scoped, sequenced for after Phase 1 stabilization): RFQ/Quotation management · Item/Material Catalog · Price History & Price-Change Flags · BIR Withholding-Tax Reporting (Form 2307 + Alphalist) · Lead-Time Auto-Calculation & Supplier Reliability · Budget vs. Actual per Project · Mobile Approvals · Cycle-Time Analytics.

**Cross-module hooks resolved by this section:**

| Open item                                                | Resolution                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| -------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **PV 5.5 #3** — RFP vs JCE-PR vs ACCTG-#### relationship | RFP-PUR-##### is the **Purchasing-side** Request For Payment (this module is its origin). ACCTG-#### is Accounting's internal request number. JCE-PR is the Accounting consolidated worksheet number. Three distinct references, one workflow: Purchasing emits RFP → Accounting receives it and creates the PV (5.5) → Disbursement executes (5.6). |
| **PV 5.5 #5** — ATC withholding tax handling             | The RFP itself computes EWT with explicit classification rates (Goods 1%, Services 2%, Rentals 5%, Professional Fee 5%/10%) — see FR-RFP-04 and FR-RFP-06. The PV inherits the computed withholding from the RFP rather than re-computing.                                                                                                           |
| **PV 5.5 #6** — PO linkage strictness                    | For supplier-payment vouchers, `po_no` is a **hard FK to a real PO record** in this module. Free-text fallback only for petty-cash, employee-reimbursement, or other no-PO transactions where the `payee_type` is `Employee` or `Other`.                                                                                                             |
| **PV 5.5 #11** — Banking integration vs manual capture   | Out of scope for Phase 1 (`payment_method = Bank Transfer` still captures `bank_reference_no` manually). A future Phase-3 banking integration is conceivable but not committed.                                                                                                                                                                      |
| **BDD 9.5 Suppliers entity ownership**                   | Purchasing 7.9 is the **authoritative Supplier master**; BDD 9.5 Quotation references it as an FK. The more comprehensive 7.9 schema (Supplier Code, Category, Item/Service Offered, etc.) supersedes 9.5's earlier preliminary fields.                                                                                                              |

### 7.2 Scope & Locked Decisions

**Decisions already locked** (not to be re-litigated during build):

1. **PO numbering matches existing files.** Local POs use `YYMM####` (year-month + 4-digit sequence; e.g., `26060001`). Import POs use the same pattern with an `IC` suffix (e.g., `2606000IC`).
2. **Approvals are offline.** The system generates the PO PDF; the physical/wet signatures happen outside the system on the printed paper. The system tracks an internal status so staff can see where each PO sits, but no in-system e-signing.
3. **Import tracker is the full 15-stage engine.** Each stage carries status, date, owner, and document-attachment slots. The 15 stages are listed verbatim in 7.8.

These three decisions are confirmed; the SRS treats them as committed.

### 7.3 Data Backbone & Entity Model

#### 7.3.1 Linking keys

Three business keys connect records across the module and must be first-class, indexed fields on every relevant entity:

- **Project** — the job/project the purchase belongs to (legacy examples: `NORECO II`, `GARCIA 3`, `WORKSHOP`, `JCE STOCK`). Note that "Project" here is **broader than Sales Order** — it includes internal cost centers like `WORKSHOP` and `JCE STOCK` that are not customer-facing projects.
- **SO No. (Sales Order No.)** — e.g., `25-10-346`. May be `N/A` for stock or workshop purchases. References BDD 9.3 Sales Order when present.
- **MR No. (Material Request No.)** — e.g., `JCE-WS-9048`, `JCE-GARCIA 3-0169`. The material request that originated the purchase need.

**Integration decision (confirmed by Bryan):** Project, SO No., and MR No. are **foreign keys** to the shared JCE-dashboard modules (Projects, Sales Orders, Material Requests), **not free text**. The Purchasing module reads/validates against those tables, so selecting a Project / SO / MR pulls authoritative records and enables cross-module navigation (a Project page shows all its POs, RFPs, and shipments). Where a referenced record does not yet exist (legacy `N/A` SO numbers, or modules not yet built — Project Management is Section 6, still pending), the system shall allow a **validated fallback value** and reconcile on migration.

> **Dependency on future Section 6 Project Management.** The "Projects" and "Material Requests" referenced as FKs above are owned by the Project Management module, which is currently a placeholder. Until Section 6 is scoped, Purchasing accepts these as free-text values that will be migrated to FKs once the parent entities exist. The system shall therefore support a "soft-FK" mode for these two keys in Phase 1, transitioning to "hard-FK" once Section 6 is built. ✅ **v1.59:** Section 6 is now scoped — the hard-FK target entities exist (`Project` 6.3.2, `MaterialRequest` 6.10); soft-FK mode now applies only to legacy rows pending migration.

#### 7.3.2 Entities

| Entity                          | Purpose                                           | Key relationships                                         |
| ------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Supplier**                    | Master record of external providers               | 1 Supplier → many Purchase Orders                         |
| **PurchaseOrder**               | Header of a PO; carries `type = Local             | Import`                                                   | belongs to 1 Supplier; → many LineItems; → many RFPs; if Import → 1 ImportShipment |
| **PurchaseOrderLineItem**       | One row of goods/services on a PO                 | belongs to 1 PurchaseOrder                                |
| **RFP**                         | Request For Payment against an approved PO        | belongs to 1 PurchaseOrder                                |
| **ImportShipment**              | Container for the 15-stage import lifecycle       | belongs to 1 PurchaseOrder (Import only)                  |
| **ImportShipmentStage**         | One of the 15 stages, with status/date/owner/docs | belongs to 1 ImportShipment                               |
| **Attachment**                  | Uploaded document file                            | polymorphic — attaches to PO, RFP, or ImportShipmentStage |
| **AuditLogEntry**               | Immutable record of a state-changing action       | references actor + affected entity                        |
| **SupplierCategory** _(lookup)_ | Controlled list for supplier categorisation       | referenced by Supplier                                    |
| **PaymentTerm** _(lookup)_      | Controlled list of payment terms (TOP)            | referenced by Supplier & PO                               |

Phase 2 introduces additional entities (RFQ, Quotation, Item/Material Catalog, ItemPriceHistory, ProjectBudget) — see 7.14.0.

#### 7.3.3 Relationship summary

```
Supplier (1) ──< PurchaseOrder (many)
PurchaseOrder (1) ──< PurchaseOrderLineItem (many)
PurchaseOrder (1) ──< RFP (many)            [Local: usually 1 · Import: usually 2]
PurchaseOrder (1) ──1 ImportShipment        [only when type = Import]
ImportShipment (1) ──< ImportShipmentStage (15)
{PurchaseOrder, RFP, ImportShipmentStage} (1) ──< Attachment (many)
```

#### 7.3.4 The `type` flag drives behavior

A single PO `type` field (`Local` | `Import`) governs five downstream behaviors. The SRS treats this as the central branch point:

| Behavior                   | Local                                         | Import                                           |
| -------------------------- | --------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------ |
| **Currency**               | PHP                                           | USD (per sample; allow other foreign currencies) |
| **VAT**                    | 12% logic applies (subject to `vatable` flag) | None (Incoterm-based)                            |
| **Numbering**              | `YYMM####`                                    | `YYMM####IC`                                     |
| **PDF template**           | Local template                                | Import template                                  |
| **Import Shipment record** | Not created                                   | Auto-created on PO approval                      |

### 7.4 PO Numbering

#### 7.4.1 Format

- **Local**: `YYMM` + zero-padded 4-digit sequence. Example: 2026-06 → `26060001`.
- **Import**: identical base + literal suffix `IC`. Example: `2606000IC` (the sample shows `2606` + `000` + `IC`; treat as `YYMM + sequence + IC`).

#### 7.4.2 Requirements

- **FR-NUM-01** — On PO save (first persist, not draft autosave), the system shall generate the PO number from the `YYMM` derived from the **PO date field (not the system clock)**, so back-dated POs number correctly, and the next sequence value for that period.
- **FR-NUM-02** — The sequence shall **reset monthly** (confirmed). The first PO of each calendar month restarts at `0001`.
- **FR-NUM-03** — Local and Import maintain **separate counters** (confirmed). Each `YYMM` period has two independent sequences. The first import of June 2026 is `…IC` regardless of how many local POs exist that month.
- **FR-NUM-04** — Number generation shall be **atomic/concurrency-safe** (DB-level sequence or transactional lock per `YYMM + type`) so two simultaneous saves never collide. Two counters per period (Local, Import) are locked independently.
- **FR-NUM-05** — PO numbers are **unique and immutable** once assigned. Voided/cancelled POs retain their number; gaps in the sequence are permitted.
- **FR-NUM-06** — Draft POs (not yet saved/submitted) do **not consume a number**. The number is assigned at the moment the PO leaves Draft.

#### 7.4.3 Acceptance criteria

- Creating the first local PO in June 2026 yields `26060001`; the next yields `26060002`.
- The first import PO of the period yields `…IC`.
- Two users saving POs at the same instant receive different sequential numbers.
- A PO dated 2026-05-30 saved on 2026-06-02 numbers under `2605`, not `2606` (period derives from PO date — confirmed).
- The local and import counters are independent: month's first local = `…0001`, month's first import = `…0001IC`.

### 7.5 PO Creation (with auto-generated PDF)

One form that branches on `type`. Supplier selection auto-fills supplier-derived fields. The system produces a print-ready PDF.

#### 7.5.1 Common header fields (both types)

| Field            | Type                | Required    | Source / Notes                                                                |
| ---------------- | ------------------- | ----------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| PO Number        | string (auto)       | system      | FR-NUM-01; read-only                                                          |
| PO Date          | date                | yes         | defaults to today; drives numbering                                           |
| Type             | enum (Local/Import) | yes         | central branch flag                                                           |
| Supplier         | FK → Supplier       | yes         | selecting it auto-fills the fields below                                      |
| Supplier Name    | string              | auto        | from Supplier                                                                 |
| Supplier Address | string              | auto        | from Supplier (Complete Address)                                              |
| TIN              | string              | auto        | from Supplier                                                                 |
| Contact Person   | string              | auto        | from Supplier (First + Last)                                                  |
| Contact No.      | string              | auto        | from Supplier (Mobile/Landline)                                               |
| Email            | string              | auto        | from Supplier                                                                 |
| Project          | ref                 | yes         | linking key                                                                   |
| SO No.           | string/ref          | conditional | linking key; allow `N/A`                                                      |
| MR No.           | string/ref          | yes         | linking key                                                                   |
| Payment Terms    | ref → PaymentTerm   | yes         | default from Supplier TOP; editable                                           |
| Purpose / Notes  | text                | optional    | free text (e.g., `FOR WAREHOUSE STOCKS`)                                      |
| Status           | enum                | system      | `Draft → For Approval → Approved → Sent → Closed` (plus `Cancelled` / `Void`) |

#### 7.5.2 Local-specific fields

| Field            | Type      | Required | Notes                                                                                              |
| ---------------- | --------- | -------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Currency         | const PHP | system   |                                                                                                    |
| Vatable          | boolean   | yes      | default `true`; the local sample carries a `VATABLE` flag. When `false`, VAT lines are suppressed. |
| Delivery Date    | date      | yes      |                                                                                                    |
| Delivery Address | string    | yes      | defaults to JCE warehouse: `2129 La Mesa Street, Ugong, Valenzuela` (sample); editable             |

#### 7.5.3 Import-specific fields

| Field            | Type        | Required | Notes                                   |
| ---------------- | ----------- | -------- | --------------------------------------- |
| Currency         | enum        | yes      | default `USD`                           |
| Incoterm         | string/enum | yes      | e.g., `DDP JCE`, `CIF CEBU`             |
| Delivery Period  | string      | yes      | e.g., `10 days upon receipt of payment` |
| Product Warranty | string      | optional | e.g., `12 months warranty`              |
| Ref. No.         | string      | optional | sales-contract reference; allow `N/A`   |
| Discount         | money       | optional | applied before grand total              |

#### 7.5.4 Line items

| Field        | Type             | Required | Notes                                                                                                                                                       |
| ------------ | ---------------- | -------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| Line # / SN  | int (auto)       | system   | sequential                                                                                                                                                  |
| Qty          | decimal          | yes      |                                                                                                                                                             |
| Unit         | string           | yes      | e.g., `BOXES`, `PCS`, `CANS`, `PC`                                                                                                                          |
| Description  | text             | yes      | Import lines must support **multi-line spec blocks** (the Shandong sample lists load capacity, division, weight, packing size, gross weight under one item) |
| Unit Price   | money            | yes      | currency = PO currency                                                                                                                                      |
| Total Amount | money (computed) | system   | Qty × Unit Price                                                                                                                                            |

- **FR-PO-01** — Line `Total Amount = Qty × Unit Price`, auto-computed, non-editable.
- **FR-PO-02** — A PO must have at least one line item before it can leave Draft.
- **FR-PO-03** — A `"Nothing Follows"` terminator line shall print after the last item (both templates show this).

#### 7.5.5 Totals & VAT logic

**Local — VAT-inclusive model** (confirmed from Eslite sample):

- The sum of line `Total Amount`s = `TOTAL` (VAT-inclusive). Sample: 60,880.
- `Amount Net of VAT = TOTAL ÷ 1.12`. Sample: 54,357.14.
- `VAT = Net × 0.12`. Sample: 6,522.86.
- `TOTAL AMOUNT = TOTAL` (= Net + VAT).
- When `vatable = false`: `Net = TOTAL`, `VAT = 0`.

**FR-PO-04** — Local VAT computation shall follow the inclusive model above and round to 2 decimals (display), preserving full precision internally.

**Import:**

- `Sub-Total` = sum of line totals (sample 927).
- `Discount` subtracted if present.
- `Grand Total` in currency, printed as e.g., `USD 927.00`. No VAT.

**FR-PO-05** — Import totals shall show `Sub-Total`, `Discount`, `Grand Total` in the PO currency, with the currency code on the grand total.

#### 7.5.6 Signatory blocks (print only)

The two templates have different signatory chains; these print on the PDF (no in-system e-signing):

- **Local (Eslite sample)**: Prepared by Purchasing Assistant · Verified by Purchasing Supervisor · Approved by Fin./Admin Manager · Supplier's Conforme = President (Engr. Jimwel C. Capillo).
- **Import (Shandong sample)**: Prepared by Asst. Purchasing Head · Verified by Senior Engineer · Approved by (role) · Supplier's Conforme = President (Engr. Jimwel C. Capillo).

- **FR-PO-06** — Signatory names/positions per template shall be **configurable (admin-managed), not hard-coded**, since staff change over time.
- **FR-PO-07** — The PDF prints names + positions in the signatory block with blank signature lines and a date line.

#### 7.5.7 PDF generation

- **FR-PO-08** — On demand (and on status → `Approved` / `Sent`), the system generates a print-ready PDF using the template matching the PO `type`.
- **FR-PO-09** — The PDF shall reproduce the existing JCE layout (header, PO No., date, supplier block, sales terms, line table, totals, notes, signatory block) so output is visually consistent with current forms.
- **FR-PO-10** — The PDF is regenerable and reflects the PO's current saved data; the file is downloadable and (optionally) stored as an Attachment.

#### 7.5.8 Status lifecycle

`Draft → For Approval → Approved → Sent → Closed`; plus `Cancelled` / `Void`.

- Number assigned on leaving Draft (FR-NUM-06).
- RFP can only be created from an Approved PO (7.7).
- Import Shipment record auto-created when an Import PO becomes Approved.

#### 7.5.9 Acceptance criteria

- Selecting a supplier populates TIN, address, contact, email, and default terms with no retyping.
- A local PO with line totals summing to 60,880 shows Net 54,357.14 and VAT 6,522.86.
- Switching `type` to Import hides VAT, shows Incoterm/Delivery Period/Warranty, and switches currency to USD.
- Generated PDF matches the corresponding template and lists configured signatories.

### 7.6 PO Monitoring Ledger

A searchable, filterable register of all POs.

#### 7.6.1 Columns

PO No. · Type (Local/Import) · Date · Project · Supplier · Description/summary · Amount (with currency) · Status · Payment Status · SO No. · MR No. · (Import) link to Shipment Tracker.

#### 7.6.2 Requirements

- **FR-LED-01** — Default view groups/collapses by Project.
- **FR-LED-02** — A `Local` / `Import` / `All` toggle filters the list.
- **FR-LED-03** — Filters: project, supplier, status, payment status, date range, amount range, type.
- **FR-LED-04** — Full-text search across PO No., supplier, project, MR No., SO No., and item descriptions.
- **FR-LED-05** — Each row links to the PO detail; Import rows additionally link to their Import Shipment tracker.
- **FR-LED-06** — Payment Status is **derived (not manually typed)**: `Unpaid / Downpayment Paid / Fully Paid`, computed from linked RFPs and (for imports) the shipment's payment-stage dates.
- **FR-LED-07** — Ledger is exportable (CSV/Excel) respecting active filters.
- **FR-LED-08** — Sortable by any column; paginated for large datasets.

#### 7.6.3 Acceptance criteria

- Filtering by Project shows only that project's POs across both types.
- An import PO row opens its 15-stage tracker in one click.
- Payment Status updates automatically when an RFP or balance payment is recorded.

### 7.7 Request For Payment (RFP)

Field layout below is taken from the actual JCE Request For Payment Form (`RPF-blank.xlsx`). The form has two zones: an **Originator zone** (filled by Purchasing) and an **Accounting zone**. Company header on the form: `JC Electrofields Power System, Inc., 2129 Lamesa St., Ugong, Valenzuela`.

#### 7.7.1 Numbering

- **FR-RFP-01** — RFP number format is `RFP-PUR-#####` (continuous running sequence; `PUR` denotes Purchasing). Example from form: `RFP-PUR-11878`. Unlike PO numbers, this is a **single continuous counter** (no monthly reset, no Local/Import split). The form labels this field "Accounting use" — confirm whether the system auto-assigns it on submit or Accounting enters it on receipt; **default assumption: system auto-assigns `RFP-PUR-#####` on submission and it is read-only thereafter.**

> ⚠️ **Format discrepancy flagged.** The Payable Voucher artifact scoped in 5.5 showed `RFP-PUR-26020188` (which parses naturally as year-month-prefixed: `2602` + `0188`). The Purchasing source doc says 5-digit continuous (`RFP-PUR-11878`). Two possible interpretations: (a) the format evolved from year-month-prefixed to continuous, or (b) the PV artifact's `26020188` is a continuous 8-digit number that happens to _look_ year-month-prefixed. Confirm which format is canonical going forward. The numbering implementation needs to honor whichever Bryan selects.

#### 7.7.2 Trigger & linkage

- **FR-RFP-02** — An RFP can only be created from a PO in **Approved** status; it auto-links to that PO and inherits Project, Supplier, TIN, Address, Payment Terms, and PO No.
- **FR-RFP-03** — Import POs generate **two RFPs**: a `Downpayment RFP` and a `Balance RFP`, each tied to the corresponding payment date in the Import Shipment tracker (stages 7 and 10). Local POs typically generate a single RFP.

#### 7.7.3 Header fields (Originator zone)

| Field                     | Type               | Source / Notes                     |
| ------------------------- | ------------------ | ---------------------------------- |
| RFP No. (`RFP-PUR-#####`) | string (auto)      | 7.7.1; "Accounting use" block      |
| Date                      | date               | RFP creation date                  |
| Project                   | FK → Project       | from parent PO                     |
| Purchase Order No.        | FK → PurchaseOrder | the approved PO                    |
| Supplier                  | from PO            | name                               |
| TIN                       | from PO            |                                    |
| Address                   | from PO            |                                    |
| Payment Terms             | from PO            |                                    |
| Date Delivered            | date               | when goods/services were delivered |
| Due Date                  | date               | payment due date (drives alerts)   |

#### 7.7.4 Line items

| Column          | Type    | Notes                             |
| --------------- | ------- | --------------------------------- |
| Reference no.   | string  | references PO line / DR / invoice |
| QTY             | decimal |                                   |
| UOM             | string  | unit of measure                   |
| Particulars     | text    | description of what is being paid |
| Unit Price      | money   |                                   |
| VAT Payment     | money   | amount under the VAT-able column  |
| Non-VAT Payment | money   | amount under the non-VAT column   |

A `"NOTHING FOLLOWS"` terminator prints after the last line.

#### 7.7.5 Totals & withholding tax (EWT)

The form computes expanded withholding tax by payment classification, then a Net Payment:

| Classification   | EWT rate (per form) |
| ---------------- | ------------------- |
| Goods            | 1% (0.01)           |
| Services         | 2% (0.02)           |
| Rentals          | 5% (0.05)           |
| Professional Fee | 5% / 10%            |

- **FR-RFP-04** — The RFP shall total the `VAT Payment` and `Non-VAT Payment` columns separately, apply the applicable EWT rate(s) by classification, and compute `Net Payment = Total − withholding tax`, for both VAT and Non-VAT columns.
- **FR-RFP-05** — The system shall render the `Amount in words` for the net payment.
- **FR-RFP-06** — EWT rates shall be **configurable (admin lookup)** since tax rules change; the values above are the current defaults. **This is the canonical source for ATC withholding-tax handling**; the Payable Voucher (5.5) inherits the computed withholding from the RFP rather than re-computing.

#### 7.7.6 Signatory blocks (print only — offline signing)

- **Originator**: Prepared by · Verified & Authorized by.
- **Accounting**: Received by · Checked by.

_Names on the sample: Prepared by Dianna Delos Reyes; Verified & Authorized by Phoebe C. Allapitan / Jocen C. Canitan; Accounting Received by Madoneth Santos; Checked by Riza Capillo / Grace Irlandez._ Per FR-PO-06, these names/positions are admin-configurable, not hard-coded.

#### 7.7.7 Requirements

- **FR-RFP-07** — Status lifecycle: `Draft → Submitted → Verified/Authorized → Received (Accounting) → Paid`. Date fields stamp at each transition (the form has a Date line under each signatory).
- **FR-RFP-08** — The downpayment + balance amounts of an import PO's RFPs should **reconcile to the PO grand total** (warn if they do not).
- **FR-RFP-09** — Recording an RFP as `Paid` updates the parent PO's derived Payment Status (FR-LED-06) and the matching import stage date (7.8, stages 7/10).
- **FR-RFP-10** — RFP generates a print-ready PDF matching the JCE form layout (header, originator/accounting zones, line table, EWT totals, amount in words, signatory blocks).
- **FR-RFP-11** — Attachments supported (e.g., proof of payment, supporting invoices).

#### 7.7.8 Acceptance criteria

- Creating an RFP from a draft PO is blocked; from an approved PO it succeeds and is pre-filled with PO header data.
- The RFP number issues as the next `RFP-PUR-#####`.
- VAT and Non-VAT columns total separately; applying the Goods classification deducts 1% EWT and yields the correct Net Payment.
- An import PO yields two linked RFPs whose net amounts reconcile to the grand total.
- Marking the balance RFP `Paid` flips the PO's payment status to `Fully Paid` and stamps import stage 10.

### 7.8 Imported-PO Tracker (15-Stage Engine)

Auto-created when an Import PO is approved. Models the full import lifecycle.

#### 7.8.1 Stage model

Each `ImportShipmentStage` has: stage number, name, owner, status (`Pending / In Progress / Done / Blocked`), date (target and/or actual), document slots, and remarks.

#### 7.8.2 The 15 stages

| #   | Stage                                                   | Owner           | Document slot(s)                                                   |
| --- | ------------------------------------------------------- | --------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| 1   | BOM (Bill of Materials)                                 | BDD / PMG       | BOM                                                                |
| 2   | MR (Material Request)                                   | PMG             | MR                                                                 |
| 3   | Purchase Order + Supplier Quotation                     | Purchasing      | PO, Quotation                                                      |
| 4   | Approval of Sir Jimwel                                  | Sir Jimwel      | —                                                                  |
| 5   | Request Performa Invoice (for payment processing)       | Supplier        | Performa Invoice                                                   |
| 6   | Technical Specification & Drawing (Purchasing approves) | Supplier        | Drawing / Spec                                                     |
| 7   | JCE Approval + Downpayment                              | JCE             | RFP / proof of payment                                             |
| 8   | Production                                              | Supplier        | —                                                                  |
| 9   | Delivery Lead Time                                      | Supplier        | —                                                                  |
