| 10  | Balance Payment                                         | JCE             | RFP / proof of payment                                             |
| 11  | Shipping Documents (arrived point of origin)            | Supplier        | Bill of Lading (ETD+ETA), Packing List, Commercial Invoice, Form E |
| 12  | Departure                                               | Transit/Carrier | —                                                                  |
| 13  | Arrival                                                 | Transit/Carrier | —                                                                  |
| 14  | Customs Clearance                                       | JCE / Broker    | clearance docs                                                     |
| 15  | Delivery Onsite                                         | JCE             | Delivery Receipt                                                   |

#### 7.8.3 Requirements

- **FR-IMP-01** — On Import PO approval, the system creates the shipment with all 15 stages in `Pending`.
- **FR-IMP-02** — Each stage independently records `status`, `target date`, `actual date`, `owner`, `remarks`, and `attachments`. Stages are **not strictly sequential** — Production (8) and Delivery Lead Time (9) may overlap, and Shipping Documents (11) arrive incrementally; the model must allow concurrent in-progress stages.
- **FR-IMP-03** — Stage 11 supports four distinct document types (`BL`, `Packing List`, `Commercial Invoice`, `Form E`), with `ETD` and `ETA` captured on the Bill of Lading.
- **FR-IMP-04** — A per-shipment progress indicator reflects completed vs. total stages.
- **FR-IMP-05** — Alerts fire on: approaching ETA (configurable lead, e.g., 7/3/1 days), due/overdue balance payment (stage 10), and stages stalled in `Blocked` beyond a threshold. **Approval gates (stages 4 and 7) are the primary stall points and should surface prominently.**
- **FR-IMP-06** — Payment dates entered here reconcile with linked RFPs (7.7) and feed the ledger's payment status (7.6).
- **FR-IMP-07** — Free-text `Remarks` per stage (the current sheet relies heavily on remarks like `AWAITING BALANCE PAYMENT`, `HOLD`, `DELIVERED - 5/20/26`).

#### 7.8.4 Acceptance criteria

- Approving an import PO spawns a 15-stage tracker.
- Uploading a Bill of Lading with ETD/ETA populates those dates and drives the ETA alert.
- Recording the balance payment marks stage 10 `Done` and updates ledger payment status.

### 7.9 Supplier CRUD

Purchasing 7.9 is the **authoritative owner of the Supplier master entity** — superseding the preliminary Supplier fields introduced in BDD 9.5 Quotation. BDD 9.5's Supplier references should be retargeted to read from this section's schema.

#### 7.9.1 Fields (from `JCE Supplier List New.xlsx`)

| Field                  | Type                   | Required    | Notes                                                 |
| ---------------------- | ---------------------- | ----------- | ----------------------------------------------------- |
| Supplier Code          | string (auto)          | system      | format `JCE 0000X`; gaps allowed (file skips `00007`) |
| Supplier Name          | string                 | yes         |                                                       |
| TOP (Payment Terms)    | ref → PaymentTerm      | yes         | **needs standardisation — see 7.9.3**                 |
| TIN                    | string                 | conditional | PH TIN format `000-000-000-000`                       |
| City                   | string                 | optional    |                                                       |
| Country                | string                 | yes         | default `Philippines`                                 |
| Zip Code               | string                 | optional    |                                                       |
| Complete Address       | string                 | yes         |                                                       |
| Warehouse Address      | string                 | optional    |                                                       |
| Contact First Name     | string                 | optional    |                                                       |
| Contact Last Name      | string                 | optional    |                                                       |
| Email                  | string                 | optional    | validate format; allow `No Email` → empty             |
| Mobile No.             | string                 | optional    |                                                       |
| Landline               | string                 | optional    |                                                       |
| Category               | ref → SupplierCategory | yes         | currently empty in file — populate via dropdown       |
| Item / Service Offered | string/tags            | yes         | e.g., `PVC PIPE`, `STEEL & I-BEAM`                    |
| Active                 | boolean                | yes         | default `true` (soft delete)                          |

#### 7.9.2 Requirements

- **FR-SUP-01** — Supplier Code auto-generates as next `JCE 0000X`; gaps from deleted/skipped records are acceptable and not back-filled.
- **FR-SUP-02** — Full CRUD with **soft delete** (Active flag) — suppliers referenced by POs are never hard-deleted.
- **FR-SUP-03** — **Duplicate guard**: warn on create/edit when Name or TIN matches an existing supplier.
- **FR-SUP-04** — Email format validation; phone fields accept the existing messy formats but should be normalised where feasible.
- **FR-SUP-05** — **Passive performance stats per supplier**, computed from linked POs: total spend, number of POs, on-time delivery % (from import stages / delivery dates), last order date.
- **FR-SUP-06** — Supplier list is searchable/filterable by Category, Item/Service, Country/City, Active status, and payment terms.
- **FR-SUP-07** — Selecting a supplier in PO creation pulls its current details (7.5.1).

#### 7.9.3 Data-quality requirements

- **FR-SUP-08** — The free-text TOP/payment-terms values in the source file are inconsistent (e.g., `30 DAYS`, `30 DAYS PDC`, `30 PDC`, `PDC 30DAYS`; `50 DP/ 50 COD` vs `50 DP/50 COD`; `90 PDC PDC`). The system shall define a controlled `PaymentTerm` list and map legacy values to it during migration.
- **FR-SUP-09** — The Category column is empty in source data; a controlled `SupplierCategory` list shall be defined and existing suppliers categorised (can be derived/seeded from "Item/Service Offered").

#### 7.9.4 Acceptance criteria

- Creating a supplier assigns the next `JCE 0000X` code.
- A supplier with linked POs cannot be hard-deleted, only deactivated.
- Payment terms are chosen from a controlled list, not free-typed.

### 7.10 Audit Log (Department-Scoped)

- **FR-AUD-01** — Capture every state-changing action within the Purchasing module: PO create/edit/status-change/void, RFP create/edit/status-change, supplier create/edit/deactivate, import stage updates, and document uploads/deletes.
- **FR-AUD-02** — Each entry records: actor (user), action, entity type + identifier, timestamp, and before/after values (or a diff) where applicable.
- **FR-AUD-03** — Entries are **immutable (append-only)**; no edit or delete by any role.
- **FR-AUD-04** — Scope is **Purchasing-only** (does not surface other departments' activity). Consistent with the per-module audit-log pattern established in BDD 9.6; a future cross-module unified audit log was flagged as a v2 enhancement.
- **FR-AUD-05** — Viewable with filters by user, date range, entity type, and action; exportable.
- **FR-AUD-06** — Retention policy: **time-bounded with archival** (configurable live window, then cold-storage archive), per the system-wide decision (NFR-AUDIT-02 / §7.13 #3).

### 7.11 Cross-Cutting Requirements

#### 7.11.1 Roles & permissions

- **FR-X-01** — Role-based access. Roles: **Purchasing Staff** (create/edit POs, RFPs, suppliers), **Purchasing Supervisor** (verify/approve internally), **Finance/AP** (RFP/payment), **Management/President** (read + oversight), **Admin** (configure signatories, lookups, users). The full role × action matrix is consolidated in the system-wide RBAC (Section 3).

#### 7.11.2 Attachments / document repository

- **FR-X-02** — Files attach to POs, RFPs, and import stages. Accept PDF, images, common office formats. Store with metadata (uploader, timestamp, type). Stage-11 import docs (BL, Packing List, Commercial Invoice, Form E) are **first-class slots**, not generic attachments.

#### 7.11.3 Notifications & alerts

- **FR-X-03** — In-app (and optionally email) alerts for: POs pending internal approval beyond N days, RFP/balance payments due or overdue, import ETA approaching, blocked import stages, missing import documents.

#### 7.11.4 Dashboard

- **FR-X-04** — A Purchasing landing dashboard surfacing: POs awaiting approval, balances due this week, shipments arriving soon, shipments missing documents, and spend snapshots.

#### 7.11.5 Reporting & analytics

- **FR-X-05** — Spend analytics by project, supplier, and month; Local vs Import split; supplier performance leaderboard. Reuses linked PO/RFP/shipment data.

#### 7.11.6 Currency / FX

- **FR-X-06** — Import POs store the foreign-currency amount and the currency code. For consolidated PHP reporting, capture an FX rate (**snapshot at PO date or payment date — confirm policy**) so spend can be reported in PHP alongside local POs.

#### 7.11.7 Non-functional (light — SRS to expand)

Concurrency-safe numbering (FR-NUM-04); audit immutability (FR-AUD-03); responsive web UI consistent with the rest of the JCE dashboard; data migration from the existing Excel files (suppliers, open POs, in-flight import shipments).

### 7.12 Resolved Decisions

1. **PO sequence reset** — resets monthly. (FR-NUM-02)
2. **Local vs Import counter** — separate counters per month. (FR-NUM-03)
3. **Numbering period basis** — derived from PO date. (FR-NUM-01)
4. **Linking keys** — Project / SO No. / MR No. are foreign keys to the shared JCE modules. (7.3.1)
5. **RFP form** — confirmed against `RPF-blank.xlsx`; fields, EWT logic, and numbering captured. (7.7)
6. **Signatory chains** — confirmed; per-template and admin-configurable. (7.5.6, 7.7.6)
7. **Approval model** — offline wet signatures; the system tracks status only.
8. **Import tracker** — full 15-stage engine confirmed. (7.8)
9. **Supplier ownership** — Purchasing 7.9 is authoritative; BDD 9.5 references.
10. **RFP numbering format — RESOLVED (finalization sweep): Bryan confirmed `RFP-PUR-YYMM####`.** Per the v1.57 review (Refinement #10), the recommended canonical format is **`RFP-PUR-YYMM####`** (year-month-prefixed, matching the PV 5.5 artifact's `RFP-PUR-26020188` which parses as `2602 + 0188`). Rationale: aligns with PO numbering convention (`YYMM####`); makes period filtering trivial; provides natural growth headroom. The form sample showing `RFP-PUR-11878` is likely either a legacy 5-digit continuous artifact (pre-format-change) or a captured snapshot from before the YYMM convention was adopted. **Confirmed by Bryan in the finalization sweep.** Once confirmed, this supersedes the FR-RFP-01 wording ("continuous running sequence"); FR-RFP-01 will be updated to the year-month-prefixed format.
11. **Ten v1.57 review refinements adopted** (v1.58) — Goods Receipt + Three-Way Matching (Phase 1, 7.15.1), SupplierBankAccount sub-entity (Phase 1, 7.15.2), Purchase Requisition (Phase 1, 7.15.3), Amount-Threshold Approval Routing (Phase 1, 7.15.4), Local PO 5-stage Tracker (Phase 1, 7.15.5), Blanket POs (Phase 2, 7.15.6), Supplier Merge Tool (Phase 1.5, 7.15.7), Item Taxonomy / Category Tree (Phase 2 refinement, 7.15.8), and Project Entity Unification as a Section 6 dependency (7.15.9).

### 7.13 Open Questions for SRS Finalization _(resolved)_

1. ✅ **FX policy — RESOLVED: dual snapshot (PO-date + payment-date).** Import (USD) POs capture the FX rate at PO date (commitment value) and at payment date (actual cost); the difference is reported as FX variance. (FR-X-06)
2. ✅ **Role matrix — RESOLVED in §3.** The Purchasing roles — Purchasing Staff, Purchasing Supervisor, Finance/AP, Management/President, and Admin — and their per-action permissions are consolidated in the system-wide RBAC matrix (Section 3). (FR-X-01)
3. ✅ **Audit retention — RESOLVED: time-bounded with archival.** Entries stay in the live store for a configurable window (e.g., 3–5 years) then archive to cold storage. (FR-AUD-06) _(Reconciled: BDD §9.6 updated to time-bounded + archival.)_
4. ✅ **Migration scope — RESOLVED: full history (system-wide policy).** All historical records migrate (≈970 import-monitoring rows, ≈145 suppliers, full PO/RFP/collection history, etc.). _Implication: a heavier, carefully-validated migration with data-cleansing — see the migration note in the consistency pass._ (7.11.7)
5. ✅ **RFP number assignment — RESOLVED: auto-assign on submit.** The system assigns the next `RFP-PUR-...` number when Purchasing submits the RFP from an approved PO. (FR-RFP-01)
6. ✅ **RFP number format — RESOLVED: `RFP-PUR-YYMM####`** (year-month-prefixed), matching the PO convention and the PV 5.5 artifact; supersedes the 5-digit continuous form sample. (FR-RFP-01)
7. ✅ **Project entity scope** — **RESOLVED in v1.59 by Section 6.3.2.** The `Project` entity is the **unifying parent**: `project_type = Customer` (Sales-Order-backed) or `Internal Cost Center` (`WORKSHOP`, `JCE STOCK`). Purchasing's `Project` FK now targets Section 6.3.2; legacy `N/A`-SO rows use validated-fallback mode until migrated. Realizes the structure proposed in 7.15.9.
8. ✅ **Material Request entity scope** — **RESOLVED in v1.59 by Section 6.10.** `MaterialRequest` (MR No.) is owned by PMG; Purchasing's `MR No.` soft-FK becomes a **hard FK** to Section 6.10 records. The import-tracker's Stage 2 (MR) references this same entity.
9. ✅ **Supplier bank accounts** — **RESOLVED in v1.58** via Section 7.15.2. `SupplierBankAccount` added as 1..N sub-entity under Supplier with full fraud-control workflow (Supervisor approval + email confirmation on bank changes). Also resolves PV 5.5 open item #9.

### 7.14 Phase 2 — Extended Scope

These features build on the Phase 1 modules above and are **approved for inclusion as a second phase**. Each depends on Phase 1 data (POs, suppliers, RFPs, import stages) already existing.

#### 7.14.0 New entities introduced in Phase 2

| Entity                      | Purpose                                 | Key relationships                                                       |
| --------------------------- | --------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **RFQ**                     | Request for Quotation header (pre-PO)   | references MR/Project; → many RFQLineItems; → many Quotations           |
| **RFQLineItem**             | An item being quoted                    | belongs to 1 RFQ; may reference an Item                                 |
| **Quotation**               | One supplier's response to an RFQ       | belongs to 1 RFQ + 1 Supplier; → QuotationLineItems                     |
| **QuotationLineItem**       | Supplier's price/terms for one RFQ line | belongs to 1 Quotation                                                  |
| **Item (Material Catalog)** | Reusable item/material master           | → ItemPriceHistory; → preferred Supplier(s); referenced by PO/RFQ lines |
| **ItemPriceHistory**        | Price paid/quoted for an item over time | belongs to 1 Item + 1 Supplier; sourced from PO line or Quotation       |
| **ProjectBudget**           | Budget allocation for a project         | references 1 Project (optionally by category/period)                    |

#### 7.14.1 RFQ / Quotation Management (pre-PO)

Lets Purchasing solicit and compare quotes before raising a PO. Directly satisfies import stage 3 ("PO with Quotation from Supplier") and gives locals the same rigor. **Note: this is distinct from BDD 9.5's existing Quotation sub-tab** — BDD 9.5 tracks quotations as part of the bid/pricing reconnaissance lifecycle; Purchasing's RFQ here is the procurement-side solicitation cycle. The two may share the underlying Supplier and Item catalog but have different lifecycles and ownership.

- **FR-RFQ-01** — Create an RFQ from a Material Request (or standalone), with one or more line items (item, qty, UOM, spec/notes) and a target response date.
- **FR-RFQ-02** — Invite multiple suppliers to the same RFQ; record each supplier's quote: unit price per line, currency, lead time, payment terms, validity date, and notes. Supplier responses may be keyed by staff or uploaded as attachments.
- **FR-RFQ-03** — **Side-by-side comparison matrix**: rows = items, columns = suppliers, showing unit/extended price, lead time, and terms; auto-highlight lowest price and shortest lead time per line and overall.
- **FR-RFQ-04** — **Award & convert**: select a winning quotation and convert it into a draft PO (prefilling supplier, line items, prices, terms, Project/SO/MR links). If the awarded quote is not the lowest, **capture a justification note (required)**.
- **FR-RFQ-05** — RFQ status lifecycle: `Draft → Sent → Responses In → Awarded → Converted → Closed`. The award decision (who, when, why) is written to the audit log.
- **FR-RFQ-06** — One RFQ can spawn multiple POs (e.g., split award across suppliers).

_Acceptance:_ An RFQ to three suppliers produces a comparison matrix; awarding one creates a prefilled draft PO and logs the awarder + justification.

#### 7.14.2 Item / Material Catalog

A reusable master of recurring items (drill bits, spray paint, cable, transformers) to speed and standardize PO/RFQ entry.

- **FR-ITM-01** — CRUD for catalog items: `item code`, `name`, `standard description`, `default UOM`, `category`, `preferred supplier(s)`, `last price + currency`, `active flag`.
- **FR-ITM-02** — PO and RFQ line entry can pick from the catalog (auto-fills description, UOM, last price) or enter an ad-hoc line.
- **FR-ITM-03** — Ad-hoc lines can be **promoted to catalog items in one action**, so the catalog grows from real usage.
- **FR-ITM-04** — Items link to one or more preferred suppliers; the catalog is searchable by code, name, category, and supplier.

_Acceptance:_ Selecting a catalog item on a PO line fills description/UOM/last price; a new ad-hoc item can be saved to the catalog.

#### 7.14.3 Price History & Price-Change Flags

- **FR-PRC-01** — Every PO line and every accepted quotation writes an `ItemPriceHistory` record (item, supplier, unit price, currency, source ref, date).
- **FR-PRC-02** — Display a **price trend per item** (and per item × supplier) over time.
- **FR-PRC-03** — When a new quote or PO line unit price exceeds the last paid price for that item by more than a **configurable threshold (e.g., 5%)**, flag/warn the user at entry.
- **FR-PRC-04** — Cross-currency comparisons use the FX policy (FR-X-06) to normalize before comparing.

_Acceptance:_ Entering a unit price 10% above the item's last paid price raises a visible warning; the item's history view shows the trend.

#### 7.14.4 BIR Withholding-Tax Reporting (PH compliance)

Builds on the RFP's existing EWT computation (7.7.5).

- **FR-BIR-01** — Generate a **BIR Form 2307** (Certificate of Creditable Tax Withheld) per supplier per payment, from RFP EWT data.
- **FR-BIR-02** — Generate a periodic **withholding summary / alphalist** (per supplier, per ATC code, per period) for filing.
- **FR-BIR-03** — Map each RFP payment classification (Goods/Services/Rentals/Professional Fee) to the correct BIR ATC code and rate; rates/codes are configurable (per FR-RFP-06).
- **FR-BIR-04** — Export in a BIR-acceptable format (Excel and/or the DAT/alphalist format).
- **FR-BIR-05** — Track 2307 issuance status per supplier per period.

_Phase 2 open question:_ confirm exact ATC codes and the required export/file format with JCE's accountant.

_Acceptance:_ A paid RFP with Goods classification produces a 2307 with 1% EWT; the period summary aggregates withholding by supplier and ATC code.

#### 7.14.5 Lead-Time Auto-Calculation & Supplier Reliability

Turns the free-text remarks in the importation monitor (`30 DAYS AFTER DOWN PAYMENT`, `45 DAYS AFTER RECEIPT`, `60 DAYS`) into computed, trackable dates.

- **FR-LT-01** — Capture lead time as structured data: a **trigger event** (after PO, after downpayment, after full payment, after receipt of payment) + number of days, in addition to free-text remarks.
- **FR-LT-02** — **Compute expected dates** (e.g., expected production complete, expected delivery, expected ETA) from the trigger event's actual date + lead days.
- **FR-LT-03** — Compare actual import-stage dates against expected dates and flag variances (`early` / `on-time` / `late`).
- **FR-LT-04** — Maintain a **supplier reliability score** from completed POs/shipments: on-time %, average delay (days), number of orders.
- **FR-LT-05** — Surface the reliability score in the supplier record (7.9) and during supplier selection in RFQ/PO so buyers see it before committing.

_Acceptance:_ A PO with "60 days after downpayment" and a recorded downpayment date shows a computed expected delivery date; a late delivery lowers that supplier's on-time %.

#### 7.14.6 Budget vs. Actual per Project

- **FR-BUD-01** — Define a budget per project (amount + currency), optionally broken down by category and/or period. Source may be manual entry or imported from the Projects/Sales module.
- **FR-BUD-02** — Compute, per project: **committed spend** (approved + open POs) and **actual spend** (paid RFPs), with **remaining budget**.
- **FR-BUD-03** — When a new PO would push committed spend over the remaining budget, **warn the buyer**; whether this is a soft warning or a hard block (with role-based override) is configurable.
- **FR-BUD-04** — A budget-utilization view/dashboard per project (budget, committed, actual, remaining, % used).

_Phase 2 open questions:_ (a) budget source — manual vs. fed from another module; (b) over-budget behavior — warn vs. hard block, and who can override.

_Acceptance:_ Raising a PO that exceeds a project's remaining budget triggers the configured warning/block; the dashboard reflects committed and actual spend.

#### 7.14.7 Mobile Approvals (lightweight digital decision)

Speeds up the two biggest stall points (import stages 4 & 7; PO status `For Approval`). Reconciles with the offline-signing decision: this captures the approval **decision** digitally for workflow/audit, while the printed PDF is still wet-signed offline.

- **FR-APP-01** — A responsive approval queue for authorized approvers (e.g., Sir Jimwel, JCE) listing items awaiting approval: POs in `For Approval` and import stages 4 (Jimwel) and 7 (JCE).
- **FR-APP-02** — Approver can `Approve` / `Hold` / `Reject` with an optional note; the decision, actor, and timestamp are recorded.
- **FR-APP-03** — A decision updates the PO/stage status and the ledger, and writes an audit entry (7.10).
- **FR-APP-04** — Notification (push/email) on a pending approval, with a reminder after N days (ties to FR-X-03).
- **FR-APP-05** — Uses existing secure user login; **this is a workflow decision, not an electronic signature** — physical signing remains offline per the Phase 1 decision.

_Acceptance:_ An approver opens the queue on a phone, approves a PO with a note, and the PO moves to `Approved` with an audit entry; the shipment's stage 4 unblocks.

#### 7.14.8 Cycle-Time Analytics

- **FR-CYC-01** — Record an `entered`/`exited` timestamp per import stage, derived from status transitions.
- **FR-CYC-02** — Compute duration per stage for each shipment.
- **FR-CYC-03** — Aggregate average/median duration per stage across shipments and identify bottleneck stages.
- **FR-CYC-04** — Filter analytics by supplier, project, period, and item type.
- **FR-CYC-05** — Also track PO cycle time (`creation → approved → sent`) for local POs, not just imports.

_Acceptance:_ After several shipments, a report shows mean days per stage and highlights the slowest stage; filtering by supplier recomputes the figures.

### 7.15 Approved Refinements (v1.58)

> **Context.** During the v1.56 cross-module wiring review, ten refinements were proposed beyond the source requirements document and approved by Bryan for inclusion. Eight are scoped here as Section 7.15 (with new requirement-code prefixes); Refinement #5 (Project entity unification) is folded into Section 6 placeholder since it's a Project Management dependency; Refinement #10 (RFP numbering format lock) is captured as a Resolved Decision in 7.12 with the recommended convention.
>
> Phase tagging on each refinement below indicates whether it is committed for Phase 1 (alongside the original 7.4–7.11 scope) or sits in Phase 2 (alongside 7.14).

#### 7.15.1 Goods Receipt & Three-Way Matching _(Phase 1)_

**Rationale.** The original 7.4–7.11 scope had a significant control gap: the RFP can be issued against an Approved PO **before goods physically arrive**, and Local POs had no receipt-tracking step at all (only the 15-stage Import Tracker captures receipt, at Stage 15). Adding a GoodsReceipt entity closes the gap, makes three-way matching possible (PO ↔ Receipt ↔ Invoice), gives Inventory a clean stock-in trigger (Trial Balance shows ₱22M+ in Inventory at year-end), and prevents JCE from paying for goods that never arrived.

**New entity — GoodsReceipt:**

| Field                 | Notes                                                                                 |
| --------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `gr_no`               | Auto-assigned, format `GR-YY-XXXX` (yearly reset, separate counter from PO/RFP).      |
| `gr_date`             | Date goods were physically received.                                                  |
| `po_id`               | FK to PurchaseOrder.                                                                  |
| `po_line_id`          | FK to PurchaseOrderLineItem — receipts are tracked per line for partial deliveries.   |
| `received_qty`        | Decimal; cumulative across multiple GRs per line must not exceed PO line ordered qty. |
| `received_uom`        | Inherited from PO line.                                                               |
| `condition`           | Enum: `Good` · `Damaged` · `Damaged Accepted (discount)` · `Returned`.                |
| `received_by`         | FK to Employee (the receiver).                                                        |
| `delivery_receipt_no` | Supplier's delivery receipt number, if provided.                                      |
| `attachments[]`       | Delivery receipt, packing list, condition photos.                                     |
| `remarks`             | Free text.                                                                            |
| `status`              | `Recorded` · `Voided`.                                                                |

**Requirements (FR-GR):**

- **FR-GR-01** — GoodsReceipt entity exists with the fields above; format `GR-YY-XXXX` with yearly reset.
- **FR-GR-02** — A PO can have **multiple GoodsReceipts** to support partial deliveries; the system tracks cumulative received qty per PO line.
- **FR-GR-03** — Cumulative received qty shall **not exceed** PO line ordered qty (system enforces; tolerance configurable in Settings).
- **FR-GR-04** — Per-line "fully received" flag flips when cumulative received qty = ordered qty; PO-level "Fully Received" status flips when all lines are fully received.
- **FR-GR-05** — **Three-way match** required before RFP can transition from Draft to Submitted: PO ordered qty + Supplier invoice qty + GR received qty must reconcile within configured tolerance (default 1% qty variance, ₱100 amount variance).
- **FR-GR-06** — Tolerance values are admin-configurable in Settings.
- **FR-GR-07** — Variance beyond tolerance is **flagged for review** with role-based override (Purchasing Supervisor or above).
- **FR-GR-08** — Damaged or Returned goods can either be (a) excluded from received_qty (returned to supplier) or (b) recorded with `condition = Damaged Accepted` with a discount adjustment that flows to the RFP.
- **FR-GR-09** — Inventory stock-in posting auto-generated from GoodsReceipt for inventoriable items (active once Inventory module exists; placeholder in Phase 1).
- **FR-GR-10** — GoodsReceipt prints to a standard JCE layout for filing; PDF export available.

**Cross-module integrations:**

- **PO 7.5** — receipt status feeds the PO's "Fully Received" flag.
- **RFP 7.7** — three-way match gates RFP submission.
- **Import Tracker 7.8** — for Import POs, GoodsReceipt records consolidate Stage 15 (Delivery Onsite) data; the tracker's stage 15 + a GoodsReceipt entry are the same event, captured in both surfaces.
- **Inventory (future)** — GR triggers stock-in posting for inventoriable items.
- **Disbursement 5.6 / PV 5.5** — payment chain blocked until three-way match passes.
- **Project Costing 5.8 (deferred)** — direct materials cost recognized at GR rather than at PO (accrual-accounting alignment).

**Open items:**

1. **Returned-goods model** — separate `GoodsReturn` entity or negative GR? Recommend separate entity for clearer audit.
2. **Tolerance defaults** — 1% qty / ₱100 amount are guesses; confirm with Accounting.
3. **Acceptance responsibility** — does Purchasing accept goods at the warehouse, or does the requesting department (e.g., site engineer for project deliveries) accept on site? Recommend role-based acceptance with both supported.

#### 7.15.2 Supplier Bank Account Sub-Entity _(Phase 1)_

**Rationale.** Resolves PV 5.5 open item #9 and Purchasing 7.13 open item #9. Currently the PV captures recipient bank details (Bank, Account Number, Account Name) per-payment manually — wasteful, error-prone, and creates no audit trail when a supplier changes banks. Banking-detail changes are also a known fraud vector (supplier email-spoofing scams targeting bank changes are common in PH SME procurement), so they merit special controls.

**New entity — SupplierBankAccount:**

| Field                            | Notes                                                                                    |
| -------------------------------- | ---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `sba_id`                         | System ID.                                                                               |
| `supplier_id`                    | FK to Supplier (7.9).                                                                    |
| `bank_name`                      | FK to new **JCE Banks lookup** (see below).                                              |
| `account_number`                 | String; uniqueness validated per supplier × bank.                                        |
| `account_name`                   | Display name of the account holder.                                                      |
| `currency`                       | PHP / USD / etc.                                                                         |
| `swift_code`                     | Optional, for international wires.                                                       |
| `iban`                           | Optional, for European banks.                                                            |
| `is_primary`                     | One Primary per currency per supplier; toggling on a new account de-toggles the old one. |
| `effective_from`, `effective_to` | Validity dates.                                                                          |
| `active`                         | Soft-delete flag.                                                                        |
| `audit_fields`                   | Standard add/edit/deactivate trail.                                                      |

**New lookup — JCE Banks:**

| Field             | Notes                                                                |
| ----------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `bank_id`         | System ID.                                                           |
| `bank_name`       | E.g., `BDO`, `Land Bank of the Philippines`, `Security Bank`, `BPI`. |
| `swift_code`      | Optional.                                                            |
| `country`         | Default `Philippines`.                                               |
| `notes`, `active` | Standard lookup.                                                     |

**Requirements (FR-SBA):**

- **FR-SBA-01** — SupplierBankAccount as 1..N child of Supplier with fields above.
- **FR-SBA-02** — `account_number + bank_name` combination unique per supplier.
- **FR-SBA-03** — One Primary per currency per supplier; Primary toggle is auto-managed.
- **FR-SBA-04** — PV 5.5 input form sources recipient bank/account/name dropdown from this entity, filtered by payment currency, with Primary suggested first.
- **FR-SBA-05** — **Fraud control:** Adding or editing a supplier bank account requires Supervisor approval **plus** an email confirmation from a known supplier email on file (or a phone call logged in the audit trail) before the new account becomes payable-from.
- **FR-SBA-06** — Audit log captures every add/edit/deactivate with actor, timestamp, and before/after values.

**Cross-module integrations:**

- **PV 5.5** — recipient bank fields source from this entity instead of manual entry; resolves PV 5.5 open item #9.
- **Disbursement 5.6** — uses the same account selected on PV.

**Open items:**

1. **Migration** — none of the existing 145 suppliers currently have bank info captured; how to seed? Recommend a one-time bulk-import sheet that Purchasing fills as the system rolls out.
2. **Phone-call confirmation alternative** — when supplier email isn't available, accept a logged phone call with the supplier's accounting contact; confirm acceptable.

#### 7.15.3 Purchase Requisition (PR) — Pre-PO Budget Authorization _(Phase 1)_

**Rationale.** The original 7.4–7.11 flow goes `MR (Material Request) → PO`. But MR is an **operational** request ("we need 50m of cable for GARCIA 3"), while PO is a **financial** commitment ("we're spending ₱120K with Eslite for that cable"). The missing middle is the Purchase Requisition (PR) — the budget-authorization step where someone with spend authority approves committing JCE money. This becomes load-bearing for the Phase 2 Budget vs. Actual (FR-BUD-01 to 04) feature because the budget check needs to happen at PR time (before sourcing) rather than at PO time (after sourcing, too late to back out without supplier friction). The PR also creates a natural amount-threshold routing trigger (small PRs auto-approve, large PRs route to a manager queue — see 7.15.4).

> **Naming disambiguation.** The format `PRQ-YY-XXXX` is used here to avoid conflict with `JCE-PR` (the Accounting Payment Request worksheet number from 5.5). `PRQ` = Purchase **Req**uisition; `JCE-PR` = Accounting Payment **R**equest.

**New entity — PurchaseRequisition:**

| Field                        | Notes                                                                                                     |
| ---------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `prq_no`                     | Auto, format `PRQ-YY-XXXX` (yearly reset, continuous counter).                                            |
| `prq_date`                   | Submission date.                                                                                          |
| `requestor`                  | FK to Employee.                                                                                           |
| `project_id`                 | FK to Project (Section 6 future).                                                                         |
| `mr_no`                      | FK to upstream Material Request (Section 6 future).                                                       |
| `justification`              | Free-text rationale.                                                                                      |
| `estimated_total`            | Estimated PR amount in `currency`.                                                                        |
| `currency`                   | PHP / USD / etc.                                                                                          |
| `urgency`                    | `Routine` · `Urgent` · `Critical`. Drives notification escalation.                                        |
| `items[]`                    | Description, qty, UOM, estimated unit price, estimated line total. May reference catalog items (Phase 2). |
| `approval_chain[]`           | Computed at submission from 7.15.4 thresholds; ordered list of approvers.                                 |
| `current_step`               | Which step in the chain is active.                                                                        |
| `status`                     | `Draft` · `Pending Approval` · `Approved` · `Rejected` · `Converted to PO` · `Cancelled`.                 |
| `approved_at`, `approved_by` | Final approval.                                                                                           |
| `rejection_reason`           | If rejected.                                                                                              |
| `po_id`                      | If converted to PO.                                                                                       |

**Requirements (FR-REQ):**

- **FR-REQ-01** — PurchaseRequisition entity with fields above; format `PRQ-YY-XXXX`.
- **FR-REQ-02** — PR can be created from an MR (auto-prefills item list) or standalone (admin only).
- **FR-REQ-03** — Approval routing per 7.15.4 threshold table; chain is computed at Submit time.
- **FR-REQ-04** — Approved PR enables PO creation; the resulting PO inherits Project, MR, requestor, items from the PR.
- **FR-REQ-05** — **Phase 2 budget check at PR Submit** — when Phase 2 FR-BUD-XX is live, the PR Submit step queries the Project's remaining budget and warns/blocks per FR-BUD-03.
- **FR-REQ-06** — Status lifecycle as above; Rejected is terminal (a new PR must be raised to retry); Cancelled is terminal but distinguished from Rejected (Cancelled = requestor withdrew, Rejected = approver declined).
- **FR-REQ-07** — PR generates a print-ready PDF.
- **FR-REQ-08** — Audit log per state change.
- **FR-REQ-09** — Urgent / Critical urgency tags shorten approval SLA (default 4 hours for Critical vs 48 hours for Routine) and trigger notification escalation.
- **FR-REQ-10** — Items may reference Phase 2 catalog (FR-ITM-02) or be ad-hoc; ad-hoc items can be promoted to catalog post-approval (FR-ITM-03).

**Cross-module integrations:**

- **MR (Section 6 future)** — upstream operational request.
- **PO 7.5** — downstream commitment; PO has `prq_id` FK to source PR.
- **Project entity (Section 6 future)** — budget context.
- **Approval Threshold 7.15.4** — routing chain.
- **Budget vs Actual (Phase 2 FR-BUD)** — budget check at PR Submit.
- **Audit Log 7.10**.

**Open items:**

1. **PR optional for small POs?** Recommend mandatory for all POs ≥ ₱20K; below that threshold, MR → PO directly is acceptable. Confirm threshold.
2. **PR-to-PO conversion: one-to-one or one-to-many?** Recommend one PR can split into multiple POs (different suppliers for different items), but each PO traces back to exactly one PR.
3. **Urgency setting authority** — who can mark `Critical`? Recommend role-based: only Supervisor and above can set Critical, since it shortens SLA.

#### 7.15.4 Amount-Threshold Approval Routing _(Phase 1)_

**Rationale.** The original 7.4–7.11 status lifecycle treats every PO/PR/RFP the same (`Draft → For Approval → Approved → Sent`), but a ₱5K stationery PO and a ₱5M heavy-equipment PO should not route through the same approval chain. An amount-threshold table in Settings drives **multi-step approval chains by amount band**; the Mobile Approvals feature (Phase 2 FR-APP-XX) becomes much more powerful when each approver sees only items in their band.

**New lookup — ApprovalThreshold:**

| Field                      | Notes                                                                         |
| -------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `threshold_id`             | System ID.                                                                    |
| `entity_type`              | `PR` · `PO` · `RFP` · `Blanket PO` (each entity has its own threshold table). |
| `min_amount`, `max_amount` | Band boundaries (max_amount = null means "no upper limit").                   |
| `currency`                 | Independent tables per currency.                                              |
| `approver_role`            | FK to Role — role-based default.                                              |
| `approver_employee`        | FK to Employee — named-approver override (optional).                          |
| `order`                    | Sequential position in the chain (1 = first approver).                        |
| `active`                   | Soft-delete flag.                                                             |

**Example PO threshold table (illustrative defaults — to confirm with Bryan):**

| Band (PHP)          | Approval chain                                                                                   |
| ------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| 0 – 50,000          | Purchasing Supervisor                                                                            |
| 50,001 – 500,000    | Purchasing Supervisor → Finance/Admin Manager                                                    |
| 500,001 – 5,000,000 | Purchasing Supervisor → Finance/Admin Manager → President                                        |
| 5,000,001+          | Purchasing Supervisor → Finance/Admin Manager → President (with Board-of-Directors notification) |

**Requirements (FR-APV):**

- **FR-APV-01** — ApprovalThreshold lookup with fields above; configurable in admin Settings.
- **FR-APV-02** — Independent threshold tables per entity type (PR, PO, RFP, Blanket PO).
- **FR-APV-03** — Multi-step chains supported (an entity may require 1, 2, 3+ sequential approvals).
- **FR-APV-04** — Multi-currency supported (separate tables per currency, since approval bands in PHP vs USD differ).
- **FR-APV-05** — `approver_role` OR `approver_employee` — role-based default, with optional named-approver override.
- **FR-APV-06** — On entity Submit, the system queries the threshold table, builds the approval chain, and **stores the resolved chain on the record** (so subsequent threshold-table changes don't retroactively change in-flight approvals).
- **FR-APV-07** — Each step requires explicit `Approve` / `Reject` / `Hold`; reject sends the entity back to Draft with reason.
- **FR-APV-08** — **Delegate-when-absent** — an approver can configure a delegate for a date range ("Vacation Mode"); system routes to delegate during that period and logs both the absent approver and the delegate.
- **FR-APV-09** — **Per-user approval queue** showing all items awaiting their action across entity types, sorted by urgency and age.
- **FR-APV-10** — Approval decisions logged in Audit Log 7.10 with actor, timestamp, decision, and optional note.

**Cross-module integrations:**

- **PR 7.15.3, PO 7.5, RFP 7.7, Blanket PO 7.15.6** — all use this routing.
- **Mobile Approvals 7.14.7** — per-user queue is the surface.
- **Audit Log 7.10**.

**Open items:**

1. **Initial threshold bands** — the table above is a guess. Confirm bands and approver roles with Bryan.
2. **Vacation Mode requirement** — recommend mandatory configuration when an approver will be out 3+ days; confirm.
3. **Multi-approver step** — should some steps allow "any 1 of N" (e.g., Phoebe OR Jocen approves) versus strict sequential? Mirrors the JV "Riza Capillo / Grace Irlandez" alternate-checkers pattern.

#### 7.15.5 Local PO Progress Tracker (5-stage) _(Phase 1)_

**Rationale.** Local POs in the original 7.4–7.11 had only basic status (`Draft → For Approval → Approved → Sent → Closed`). A Local PO sitting in `Sent` for two months with no follow-up is invisible — there's no way to tell if goods arrived, if the invoice came in, if payment was due. A lightweight 5-stage tracker (parallel to the 15-stage Import Tracker) catches stalled POs and creates a clear visual progress indicator.

**New entity — LocalPOTracker:**

| Stage | Stage name           | Trigger                                                                                                   | Document slot(s)                    |
| ----- | -------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------- |
| 1     | **PO Sent**          | Status flips to `Sent` (PO transmitted to supplier)                                                       | Supplier acknowledgement (optional) |
| 2     | **Goods Received**   | First GoodsReceipt (7.15.1) posts; sub-status `Partial` until cumulative = ordered, then `Fully Received` | GoodsReceipts (FK to 7.15.1)        |
| 3     | **Invoice Received** | Supplier invoice arrives                                                                                  | Supplier invoice attachment         |
| 4     | **Paid**             | Linked RFP marked Paid                                                                                    | RFP reference                       |
| 5     | **Closed**           | Auto-advances when Paid + Fully Received; manual override allowed                                         | —                                   |

**Requirements (FR-LPO):**

- **FR-LPO-01** — 5-stage tracker auto-created on Local PO transition to `Approved`.
- **FR-LPO-02** — Stage 1 advances automatically on PO Send (status = Sent).
- **FR-LPO-03** — Stage 2 advances when first GoodsReceipt (7.15.1) posts; sub-status `Partial` / `Fully Received`.
- **FR-LPO-04** — Stage 3 captures supplier invoice number, date, and attachment.
- **FR-LPO-05** — Stage 4 advances automatically when linked RFP marked Paid.
- **FR-LPO-06** — Stage 5 auto-advances when Paid + Fully Received; manual override allowed by Supervisor.
- **FR-LPO-07** — Per-stage target date, actual date, owner, remarks.
- **FR-LPO-08** — Alerts on stages stalled beyond configurable threshold (default 14 days).
- **FR-LPO-09** — Default for all Local POs ≥ ₱20K; opt-in for smaller POs. Threshold configurable.

**Cross-module integrations:**

- **GoodsReceipt 7.15.1** — drives Stage 2.
- **RFP 7.7** — drives Stage 4.
- **PO Monitoring Ledger 7.6** — adds a "Current Stage" column for Local POs.
- **Cycle-Time Analytics 7.14.8** — Local PO cycle measurement now possible per FR-CYC-05.

**Open items:**

1. **Default threshold** — recommend ₱20K but open to alternate.
2. **Stage skipping** — should small Local POs (e.g., petty-cash purchases) be allowed to skip directly from PO Sent to Paid? Recommend no — even small POs benefit from the audit trail.

#### 7.15.6 Blanket Purchase Orders _(Phase 2)_

**Rationale.** Some supplies (office stationery, fuel for service vehicles, weekly cleaning supplies) are bought repeatedly from the same supplier under the same terms. Cutting a fresh PO each time generates approval overhead disproportionate to the routine nature of the purchase. A Blanket PO has a total ceiling and a draw-down mechanism — individual releases against the blanket are tracked but don't need fresh approval until the ceiling is hit.

**New entities — BlanketPO and BlanketRelease:**

| Entity             | Key fields                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                   |
| ------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **BlanketPO**      | `blanket_id`, `blanket_no` (format `BPO-YY-XXXX`), `supplier_id`, `project_id` (optional — could be operational/overhead blanket), `ceiling_amount`, `currency`, `start_date`, `end_date`, `payment_terms`, `items[]` (catalog ref or free description with unit-price ceiling), `approval_threshold_chain` (uses 7.15.4), `status` (`Draft` / `Active` / `Suspended` / `Exhausted` / `Expired` / `Closed`). |
| **BlanketRelease** | `release_id`, `blanket_id` (FK), `release_no` (auto, e.g., `R-001` per blanket), `release_date`, `release_amount`, `items_released[]`, `delivery_address`, `child_po_id` (FK — the auto-generated child PO that flows through normal lifecycle).                                                                                                                                                             |

**Requirements (FR-BLK):**

- **FR-BLK-01** — BlanketPO entity with ceiling-amount tracking; format `BPO-YY-XXXX`.
- **FR-BLK-02** — BlanketRelease as 1..N child of BlanketPO.
- **FR-BLK-03** — Cumulative released ≤ ceiling (system enforces; warns at 80% utilization; blocks at 100% unless override with supervisor sign-off).
- **FR-BLK-04** — Each Release auto-generates a **child PO** that flows through the normal Goods Receipt + RFP + payment lifecycle; the child PO carries the blanket reference but does not require fresh approval (parent's approval covers it).
- **FR-BLK-05** — Blanket PO approval uses 7.15.4 threshold based on `ceiling_amount` (so a ₱1M blanket goes through the same chain as a single ₱1M one-shot PO).
- **FR-BLK-06** — Status lifecycle `Draft → Active → Suspended/Exhausted/Expired/Closed`. Expired = past `end_date`; Exhausted = ceiling hit; Suspended = admin paused (e.g., supplier under review).
- **FR-BLK-07** — Expired or Exhausted blanket cannot accept new releases; admin must create a new Blanket PO (with fresh approval).
- **FR-BLK-08** — Blanket reporting: utilization %, average release size, release frequency, projected exhaustion date.

**Cross-module integrations:**

- **Item Catalog Phase 2 7.14.2** — blanket items reference catalog where possible.
- **Approval Threshold 7.15.4** — blanket gets approved per its ceiling amount.
- **PO Monitoring Ledger 7.6** — blankets + their child release POs both visible.
- **Spend Analytics FR-X-05** — blanket utilization is a key metric.

**Open items:**

1. **Auto-renewal of nearing-expiry blankets** — should the system auto-suggest renewal 30 days before expiry, or require fully-fresh creation each time? Recommend suggest-renewal with one-click "carry forward" that creates a new blanket and requires fresh approval.
2. **Multi-supplier blanket** — should one blanket support multiple suppliers (e.g., split fuel blanket across two stations)? Recommend single-supplier per blanket for clarity; multi-supplier needs are handled with multiple parallel blankets.

#### 7.15.7 Supplier Merge Tool _(Phase 1.5 — non-blocking)_

**Rationale.** 145 supplier records from the source `JCE Supplier List New.xlsx` almost certainly include duplicates (different spellings, different contact info for the same entity, formatting variations). The duplicate guard (FR-SUP-03) prevents _new_ duplicates but does nothing for the existing data. A Supplier Merge tool lets the admin consolidate duplicates by selecting a primary record and rolling all referenced records into it.

**Workflow:**

1. Admin clicks **"Find Duplicate Suppliers"** in Supplier admin.
2. System runs detection: TIN exact match + Name fuzzy match (Levenshtein distance ≤ N, normalized lowercase) + same email or phone.
3. System shows **suggested duplicate groups** (e.g., `ABB Inc.` + `ABB Hitachi` + `A.B.B.` — three records likely same).
4. Admin reviews each group, picks the **primary** record.
5. Admin previews what will be affected: count of POs, RFPs, Quotations, etc. referencing the duplicates.
6. Admin confirms; system atomically:
   - Repoints all FK references from duplicates → primary.
   - **Deactivates** (not deletes) duplicate records (preserves audit trail).
   - Logs the merge event with full before/after detail.
7. Merge is **reversible within 30 days** — admin can un-merge if the consolidation was wrong; system restores the deactivated records and repoints FKs.

**Requirements (FR-MRG):**

- **FR-MRG-01** — Duplicate detection algorithm: TIN exact + Name fuzzy (configurable Levenshtein threshold) + same email/phone.
- **FR-MRG-02** — Admin-only "Merge Suppliers" workflow (gated by Admin role).
- **FR-MRG-03** — Preview shows count of affected records (POs, RFPs, Quotations, etc.) before confirmation.
- **FR-MRG-04** — All FK references repoint atomically (transactional).
- **FR-MRG-05** — Duplicates **deactivated**, not deleted (audit preservation).
- **FR-MRG-06** — Merge event logged in Audit Log 7.10.
- **FR-MRG-07** — Merge reversible within **30 days**; reversal restores duplicates and repoints FKs.
- **FR-MRG-08** — Bulk merge mode: process multiple suggested groups in one session for initial post-migration cleanup.

**Cross-module integrations:**

- **Audit Log 7.10** — merge events permanently retained.
- **All entities referencing Supplier** — auto-repoint on merge.

**Open items:**

1. **Reversibility window** — 30 days reasonable? Confirm or adjust.
2. **Authority** — Admin role only, or also Purchasing Supervisor? Recommend Admin only since merge affects historical records.

#### 7.15.8 Item Taxonomy / Category Tree _(Phase 2 refinement of 7.14.2)_

**Rationale.** The Phase 2 Item Catalog (7.14.2) defines items but doesn't define their organization. As the catalog grows (potentially thousands of items), a flat list becomes unsearchable. A two-level category tree (`Category > Sub-category`) gives structure, drives spend analytics, and feeds the SupplierCategory lookup (FR-SUP-09) which is currently empty in source data.

**New lookup — ItemCategory:**

| Field                | Notes                                             |
| -------------------- | ------------------------------------------------- |
| `category_id`        | System ID.                                        |
| `category_code`      | Short code (e.g., `MEP-ELEC` for MEP Electrical). |
| `category_name`      | Display name (e.g., `Electrical Cable & Wire`).   |
| `parent_category_id` | Self-FK for hierarchy (null at root).             |
| `level`              | Computed (1 = root, 2 = sub-category).            |
| `description`        | Optional explanation.                             |
| `active`             | Soft-delete.                                      |

**Example tree:**

```
MEP (Mechanical/Electrical/Plumbing)
├── Plumbing
│   ├── PVC Pipes
│   ├── Fittings & Valves
│   └── Sanitary Fixtures
├── Electrical
│   ├── Cable & Wire
│   ├── Switchgear
│   └── Lighting
└── HVAC
    ├── Ducting
    └── Insulation
Office
├── Supplies (Stationery)
├── Equipment
└── Furniture
Vehicle
├── Fuel & Lubricants
├── Parts & Spares
└── Maintenance Services
```

**Requirements (extending FR-ITM):**

- **FR-ITM-05** — Items in the catalog (7.14.2) link to a `category_id`.
- **FR-ITM-06** — Two-level minimum (`Category > Sub-category`); three-level supported but not required.
- **FR-ITM-07** — Category drives **Spend Analytics drill-down** (FR-X-05): see spend by category, drill into sub-category, drill into items.
- **FR-ITM-08** — `SupplierCategory` (FR-SUP-09) is seeded from item categories: a supplier offering items in `MEP > Plumbing > PVC Pipes` gets the `MEP > Plumbing` category tag on their supplier record.

**Cross-module integrations:**

- **Spend Analytics FR-X-05** — category drill-down.
- **SupplierCategory FR-SUP-09** — now defined via item taxonomy rather than empty.

**Open items:**

1. **Three-level depth** — needed for `Category > Sub-category > Item Type`? Recommend optional, with most categories ending at two levels.
2. **BIR ATC code linkage** — some categories imply Goods vs Services classification; should the category carry a default ATC? Recommend yes, with per-item override on the catalog.

#### 7.15.9 Section 6 Project Management Dependency — Project Entity Unification

_(Project unification was Refinement #5 from the v1.57 review; it belongs in Section 6 Project Management when that module is scoped, but it's captured here as a confirmed direction.)_

**Confirmed direction:** when Section 6 Project Management is scoped, the **Project** entity shall be the unifying parent of both customer projects (backed by Sales Orders) and internal cost centers (`WORKSHOP`, `JCE STOCK`). The recommended structure:

| Field                    | Notes                                                                                                                                                                                                       |
| ------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| `project_id`             | System ID.                                                                                                                                                                                                  |
| `project_code`           | Display identifier; for `project_type = Customer`, matches the Sales Order No. (YY-MM-XXX); for `project_type = Internal Cost Center`, follows a parallel convention (e.g., `WORKSHOP`, `STOCK`, `OFFICE`). |
| `project_type`           | Enum: `Customer` · `Internal Cost Center`.                                                                                                                                                                  |
| `project_name`           | Display name.                                                                                                                                                                                               |
| `sales_order_id`         | FK to BDD 9.3 Sales Order — required when `project_type = Customer`; null when `Internal Cost Center`.                                                                                                      |
| `start_date`, `end_date` | Project timeline.                                                                                                                                                                                           |
| `status`                 | Active / On Hold / Completed / Cancelled.                                                                                                                                                                   |
| `budget` _(Phase 2)_     | Total budget allocation.                                                                                                                                                                                    |

This structure means **all cost-tracking, budgeting, and reporting keys off `project_id`** rather than scattering between SO# (for customer projects) and free-text labels (for cost centers). Cross-references throughout the SRS that currently say "SO# / project label" will collapse to "project_id" when Section 6 is scoped.

✅ **Realized in v1.59.** Section 6 has been scoped: the `Project` entity (6.3.2) implements exactly this structure (`project_id`, `project_code`, `project_type`, `sales_order_id` FK, `status`, etc.), and the full Material Request lifecycle is defined in 6.10. Cross-references that previously read "SO# / project label" now collapse to `project_id` per this direction.

---

## 8. Engineering Department Module _(placeholder)_

_This section will cover how the Engineering department uses the dashboard — the records and activities it manages, the tasks it performs, and the access its staff require._

> **Status: Pending stakeholder interview.** Requirements for the Engineering module have not yet been gathered. The structure below mirrors the HR module and will be completed after the Engineering department is interviewed. Nothing here should be treated as a committed requirement.

> **Cross-module dependency surfaced by Purchasing (v1.57).** Purchasing's Imported-PO Tracker (7.8) begins with **Stage 1: BOM (Bill of Materials)** with `BDD / PMG` listed as the owner. Engineering likely shares ownership or contributes to BOM generation — the technical specifications that drive what gets sourced internationally. Stage 6 (Technical Specification & Drawing) and the production-control review (Sir Jimwel's approval, Stage 4) also lean on engineering review. When this module is scoped, expect a BOM entity and a Technical Drawing review workflow that hand off into Purchasing's 15-stage import tracker.

**8.1 Module Overview** — _TBD: purpose of the Engineering module and the main areas it will contain._

**8.2 Feature Area 1** — _TBD: name, purpose, confirmed scope, expected behaviors, and pending information._

**8.3 Feature Area 2** — _TBD: name, purpose, confirmed scope, expected behaviors, and pending information._

**8.4 Roles & Permissions** — _TBD: which Engineering roles exist and what each may view and do (see Section 3)._

**8.5 Pending Information** — _TBD: list specific items to be provided by the client (formats, field lists, workflows)._

---

## 9. Business Development Department (BDD) Module _(finalized v1.65 — pending JCE artifacts)_

This section covers how the Business Development Department uses the dashboard. BDD owns the company's customer-facing pipeline — sales orders, offers, quotations — and also provides the source content for the public website's portfolio and product pages. The module is structured around five working areas plus its audit log, the standard roles & permissions, and pending-information subsections.

### 9.1 Module Overview

The BDD module covers five working areas plus its audit log:

- **Website Details** (9.2) — the content that powers the public website: showcase Projects, the Products and Major Services catalog, and Inquiries captured from the website.
- **Sales Order** (9.3) — the internal list of projects JCE is or has worked on, including project details, client, location, and timeline.
- **Offers** (9.4) — formal offers extended to clients. Create-and-archive only; no edits, but history is retained.
- **Quotation** (9.5) — itemized quotations with **multi-supplier comparison per line item**.
- **Audit Logs** (9.6) — record of changes within the BDD module.

The areas are interrelated: Sales Order entries are the canonical "Projects" list and feed both the Timekeeping module's Working Project dropdown (Section 4.2) and the OB / Travel form's Sales Order # field (Section 4.3.1); Website Details > Projects are a curated public-facing subset; Offers and Quotations sit upstream of a Sales Order being won.

### 9.2 Website Details _(fully scoped)_

**Purpose.** To house the content the **public website (Section 11) displays** — JCE's project portfolio, the products and major services it offers, and the inbox of inquiries that come in through the site. BDD owns and maintains all of it. Three sub-areas, each documented below.

**Company context** _(informed by JCEPSI Company Profile 2026, used here to ground the inquiry-form ideation in JCE's actual offerings)._ JC Electrofields Power System, Inc. (JCEPSI) was founded in 1997 and has grown to ~124 staff (registered Civil and Electrical Engineers plus technicians). It is based at 3074 F. Bautista St., Brgy Ugong, Valenzuela City; sales contact is `sales@jcepower.com`; web is `www.jcepower.com`. JCE's core business spans **power transformers** (Distribution, Current, Potential; 15 KV to 230 KV via Shenda Electric exclusive distributorship), **substation design and construction up to 230 KV**, **transmission lines design and construction up to 230 KV**, **switchgear (HVSG / MVSG / LVSG) design / fabrication / assembly**, **engineering consultancy for PV solar and other renewable energy generating plants**, **direct connection applications to NGCP via 69 KV lines**, and **maintenance and servicing** of substations and plant electrical systems. JCE serves Electric Cooperatives, Power Generating Plants (especially solar farms), Manufacturing Plants, Industrial Companies, and Commercial customers. This context shapes the inquiry-form taxonomy proposed in 9.2.3 below.

**Workflow model.** Unlike Offers and Quotations (which use the strict no-edit + event-stream pattern because their substantive terms shouldn't change once captured), Website Details content is **marketing content** that is naturally updated over time — typos get fixed, photos get refreshed, descriptions get sharpened. So website content uses the **standard edit pattern with audit logging** (Section 9.6 records who changed what, when), rather than strict immutability.

**Public-website integration.** _Confirmed: the public website pulls content **live** from the system's database._ When BDD updates a Project, Service, Product, or changes the Published status of any record, the public site reflects the change on its next page load (or per the website's caching policy). This eliminates a "publish to static site" step and gives BDD a single place to manage content.

#### 9.2.1 Projects (Showcase)

**Purpose.** A curated portfolio of completed projects shown on the public website to demonstrate JCE's track record. _Confirmed: this is a **separate dataset** from Sales Orders (Section 9.3), not a curated view of it._ The marketing-friendly entry that lives here is composed by BDD with the public audience in mind — different photos, different language, possibly different selection criteria than the operational Sales Order record.

_Record fields_

- **Project Name** — short title for the public website (e.g., "20/25 MVA Substation Upgrade — NORECO II").
- **Client Name** — free text.
- **Show Client Name on Public Site** — _confirmed: per-project setting._ Boolean toggle controlled by BDD. When off, the public site displays the project without the client's name (useful when a client requires confidentiality); the name still exists on the internal record for BDD's own reference.
- **Location** — typically the city or province (e.g., "Negros Oriental").
- **Description** — multi-line text describing what the project did, suitable for the public site.
- **Date Completed** — when the project wrapped (helps with sorting "recent" projects).
- **Photos** — up to **10 images per project**, _confirmed._ Each photo carries an optional caption and a sort order; one photo is designated the **cover photo** for list-view thumbnails. The system **auto-compresses** uploaded images for web delivery (see "Photo storage and constraints" below).
- **Tags / Category** _(optional)_ — e.g., "Substation", "Transformer", "Switchgear", "Solar", "Transmission Line", "Renewable Energy", "Maintenance" — for grouping on the public site.
- **Status** — single-select: **Published · Draft · Hidden** — so BDD can stage content before it goes live and can quietly retire entries without deleting them.
- **Sort Order** — manual override so featured projects appear first.

_System-managed_

- **Archived** — soft-delete flag.
- **Audit trail** — change history (Section 9.6).

#### 9.2.2 Products and Major Services

**Purpose.** The catalog of what JCE offers to clients, shown on the public website's "Products" and "Services" pages. The structure mirrors Projects (name, description, photos) but applies to JCE's _offerings_ rather than past projects. \*Confirmed: kept as **two distinct lists\*** — they represent different things on the public site (services are things JCE _does_, products are things JCE _sells or supplies_) and the public website groups them separately. _Confirmed: no additional fields beyond name + description + photos are needed at this stage._

_Services record fields_

- **Service Name** — e.g., "Substation Design and Construction (up to 230 KV)", "Transmission Line Design and Construction", "Switchgear Design / Fabrication / Assembly", "Electrical Consulting / Billing / Upgrading", "Substation Maintenance and Servicing", "Engineering Consultancy for PV Solar".
- **Description** — multi-line text describing the service.
- **Photos** — up to **10 images** with sort order and captions; one cover photo. Auto-compressed.
- **Status** — Published · Draft · Hidden.
- **Sort Order** — manual override for display priority.

_Products record fields_

- **Product Name** — e.g., "Power Transformer (15 KV – 230 KV)", "Distribution Transformer", "HVSG / MVSG / LVSG Switchgear", "SF-6 Power Breaker", "Capacitor Bank", "Recloser", "Lightning Arrester", "Steel & Concrete Poles".
- **Description** — multi-line text describing the product.
- **Photos** — up to **10 images** with sort order and captions; one cover photo. Auto-compressed.
- **Status** — Published · Draft · Hidden.
- **Sort Order** — manual override.

#### 9.2.3 Inquiries

**Purpose.** The inbox of leads and questions that come in through the public website's contact / inquiry form. BDD processes these — qualifies the lead, replies to the inquirer, and (when relevant) converts the inquiry into an Offer or a Sales Order.

**Public-website inquiry form — fields** _(confirmed and adopted by the Public Website scope, §11.4, in v1.71)._

_Contact information_

- **Name** _(required)_
- **Company / Organization** _(required)_
- **Position / Role** _(optional)_
- **Email** _(required)_
- **Phone** _(required)_ — many JCE leads come from organizations that prefer a phone follow-up
- **Industry** _(optional dropdown)_ — Electric Cooperative · Manufacturing · Solar / Renewable Energy · Commercial / Industrial · Government / Public Sector · Other

_Inquiry details_

- **Inquiry Type** _(required dropdown, aligned to JCE's offerings)_ —
  - Power Transformer Supply (Distribution / Power / CT / PT)
  - Substation Design & Construction (up to 230 KV)
  - Transmission Line Design & Construction (up to 230 KV)
  - Switchgear (HVSG / MVSG / LVSG)
  - Solar / Renewable Energy Project
  - Maintenance & Servicing (Substation / Plant Electrical)
  - Direct Connection Application (NGCP via 69 KV)
  - Engineering Consultancy
  - Other (free text)
- **Project Location** _(optional)_ — text field; especially relevant for utility-scale and transmission/substation work
- **Estimated Timeline** _(optional dropdown)_ — Immediate / Emergency · Within 3 months · 3–6 months · 6–12 months · Long-term planning (>12 months)
- **Subject** _(required)_ — short subject line
- **Message** _(required)_ — multi-line text body of the inquiry

_Optional, useful for qualification_

- **Budget Range (PHP)** _(optional dropdown)_ — Under 1M · 1M–10M · 10M–50M · 50M–100M · Over 100M · Prefer not to say
- **How did you hear about us?** _(optional dropdown)_ — Google / Web Search · Referral · Past Project · Trade Show or Event · Other

_Inquiry record fields stored in the system (mirrors the form plus system-added metadata)_

- All form fields above, plus:
- **Date Received** — system-set timestamp when the form is submitted.
- **Source** — how the inquiry arrived; defaults to "Website Contact Form" for inquiries arriving via the form, but the field is open for BDD to log inquiries that arrive via other channels (email, phone, LinkedIn, trade show) so all leads live in one inbox.

_BDD workflow fields_

- **Status** — single-select dropdown: **New · In Review · Replied · Closed · Spam**.
- **Assigned To** — selected from the employee database; the BDD team member handling this inquiry.
- **Internal Notes** — free-text notes (not visible to the inquirer).
- **Linked Offer** _(optional)_ — link to an Offer (Section 9.4) if the inquiry was converted into an offer JCE prepared.
- **Linked Sales Order** _(optional)_ — link to a Sales Order (Section 9.3) if the inquiry led directly to a project.

**Lead conversion to Offer — prefill flow.** _Confirmed._ When BDD decides an Inquiry should become an Offer, the system offers a **"Create Offer from Inquiry"** action that **prefills a new Offer draft** from the inquiry's data:

- Inquiry's **Subject** → Offer's Subject
- Inquiry's **Company / Organization** → Offer's Client
- Inquiry's **Inquiry Type + Message** → Offer's Subject context (BDD reviews and refines before sending)
- **Sent By** defaults to the Inquiry's Assigned To (the BDD team member handling the lead)
- The new Offer is created in **draft state** for BDD to complete (Total Amount, Ref. No., final Subject) and then issue
- The **Linked Offer** field on the Inquiry is auto-populated with the new Offer's record so the conversion is traceable both directions

This same prefill pattern can also be used when an inquiry leads directly to a Sales Order (rare; typically the path is Inquiry → Offer → Sales Order). When that happens, the Sales Order is created from the Offer rather than directly from the Inquiry.

_System-managed_

- **Archived** — soft-delete flag (useful for old spam or clearly cold inquiries).
- **Audit trail** — change history (Section 9.6).

**Cross-module integrations**

- **Public Website (Section 11)** consumes Projects, Services, and Products (the Published ones) and submits inquiries through its contact form into the Inquiries inbox.
- **Offer (Section 9.4) and Sales Order (Section 9.3)** — Inquiries can be linked to Offers and Sales Orders when leads convert, creating a paper trail from first contact to closed project.

**Photo storage and constraints.** _Confirmed defaults._

- **Maximum photos per record:** **10** (Projects, Services, Products).
- **Auto-compression:** _Confirmed by Bryan._ The system **automatically compresses** uploaded images on save for web delivery. Originals are not retained — the compressed version is what is stored and served.
- **Accepted formats:** JPG / JPEG and PNG (the two formats the web routinely supports without conversion).
- **Per-photo maximum upload size (pre-compression):** 10 MB (a reasonable cap for modern phone-camera output).
- **Resulting served size:** the system targets ~200–400 KB per compressed image, with longest edge ~1600 px — large enough to look sharp on full-page hero shots, small enough for fast page loads on slower connections.
- **Thumbnail generation:** the system generates and caches a list-view thumbnail (~400 px longest edge) from each photo so the website's project list / catalog pages load quickly without reprocessing on every request.

**Open items for Website Details.** All seven open items from v1.33 were resolved in v1.34. No outstanding items at the Website Details level beyond what surfaces during build.

### 9.3 Sales Order _(fully scoped)_

**Purpose.** The internal **list of projects** JCE is working on or has worked on. Each Sales Order represents one project for one client, with all the project's commercial and operational details. This is the **canonical project record** that other modules reference:

- **Timekeeping (Section 4.2)** — the _Working Project_ dropdown on every timesheet row is sourced from active and recently completed Sales Orders (so back-dated entries work).
- **OB / Travel (Section 4.3.1)** — the _Sales Order #_ field on the form points to a specific Sales Order record.
- **Accounting Sales (5.3) and Collections (5.4)** — billing and payment records reference the Sales Order via SO#.
- **Accounting Payable Voucher (5.5) and Disbursement (5.6)** — outgoing payments are project-tagged via SO# for cost roll-up.
- **Accounting Journal Voucher (5.7)** — project-tagged JVs (e.g., Cash Advance Liquidation expenses) carry SO#.
- **Purchasing PO (7.5) and Import Shipment (7.8)** — every Purchase Order and Import Shipment references the SO# as one of three linking keys (Project / SO No. / MR No.). Per Purchasing 7.3.1, the broader "Project" concept extends beyond Sales Orders to include internal cost centers (`WORKSHOP`, `JCE STOCK`) — Section 6 Project Management will scope the unified Project entity.

The SO# is therefore the **most heavily-referenced identifier in the system** — its global-uniqueness invariant is load-bearing.

**Source document.** _Confirmed._ JCE's existing **"SALES ORDER MONITORING" spreadsheet** (single sheet, ~1,107 rows spanning 2021–2026) is the source of the historical record and most of the field list. The system adds two new fields on top of the spreadsheet contents (Start Date, Project Duration). The system also **normalizes** several free-text fields from the spreadsheet (offer stage and Requested By) into clean dropdowns so the messy variants in legacy data (e.g., "W/OFFER", "W/ OFFER", "WITH OFFER" all meaning the same thing) become consistent and queryable going forward.

**Confirmed functions.** Add · Edit · Archive · Filter. _(No hard delete — archive preserves history, matching Employee Management's convention.)_

**Record fields.** _Confirmed._ Each Sales Order carries the following fields, grouped by purpose.

_Identification_

- **Date** — the date the Sales Order is logged in the system. Auto-populated to the current date on creation; editable. (Matches the "DATE" column in the spreadsheet.)
- **Sales Order No.** — **the system-wide primary key for projects.** _Confirmed by Bryan._ The Sales Order No. is the universal project identifier across the entire JCE System. **BDD assigns it** — manually entered following the convention **YY-MM-XXX** (e.g., `26-05-378`). The system can suggest the next number from the convention but BDD has final say. Every module that needs to reference a project does so by Sales Order No.:
  - **HR Timekeeping** — the Working Project column on every timesheet row stores a Sales Order No. (Section 4.2).
  - **HR OB / Travel** — the Sales Order # field on every OB record stores a Sales Order No. (Section 4.3.1).
  - **Future Accounting / Payroll** — labor cost rolls up to projects via Sales Order No.; billing handover (Turned Over flag) is keyed by Sales Order No.
  - **Future Project Management, Purchasing, Engineering** — same identifier.

  Because of this load-bearing role, the system **enforces global uniqueness** on Sales Order No. — no duplicates are permitted, and the field is **required** on every Sales Order record. BDD owns the namespace; the system protects its integrity.

_Client and scope_

- **Client / Customer** — the client name (e.g., "FORTUNE TOWN PROPERTIES CORP."). **Free text.** _Confirmed._ A separate Clients table is not modeled at this stage; if client-level history across multiple Sales Orders becomes important later, the field can be promoted to a relation against a Clients entity without breaking the Sales Order schema.
- **Project Name** — short title for the project (e.g., "Real Steel Canteen Power Supply"). _Confirmed: split from Description._ Used in the dropdown labels in Timekeeping and OB / Travel alongside the Sales Order No.
