- **Description / Scope of Work** — multi-line text describing the project's scope (e.g., the detailed work list from the spreadsheet's combined field). _Confirmed: split from Project Name._
- **Contract Amount** — the contract value in **PHP** (Philippine Peso). _Confirmed: PHP is the only currency; no foreign-currency projects._ Optional — not all Sales Orders have an amount entered at creation; it can be filled in later as offers and contracts firm up.

_Workflow stage_

- **Remarks (Offer / Contract Stage)** — _normalized dropdown_ capturing where the project is in the commercial process. From the legacy spreadsheet, the variants seen include: No Offer Yet · With Offer · Without Offer · With NOA (Notice of Award) · With NTP (Notice to Proceed) · With P.O. (Purchase Order) · With SOA (Statement of Account) · With Contract · Cancelled · Done. _(To confirm with BDD: the final dropdown values and whether any are missing.)_
- **Project Status** — _normalized dropdown_ capturing the operational status of the project. Values from the legacy data: **Ongoing Project · On Hold · Project Completed · Cancelled**. Status drives downstream behavior — only Ongoing, On Hold, and recently Completed Sales Orders appear in the Timekeeping Working Project dropdown (per Bryan's confirmation).
- **Turned Over** — yes / no. Indicates whether a completed project has been handed over to the Accounting department for billing. Often paired with a note in the Notes field describing the handoff (e.g., "Turned over to Accounting Ma'am Tina").

_People_

- **Requested By** — selected from the **employee database** (Section 4.1). In the legacy spreadsheet this was free text mixing department codes ("BDD", "PMG") and informal references ("C/O Sir JCC", "Ma'am Owen"); the system replaces that with a clean person reference. The employee's department auto-populates from their record.

_Timing — newly added on top of the spreadsheet_

- **Start Date** — when the project actually starts (distinct from the Date field, which is when the Sales Order was logged). _Confirmed by Bryan._
- **Project Duration** — expressed in **calendar days** (Bryan's choice). The system computes a derived **End Date** = Start Date + Duration. _(Note: per Bryan, the model is start date + duration; no separate End Date is entered.)_

_Progress-billing structure — added in v1.48 to support structured Billing Breakdowns on the SOA input (Section 5.3)_

When a Sales Order is large enough to be billed in multiple progress installments (typical of long-duration contract work — substation construction, transmission lines, solar farms), three percentage fields capture the contract's billing structure. **All three vary per contract per Bryan's confirmation** and are set at contract award. When all three are null or zero, the contract has no progress-billing structure and the SOA input uses simple line items.

- **Down Payment %** — percentage of the Contract Amount the client pays up front (typical value: 20%; varies per contract). Used to compute the Down Payment Amount displayed on the SOA Billing Breakdown for context (`Contract Amount × Down Payment %`).
- **Retention %** — percentage of each progress billing's Gross amount held back by the client until project completion (typical value: 10%; varies per contract). Applied to each SOA's Billing Breakdown as `Gross × Retention %`.
- **DP Recoupment %** — percentage of each progress billing's Gross amount used to recoup (offset) the Down Payment liability (typical value: 20%; varies per contract). Applied to each SOA's Billing Breakdown as `Gross × DP Recoupment %`.

_Derived values (computed from the Sales Order plus the cumulative SOA history)_

- **Down Payment Amount** = `Contract Amount × Down Payment %`. Recorded once at the start of the project (typically the first SOA on the SO is the Down Payment billing).
- **Cumulative Billed to Date** = sum of Gross amounts across all SOAs issued on this SO.
- **Cumulative Retention Held** = sum of Retention amounts across all SOAs.
- **Cumulative DP Recouped** = sum of DP Recoupment amounts across all SOAs. When this reaches the Down Payment Amount, the DP is fully recouped and subsequent billings skip the Recoupment line.
- **Remaining Contract Balance** = `Contract Amount − Cumulative Billed to Date`.

_Additional context_

- **Notes** — free-text additional context (e.g., "Internal offer hold as per Sir JCC", "Advanced turned over 03-24-2026"). Mirrors the additional-notes column in the legacy spreadsheet.

_System-managed_

- **Archived** — soft-delete flag managed by the Archive function. _Confirmed: archiving is **manual only**._ The system does not auto-archive completed projects after a fixed period — BDD decides when a Sales Order is removed from active lists. Archived records remain queryable for historical reporting.
- **Audit trail** — change history (who changed what, when), surfaced under Audit Logs (Section 9.6).

**Status lifecycle.** _Confirmed from the spreadsheet data._ A Sales Order moves through:

```
Ongoing Project ─┬─► On Hold ─┬─► Ongoing Project
                 │             └─► Cancelled
                 ├─► Project Completed ─► Turned Over (yes/no)
                 └─► Cancelled
```

- **Ongoing Project** is the default starting state.
- **On Hold** is a temporary pause; a project can return to Ongoing.
- **Project Completed** indicates the work is done; the separate **Turned Over** flag indicates whether the project has been handed off to Accounting for billing.
- **Cancelled** is a terminal state.
- **Archived** is a soft-delete state, separate from Project Status — typically applied long after a project is Completed or Cancelled to remove it from active lists.

_No status transition restrictions._ _Confirmed._ BDD can freely move a Sales Order between any two statuses (e.g., Cancelled → Ongoing Project if a project is revived). The audit log (Section 9.6) preserves the history of every transition.

**Cross-module integrations.**

- **Timekeeping Working Project dropdown** (Section 4.2) is sourced from Sales Orders with Project Status in {Ongoing Project, On Hold, Project Completed} and Archived = no. This is Bryan's option (b) — active plus recently completed — so back-dated time entries can be made on projects that just wrapped.
- **OB / Travel Sales Order #** (Section 4.3.1) is a dropdown into the Sales Orders table; the same visibility rule applies.

**Filter options for the list view.** Default filters should include Project Status, Remarks (Offer stage), Client / Customer, Requested By, Turned Over, and a Date range. _(Specific defaults to confirm with BDD.)_

**Open items for Sales Order.** All seven of the open items from v1.26 were resolved in v1.27 (see revision history). No outstanding items at the Sales Order level beyond the normal small clarifications BDD may surface during build (e.g., filter default sort order, dropdown UX details).

### 9.4 Offers _(fully scoped)_

**Purpose.** To record every formal offer JCE extends to a client — what was quoted, when it was sent, who sent it, the client's response, and (if awarded) the resulting Purchase Order or Notice of Award. Offers are the **upstream record** that precedes a Sales Order: when an offer is awarded, BDD creates a Sales Order and links the offer to it; the project then moves into operations. The Offers list is also where BDD tracks the company's win/loss history.

**Source document.** _Received._ JCE's **"LIST OF OFFER"** spreadsheet — two sheets:

- **JCEPSI** (JC Electrofields Power Systems Inc.) — ~544 offers spanning 2021–2026.
- **JICA** — a separate JCE entity with its own smaller offers list.

_Confirmed: JCEPSI and JICA are kept as **separate entities** in the system, each with its own Offers stream._ The Offers module presents one entity's list at a time — the user switches between JCEPSI Offers and JICA Offers via an entity toggle, and the default view in each session is whichever entity the user worked on last. Ref. No. sequencing is **per-entity** (JCEPSI's `CIWEC-26-002` and a hypothetical JICA `CIWEC-26-001` don't share a counter). Reporting that spans both entities is possible when explicitly requested (e.g., a cross-entity year-end summary), but the day-to-day experience is one entity at a time.

_(Scope note: this entity separation is currently modeled only in Offers, since that's where the JCEPSI/JICA distinction shows up in the source data. If JICA also has its own Sales Orders, employees, or other operational streams, that's a larger architectural conversation — at present the rest of the system assumes a single JCE namespace. Flag at the next BDD or owner check-in if JICA has its own operational footprint.)_

Many of the legacy free-text fields (Status, Remarks variants) are normalized into clean dropdowns, the same way Sales Order's Remarks were cleaned up.

**Confirmed functions.** Create · Archive. **Strict no-edit on the offer record.** _Confirmed._

**The strict-immutability model.** _Confirmed by Bryan (answer 3 = strict)._

Once an Offer record is created, **no field on the Offer record itself can be edited**. To track how the offer progresses through its lifecycle (status changes, the client awarding it with a P.O./NOA, the eventual COC, receipt of payment), the system uses an **append-only Offer Events** stream linked to the offer. Every workflow update — status change, P.O./NOA recording, COC date, receipt, remark — is a **new Event record** appended to the Offer's history rather than an in-place edit.

The "current state" of an offer is **derived** from its event stream: the latest Status event gives the current status, the latest P.O./NOA event gives the current Purchase Order details, and so on. To the user this looks like ordinary updating ("change status to Awarded"), but the underlying model records each change as a discrete, timestamped event by a specific user — giving a complete, tamper-evident history with no special audit-trail engineering needed; the model _is_ the audit trail.

If BDD needs to issue a substantively different offer (different terms, amount, subject), they create a **revision** — a new Offer record with `Rev.NN` appended to the Ref. No. and a link back to the predecessor. The predecessor's current status becomes **Revised** (recorded as an event). This is how the legacy data already works (Ref. Nos. like `DPC-23-007Rev.02` are common).

**Record fields.** Split into immutable Offer fields and append-only Event types.

_Offer record — immutable once created_

- **Entity** — single-select: **JCEPSI · JICA**. _Confirmed._ Determines which Offers stream the record belongs to. Required on every Offer record; set at creation and (like all Offer fields) **not editable afterward**. A Ref. No. counter is maintained per-entity, so the two streams' numbering is independent.
- **Reference Number (Ref. No.)** — the offer's own identifier, format **`CLIENT_CODE-YY-XXX[Rev.NN]`** (e.g., `CIWEC-26-001`, `DPC-23-007Rev.02`). _Confirmed: system-assisted generation._ BDD picks (or types) the client code prefix; the system auto-suggests the next sequential number for that client-code/year combination. BDD can override the suggestion. The full Ref. No. is enforced unique system-wide.
- **Offer Date** — the date the offer was created.
- **Date Emailed** — the date the offer was emailed to the client. May be the same as Offer Date or later.
- **Sent By** — selected from the **employee database** (Section 4.1). Replaces the legacy initials column (MMR, JPA, DDA, etc.) with a clean employee reference.
- **Client** — the client name. **Free text** (same convention as Sales Order).
- **Subject** — the subject line / description of what is being offered (e.g., "Demolition of the Transformer Pad — CIWEC Kaysakat Antipolo").
- **Total Amount** — the offered value in **PHP**.
- **Previous Revision** — _system-managed link._ If this offer is a revision of a previous offer (Ref. No. has `Rev.NN`), this field points to the predecessor's Offer record, building a revision chain.

_System-managed on the Offer record_

- **Created By, Created Date** — set at creation, immutable.
- **Archived** — soft-delete flag managed by the Archive function.

_Offer Events — append-only stream linked to each Offer_

Each Event has: an **Offer ID** (link to the parent Offer), an **Event Type** (one of the values below), the **Event Data** (the new value or details for that event type), the **Timestamp**, and the **User** who recorded it.

| Event Type              | What it records                                        | "Current state" implication                                               |
| ----------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------ | ------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Status Change**       | New status value (from the dropdown below)             | The latest Status Change event gives the offer's current status.          |
| **P.O. / NOA Received** | Purchase Order number or NOA reference + P.O./NOA Date | The latest such event gives the current P.O./NOA details.                 |
| **COC Date**            | Certificate of Completion date                         | The latest event sets the COC date.                                       |
| **Receipt**             | Receipt reference (when payment is received)           | The latest event sets the receipt.                                        |
| **Remark**              | Free-text note about the offer's progress              | All Remark events accumulate as a chronological note thread.              |
| **Sales Order Linked**  | The Sales Order No. created when the offer was awarded | The latest event records the link (typically one per offer once awarded). |

When the user views an Offer in the system, the system materializes the **current view** from these events: current status, current P.O./NOA, current COC, etc. The full event history is also viewable for audit.

**Status values (normalized dropdown).** _Confirmed list:_

**Waiting for Client Response · Acknowledged · For Revision · Revised · Awarded · Not Awarded · Offer Lapsed · Cancelled**

_Note: "Ongoing" was removed from the legacy list — once an offer is Awarded, the project moves into Sales Order territory (Section 9.3), where Project Status takes over. The 3 legacy `Ongoing` instances in the source data are treated as data-entry artifacts of the spreadsheet era._

**Status lifecycle.**

```
Waiting for Client Response ─┬─► Acknowledged ─► Awarded ─► (BDD manually creates a Sales Order)
                             ├─► For Revision ─► (new Rev.NN offer created; this offer → Revised)
                             ├─► Not Awarded
                             ├─► Offer Lapsed
                             └─► Cancelled
```

- **Waiting for Client Response** is the default starting state after the offer is sent.
- **Acknowledged** means the client has confirmed receipt.
- **For Revision** is set when the client requests changes; BDD then creates a new Offer record with `Rev.NN`, and this offer's status moves to **Revised** (as a final state for this version).
- **Awarded** is set when the client formally awards the offer. A **P.O. / NOA Received** event typically follows.
- **Not Awarded · Offer Lapsed · Cancelled** are terminal states for offers that don't become projects.

**Cross-module integrations.**

- **Sales Order (Section 9.3) ← Offers (manual conversion).** _Confirmed._ When an offer is Awarded, BDD **manually creates** a corresponding Sales Order in Section 9.3 and links it back to the source Offer (the system records a **Sales Order Linked** event on the offer). The system does not auto-create the Sales Order — BDD decides when and how the project is brought into operational tracking. This keeps the conversion under human control (which matters because the Sales Order's Project Name, Description, Start Date, and Project Duration may need to be set differently than the offer's Subject and Total Amount alone would suggest).
- **Audit Logs (Section 9.6).** The Offer Events stream _is_ the audit trail for everything that changes on an offer after creation. Section 9.6 aggregates these alongside other BDD module events for a unified Audit Log view.

**Open items for Offers.** All six open items from v1.28 were resolved in v1.29. No outstanding items at the Offers level beyond the normal small clarifications BDD may surface during build (e.g., default sort order in the list view, what "Client Code" picker looks like for the system-assisted Ref. No. generation).

### 9.5 Quotation _(fully scoped)_

**Purpose.** To track every supplier quote request JCE issues when sourcing equipment or materials for a client's project, and to make **cross-supplier comparison per item** easy. When a client needs (say) a 20/25 MVA Power Transformer, BDD reaches out to multiple suppliers (Shenda, Siemens, ABB, Chint, etc.) for prices. The Quotation module records every touch, captures the price each supplier returned, and supports comparing them side-by-side so BDD can pick a winner.

**Source document.** _Received._ JCE's **"LIST OF QUOTATION"** spreadsheet — three sheets:

- **EC** (Electrical Contracting) — prefix `Q-EC-` — 31 rows.
- **Workshop** — prefix `Q-WS-` — 33 rows.
- **Solar** (Solar Project) — prefix `Q-SP-` — 10 rows.

_Confirmed: EC, Workshop, and Solar are kept as **separate streams** in the system_ — same model as JCEPSI/JICA for Offers. The user switches between categories via a toggle; Ref. No. sequencing is per-category; cross-category reporting is the exception, not the default.

The spreadsheet uses only five active columns (Ref. No., Item, Client, Supplier, Remarks) — **no price field, no date field**. The system adds both: a Price field per supplier and a Response Date, so the actual comparison can live inside the system rather than across attached PDFs and email threads.

**Model — hierarchical (confirmed).** A two-level structure:

- **Quotation Request** _(parent)_ — one record per item being quoted, owned by BDD on behalf of a specific client/project. Has one Ref. No. (e.g., `Q-EC-26007`).
- **Supplier Quotes** _(children)_ — one record per supplier asked to quote, attached to the parent. Each carries the supplier, their price (when received), and their response status.

This makes comparison automatic — opening a Quotation Request shows every supplier's response side-by-side, with prices laid out for the BDD team to weigh. **Legacy sub-lettered Ref. Nos (`Q-SP-26007A`, `Q-SP-26007B`, etc.)** are migrated by grouping them under their parent (`Q-SP-26007`) as Supplier Quote children; new records don't use sub-letters at all.

**Strict no-edit + event-stream model (confirmed).** _Same pattern as Offers (Section 9.4)._ Both the Quotation Request and each Supplier Quote are **immutable once created**. Workflow updates — a supplier finally responding with a price, status changes, the BDD team selecting a winning supplier — become **append-only events** linked to the respective record. The current state of a quotation (current statuses, current selected supplier, latest prices) is **derived** from the event stream; the model itself is the audit trail.

#### Record fields

_Quotation Request (parent — immutable)_

- **Category** — single-select: **EC · Workshop · Solar**. Determines the Ref. No. prefix and which stream the record belongs to.
- **Reference Number** — format `Q-{CAT}-YYNNN` (e.g., `Q-EC-26007`). System-assisted generation, per-category counter (same pattern as Offers). BDD can override the suggestion. Enforced unique within its category.
- **Item** — what is being quoted (e.g., "20/25 MVA Power Transformer", "Surge Arrester", "Steel Structure").
- **Client** — the client the item is being quoted for. Free text (same convention as Sales Order / Offers).
- **Offer link** _(optional)_ — link to an Offer record (Section 9.4) when the quotation feeds into an Offer JCE is still preparing. Used while the proposal is being assembled.
- **Sales Order link** _(optional)_ — link to a Sales Order record (Section 9.3) once the project is awarded and the supplier needs to be confirmed for active execution. Used after the Offer is awarded and a Sales Order exists.
- **Request Date** — when BDD started requesting quotes. _System-added on top of the legacy spreadsheet for filtering and reporting._
- **Notes** — free-text additional context on the request itself.

_Supplier Quote (child — immutable; one per supplier asked)_

- **Supplier** — selected from the **Suppliers entity** (see below). Each supplier is a maintained record, not free text.
- **Legacy Ref. No.** _(optional)_ — preserved during migration for any legacy row that had its own Ref. No. (e.g., `Q-EC-26007A`); not used for new records.
- **Response Date** — when the supplier responded (whether with a price or as "No Quote").
- **Notes** — free-text per supplier (captures the longer legacy phrases like "WAITING FOR MAIN MVSG OFFER", "DONE WAITING FOR SWITCHGEAR").

_Supplier Quote events (append-only stream)_

| Event Type             | What it records                                              | "Current state" implication                                                                                                                           |
| ---------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------ | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Status Change**      | New response status (see dropdown below)                     | Latest event sets the supplier's current status.                                                                                                      |
| **Price Recorded**     | Quoted price in PHP                                          | Latest event sets the supplier's current quoted price.                                                                                                |
| **Selected as Winner** | Marker that BDD has chosen this supplier from the comparison | At most one Supplier Quote per parent should have this event; latest such event across children determines the Quotation Request's Selected Supplier. |
| **Note Added**         | Free-text note                                               | All Note events accumulate as a chronological thread.                                                                                                 |

_Response Status — normalized dropdown:_ **Waiting · For Revision · Done (Quote Received) · No Quote · Other (with note)**. Longer legacy phrases are captured as Notes rather than as new statuses.

_System-managed_

- **Archived** — soft-delete flag on the parent Quotation Request (children are archived with their parent).
- **Audit trail** — the event stream above _is_ the audit trail; Section 9.6 aggregates across the BDD module.

#### Suppliers entity _(ownership transferred to Purchasing 7.9 in v1.57)_

> **Authoritative source.** As of v1.57, the Suppliers entity is **owned by Purchasing (Section 7.9)** with the comprehensive field schema captured there (Supplier Code `JCE 0000X`, Name, TOP/Payment Terms, TIN, City/Country/Zip, Complete Address, Warehouse Address, Contact First/Last Name, Email, Mobile, Landline, Category, Item/Service Offered, Active flag). BDD 9.5 Quotation **references** the same Supplier records by FK — there is one canonical supplier master across the system, not two.

The preliminary field set originally drafted here (Supplier Name, Country, Contact Person, Contact Email/Phone, Notes, Active) is fully covered by Purchasing 7.9's schema and is preserved as additive context only; the authoritative schema is in 7.9.1.

**Cross-module reuse pattern.** Quotation Requests in BDD 9.5 select suppliers from the Purchasing 7.9 master list during the quote-solicitation phase of a bid. When the project is later awarded and procurement begins, the same Supplier records back the actual Purchase Orders in 7.5. One supplier, one record, two distinct workflows: BDD's bid-time quoting and Purchasing's procurement-time ordering.

#### Cross-module integrations

- **Offer (Section 9.4) ← Quotation.** A Quotation Request typically attaches to an Offer being prepared, so when BDD assembles the Offer's pricing they reference the quoted supplier prices.
- **Sales Order (Section 9.3) ← Quotation.** After the Offer is awarded and a Sales Order is created, the Quotation Request can be linked to the Sales Order so the chosen supplier and price are traceable from the project record.
- ✅ **Suppliers entity → Purchasing module (Section 7.9)** — **CONFIRMED in v1.57.** Purchasing 7.9 is the authoritative owner of the Supplier master; BDD 9.5 Quotation references the same records. See the Suppliers entity note above for the schema reference.
- **Audit Logs (Section 9.6).** The append-only Supplier Quote event stream is the audit trail; Section 9.6 aggregates.

**Open items for Quotation.** All eight open items from v1.31 were resolved in v1.32 (see revision history). One small follow-up remains for the BDD interview:

- Whether to also support **uploading the supplier's quote document** (PDF, email) per Supplier Quote — the file-upload pattern is already established in HR Requests, and Quote documents would mirror that for offline reference; not required by Bryan but a reasonable add.

_(The earlier open item about promoting the Suppliers entity to its own subsection was resolved in v1.57: Suppliers now lives in Purchasing 7.9 as the authoritative master, and BDD 9.5 references it via FK.)_

### 9.6 Audit Logs _(fully scoped)_

**Purpose.** A unified, searchable record of **every change made within the BDD module** — who changed what, when, and (where applicable) why. The Audit Log is BDD's accountability surface: when a Sales Order's status changes, an Offer is awarded, a Supplier Quote's price is updated, or a Website Project's photo is replaced, the event is recorded here and BDD leads (and the company owner) can review the trail at any time.

**What's already audit-trail-shaped, and what 9.6 adds.** The other BDD subsections already do most of the work — 9.6 mainly **aggregates** rather than introduces new logging:

- **Offers (9.4)** and **Quotation (9.5)** use the **strict no-edit + event-stream pattern**, so every workflow change is already an immutable event linked to the record. 9.6 surfaces these in a unified timeline alongside everything else.
- **Sales Order (9.3), Website Details (9.2), and Suppliers (within 9.5)** use the **standard edit pattern with audit logging**. Each edit is logged as a discrete entry that 9.6 reads.
- **Inquiries (9.2.3)** logs status changes, assignments, and linked-Offer/Sales-Order conversions.

So 9.6's job is not to invent audit logging but to give BDD a **single timeline view across the module** with filters and search.

**Audit Log entry structure.** Each entry captures:

- **Timestamp** — when the change happened.
- **User** — selected from the employee database; who made the change.
- **Module Area** — which BDD subsection (Sales Order · Offer · Quotation · Supplier Quote · Supplier · Website Project · Service · Product · Inquiry).
- **Action Type** — Created · Edited · Status Change · Archived · Restored · Event Appended (for the event-stream records) · Linked / Unlinked (for cross-record links like Inquiry → Offer).
- **Record Type and Record ID** — the canonical identifier of the affected record (e.g., Sales Order `26-05-378`, Offer Ref. No. `CIWEC-26-001`, Inquiry ID, etc.).
- **Field Changed** _(when applicable)_ — the specific field that was edited.
- **Old Value · New Value** _(when applicable)_ — the values before and after, captured for fields where this is meaningful (status changes, prices, contract amounts, dates). Long free-text fields show a "before/after" indicator without the full text in the log entry; the full text is viewable in the record's own history.
- **Context Note** _(optional)_ — a free-text reason / note the user can add when making a sensitive change (e.g., "Status flipped back to Ongoing — project was revived per client request").

**View and search.** The Audit Log view supports:

- **Filter by user** — all changes by a specific person.
- **Filter by date range** — what happened this week, this month, between two dates.
- **Filter by module area** — only Sales Order changes, only Offer events, etc.
- **Filter by action type** — only Status Changes, only Archives, only Edits.
- **Filter by specific record** — every change to Sales Order `26-05-378`, or every event on Offer `CIWEC-26-001`.
- **Free-text search** across Context Notes and the Field Changed column.
- **Combined filters** — e.g., "all Status Changes on Offers by MMR in May 2026."

**Export.** The Audit Log view supports export to **CSV** (for analysis in spreadsheets) and **PDF** (for review or compliance hand-offs). Exports respect the active filters — exporting "Status Changes on Offers by MMR in May" produces a file with exactly those rows.

**Access control.** _(Detailed permission matrix to be defined when RBAC is fully scoped — see Section 3.)_ The Audit Log is read-only and visible to:

- **BDD lead / admin** role — full view across the module.
- **Company owner** — full view (the owner has full access everywhere per Section 3).
- Other BDD staff — _(to confirm during RBAC scoping)_ probably restricted to viewing audit history of records they have access to, not the full cross-module log.

**Retention.** _Time-bounded with archival_ (reconciled to the system-wide policy in the finalization sweep). Audit Log entries stay in the live store for a configurable window (e.g., 3–5 years) then archive to cold storage — matching Purchasing §7.13 #3 and NFR-AUDIT-02.

**Real-time logging.** Every change is logged **as it happens**, not in a nightly batch. Users see new entries in the Audit Log view immediately after refresh.

**Cross-module audit (system-wide view) — flagged as a future enhancement.** Section 9.6 is scoped to the **BDD module**. Other modules have their own audit mechanisms:

- **Employee Management (4.1)** — explicit audit-trail field on the employee record.
- **Timekeeping (4.2)** — verification batches lock periods and preserve who verified what, when.
- **HR Requests (4.3)** — paper-first plus uploaded scans serve as the primary audit; system changes are logged at the record level.

A future enhancement would be a **system-wide unified audit log** that aggregates 9.6 alongside HR-module audit data into one timeline (useful for the company owner and for compliance reporting). That's out of scope for the current draft but worth noting as a natural follow-on — the data is already there in each module's local audit; what's missing is the cross-cutting view. To be revisited after the remaining department modules (Accounting, PM, Purchasing, Engineering) are scoped, since each will add its own audit surface.

**Open items for Audit Logs — small, none blocking:**

- **Notifications for sensitive changes.** Confirm whether BDD wants real-time alerts (email or in-app) when specific high-stakes changes occur — e.g., a Contract Amount on a Sales Order is modified, a Supplier Quote's Selected as Winner event fires, an awarded Offer's status is changed. Not required for v1; can be added later.
- **Per-record audit view.** Confirm that opening any BDD record (a Sales Order, Offer, etc.) should show a per-record "History" tab that filters the global Audit Log to just that record's entries. Recommended; minimal UI cost.

### 9.7 Roles & Permissions

✅ **RESOLVED: BDD Staff + BDD Lead/Admin (+ Owner).** **BDD Staff** create/edit sales orders, offers, quotations, website content, and inquiries; **BDD Lead/Admin** additionally approve, access the audit log, and manage configuration; **Owner** has full access. Detailed per-action permissions are consolidated in the system-wide RBAC matrix (Section 3).

### 9.8 Pending Information _(client-input required)_

The following artifacts are **JCE to provide / confirm**; the BDD field lists already drafted (9.2–9.5) are held as **provisional** until reconciled against them:

- The Sales Order / Projects spreadsheet (for the full Sales Order field list).
- The current offer and quotation templates (for the Offer and Quotation field lists).
- Supplier list source (for Quotation comparisons).
- Website inquiry form structure (for Inquiries scope).

### 9.9 Finalization Decisions (v1.65)

BDD's six feature areas (9.2–9.6) were already fully scoped; the residual items are resolved here (Bryan, finalization 2026-06-03).

1. **Offer-stage dropdown (Sales Order Remarks) = full normalized set:** No Offer Yet · With Offer · Without Offer · With NOA · With NTP · With P.O. · With SOA · With Contract · Cancelled · Done (admin-editable).
2. **Sensitive-change notifications — IN v1.** Real-time in-app/email alerts fire on high-stakes events: a Sales Order **Contract Amount** edit, a Supplier Quote **"Selected as Winner"**, and an **awarded Offer's status change**. (Builds on the audit infrastructure, §9.6.)
3. **Per-record History tab — IN v1.** Every BDD record (Sales Order, Offer, Quotation, etc.) shows a **History** tab filtering the global Audit Log to that record's entries.
4. **BDD Staff audit access** is **scoped to records they can access**; **BDD Lead/Admin** see the full BDD log (consistent with §3 RBAC).
5. **Suppliers entity** is owned by **Purchasing §7.9** (authoritative master); BDD §9.5 references it via FK — confirmed.
6. **Quotation legacy sub-lettered Ref. Nos** (`Q-SP-26007A/B`) migrate by grouping under their parent (`Q-SP-26007`) as Supplier-Quote children; new records don't use sub-letters — confirmed.
7. **Inquiry-form field list (9.2.3)** is **deferred to Public Website (§11) scoping**; the currently-drafted fields are provisional.

_Build-time clarifications_ (no decision needed now): list-view default sort orders and the Client-Code picker UX will be settled during build. _Client-input_ artifacts remain as listed in §9.8.
