## 10. Warehouse Department Module _(finalized v1.70)_

> **Source & origin.** This module was promoted to a standalone department in v1.60. Its requirements were originally captured inside Project Management as §6.9 (Warehouse / Inventory Master) and the stock-handling half of the §6.10 Material Request workflow, derived from review of the MR form `JCE-GARCIA 3-0257.xlsx` (footer code `JCE-F-WMS02`). Promoting it reflects that the **Warehouse is the authoritative owner of the inventory master** — the system of record for "what's in stock" that every other module reads. Project Management still **authors** Material Requests (§6.10); the Warehouse **fulfills** them. Requirement codes use Section-10-unique prefixes (`FR-WIM`, `FR-STK`, `FR-RSV`, `FR-WMR`, `FR-WLG`, `FR-WXC`, and the v1.61 additions `FR-LED`, `FR-MRR`, `FR-REL`, `FR-LOC`, `FR-TRF`, `FR-LCK`).

> **v1.61 expansion — operational document layer.** v1.60 scoped the _backbone_ (item master, movements, reservations, MR verification). v1.61 adds the **operational document layer** that warehouse and site staff actually work in day-to-day, designed against two real inputs: the company's **MRR Summary** (232 Material Receiving Report records — `MRR No / Receive Date / Particulars / Supplier / Invoice No / Project / PO Number / MR Number / Requested By / Accountability Code / Purpose`; 97% carry a PO#, 93% an MR#, 95% an Invoice#) and the **ARSD warehouse dashboard** used as the UX reference (project cards, a Stock Monitoring Ledger, Delivery Receipts, Release Forms, and a Lock/Unlock approval toggle). Eight design decisions were locked with Bryan on 2026-06-03 (10.2). The headline effects: the **Material Receiving Report (MRR) is the single Goods-Receipt document** (it _is_ Purchasing's 7.15.1 GoodsReceipt — one record, two consumers, no double entry); a **BOQ-driven Stock Monitoring Ledger** (10.4) replaces the abstract per-item view as the primary screen; **multi-location stock with a two-sided Stock Transfer** (10.8) and a **formal goods-receiving workflow** are promoted **from Phase 2 into Phase 1**; and every operational document runs a **3-state lock gate** (10.12).

> **Ownership boundary with Project Management (§6).** The `InventoryItem`, `StockMovement`, and `StockReservation` entities — listed for relationship context in §6.3.2 — are **owned and defined here** (10.3). PMG's MR workflow (§6.10) consumes the Warehouse stock-verification service (10.7); the MR header, line structure, numbering, canvass sheet, and "For Purchase" computation remain PMG's. This module is the downstream supplier of stock truth and the upstream gate before a purchase need reaches Purchasing (§7).

### 10.1 Module Overview

The Warehouse module is JCE's **inventory system of record**. It owns the item master, every stock movement (receipt, issue, transfer, adjustment), the reservation ledger that keeps two requests from claiming the same units, and the **operational documents** site and warehouse staff fill in: the **Material Receiving Report (MRR)**, the **Release Form**, and the **Stock Transfer**. It is deliberately **movement-driven**: stock-on-hand is never typed — it is the running sum of immutable movements, so the balance is always reconcilable and auditable. The documents are the human-facing front end; each posts the movements behind it.

The module sits at the centre of the materials flow: **Project Management authors a Material Request → the Warehouse verifies availability, reserves in-stock quantities, and issues them on fulfillment → only the unfulfillable remainder ("For Purchase") flows to Purchasing (§7) → goods purchased come back as an MRR** that increases stock-on-hand and, because the MRR carries the PO number and supplier invoice, **completes Purchasing's three-way match** (10.6). The inventory master is **built from scratch** (no legacy stock master is imported); planned quantities are **seeded from each project's Bill of Quantities** (the PMG BOQ, §6) and the ledger grows as MRRs and MRs introduce off-BOQ items on the fly.

The primary screen is the **Stock Monitoring Ledger** (10.4) — one ledger per project or cost centre, showing each item's planned, undelivered, delivered, utilized, running-balance and variance figures across locations — mirroring the ARSD reference dashboard but driven by the JCE BOQ.

The module is organized into Phase 1 functional areas plus cross-cutting services, with a smaller deferred Phase 2 set:

- **Phase 1**: Stock Monitoring Ledger (BOQ-driven) · Inventory Item Master · Material Receiving Report (MRR = Goods Receipt) · Release Form · Multi-Location & two-sided Stock Transfer · Stock Movements · Stock Reservation · MR Stock-Verification Service · Document Lifecycle & Lock Gate · department-scoped Audit Log — plus cross-cutting roles, attachments, notifications, dashboard, and reporting.
- **Phase 2** (deferred): inventory reorder points & low-stock alerts, periodic stock-take / cycle counting, structured bin/location & barcode management, and a returnable-tool custody ledger — see 10.17.

### 10.2 Scope & Locked Decisions

**Carried over from the PMG scoping (2026-06-03):**

1. **The inventory master is built from scratch.** No existing stock master is imported. Planned quantities seed from the project BOQ; the ledger grows as MRRs/MRs add items on the fly; every receipt/issue adjusts stock. (Was §6.2 #2.)
2. **Inventory-first is enforced by the MR form.** The MR's `REQUESTED → AVAILABLE STOCK → FOR PURCHASE` columns require a stock check (the Warehouse service in 10.11) before any purchase quantity is produced. Only "For Purchase" lines flow to Purchasing. (Was §6.2 #3.)
3. **Stock-on-hand is derived, never typed.** It is the running balance of `StockMovement` records; only a movement can change it. (FR-STK-03.)
4. **Reservation is in v1.** `available = on_hand − reserved`; approving an MR reserves stock so a second MR cannot over-commit the same units. (FR-RSV-01.)
5. **Stock verification does not auto-update accomplishment %.** The Warehouse's actual stock/delivery state and the PM head's manually-entered accomplishment % are **not auto-synced** in v1; the only link is the read-only cross-check in §6.6.9. (Was §6.2 #1.)
6. **Signatures are offline.** Printed blocks (Verified by Warehouse, Received by) print for wet-signing; the system records status only. (Was §6.2 #8.)

**v1.61 decisions locked with Bryan (2026-06-03):**

7. **Ledger driven by BOQ, with manual additions.** Customer-project ledgers seed planned quantities from the PMG BOQ so Variance is meaningful; internal cost centres (`WORKSHOP`, `MOTORPOOL`, `JCE STOCK`) have no plan and run a free item master where the ledger is simply Delivered − Utilized = On Hand (no variance column). Off-BOQ rows may be added on the fly; they are **tagged "no plan," show Variance "—,"** and an admin may **promote** a row onto the plan (set a planned qty) — usually the signal of a Variation Order (PMG §4.4). (FR-LED-01..05.)
8. **Multi-location, Main Office central.** A central **Main Office** warehouse holds most stock, with per-site warehouses. On-hand is tracked **per location**; the ledger rolls all locations up per project. (FR-LOC.)
9. **Stock Transfer is two-sided.** Office→site moves run **Dispatch → (In Transit) → Confirm Receipt**; the destination confirms before the stock lands, so units in motion are visible and never double-counted. (FR-TRF.)
10. **3-state lock gate on every operational document.** MRR, Release Form, and Stock Transfer run **Draft → For Checking → Locked**. Site engineers create/submit; the Warehouse Admin/Owner reviews and Locks. Locked = immutable (system-wide strict-no-edit pattern); corrections require an **admin Unlock**, which is audit-logged. This is the ARSD Lock/Unlock toggle formalized. (FR-LCK.)
11. **The MRR _is_ the Goods Receipt.** The MRR is the single goods-receipt record Purchasing's three-way match (§7.15.1) needs — one record, two consumers. A **Locked** MRR completes the PO + Invoice + Receipt match and releases the Purchasing RFP. The warehouse and purchasing never log the same delivery twice. (FR-MRR-08.)
12. **Partial deliveries: one PO → many MRRs (cumulative).** A PO may be received across several MRRs; the three-way match tracks cumulative received vs. PO quantity. (Mirrors the real MRR Summary, where multiple MRRs share one PO#.) (FR-MRR-06.)
13. **Release recipient is free text + the releasing warehouseman.** "Received by" is a typed name (field crews are not all system users), captured alongside the warehouseman who released. (FR-REL-03.)
14. **Site engineers are scoped to assigned sites.** A site engineer sees and logs MRRs/Releases only for projects assigned to them (the ARSD warehouseman pattern); the Owner/Admin sees all sites. Off-BOQ rows an engineer adds are reviewed at the lock step. (FR-WXC roles; 10.14.1.)
15. **Numbering: continuous, year-prefixed.** MRR keeps the existing `MRR-YYYY-####` continuous sequence (matches the current MRR books, no monthly reset); Release Form is `REL-YYYY-####` continuous; Stock Transfer is `TRF-YYYY-####`. (FR-WXC-06.)
16. **Accountability Code is an optional field in v1.** Filled on only 1 of 232 historical MRRs; kept as an optional custody flag on the MRR/Release. A full returnable-tool custody ledger is **deferred to Phase 2** (10.17). (FR-MRR-09.)

### 10.3 Data Backbone & Entity Model

#### 10.3.1 Entities

| Entity                            | Purpose                                                                                                   | Key relationships                                                                            |
| --------------------------------- | --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- | -------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **InventoryItem**                 | Warehouse stock-master record (per project ledger or shared cost-centre master)                           | 1 Item → many StockMovement; → many MrLine (§6.10); → 0..1 BoqLine (§6)                      |
| **ProjectLedger**                 | The per-project / per-cost-centre Stock Monitoring view (seeds planned qty from BOQ)                      | 1 Project (§6.3.2) → many LedgerRow                                                          |
| **LedgerRow**                     | One item line in a ProjectLedger: planned, delivered, utilized, balance, variance                         | belongs to 1 ProjectLedger + 1 InventoryItem; 0..1 BoqLine (§6)                              |
| **MaterialReceivingReport (MRR)** | Receiving document = the **GoodsReceipt** (§7.15.1). Header + lines; carries PO#, Invoice#, MR#, Supplier | references 1 PO (§7.5), 1 MR (§6.10), 1 Project, 1 Location; → MRR lines → Receipt movements |
| **ReleaseForm**                   | Issuance document; items released to a named recipient                                                    | references 1 Project + 1 Location; → release lines → Issue movements                         |
| **StockTransfer**                 | Two-sided inter-location movement (dispatch + confirm receipt)                                            | references origin + destination Location; → paired Out/In movements                          |
| **Location** _(a.k.a. Warehouse)_ | A physical stock location — **Main Office (central)** or a site warehouse                                 | → many StockMovement; per-location balances                                                  |
| **StockMovement**                 | Immutable receipt / issue / transfer / adjustment affecting on-hand at one Location                       | belongs to 1 InventoryItem + 1 Location; references its source document                      |
| **StockReservation**              | Stock committed to an approved MR line but not yet issued                                                 | belongs to 1 InventoryItem + 1 MrLine (§6.10)                                                |
| **Attachment**                    | Uploaded file (DR photo, supplier invoice, delivery proof, adjustment evidence)                           | polymorphic — MRR / ReleaseForm / StockTransfer / InventoryItem / StockMovement              |
| **AuditLogEntry**                 | Immutable record of a state-changing action (incl. every Lock / Unlock)                                   | references actor + affected entity                                                           |
| **Unit** _(lookup)_               | Controlled unit list (lot, set, pcs, kg, assy…) — shared with §6.3.2                                      | referenced by InventoryItem, document lines                                                  |

Phase 2 introduces additional entities (`ReorderRule`, `StockCount`, `CustodyRecord`) — see 10.17.

#### 10.3.2 Relationship summary

```
Project (§6.3.2) (1) ──< ProjectLedger (1) ──< LedgerRow (many) >── InventoryItem
LedgerRow >── 0..1 BoqLine (§6)                 [planned qty seeded from BOQ; off-BOQ rows have none]

MRR (1) ──< MRR line (many) ── posts ─→ StockMovement (Receipt) @ Location
MRR >── 1 PO (§7.5)  ·  1 MR (§6.10)  ·  1 Supplier (§7.9)   [97% / 93% / 100% in source data]
MRR (Locked) ── completes ─→ Three-Way Match (§7.15.1) ── releases ─→ RFP (§7.7)

ReleaseForm (1) ──< release line (many) ── posts ─→ StockMovement (Issue) @ Location
StockTransfer  ── posts ─→ StockMovement (Transfer-Out @ origin) + (Transfer-In @ destination)

InventoryItem (1) ──< StockMovement (many)      [per Location]
InventoryItem (1) ──< StockReservation (many) >── MrLine (§6.10)
StockMovement (Issue) ── consumes ─→ StockReservation
{MRR, ReleaseForm, StockTransfer, InventoryItem, StockMovement} (1) ──< Attachment (many)
```

#### 10.3.3 The on-hand / reserved / available model

The three-way stock distinction that prevents double-commitment — now evaluated **per location**, with a project rollup:

| Quantity       | Definition                                            | Changed by                                                          |
| -------------- | ----------------------------------------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **On-hand**    | Physically at a Location right now                    | `StockMovement` only (Receipt +, Issue −, Transfer ±, Adjustment ±) |
| **Reserved**   | Committed to an approved MR line, not yet issued      | `StockReservation` create (+) / release (−)                         |
| **Available**  | `on_hand − reserved` — what a new request can draw on | derived; never stored as a free value                               |
| **In transit** | Dispatched on a Stock Transfer, not yet received      | open `StockTransfer` (counted at neither location's on-hand)        |

The MR form's **Available Stock** column (§6.10.2) reflects **available** (summed across locations unless the MR is location-specific), so a second MR sees stock already committed to a first.

### 10.4 Stock Monitoring Ledger

The **primary screen** of the module and the JCE analogue of the ARSD "Stock Monitoring Ledger." One ledger per project (or internal cost centre). For customer projects it is **seeded from the PMG Bill of Quantities** (§6) so each line carries a planned quantity; for cost centres it is a free item master with no plan.

#### 10.4.1 Columns

Per item row: **WBS / BOQ ref** (customer projects only) · **Item description** · **Resource / unit** · **Unit cost** (optional) · **Planned** (from BOQ) · **Undelivered** (`planned − delivered`) · **Delivered** (Σ Locked MRR receipts) · **Utilized** (Σ Locked Release issues) · **Running Balance** (`delivered − utilized`, the on-hand for this project) · **Variance** (`delivered − planned`, or "—" for off-BOQ / cost-centre rows). A **location breakdown** expands each row into per-Location on-hand (Main Office + each site).

#### 10.4.2 Requirements

- **FR-LED-01** — A customer project's ledger shall **seed planned quantities from its BOQ** (§6); each ledger row links to its `BoqLine`. Internal cost centres shall run a free item master with **no planned column and no Variance** (Running Balance = Delivered − Utilized).
- **FR-LED-02** — Delivered, Utilized, and Running Balance shall be **derived** from Locked MRR receipts and Locked Release issues respectively; they shall not be directly editable.
- **FR-LED-03** — `Variance = Delivered − Planned` for BOQ-backed rows. Off-BOQ rows (no `BoqLine`) shall display Variance as **"—"**, never a misleading negative.
- **FR-LED-04** — A site engineer or admin may **add an off-BOQ item row** while receiving; the row is tagged "no plan." (Engineer-added rows are reviewed at the MRR lock step, 10.12.)
- **FR-LED-05** — An admin may **promote** an off-BOQ row onto the plan by setting a planned quantity; the action is audit-logged and flagged as a likely Variation Order (PMG §4.4).
- **FR-LED-06** — The ledger shall roll up all Locations into the project figures and offer a per-Location breakdown (Main Office central + sites). A **cross-project / all-sites** view shall be available to Admin/Owner.
- **FR-LED-07** — The ledger shall be searchable by WBS, item, or resource, and exportable (mirrors the ARSD download).

#### 10.4.3 Acceptance criteria

- A customer project ledger opens pre-populated with its BOQ lines at Planned = BOQ qty, Delivered/Utilized = 0.
- Locking an MRR for a line increases that line's Delivered and Running Balance; locking a Release increases Utilized and decreases Running Balance.
- An off-BOQ item delivered to a project appears as a row with Variance "—"; after an admin sets a planned qty, Variance computes normally.
- A cost-centre ledger shows no Planned/Variance columns — only Delivered, Utilized, Running Balance.

### 10.5 Inventory Item Master

#### 10.5.1 Fields

Item code (system or manual), description, unit (FK to Unit lookup), per-Location stock-on-hand (derived, 10.3.3), optional category, optional notes. Customer-project items link to a `BoqLine`; cost-centre items are free.

#### 10.5.2 Requirements

- **FR-WIM-01** — The Warehouse role may perform full item-master CRUD: create items, edit descriptive fields, and deactivate items.
- **FR-WIM-02** — New items may be created **on the fly** from the PMG MR form (§6.10), an MRR (10.6), or directly on the ledger (10.4), and shall be persisted to the master immediately, reusable later.
- **FR-WIM-03** — Creating an item with no movements shall show stock-on-hand = 0 at every Location.
- **FR-WIM-04** — Item code shall be unique; the system shall prevent duplicate active items with the same code.

#### 10.5.3 Acceptance criteria

- Creating an item with no movements shows stock-on-hand = 0.
- An item added via the MR form or an MRR appears in the master immediately and is reusable later.

### 10.6 Material Receiving Report (MRR) — the Goods Receipt

The MRR is the receiving document and **the single GoodsReceipt record** in the system (decision 10.2 #11). It mirrors the company's MRR Summary and the ARSD "Delivery Receipt," but keeps JCE terminology and links. **One Locked MRR completes Purchasing's three-way match (§7.15.1) and releases the RFP (§7.7)** — warehouse and purchasing never log the same delivery twice.

#### 10.6.1 Fields

**Header:** MRR No. (`MRR-YYYY-####`, continuous), Receive Date, **Project** (FK §6.3.2), **Receiving Location** (Main Office or a site), Supplier (FK §7.9), **PO Number** (FK §7.5), **MR Number** (FK §6.10), **Invoice Number**, Requested By, **Accountability Code** (optional, 10.2 #16), Purpose, Warehouseman (logger), **DR Photo** + **Delivery Proof** attachments (ARSD reference). **Lines:** item (existing or off-BOQ new), description/particulars, quantity received, unit. **Status:** Draft → For Checking → Locked (10.12).

#### 10.6.2 Requirements

- **FR-MRR-01** — A site engineer may create an MRR for an **assigned** project (10.14.1); it logs to that project's ledger and the chosen Receiving Location.
- **FR-MRR-02** — Each MRR line shall reference an existing `InventoryItem`/`BoqLine` or create an **off-BOQ** item on the fly (FR-LED-04).
- **FR-MRR-03** — On **Lock**, each line shall post a Receipt `StockMovement` at the Receiving Location, increasing Delivered and Running Balance on the ledger (10.4). A Draft/For-Checking MRR posts **nothing**.
- **FR-MRR-04** — The header shall carry PO#, MR#, and Invoice# as **foreign keys** (soft-FK fallback where the referenced record is not yet in-system), matching the 97% / 93% / 95% fill rates of the source data.
- **FR-MRR-05** — DR Photo is required to Lock; Delivery Proof is optional (mirrors the ARSD record).
- **FR-MRR-06** — **Partial deliveries:** one PO may be received across **multiple MRRs**; the system shall track cumulative received quantity per PO line. (Decision 10.2 #12.)
- **FR-MRR-07** — Editing is allowed only in Draft/For Checking; a Locked MRR is immutable and requires an admin Unlock to correct (10.12).
- **FR-MRR-08** — A **Locked** MRR shall satisfy the _Receipt_ leg of Purchasing's three-way match (PO + Invoice + Receipt, §7.15.1); when the match is complete for a PO, the corresponding RFP becomes submittable. No separate GoodsReceipt entry is made in Purchasing.
- **FR-MRR-09** — Accountability Code is an optional field; no custody ledger is built in v1 (Phase 2, 10.17).

#### 10.6.3 Acceptance criteria

- Locking an MRR posts Receipt movements, increases the ledger's Delivered, and (when PO + Invoice + Receipt all present) makes the PO's RFP submittable in Purchasing.
- A Draft MRR changes no stock figures.
- Two MRRs against the same PO accumulate toward that PO's received quantity; neither over-receives silently.

### 10.7 Release Form

The issuance document — the JCE analogue of the ARSD "Release Form." Releasing stock drives the ledger's **Utilized** column.

#### 10.7.1 Fields

Release No. (`REL-YYYY-####`, continuous), Date, **Project** + **Releasing Location**, **Received By** (free-text name, 10.2 #13), Warehouseman (releaser), lines (item, description, quantity, unit), optional signed-slip attachment. Status: Draft → For Checking → Locked.

#### 10.7.2 Requirements

- **FR-REL-01** — A site engineer may create a Release Form for an **assigned** project; on **Lock** each line posts an Issue `StockMovement` at the Releasing Location, increasing Utilized and decreasing Running Balance (10.4).
- **FR-REL-02** — A Release shall not drive a Location's on-hand below zero unless an admin override is exercised (ties to negative-stock guard, 10.16 #1).
- **FR-REL-03** — **Received By** is a typed name; the releasing Warehouseman is recorded from the logged-in user. Neither requires the recipient to be a system user.
- **FR-REL-04** — Editing is allowed only in Draft/For Checking; a Locked Release is immutable (admin Unlock to correct).

#### 10.7.3 Acceptance criteria

- Locking a Release posts Issue movements and increases the ledger's Utilized for those items.
- A release that would drive on-hand negative is blocked unless an admin overrides.

### 10.8 Multi-Location & Stock Transfer

Most stock sits at the central **Main Office**; site warehouses hold what each project needs. On-hand is tracked per Location, and stock moves between them on a **two-sided** transfer (decision 10.2 #8–9).

#### 10.8.1 Requirements

- **FR-LOC-01** — The system shall maintain a list of **Locations**, with **Main Office** designated the central warehouse; every stock figure is held per Location and rolled up on the ledger (FR-LED-06).
- **FR-LOC-02** — An MRR may receive to, and a Release may issue from, any Location the actor is permitted to act on.
- **FR-TRF-01** — A **Stock Transfer** moves items from an origin Location to a destination Location in two steps: **Dispatch** (origin) → **Confirm Receipt** (destination). Between the two, the quantity is **In Transit** and counted at neither Location's on-hand (10.3.3).
- **FR-TRF-02** — Dispatch posts a Transfer-Out movement at the origin; Confirm Receipt posts the matching Transfer-In at the destination. A transfer is only complete when received.
- **FR-TRF-03** — The destination may record a **received quantity** that differs from dispatched (shortage/damage), raising a discrepancy flag for admin review rather than silently adjusting.
- **FR-TRF-04** — A Stock Transfer follows the same Draft → For Checking → Locked lifecycle on each side (10.12); numbering `TRF-YYYY-####`.

#### 10.8.2 Acceptance criteria

- A dispatched-but-unreceived transfer shows its quantity as In Transit, absent from both Locations' on-hand.
- Confirming receipt lands the stock at the destination; a received-vs-dispatched mismatch raises a discrepancy flag.

### 10.9 Stock Movements

#### 10.9.1 Movement model

Each `StockMovement` carries: type (`Receipt | Issue | Transfer-Out | Transfer-In | Adjustment`), quantity, date, actor, **Location**, source-document reference (MRR / Release / Transfer / manual), and a **reason** (required for Adjustments). Movements are posted by **locking** the document above them, not entered directly — except Adjustments, which the Warehouse posts directly with a reason.

#### 10.9.2 Requirements

- **FR-STK-01** — A Receipt shall increase on-hand at its Location; an Issue shall decrease it; a Transfer shall move it between Locations; an Adjustment shall apply a signed delta with a mandatory reason.
- **FR-STK-02** — Every movement is **immutable** once posted; corrections are made by posting a compensating movement (or unlocking the source document), never by editing history.
- **FR-STK-03** — On-hand shall always equal the sum of its movements at a Location and shall **not be directly editable**; only a movement changes it.
- **FR-STK-04** — Each movement shall record actor, timestamp, Location, and source reference; Adjustments shall additionally require a reason.

#### 10.9.3 Acceptance criteria

- A Receipt then an Issue at one Location leaves on-hand = received − issued there.
- On-hand cannot be edited directly; only movements (via document locks or Adjustments) change it.
- An Adjustment without a reason is rejected.

### 10.10 Stock Reservation

#### 10.10.1 Requirements

- **FR-RSV-01** — `available = on_hand − reserved` at all times.
- **FR-RSV-02** — Approving an MR (§6.10) shall create a `StockReservation` against each in-stock line, decrementing **available** (not on-hand).
- **FR-RSV-03** — Fulfilling a reserved line shall post an Issue (via a Release Form, 10.7), which **consumes the reservation** and decrements on-hand.
- **FR-RSV-04** — Cancelling or closing an MR shall **release** its outstanding reservations back to available.
- **FR-RSV-05** — Two MRs shall not reserve the same units — the second sees the reduced available and cannot over-commit.

#### 10.10.2 Acceptance criteria

- `available = on_hand − reserved` at all times; approving an MR reduces available and cancelling it restores available.
- Two MRs cannot reserve the same units — the second sees reduced available and cannot over-commit.
- Issuing a reserved line (Release) decrements on-hand and clears the reservation.

### 10.11 MR Stock-Verification Service

This is the Warehouse's side of the inventory-first MR workflow. PMG authors the MR (§6.10); the Warehouse service supplies live stock figures and executes the reserve/issue lifecycle. The MR header, line columns, canvass sheet, numbering, and "For Purchase" computation are defined in §6.10 and are **not** duplicated here.

#### 10.11.1 Requirements

- **FR-WMR-01** — On item selection in an MR line, the service shall return **Available Stock** = `on_hand − reserved` from the live master.
- **FR-WMR-02** — The service shall expose the figures PMG needs to compute `For Purchase = max(0, Requested − Available)` (the computation itself lives in §6.10, FR-MR-02).
- **FR-WMR-03** — On MR approval, the service shall reserve the in-stock quantity per line (FR-RSV-02); on fulfillment it shall issue against the reservation via a Release Form (FR-RSV-03).
- **FR-WMR-04** — A line with Available ≥ Requested produces zero "For Purchase" and no Purchasing hand-off (mirrors §6.10 FR-MR-04).
- **FR-WMR-05** — The Warehouse records a **"Verified by Warehouse"** status on the MR (offline signature; status-only, 10.2 #6).

#### 10.11.2 Acceptance criteria

- Selecting an existing item on an MR returns Available Stock = on-hand − reserved from the live master.
- Approving an MR reserves the in-stock quantities; fulfilling them (Release) posts Issues and clears the reservations.
- A fully-stocked line generates no Purchasing hand-off.

### 10.12 Document Lifecycle & Lock Gate

Every operational document — MRR (10.6), Release Form (10.7), and Stock Transfer (10.8) — runs the same three-state gate, the formalized ARSD Lock/Unlock toggle (decision 10.2 #10).

#### 10.12.1 The three states

| State            | Who                     | Meaning                                                                                                      |
| ---------------- | ----------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------ |
| **Draft**        | Site engineer           | Being filled in; editable; posts no stock movements.                                                         |
| **For Checking** | Site engineer submits   | Awaiting Warehouse Admin/Owner review; still posts nothing.                                                  |
| **Locked**       | Warehouse Admin / Owner | Reviewed and accepted; **immutable**; **posts its stock movements** and (for MRR) feeds the three-way match. |

#### 10.12.2 Requirements

- **FR-LCK-01** — Stock movements and ledger effects post **only on Lock**, never in Draft or For Checking.
- **FR-LCK-02** — A Locked document is **immutable**; correcting it requires an **Unlock**, permitted to **Admin/Owner only**.
- **FR-LCK-03** — Unlocking a posted document shall reverse its stock movements (or block the unlock if doing so would drive on-hand negative, prompting a compensating action); re-locking re-posts them.
- **FR-LCK-04** — Every Lock and Unlock shall write an `AuditLogEntry` with actor, timestamp, and document reference (10.13).
- **FR-LCK-05** — Site engineers may create and submit (Draft → For Checking) but may **not** Lock or Unlock; those are Admin/Owner actions.

#### 10.12.3 Acceptance criteria

- A For-Checking MRR/Release/Transfer has posted no stock movements; locking it posts them.
- A site engineer cannot Lock; only Admin/Owner can.
- Unlocking a locked MRR reverses its receipts and reopens the three-way match; both lock and unlock appear in the audit log.

### 10.13 Audit Log (Department-Scoped)

- **FR-WLG-01** — The system shall record an immutable `AuditLogEntry` for each state-changing action: item create/edit/deactivate, every stock movement, reservation create/release, MR verification, **every document Lock and Unlock** (10.12), and **off-BOQ row add / promote-to-plan** (10.4).
- **FR-WLG-02** — Each entry shall capture actor, timestamp, affected entity, and before/after values where applicable.
- **FR-WLG-03** — Audit entries shall be append-only (not editable or deletable through the application). Retention horizon defers to the global policy (Section 12 / Appendix open items).

### 10.14 Cross-Cutting Requirements

#### 10.14.1 Roles & permissions

| Role                | Capability                                                                                                                                                                      |
| ------------------- | ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| **Site Engineer**   | Create and submit (Draft → For Checking) MRRs and Release Forms for **assigned projects only** (10.2 #14); add off-BOQ rows; read their sites' ledgers. **Cannot Lock/Unlock.** |
| **Warehouse Admin** | All site-engineer powers across **all** sites; **Lock / Unlock** documents; promote off-BOQ rows onto the plan; maintain the item master; post Adjustments; confirm transfers.  |
| **PMG Staff**       | Author MRs (§6.10); read stock figures via the verification service; may create items on the fly (FR-WIM-02).                                                                   |
| **Purchasing**      | Reads For-Purchase hand-offs and the three-way-match state fed by Locked MRRs (§7.15.1); does **not** re-enter receipts.                                                        |
| **Department Head** | Approves MRs (offline; system records status).                                                                                                                                  |
| **Owner**           | Full access — all sites, all actions.                                                                                                                                           |

The full role × action matrix is produced centrally in Section 3 (RBAC); this table is the Warehouse input to it.

#### 10.14.2 Attachments

- **FR-WXC-01** — Files may be attached to MRRs (DR Photo, Delivery Proof, supplier invoice), Release Forms (signed slip), Stock Transfers, items, and movements. The `Attachment` entity is polymorphic (10.3.1). DR Photo is required to Lock an MRR (FR-MRR-05).

#### 10.14.3 Notifications & alerts

- **FR-WXC-02** — The system shall raise alerts for: a document **awaiting checking/lock**, an MR awaiting Warehouse verification, a **transfer dispatched but unconfirmed** beyond a threshold, a **transfer discrepancy** (FR-TRF-03), a reservation outstanding beyond a threshold, and (Phase 2) low stock against a reorder point (10.17).

#### 10.14.4 Dashboard

- **FR-WXC-03** — The Warehouse landing page shall present **project / cost-centre cards** (the ARSD home pattern) plus: documents awaiting checking/lock, open MRs awaiting verification, transfers in transit, items with outstanding reservations, and (Phase 2) items below reorder point. Site engineers see only their assigned projects.

#### 10.14.5 Reporting

- **FR-WXC-04** — The module shall provide: the **Stock Monitoring Ledger** per project and an all-sites rollup (10.4); a per-Location on-hand / reserved / available report; a stock-movement ledger per item (running balance); registers of MRRs, Releases, and Transfers; and an MR-fulfillment register. All exportable.

#### 10.14.6 Numbering & units

- **FR-WXC-05** — Quantities use the controlled Unit lookup (shared with §6.3.2), displayed at the precision the warehouse keeps stock in.
- **FR-WXC-06** — Document numbering is **continuous, year-prefixed**: MRR `MRR-YYYY-####`, Release `REL-YYYY-####`, Transfer `TRF-YYYY-####` — no monthly reset (10.2 #15).

### 10.15 Resolved Decisions

1. **Inventory master built from scratch**; planned qty seeds from the BOQ; items may be added on the fly. (10.2 #1/#7, FR-WIM-02, FR-LED-01)
2. **Inventory-first** is enforced by the MR form via the Warehouse verification service. (10.2 #2, FR-WMR-01)
3. **Stock-on-hand is movement-derived**, never directly editable; posts only on document Lock. (10.2 #3/#10, FR-STK-03, FR-LCK-01)
4. **Reservation in v1** — `available = on-hand − reserved`; MR approval reserves stock. (10.2 #4, FR-RSV-01)
5. **No auto-sync** between physical stock/delivery and the PM head's accomplishment % — only the read-only cross-check (§6.6.9). (10.2 #5)
6. **Warehouse/receiving signatures are offline**; the system records status only. (10.2 #6)
7. **Warehouse is the authoritative owner** of the inventory entities; PMG (§6.10) and Purchasing (§7) reference these. (Promotion decision, v1.60)
8. **Ledger is BOQ-driven with off-BOQ additions**; off-BOQ rows show Variance "—" and can be promoted to plan. (10.2 #7, FR-LED-03/05)
9. **Multi-location with Main Office central**; two-sided Stock Transfer with an In-Transit state. (10.2 #8/#9, FR-LOC/FR-TRF)
10. **3-state lock gate** (Draft → For Checking → Locked) on MRR/Release/Transfer; admin-only Unlock, audit-logged. (10.2 #10, FR-LCK)
11. **MRR is the single Goods Receipt** completing Purchasing's three-way match; no double entry. (10.2 #11, FR-MRR-08)
12. **Partial deliveries** — one PO → many MRRs, cumulative match. (10.2 #12, FR-MRR-06)
13. **Release "Received By" is free text** + the releasing warehouseman. (10.2 #13, FR-REL-03)
14. **Site engineers scoped to assigned sites.** (10.2 #14, 10.14.1)
15. **Continuous year-prefixed numbering.** (10.2 #15, FR-WXC-06)
16. **Accountability Code optional in v1**; custody ledger deferred. (10.2 #16, FR-MRR-09)

### 10.16 Open Questions for SRS Finalization _(all resolved — finalization sweep)_

1. ✅ **Negative-stock guard — RESOLVED: hard-block with admin override.** A Release/Transfer that would drive a Location's on-hand below zero is blocked by default; an admin override (recorded with a reason) covers known receipt-timing gaps (FR-REL-02).
2. ✅ **Off-BOQ promotion ↔ Variation Order — RESOLVED: flag in v1.** Promoting an off-BOQ ledger row to plan flags it for the PM head; auto-drafting a PMG Variation Order is deferred to Phase 2 (§10.17.5).
3. ✅ **MRR PO/MR mismatch — RESOLVED: warn-and-allow with discrepancy flag.** An MRR that doesn't fully reconcile to its referenced PO/MR lines may still be locked, but raises a discrepancy flag to admin.
4. ✅ **Cross-project / Main Office stock — RESOLVED: project-scoped balances, JCE STOCK central.** Stock is tracked per project / cost-centre (not one shared pool); non-project receipts are held under a `JCE STOCK` cost-centre ledger at Main Office and Stock-Transferred to the site/project as needed.
5. ✅ **Item code scheme — RESOLVED: system-generated only.** The system assigns every item code; users cannot set or edit them, guaranteeing uniqueness (FR-WIM-04).
6. ✅ **MR# cleanup — RESOLVED: standardize on PMG MR numbering.** The PMG MR numbering (§6.10) is the hard-FK source going forward; legacy strings are display-only on migrated rows.

### 10.17 Phase 2 — Extended Scope

Built on the Phase 1 backbone above; approved for a second phase. (Note: multi-location, the two-sided transfer, and the formal goods-receiving / three-way-match workflow were **promoted into Phase 1** in v1.61 and are no longer here.)

#### 10.17.0 New entities introduced in Phase 2

| Entity            | Purpose                                            | Key relationships                               |
| ----------------- | -------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------- |
| **ReorderRule**   | Per-item minimum level / reorder point             | belongs to 1 InventoryItem (per Location)       |
| **StockCount**    | A periodic stock-take / cycle-count event          | → variance Adjustments                          |
| **CustodyRecord** | Returnable tool/equipment custody (who holds what) | references InventoryItem + holder; Return event |

#### 10.17.1 Inventory reorder points & low-stock alerts

Per-item, per-Location minimum levels (`ReorderRule`); auto-flag / auto-draft replenishment requests, feeding the notification in FR-WXC-02. (Aligns with §6.15.5.)

#### 10.17.2 Periodic stock-take / cycle counting

Scheduled physical counts (`StockCount`) that reconcile counted vs. system on-hand and post variance Adjustments with audit trail.

#### 10.17.3 Returnable-tool custody ledger

Promote the optional Accountability Code (10.2 #16) into a `CustodyRecord` ledger: a release of a returnable tool marks it OUT to a holder; a Return event puts it back on hand.

#### 10.17.4 Bin / location & barcode management

Structured bin locations **within** a Location, and barcode/QR scanning for faster, lower-error receipts, issues, and counts.

#### 10.17.5 Off-BOQ → Variation Order auto-draft

Promoting an off-BOQ ledger row to plan auto-drafts a PMG Variation Order (§6 §4.4) for the PM head's confirmation (10.16 #2).

---

## 11. Public Website Component _(scoped — v1.71)_

The Public Website is **component 1 of the two-part JCE System** (§1.3) — the external, public-facing site, distinct from BDD's internal §9.2 _Website Details_ (which is the content-management side). The website **renders the Published content BDD curates** (projects, products & services) and **feeds visitor inquiries back into BDD's §9.2.3 inbox**. Its focus is being found and accurately represented across both traditional search engines (SEO) and AI-driven answer tools (GEO). Requirement codes use the `FR-WEB` prefix.

### 11.1 Module Overview

A marketing/informational website that showcases JCE's capabilities, surfaces its project portfolio and product/service catalog, publishes news and careers content, and captures leads through a public inquiry form. It is **not** a separate content store: the dashboard (BDD §9.2) is the single source of truth, and the website reads from it. The build covers the site structure, design, the inquiry pipeline, and technical SEO + GEO; ongoing content marketing/ad campaigns are a separate, later effort.

### 11.2 Site Scope & Pages

- **Home** — company overview, key capabilities, featured projects/products, calls to action.
- **About** — company profile, history, certifications/accreditations, key clients, leadership.
- **Projects (Showcase)** — portfolio rendered from BDD's Published Projects (§9.2.1).
- **Products & Major Services** — catalog rendered from BDD's Published Products & Services (§9.2.2): transformers, substation & transmission-line design/build (up to 230 KV), switchgear (HV/MV/LV), solar/renewables, maintenance & servicing, direct-connection applications, engineering consultancy.
- **News / Blog** — articles and updates (supports SEO/GEO content).
- **Careers** — open positions and an application path.
- **Contact / Inquiry** — the public inquiry form (11.4).

**FR-WEB-01** — The site shall present the pages above with consistent navigation and a mobile-responsive layout.

### 11.3 Content Integration with BDD

- **FR-WEB-02** — The website shall render **Published** Projects, Products, and Services **live from BDD §9.2**; only records BDD marks Published appear publicly (drafts/internal records never surface).
- **FR-WEB-03** — The BDD dashboard is the **CMS** — BDD authors/curates content there (§9.2); there is no separate website content store.
- **FR-WEB-04** — Submitted inquiries POST directly into BDD's **§9.2.3 Inquiries inbox** as new inquiry records, with system metadata (timestamp, source page, status = New).

### 11.4 Public Inquiry Form

The public form adopts the field set already drafted in **BDD §9.2.3** (confirmed here):

- _Contact:_ Name*, Company/Organization*, Position (optional), Email*, Phone*, Industry (optional dropdown).
- _Inquiry details:_ Inquiry Type* (aligned to JCE offerings), Project Location (optional), Estimated Timeline (optional), Subject*, Message\*.
- _Qualifiers (optional):_ Budget Range (PHP), How did you hear about us?
- _(\* = required.)_

**FR-WEB-05** — The form shall validate required fields and include anti-spam protection (e.g., CAPTCHA / honeypot) before creating a BDD inquiry record.
**FR-WEB-06** — On submission, the inquirer receives an acknowledgement and BDD is notified per the BDD notification rules (§9.9).

### 11.5 Search Engine Optimization (SEO) — strategy & process

**Keyword themes** (from JCE's services, credentials, and markets):

- _Capability + scope:_ "power substation design and construction Philippines," "transmission line construction up to 230 kV," "power transformer supplier Philippines," "distribution / current / potential transformer supplier," "HVSG MVSG LVSG fabrication," "NGCP direct connection 69 kV," "solar farm EPC / testing and commissioning Philippines," "substation maintenance and servicing," "Shenda Electric power transformer distributor Philippines."
- _Audience segments:_ electric cooperatives, manufacturing plants, solar/renewable developers, industrial & commercial, and government / public sector.
- _Local:_ "electrical contractor Valenzuela / Metro Manila," paired with nationwide project coverage.

- **FR-WEB-07** _(technical / on-page)_ — Clean semantic markup; a unique title + meta description per page; correct heading hierarchy; descriptive, keyword-relevant URLs; image alt text; internal linking between Service pages and related Project case studies; auto-generated `sitemap.xml` and `robots.txt`; canonical URLs; strong Core Web Vitals (fast load, mobile-first); HTTPS.
- **FR-WEB-08** — Human-readable, keyword-relevant URLs and headings for the core service / industry terms above.
- **FR-WEB-14 — Content plan.** A dedicated **Service page per major capability** (transformers; substations ≤ 230 kV; transmission lines; switchgear HV/MV/LV; NGCP direct connection; solar / renewable EPC & consultancy; maintenance & servicing) and the **portfolio rendered as case-study pages** (e.g., "120 MWp Gigasol Alaminos Solar Farm," "Pilipinas Shell 20/25 MVA 69 kV substation," "NGCP Cebu-Negros-Panay 230 kV backbone"), each with scope, capacity/rating, location, and client. The News/Blog supplies fresh, indexable content.
- **FR-WEB-15 — Local SEO.** Consistent **NAP** across the site and directories (JC Electrofields Power System, Inc. · 3074 F. Bautista St., Brgy Ugong, Valenzuela City, Metro Manila · (02) 8562-8540 · sales@jcepower.com), a claimed/optimized **Google Business Profile**, and `LocalBusiness` structured data (§11.6).

### 11.6 Generative Engine Optimization (GEO) — strategy & process

GEO ensures AI answer engines (ChatGPT, Gemini, Perplexity, Google AI Overviews) **find and accurately represent** JCE.

- **FR-WEB-16 — Canonical entity & facts.** Publish a single authoritative description AI tools can extract: _JC Electrofields Power System, Inc. (JCEPSI), founded 1997, headquartered in Valenzuela City, Metro Manila; ~124 engineers and technicians; led by President Engr. Jimwel C. Capillo. Designs and constructs **power substations and transmission lines up to 230 kV**; supplies power / distribution / CT / PT transformers and HVSG/MVSG/LVSG; provides **NGCP direct-connection (69 kV)**, solar / renewable-energy EPC & consultancy, and substation / plant-electrical maintenance; **exclusive Philippine distributor of Shenda Electric power transformers**._ Entity naming is kept consistent sitewide.
- **FR-WEB-09** _(structured data)_ — schema.org **`Organization` + `LocalBusiness`** (NAP, `foundingDate` 1997, logo, `sameAs` to social/LinkedIn), **`Service`** per capability, **`Project` / `CreativeWork`** per portfolio case study (with location and capacity), **`FAQPage`**, and `BreadcrumbList`.
- **FR-WEB-10 / FR-WEB-17 — Content for extraction.** Clear, factual, self-contained blocks: a capabilities fact-sheet, a nationwide coverage statement, and an **FAQ** answering the questions buyers ask AI ("Who builds power substations up to 230 kV in the Philippines?", "Is there an NGCP direct-connection (69 kV) contractor?", "Where is JC Electrofields located?"). Proof points — utility-scale solar farms (including the country's first largest urban solar farm, in Valenzuela), substations for Pilipinas Shell, BATELEC, LUELCO and BOHECO, and the NGCP 230 kV backbone — are stated as plain, extractable facts.
- **FR-WEB-18 — Monitoring & measurement.** Track SEO KPIs (organic sessions, target-keyword rankings, indexed pages and Core Web Vitals via Search Console, and inquiry conversions) **and** periodically query the major AI assistants to verify JCE is surfaced and accurately described, correcting content where it is missing or wrong.

**Process & ownership.** BDD authors and curates the content (services, projects, news) in the dashboard (§9.2); the web/dev team implements and maintains technical SEO + structured data; SEO/GEO performance is reviewed on a regular cadence (e.g., quarterly) against the KPIs above.

### 11.7 Branding & Design Direction

- **FR-WEB-11** — Visual identity aligned with the JCE brand and consistent with the internal dashboard (logo, color, typography). The site follows the **system-wide glassmorphism design language** — green/orange/white, glass chrome over solid content surfaces — defined in **§12.9**. Brand assets and guidelines are **client-input** (11.9).

### 11.8 Hosting, Performance & Non-Functional

- **FR-WEB-12** — The site shall meet the system-wide NFRs (§12): performance/load times, security (HTTPS/TLS), availability, and accessibility. The hosting platform/domain arrangement is a deployment decision outside this SRS (11.9).
- **FR-WEB-13** — Language: **English (Philippine context)** in v1; a Filipino/bilingual option is a later enhancement.

### 11.9 Client-Input & Pending Items

JCE to provide before/at build: existing site **content & assets** (logo, project photos, copy, certifications), **brand guidelines**, **domain & hosting** access, **analytics** access (e.g., Google Analytics/Search Console), and ownership/cadence for **News** and **Careers** content. The exact target keyword set and any redirect map from the current site are confirmed during build.

### 11.10 Acceptance Criteria

- Only BDD-Published projects/products/services appear on the public site; unpublished records never surface.
- A completed inquiry form creates a new record in BDD's §9.2.3 inbox and acknowledges the visitor.
- Each page exposes correct title/meta/canonical and is included in the sitemap; schema.org structured data validates.
- The site is mobile-responsive and meets the §12 performance and security targets.

---

## 12. Non-Functional Requirements

Non-functional requirements describe _how well_ the system should work, rather than _what_ it does. The following are reasonable expectations for an internal business system of this kind. Specific targets (exact numbers and thresholds) should be confirmed with the company owner; where they are not yet set, they are marked TBD.

### 12.1 Performance

- **NFR-PERF-01** — Common interactions (open a record, submit a request, run a routine search) shall respond in **under 2 seconds** under normal load; heavier operations (multi-period reports, large registers) in **under 5 seconds**.
- **NFR-PERF-02** — List and search results shall paginate and use indexed queries so response time stays roughly constant as data grows (employee records, time entries, transactions, ~970+ import rows).
- **NFR-PERF-03** — Document/PDF generation (PO, RFP, accomplishment report, vouchers) shall complete in **under 5 seconds**.
- **NFR-PERF-04** — The system shall support at least **50 concurrent users** without noticeable degradation. _(Confirm peak concurrency with the owner.)_

### 12.2 Security

- **NFR-SEC-01** — Access requires authenticated login; passwords are stored **hashed** (e.g., bcrypt/argon2), never in plaintext.
- **NFR-SEC-02** — Password policy (minimum length/complexity), failed-attempt **account lockout**, and secure password reset.
- **NFR-SEC-03** — All traffic over **HTTPS/TLS 1.2+**; sessions use secure cookies with an **idle timeout** (default 30 min, configurable).
- **NFR-SEC-04** — RBAC (Section 3) is enforced **server-side** on every request (least privilege); client-side hiding is never the only control.
- **NFR-SEC-05** — Sensitive fields at rest — compensation, bank-account details — are **encrypted**; access follows §3.7.
- **NFR-SEC-06** — Security-relevant events (logins, permission/role changes, bank-detail changes) are logged (ties to §12.7).
- **NFR-SEC-07** — Application guards against common web vulnerabilities (OWASP Top 10: injection, XSS, CSRF, broken access control). MFA for **Owner/Admin** is recommended. _(Confirm.)_

### 12.3 Data Privacy (RA 10173)

- **NFR-PRIV-01** — The system shall comply with the Philippine **Data Privacy Act (RA 10173)** for processing employee and client personal data.
- **NFR-PRIV-02** — Personal/sensitive information is access-restricted via RBAC; compensation is limited to Payroll Officer + Owner (§3.7).
- **NFR-PRIV-03** — Support data-subject rights in practice (access and correction of one's own personal data — aligns with employee self-service).
- **NFR-PRIV-04** — Defined **retention periods** for personal data and secure disposal when no longer required.
- **NFR-PRIV-05** — A breach-response process and an organizational Data Protection Officer are assumed (organizational, outside the software). _(Confirm.)_

### 12.4 Availability, Backup & Disaster Recovery

- **NFR-AVL-01** — Dependable during business hours; target uptime **≥ 99%** during those hours. _(Confirm window, e.g., Mon–Sat 7am–7pm.)_
- **NFR-AVL-02** — Maintenance scheduled **outside business hours** with advance notice.
- **NFR-AVL-03** — **Automated daily backups** with a defined retention (recommended: 30 daily + 12 monthly).
- **NFR-AVL-04** — Recovery objectives: **RPO ≤ 24 hours, RTO ≤ 1 business day**. _(Confirm.)_
- **NFR-AVL-05** — Backups are **restore-tested** periodically.
- **NFR-AVL-06** — Transactional integrity is guaranteed for critical operations — stock-on-hand vs. movements, period locking, and ledger postings are **atomic and consistent** (ties to each module's NFR notes).

### 12.5 Scalability

- **NFR-SCALE-01** — Accommodate growth in employees, users, and transaction volume without re-architecture. _(Confirm target figures.)_
- **NFR-SCALE-02** — **Modular architecture**: the pending modules (Engineering, Public Website) and Phase-2 features attach to the existing backbone without rebuilds.
- **NFR-SCALE-03** — Supports **full-history** data retention (per the migration decision) with archival of cold data to keep the live store performant.

### 12.6 Usability & Accessibility

- **NFR-USE-01** — Understandable to non-technical staff; **consistent layouts** across modules so learning one eases the others.
- **NFR-USE-02** — Printable outputs match their source where required — **byte-faithful** accomplishment report (§6), and BIR/statutory forms (§5).
- **NFR-USE-03** — Optimized for **desktop** (primary) and usable on tablet; supports current versions of Chrome, Edge, Firefox, and Safari.
- **NFR-USE-04** — **Philippine locale**: ₱ currency formatting, PH date formats, and amount-in-words in PHP.
- **NFR-USE-05** — Reasonable accessibility practices (keyboard navigation, sufficient contrast, labeled inputs). _(Confirm any formal WCAG level.)_

### 12.7 Auditability & Traceability

- **NFR-AUDIT-01** — **Immutable, append-only audit logs** across modules capturing actor, timestamp, action, and before/after values for state-changing operations.
- **NFR-AUDIT-02** — Audit retention is **time-bounded with archival** (finalization decision; the BDD §9.6 'indefinite' note is reconciled to this policy).
- **NFR-AUDIT-03** — Document status lifecycles are fully tracked even though approvals/signatures are executed **offline**.

### 12.8 Maintainability & Supportability

- **NFR-MNT-01** — Business configuration (rates, contribution tables, lookups, signatories, approval thresholds) is **externalized** and admin-editable — no hard-coded values (per the Accounting Settings principle, §5.2.1).
- **NFR-MNT-02** — Core computations (payroll, BOQ weights, progress billing, tax) are **deterministic and reproducible** given the same inputs and effective-dated configuration.
- **NFR-MNT-03** — Reference data is **versioned by effectivity date** so historical periods reproduce figures correct at the time.

### 12.9 User Interface Design Language (Glassmorphism)

A single visual language applies across **both** the public website (§11) and the internal dashboard (all department modules), so JCE presents one cohesive, modern identity.

- **NFR-UI-01 — Palette.** Brand colors are **green (primary), orange (accent), and white**, applied consistently across the website and dashboard. (Exact hex values per JCE brand guidelines — client-input.)
- **NFR-UI-02 — Glass chrome.** Framing and navigation surfaces — top bars, sidebars, nav, cards, modals, and section headers — use a **frosted-glass (glassmorphism)** treatment: translucent panels with background blur, subtle light borders, and rounded corners.
- **NFR-UI-03 — Solid content surfaces.** **Data-dense content — tables, ledgers, forms, and reports (accomplishment reports, stock ledgers, PO/voucher registers, payslips) — uses high-contrast solid surfaces, not glass**, to protect legibility. The rule of thumb: _glass for chrome, solid for content._
- **NFR-UI-04 — Motion.** Subtle, immersive transitions are allowed (fade-ins, hover lift, gentle backdrop motion) but must be tasteful and **must honor `prefers-reduced-motion`** (no motion for users who opt out).
- **NFR-UI-05 — Legibility & contrast.** Text on glass shall meet **WCAG AA** contrast; panel opacity/tint is set high enough to guarantee it. Glass is never used where it would compromise reading figures or filling forms.
- **NFR-UI-06 — Performance.** `backdrop-filter` blur is GPU-intensive; the number of simultaneously blurred layers is capped and a **solid-color fallback** is provided where `backdrop-filter` is unsupported or the device is low-powered (ties to §12.1).
- **NFR-UI-07 — Consistency via a shared component library.** Buttons, cards, nav, tables, forms, and modals are implemented **once** in a shared library and reused by every module and the website, so learning one screen carries to the rest.

| 1.8 — Draft  | May 25, 2026 | _[Prepared by]_ | **Pragmatic simplification.** Day Type is now **timekeeper-selected from a dropdown beside Date** (six values), not system-derived — the system does not maintain an automated holiday calendar. **Rest Day is added back as a Day Type value** (= Sunday, always for all employees), not a separate flag. Holiday calendar configuration and per-employee rest-day schedule removed as derived requirements. Compound-case handling (e.g., Regular Holiday on Sunday) flagged for confirmation.                                                                                                        |
| 1.9 — Draft  | May 25, 2026 | _[Prepared by]_ | **Travel Time removed** from the Regular Day bucket (all six Day Type buckets now uniform: Regular Hours / Overtime / Night Differential). **Auto-calculate confirmed** as the Manhours Distribution model — the system computes the split from Day Type + Time In + Time Out; the timekeeper does not enter sub-category numbers manually. Specific calculation rules (lunch break, OT threshold on non-regular days, night-shift overlap with OT, night window, non-standard shifts) flagged in the Appendix as the next set of open items.                                                                                                                                                                          |
| 1.10 — Draft | May 25, 2026 | _[Prepared by]_ | **Auto-calculate engine fully specified.** Added a new "Calculation rules" subsection in 4.2 with configuration constants (8-hour workday; lunch break 12–1 PM; OT meal break 10–11 PM; night meal break 2–3 AM; Night Differential window 11 PM–6 AM), the algorithm (elapsed → fully-spanned break deductions → Regular/OT split at 8 → Night Diff overlap counted separately), and a worked example (8 AM – 2 AM yields Regular 8 / OT 8 / Night Diff 3). Confirmed: lunch break is _span-based_ (not always-deducted); 8-hour OT threshold applies to _all_ Day Types; Night Differential is a _separate count_ that can overlap with Overtime. JCE standard shifts are 7 AM–4 PM and 8 AM–5 PM; both lunch-spanning. Remaining open: payroll multiplier composition for compound cases (multiplicative vs additive) — flagged for the Accounting module.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| 1.11 — Draft | May 25, 2026 | _[Prepared by]_ | **Multiplier composition is additive** — JCE combines premiums by adding (e.g., OT + Night Diff = 125% + 10% = 135%), not multiplying (DOLE's 137.5%). Documented in the DOLE matrix mapping subsection of 4.2 and noted as the rule the future Accounting / Payroll module must encode. Confirms that the Timekeeping data model (Regular / OT / Night Diff as separate counts) is already correctly shaped — payroll sums premiums independently and does not need to know which specific OT hours overlap with Night Diff.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                    |
| 1.12 — Draft | May 25, 2026 | _[Prepared by]_ | **Compound "holiday on Sunday" cases handled additively.** When a Holiday Day Type is picked and the row's Date is a Sunday, the system auto-detects Sunday from the Date (pure date arithmetic, no calendar needed) and adds the Rest Day premium (30%) to the Holiday premium. Example: Regular Holiday on Sunday = base + 100% + 30% = 230%. Captured in the DOLE matrix mapping subsection of 4.2 with a worked premium table. The Timekeeping data model needs no change. The auto-detect rule and additive table belong to the payroll multiplier configuration in the future Accounting / Payroll module.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                |
| 1.13 — Draft | May 25, 2026 | _[Prepared by]_ | **Premium values confirmed — same as DOLE.** JCE uses the same per-Day-Type and per-modifier premium values as the DOLE matrix (Rest Day +30%, Special Holiday +30%, Double Special +50%, Regular Holiday +100%, Double Regular +200%, OT +25%, Night Diff +10%); only the composition is additive. Added a reference table to the DOLE matrix mapping subsection of 4.2 so the Accounting / Payroll module has a single canonical source for premium configuration. | 1.14 — Draft | May 25, 2026 | _[Prepared by]_ | **Verification workflow and pay-period cutoffs added to 4.2.** Three pay-period schedules supported — Monthly (8–22 / 23–7, semi-monthly), Daily (6–20 / 21–5, semi-monthly), Weekly (Sunday–Saturday) — selected per employee by their Salary Rate Category. Time is entered week-by-week regardless of schedule and aggregated into the right pay period. At cutoff, the timekeeper reviews each batch and marks it Verified, locking it for hand-off to the future Accounting / Payroll module. Re-open allowed with audit trail; verifier + timestamp recorded per batch. Section 4.1 updated to note the Salary Rate Category → pay-period-type linkage.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                     |
| 1.15 — Draft | May 25, 2026 | _[Prepared by]_ | **Multi-project day allocation pattern confirmed.** Each day has one shared Time In / Time Out and one auto-calculated Manhours Distribution. When an employee works on multiple projects that day, the timekeeper enters one row per project (all rows share the same Date, Day Type, Time In, Time Out), and the day's Regular / OT / Night Diff counts are **divided evenly across the N rows**. Fractional counts are allowed. Added a multi-project worked example to Section 4.2. Default is even N-way split; future flexibility could allow timekeeper-overridable splits if needed. | 1.16 — Draft | May 25, 2026 | _[Prepared by]_ | **HR Requests — paper-first model and OB / Travel form scoped.** Section 4.3 reframed: the system is a **digital record of the paper workflow**, not a digital approval workflow. HR enters fully-signed paper forms into the system and uploads a scanned copy. Common record shape added (form number entered by HR, date filed, employees from database, approvers, scanned upload, status). New **Section 4.3.1** fully scopes the Official Business / Travel Form — form-level fields (Project Name, Sales Order #, Destination, Reasons for Leaving, Departure/Return date and time, approvers, scan) collapsed from per-row to form-level (one team, one trip, one set of details); per-row content is just the employee list. | 1.21 — Draft | May 25, 2026 | _[Prepared by]_ | **Section 4.3.5 Leave of Absence — Without Pay fully scoped.** Single-employee form (a structural departure from OB/OT's multi-employee pattern). Fields: system-generated internal ID (no paper form number), date filed, employee, position (auto-populated), Date of Absence range, No. of Days (system-calculated), Leave Type single-select (Vacation/Sick/Paternity/Maternity/Others — all five usable for Without Pay), Reason, four signers (Requester = the employee, Section Head Approved, Plant Operation Head Noted, HR Acknowledged), scanned upload, status. **Cross-module change — LOA pushes to Timekeeping** (unlike OB/OT which were kept separate): the system auto-creates Timekeeping rows for each working day in the leave range, marked "Leave Without Pay" via a new row-level **Leave Status** field; these rows appear in the timekeeper's verification batch and travel to Payroll, which knows not to pay them. Leave balance is **not** touched (LOA Without Pay is unpaid). Bryan confirmed two separate paper templates: 4.3.4 LOA With Pay will be scoped in parallel when the paper is obtained. Section 4.2 verification workflow updated to mention LOA-sourced auto-created rows. | 1.22 — Draft | May 25, 2026 | _[Prepared by]_ | **Section 4.3.3 Request for Leave fully scoped — HR Requests module complete.** The Request for Leave form (RFL-YY-XXX) is what Bryan was originally calling "Leave with Pay" — those two entries from the original list have been consolidated, so the final HR Requests count is **four forms, not five**. Section 4.3.4 is intentionally skipped (consolidated). RFL is single-employee with sequential form number entered by HR. Fields auto-populate from employee record (Name, Position, Dept, Length of Service). Type of Leave: Vacation/Sick/Others Specify (Paternity/Maternity go through Others). Pay type checkbox (With Pay default / Without Pay) drives both leave-balance behavior and the Timekeeping Leave Status marker. Pre-approved default with After-the-fact emergency exception (requires proof/evidence). Four signers reach up to President/VP, system picks names from employee DB (not hard-coded template names). Cross-module: auto-creates Timekeeping rows with Leave Status = "Leave With Pay" or "Leave Without Pay" based on the Pay type. **HR Requests module fully scoped; all four forms (4.3.1, 4.3.2, 4.3.3, 4.3.5) have full subsections.** | 1.23 — Draft | May 25, 2026 | _[Prepared by]_ | **Hard-copy upload requirement elevated** in Section 4.3. The file upload is now spelled out as a system-wide, required feature for every HR Request record (OB, OT, Request for Leave, LOA Without Pay) — the system should not allow a record to be saved as Approved/Recorded without the uploaded scan of the signed hard copy. Added file format guidance (PDF preferred, JPG/PNG), multi-file support per record, and a small "to confirm" line for max file size, retention period, and minimum resolution. The upload is the system's audit-trail evidence that the paper workflow actually completed. | 1.24 — Draft | May 25, 2026 | _[Prepared by]_ | **Leave entitlements decided — JCE does not track them.** Updated Section 4.3 intro, Section 4.3.3 Request for Leave, and the appendix to reflect that JCE does not maintain leave entitlements per employee. Leave-type requests are **recorded only** for audit and HR reporting; no balance is enforced. The Pay-or-no-pay decision is made entirely through the Pay type field on each form, which drives the Leave Status marker on auto-created Timekeeping rows. No leave-balance fields needed on the employee record — Section 4.1 stays as-is. Resolves the largest remaining open architectural item in the HR module. Sales Order partially scoped: it is the canonical list of projects and feeds the Working Project dropdown in Timekeeping (4.2) and the Sales Order # field on OB / Travel (4.3.1). Confirmed functions: Add, Edit, Archive, Filter. Add = spreadsheet fields + Start Date + Project Duration. | 1.29 — Draft | May 25, 2026 | _[Prepared by]_ | **Offers fully scoped — all 6 open items resolved.** (1) JCEPSI and JICA are **separate entities**, modeled as a single Offers table with an Entity field. (2) **Revision = new record** with `Rev.NN` and a link to the predecessor; original status moves to Revised. (3) **MAJOR — strict no-edit confirmed.** The Offer record itself is fully immutable; all workflow updates (status, P.O./NOA, COC date, receipt, remarks) become append-only **Offer Events** linked to the offer. The current state of an offer is derived from its event stream; the model _is_ the audit trail. (4) Ref. No. generation is **system-assisted**: BDD picks the client code; the system suggests the next sequential number; BDD can override. Globally unique. (5) **Award → Sales Order conversion is manual** — BDD creates the Sales Order separately and the system records a Sales Order Linked event on the offer. (6) Final status list accepted; **Ongoing removed** (it's Sales Order territory). Section restructured to separate Immutable Offer Record fields from the append-only Event Types stream. | 1.34 — Draft | May 25, 2026 | _[Prepared by]_ | **Website Details fully scoped — all 7 open items resolved + company-context ideation.** (1) Inquiry form fields **ideated** from JCEPSI Company Profile 2026 — 124-staff power-industry firm with offerings in power transformers (15–230 KV), substation design/construction up to 230 KV, transmission lines, switchgear (HVSG/MVSG/LVSG), solar/renewable energy consultancy, NGCP direct connection, maintenance and servicing. Form has Contact Info section (Name, Company, Position, Email, Phone, Industry) + Inquiry Details section (Inquiry Type dropdown aligned to JCE's services, Project Location, Estimated Timeline, Subject, Message) + Optional Qualification fields (Budget Range, How Heard). (2) Lead conversion **prefills an Offer draft** from inquiry data (Subject → Subject, Company → Client, Assigned To → Sent By); the Offer is created in draft state for BDD to refine. (3) Public website **pulls content live** from the database — no static-site publishing step. (4) Client Name visibility is a **per-project setting** (Show Client Name on Public Site boolean) — supports varying confidentiality requirements. (5) Photo constraints: **max 10 photos per record**, **auto-compressed** by the system on save; JPG/PNG, up to 10 MB pre-compression, target ~200–400 KB compressed at ~1600 px longest edge, with cached thumbnails. (6) Services and Products kept **distinct** as two separate lists. (7) No extra fields beyond name + description + photos. Added a company-context paragraph at top of Section 9.2.
