| 1.36 — Draft | May 25, 2026 | _[Prepared by]_ | **Employee Management default list view — grouped by Pay Period Type.** Updated Section 4.1's View (Read) behavior to specify that the default Employee Management screen presents employees in three labeled sections stacked on one page: **Daily** (top), **Weekly** (middle), **Monthly** (bottom). Section headers are visual separators; all three groups visible at once so HR doesn't need to read a column to know which group an employee belongs to. Module Overview surfaces the cross-module hooks already implied by earlier sections: **Payroll** consumes Timekeeping's verified batches and HR Requests' Leave Status markers (using JCE's additive premium math and confirmed premium values); **Sales / Collections** reference Sales Order; **Payable Voucher / Disbursement** reference the Suppliers entity from BDD (Section 9.5); **Project Costing** aggregates labor from Timekeeping (with the per-project even-split allocation already in 4.2) plus materials from future Purchasing. | 1.38 — Draft | May 25, 2026 | _[Prepared by]_ | **Section 5.2.1 Payroll Settings sub-tab fully scoped** with comprehensive configuration spec. Settings is the central, versioned configuration store for the Payroll engine — no values hard-coded; all rates, contribution schedules, policy thresholds, and scheduling rules read at computation time. **23 formal SET-XXX requirements** captured verbatim (SET-001 through SET-060). Six configuration sections: (1) **Pay Rate Multipliers** matrix — 10 day-type rows × 3 columns (Regular / Night shift / Overtime), values 100% to 507%; (2) **Government Mandatory Contributions** — SSS (2025 circular, 4.5%/9.5% split, MSC ₱5K–₱35K, 45-bracket table), PhilHealth (5% premium, ₱10K floor / ₱100K ceiling, 50% employee share), Pag-IBIG (1% or 2% by band, ₱10K Maximum Fund Salary, ₱200 cap); (3) **Withholding Tax** — TRAIN-law graduated table; (4) **Tardiness Policy** — six clock-in windows mapping to time deductions; (5) **Deduction Calendar** — per-cutoff and per-week schedules for semi-monthly/daily and weekly frequencies; (6) **Cut-Off Periods** — date ranges matching Section 4.2. ⚠️ **MAJOR conflict surfaced and flagged for Bryan's reconciliation**: the Pay Rate Multipliers matrix uses **multiplicative** composition (e.g., Rest day OT = 130% × 1.3 = 169%), but Section 4.2's v1.11 confirmation said JCE uses **additive** composition (e.g., 1.25 + 0.10 = 1.35). Settings spec is taken as authoritative in v1.38; the additive language in 4.2 needs to be revised — OR the matrix needs to be recomputed — once Bryan confirms which model is correct. Open item: tax-table frequency (SET-031). | 1.39 — Draft | May 25, 2026 | _[Prepared by]_ | **Composition reconciled to MULTIPLICATIVE (DOLE standard); Statutory Leave Benefits added to Settings.** Bryan provided the official DOLE Handbook on Workers' Statutory Monetary Benefits, confirming JCE follows the standard multiplicative DOLE composition. (1) **Section 4.2's "JCE's multiplier composition rule" subsection rewritten from additive to multiplicative.** Compound case examples updated: Regular Holiday on Sunday is **260%** (2.0 × 1.3), not the earlier additive 230%; Special Holiday on Sunday is **169%** (1.3 × 1.3), not 160%; Double Regular Holiday on Sunday is **390%**, not 330%. Premium values reference table reframed as row-level multipliers (× 1.3 Rest Day, × 2.0 Regular Holiday, etc.) instead of premium increments. Data-model note updated to acknowledge that multiplicative compound cases require the Payroll engine to derive Night-Diff-and-OT overlap from the worked window (deterministic under JCE's standard schedules). All in-doc references to "additive premium math" updated to "multiplicative DOLE pay-rate matrix." (2) **Section 5.2.1 composition warning removed and open item closed** — Settings matrix is authoritative; v1.11 additive record is superseded with a historical note. (3) **Section 5.2.1 Section 7 added: Statutory Leave Benefits.** SET-070 / SET-071 / SET-072 capture three DOLE-mandated leaves: **Service Incentive Leave** (5 days after 1 year of service, employer-paid), **Maternity Leave** (105 / 120 / 60 days depending on case, SSS-paid — employer pays zero for these days), **Paternity Leave** (7 days, employer-paid). Connection to HR Requests Section 4.3.3 noted; the v1.24 "JCE does not track leave entitlements" decision may now need partial revisiting for statutory leaves specifically — flagged for future review. Remaining open item: SET-031 tax-table frequency. | 1.40 — Draft | May 25, 2026 | _[Prepared by]_ | **Section 5.2.2 Payroll Loans sub-tab preliminary scope.** Bryan provided a comprehensive loan-model spec. Captured the shared loan-object architecture: one record structure (Employee, Type, Sub-type, Reference Number, Principal, Date Granted, Terms of Payment, Deduction Schedule, Running Balance, Status, append-only Ledger), three Types (Government / Company / Cash Advance) distinguished only by ownership and schedule rules — the Payroll engine itself treats all loans uniformly at compute time. Government Loans use the Settings Deduction Calendar (SSS on 15th / Week 4, Pag-IBIG on 30th / Week 1); Company Loans use Accounting-defined schedules; Cash Advances are typically short-term and interest-free. Terms-of-payment engine: two entry modes (by term count or by fixed amortization), with **final installment trueing** (last deduction = remaining balance, never over-collects) and **auto-stop** on zero balance. Ledger uses the same event-stream pattern as Offers (9.4) and Quotation (9.5) — append-only, model is the audit trail. Critical behaviors: multiple concurrent loans summed at each run, re-run protection on (loan + payroll run + cutoff), all adjustments (hold / restructure / extra payment / override) logged. **Four open decisions** flagged for confirmation: (1) insufficient net pay policy — skip / partial-deduct / carry forward; (2) Cash Advance as distinct Type or variant of Company Loan; (3) interest on Company Loans (zero or interest-bearing); (4) deduction priority order when net pay is tight.                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                                            |
| 1.41 — Draft | May 25, 2026 | _[Prepared by]_ | **Section 5.2.2 Loans fully scoped — all 4 open decisions resolved.** (1) **Insufficient net pay = Skip** — when total scheduled loan amortizations exceed available net pay, the engine skips all loans for that employee that cutoff (no carry-forward, no partial deduct, no balance decrement); skipped loans surface in the payroll review with an indicator. (2) **Cash Advance stays a distinct Type** with HR ownership; separate sub-type list and reporting from Company Loans. (3) **All Company Loans and CAs are zero-interest**; the Principal is the total to repay (Government loans already have their interest baked into HR-entered amortization). (4) **No automated deduction priority** — the payroll officer reviews each skip case-by-case and chooses which loans to push through; each manual deduction writes a ledger entry with user, timestamp, amount, and optional reason. The system may surface a suggested review order in the UI (government loans first, since SSS/Pag-IBIG deductions have legal weight), but the engine never auto-deducts past the skip rule. Skip + officer review is the governance model: skip is the safe default (never leaves an employee with negative net pay, never silently carries a shortfall), and the officer review with logged reasons is the escape valve. | 1.58 — Draft | May 25, 2026 | _[Prepared by]_ | **Section 7.15 Approved Refinements added (10 architectural additions to Purchasing); two cross-module open items resolved.** Per Bryan's confirmation, the ten refinements proposed during the v1.57 review are scoped: **(1) 7.15.1 Goods Receipt + Three-Way Matching** — new `GoodsReceipt` entity (gr*no `GR-YY-XXXX`, po_id, po_line_id, received_qty, condition Good/Damaged/Damaged Accepted/Returned, received_by, attachments) supporting partial deliveries; **10 FR-GR requirements** including three-way match (PO ↔ GR ↔ Invoice) gating RFP submission, configurable tolerance with role-based override, auto Inventory stock-in posting (when Inventory exists), printable. Closes a significant Phase 1 control gap. **(2) 7.15.2 Supplier Bank Account sub-entity** — new `SupplierBankAccount` 1..N child of Supplier plus new `JCE Banks` lookup; **6 FR-SBA requirements** including fraud-control workflow (Supervisor approval + email confirmation on bank changes, common PH SME procurement fraud vector). **Resolves PV 5.5 open item #9 and Purchasing 7.13 open item #9.** **(3) 7.15.3 Purchase Requisition** — new `PurchaseRequisition` entity (format `PRQ-YY-XXXX` deliberately distinct from `JCE-PR` to avoid Accounting Payment Request namespace collision) inserted between MR (operational) and PO (financial commitment); **10 FR-REQ requirements** including approval routing via 7.15.4, urgency tags Routine/Urgent/Critical with SLA differentiation, Phase 2 budget-check at PR Submit. Makes Phase 2 Budget vs Actual feasible since budget check happens BEFORE sourcing rather than after. **(4) 7.15.4 Amount-Threshold Approval Routing** — new `ApprovalThreshold` Settings lookup with multi-step chains per entity_type (PR/PO/RFP/BlanketPO) per currency; **10 FR-APV requirements** including per-record chain freeze at submission (subsequent threshold changes don't retroactively change in-flight approvals), Vacation Mode delegate-when-absent, per-user approval queue. Example PHP defaults: 0-50K Supervisor, 50K-500K Supervisor→Finance, 500K-5M Supervisor→Finance→President. **(5) 7.15.5 Local PO 5-stage Tracker** — parity with import tracker; stages PO Sent → Goods Received → Invoice Received → Paid → Closed; **9 FR-LPO requirements** with auto-advance via GR/RFP triggers and stalled-stage alerts (default 14-day threshold). Default for Local POs ≥ ₱20K. **(6) 7.15.6 Blanket Purchase Orders** *(Phase 2)_ — new `BlanketPO` (`BPO-YY-XXXX`) + `BlanketRelease` entities with ceiling tracking; **8 FR-BLK requirements** including auto-generated child POs per release, utilization warnings at 80% / blocks at 100% with supervisor override, expiry handling. For routine recurring supplies (fuel, stationery, cleaning). **(7) 7.15.7 Supplier Merge Tool** _(Phase 1.5)_ — duplicate detection (TIN exact + Name fuzzy Levenshtein + email/phone match) with 8 FR-MRG requirements, 30-day reversibility, atomic FK repointing, soft-delete on duplicates. For cleaning up 145 legacy supplier records. **(8) 7.15.8 Item Taxonomy / Category Tree** _(Phase 2 refinement of 7.14.2)\_ — two-level minimum (`Category > Sub-category`) with example tree (MEP > Plumbing > PVC Pipes; Office > Supplies; Vehicle > Fuel); FR-ITM extended to 05-08; drives Spend Analytics drill-down and seeds the empty SupplierCategory. **(9) 7.15.9 Project Entity Unification handoff to Section 6** — confirmed direction documented (Project parent + `project_type` Customer/Internal Cost Center; Customer projects link to Sales Order, Internal cost centers don't); full scope happens during Section 6 Project Management interview. **(10) RFP numbering format — recommended resolution captured in 7.12** as Resolved Decision #10 with recommendation for **`RFP-PUR-YYMM####`** (year-month-prefixed, aligns with PO numbering and matches the PV artifact's `26020188` parse), awaiting Bryan's final confirm. Also added Resolved Decision #11 listing all ten refinements adopted. **Cross-section updates**: PV 5.5 open item #9 and Purchasing 7.13 open item #9 both marked RESOLVED via 7.15.2. Total new requirement codes introduced in 7.15: ~60 across FR-GR, FR-SBA, FR-REQ, FR-APV, FR-LPO, FR-BLK, FR-MRG (plus FR-ITM extensions). | 1.56 — Draft | May 25, 2026 | _[Prepared by]_ | **Section 7 Purchasing Department Module fully scoped from Bryan's comprehensive requirements document (dated 2026-06-02); three PV 5.5 open items resolved.** Source: Bryan provided a complete pre-structured Purchasing requirements document derived from review of JCE Supplier List New.xlsx (~145 suppliers), IMPORTATION MONITORING 05-26-26.xlsx (~970 rows), the Eslite local PO sample (`PO 26060001`), the Shandong JNDO import PO sample (`PO 2606000IC`), and the RFP form (`RPF-blank.xlsx`). **All requirement codes preserved as `FR-XXX-NN` from source doc** for 1:1 traceability. Section structure mirrors source: 7.1 Module Overview, 7.2 Scope & Locked Decisions (PO numbering, offline approvals, 15-stage import tracker), 7.3 Data Backbone & Entity Model (3 linking keys Project/SO No./MR No. as FKs, 10 entity types, type-flag-drives-behavior branch table), 7.4 PO Numbering (FR-NUM-01 through 06: YYMM#### Local + YYMM####IC Import with monthly reset, separate counters per type, atomic concurrency, immutable post-assignment, deferred until leaving Draft), 7.5 PO Creation with both-types-on-one-form pattern (FR-PO-01 through 10: common header + Local-specific Vatable+Delivery, Import-specific Currency+Incoterm+Warranty+Discount, line items with computed totals, **VAT-inclusive Local model** (TOTAL ÷ 1.12 = Net, Net × 0.12 = VAT — confirmed against Eslite 60,880 → 54,357.14 + 6,522.86), Import Sub-Total + Discount + Grand Total, signatory blocks admin-configurable per template, PDF generation, status lifecycle Draft → For Approval → Approved → Sent → Closed), 7.6 PO Monitoring Ledger (FR-LED-01 through 08: default group by Project, Local/Import/All toggle, full-text search, **Payment Status derived (not manually typed)** from linked RFPs + import stage dates, CSV/Excel export respecting filters), 7.7 RFP (FR-RFP-01 through 11: format `RFP-PUR-#####` continuous counter, **two RFPs per Import PO** for Downpayment + Balance, **EWT rates explicit** Goods 1%/Services 2%/Rentals 5%/Prof Fee 5-10%, Net Payment computation, Amount in words, two zones Originator+Accounting with separate signatories, status Draft → Submitted → Verified → Received → Paid, reconciles to PO grand total), 7.8 Imported-PO Tracker (FR-IMP-01 through 07: full 15-stage engine with stage catalog incl owners and document slots — BOM/MR/PO+Quotation/Sir Jimwel Approval/Performa Invoice/Tech Spec/JCE Approval+DP/Production/Lead Time/Balance Payment/Shipping Docs incl BL+ETD+ETA/Departure/Arrival/Customs/Onsite, **non-sequential** stage model allowing concurrent Production+Lead Time, alerts on approaching ETA, due/overdue payment, blocked stages — approval gates 4 & 7 as primary stall points), 7.9 Supplier CRUD (FR-SUP-01 through 09: **Purchasing 7.9 is authoritative Supplier owner, superseding BDD 9.5's preliminary fields**, JCE 0000X auto-numbering with gaps allowed, soft-delete only when referenced by POs, duplicate guard on Name+TIN, performance stats computed from POs, **two data-quality cleanups required** — PaymentTerm controlled list to replace messy free-text (30 DAYS / 30 DAYS PDC / 30 PDC / PDC 30DAYS variants) and SupplierCategory controlled list seeded from Item/Service Offered), 7.10 Audit Log (FR-AUD-01 through 06: department-scoped, append-only immutable, matches per-module audit pattern from BDD 9.6), 7.11 Cross-Cutting (FR-X-01 through 06: role suggestions, attachments with stage-11 first-class slots for BL/PackingList/CommercialInvoice/FormE, notifications, dashboard, spend analytics, **FX policy open question** for PHP-consolidated reporting of USD POs), 7.12 nine Resolved Decisions list, 7.13 nine Open Questions for SRS Finalization, 7.14 **Phase 2 Extended Scope** with 7.14.0 new entities (RFQ, RFQLineItem, Quotation, QuotationLineItem, Item Catalog, ItemPriceHistory, ProjectBudget) and 8 features: 7.14.1 RFQ/Quotation Management (FR-RFQ-01 through 06: side-by-side comparison matrix, award-and-convert to PO with justification when not lowest, **distinct from BDD 9.5 Quotation** which is bid-side), 7.14.2 Item Catalog (FR-ITM-01 through 04: ad-hoc-to-catalog promotion), 7.14.3 Price History + Change Flags (FR-PRC-01 through 04: threshold-warn on increases), 7.14.4 BIR Withholding-Tax Reporting (FR-BIR-01 through 05: BIR Form 2307, alphalist, ATC codes, DAT export), 7.14.5 Lead-Time + Supplier Reliability (FR-LT-01 through 05: structured lead-time with trigger events, supplier reliability score surfaced in record), 7.14.6 Budget vs Actual per Project (FR-BUD-01 through 04: committed + actual + remaining with warn/block on over-budget), 7.14.7 Mobile Approvals (FR-APP-01 through 05: digital decision workflow for stages 4 & 7 — workflow decision NOT e-signature, paper still wet-signed), 7.14.8 Cycle-Time Analytics (FR-CYC-01 through 05: stage timestamps, bottleneck identification, also local PO cycle). **Three PV 5.5 open items marked RESOLVED**: (a) **#3 RFP vs JCE-PR vs ACCTG-#### reference relationship** — three distinct workflow stages (Purchasing emits RFP-PUR → Accounting receives and creates ACCTG-# and PV → consolidated in JCE-PR worksheet); (b) **#5 ATC withholding-tax handling** — RFP 7.7.5 computes EWT by classification with configurable rates, PV inherits rather than re-computes; (c) **#6 PO linkage strictness** — hard FK for Supplier-payee vouchers with PO reference, optional/free-text for Employee or Other payees (advances, reimbursements, petty cash). **Two dependencies on future Section 6 Project Management** flagged: Project entity scope (includes internal cost centers WORKSHOP/JCE STOCK beyond customer Sales Orders) and Material Request entity (MR No. as FK to a module that doesn't yet exist) — Purchasing accepts "soft-FK" mode in Phase 1, transitioning to hard-FK when Section 6 is built. **Format discrepancy flagged**: PV artifact showed `RFP-PUR-26020188` (year-month-prefixed look) but Purchasing form shows `RFP-PUR-11878` (5-digit continuous) — confirm canonical format. **Supplier bank accounts** flagged as recommended addition (1..N child of Supplier) to address PV 5.5 open item #9 about pre-fill of recipient bank details. |

cash_advance_ref) and `lines[]` array (N debit lines + N credit lines with reference, account_code FK to CoA, description, debit/credit decimals). **Workflow**: Draft → Pending Check → Posted → Reversed (simpler two-stage than PV's three-stage). **Strict no-edit on Posted JVs** with reversal-as-new-JV pattern (mirroring Offers 9.4, Quotation 9.5). **Cross-module integrations**: PV 5.5 (reference_cv_id), CoA 5.2.1 §8 (every line), Project Costing 5.8 (project-tagged JVs roll into project cost), Reporting 5.9 (trial balance, General Ledger, Cash Advance Aging report). **Cash Advance modeled as first-class entity recommended (Option 3)** — `cash_advance` record with outstanding_balance, source_cv, liquidating_jv, supporting an "Outstanding Cash Advances" dashboard widget and stale-advance flagging. **10 UX considerations** including Cash Advance Liquidation wizard with auto-balance check, line-level CoA fuzzy search, reversal auto-flip with mandatory review, balance enforcement at every Save, strict no-edit on Posted. **7 JV-XXX requirements** (balance enforcement Dr = Cr exact, active-CoA-account validation, unique series numbering, Posted immutability, Cash Advance Liquidation category requires reference_cv and CA ref, print/PDF matching legacy form, audit log on state transitions). **11 open items**: Cash Advance modeling option (recommend Option 3), stage vs separate), strict no-edit confirmation, reversal mechanic (auto-flip vs manual rebuild), jv_category enumeration completeness, returned-cash flow vs separate bank deposit, Project Overhead as model**: PV authorization posts `Dr Expense/Asset / Cr 20004 Voucher's Payable`; Disbursement execution posts `Dr 20004 Voucher's Payable / Cr Cash-In-Bank (10001-10012)`. `20004` is the clearing/transit account between authorized-but-unpaid and paid. **Resolved PV open item #14**: PV and Disbursement are two linked records with two separate journal entries — not a single record with a status change. **Resolved CoA open item #4 (`20001 AP` vs `20004 VP`)**: 20001 holds long-cycle supplier invoices received but not yet authorized; 20004 holds short-cycle authorized-but-not-paid obligations (the PV→Disbursement transit). **Section 5.6 fully scoped** with: data model (disbursement_id, disbursement_date, cv_no FK, payment_method Check/Bank Transfer/Cash, a separate entity with 1..N Disbursement children — cleaner for void-the-whole-check workflows); cross-module integrations (PV 5.5, CoA 5.2.1 §8, Suppliers, Employees, Project Costing 5.8, Reporting 5.9, future bank reconciliation); input flow `+ Record Disbursement` opened from an Approved CV; 10 UX considerations (single-screen register view, ALL PROJECT filter, period/bank/method filters, group-by-check, check-duplicate warning, void workflow, bank-reconciliation prep, landscape print layout); **7 DISB-XXX requirements** (1:1 PV-Disbursement linkage, automatic journal posting and CV status flip on Save, multi-CV check support, void with reversing entry, print/PDF in bank-pivot layout, filtering by project/bank/method/date, audit append-only). **10 open items**: Check-entity | 1.61 — Draft | June 3, 2026 | _[Prepared by]_ | **Section 10 Warehouse expanded with the operational document layer** (8 decisions locked with Bryan 2026-06-03), designed against the company's **MRR Summary** (232 Material Receiving Report records — `MRR No / Receive Date / Particulars / Supplier / Invoice No / Project / PO Number / MR Number / Requested By / Accountability Code / Purpose`; **97% carry a PO#, 93% an MR#, 95% an Invoice#** — confirming the MRR is structurally the Purchasing↔PMG link) and the **ARSD warehouse dashboard** as the UX reference (project cards, Stock Monitoring Ledger, Delivery Receipts, Release Forms, Lock/Unlock). **Key promotions from Phase 2 into Phase 1:** multi-location stock, two-sided Stock Transfer, and the formal goods-receiving / three-way-match workflow. **The eight locked decisions:** (1) **Ledger is BOQ-driven with off-BOQ manual additions** — customer projects seed planned qty from the PMG BOQ so Variance is real; off-BOQ rows tag "no plan," show Variance "—," and an admin may promote a row onto the plan (likely Variation Order, PMG §4.4); internal cost centres (`WORKSHOP`/`MOTORPOOL`/`JCE STOCK`) run a free item master with no plan/variance (new §10.4 Stock Monitoring Ledger, FR-LED-01..07). (2) **Multi-location, Main Office central** — on-hand per Location, project rollup (§10.8, FR-LOC). (3) **Two-sided Stock Transfer** — Dispatch → In Transit → Confirm Receipt, with discrepancy flag (FR-TRF-01..04). (4) **3-state lock gate** (Draft → For Checking → Locked) on MRR/Release/Transfer; site engineers submit, Admin/Owner Locks; Locked = immutable; admin-only Unlock reverses movements; all audit-logged (new §10.12, FR-LCK-01..05). (5) **MRR _is_ the single GoodsReceipt** — a Locked MRR completes Purchasing's three-way match (§7.15.1) and releases the RFP (§7.7); no double entry between warehouse and purchasing (new §10.6, FR-MRR-08). (6) **Partial deliveries** — one PO → many MRRs, cumulative match (FR-MRR-06). (7) **Release "Received By" is free text** + releasing warehouseman; recipients need not be system users (new §10.7, FR-REL-03). (8) **Site engineers scoped to assigned sites** (ARSD warehouseman pattern); **MRR/Release/Transfer numbering** continuous year-prefixed (`MRR-YYYY-####`, `REL-YYYY-####`, `TRF-YYYY-####`, no monthly reset, FR-WXC-06); **Accountability Code optional in v1** with custody ledger deferred to Phase 2. **Structural changes:** §10 renumbered to 17 subsections; new entities `ProjectLedger`/`LedgerRow`, `MaterialReceivingReport`, `ReleaseForm`, `StockTransfer`, `Location` added to 10.3 (GoodsReceipt no longer a Phase-2 entity — it _is_ the MRR); StockMovement extended with Transfer-Out/Transfer-In types and a per-Location dimension; roles table adds **Site Engineer** vs **Warehouse Admin** with the Lock/Unlock boundary; Resolved Decisions grow to 16, Open Questions reset to 6 warehouse-specific items (negative-stock guard, off-BOQ→VO auto-draft, MRR/PO reconciliation policy, Main Office `JCE STOCK` receiving pattern, item-code scheme, MR# standardization); Phase 2 trimmed to reorder points, stock-take, returnable-tool custody, bins/barcode, and off-BOQ→VO auto-draft. New requirement-code prefixes: `FR-LED`, `FR-MRR`, `FR-REL`, `FR-LOC`, `FR-TRF`, `FR-LCK`. ### 13.2 Pending Items and Open Questions

The items below are compiled from the TBD and "pending" markers throughout this document. They represent the information still needed before the corresponding sections can be completed.

**HR Module — resolved through v1.7:**

- ✅ Employee removal = **archive, not delete** (Section 4.1).
- ✅ **Status lifecycle**, **employment type**, **contract-duration tracking** with a sub-6-month expiry alert, and **audit trail** confirmed (Section 4.1).
- ✅ **PIS field list received and incorporated** — identification, compensation (sensitive), government IDs (sensitive, PH statutory), personal information, emergency contact, and other fields, with computed and sensitive fields flagged (Section 4.1).
- ✅ **BIO Nos identified as the Timekeeping link** between an employee record and recorded time (Sections 4.1, 4.2).
- ✅ Timekeeping basics confirmed — **weekly per-employee entry**, **per-project costing**, **mirror the existing spreadsheet**, **finalize-to-Accounting** payroll hand-off (Section 4.2).
- ✅ **Timekeeping field list received and incorporated** — Employee Number, Name, Position, Working Project, Remarks, Date, Time In/Out, and the full **Manhours Distribution** (Regular Day, Rest Day, Special Holiday, Regular Holiday, Absences and Undertime). Auto-populated fields and cross-module links flagged (Section 4.2).
- ✅ HR request types confirmed — the **five forms** (Official Business/Travel, Request for Leave, Overtime, Leave of Absence With Pay, Leave of Absence Without Pay), with **leave-balance integration** (Section 4.3).
- ✅ **DOLE-aligned Manhours Distribution model applied** — six Day Type buckets (Regular Day, **Rest Day = Sunday**, Special Holiday, Double Special Holiday, Regular Holiday, Double Regular Holiday). **Day Type is timekeeper-selected** from a dropdown beside Date; no automated holiday calendar is maintained. Rest Day is fixed at Sunday for all employees, so no per-employee rest-day schedule is needed. The DOLE multiplier table lives as configuration in the future Accounting / Payroll module (Section 4.2).
- ✅ **Auto-calculate engine specified** — system computes Regular Hours, Overtime, and Night Differential from Day Type + Time In + Time Out. **Lunch break** (12–1 PM), **OT meal break** (10–11 PM), and **night meal break** (2–3 AM) are deducted from elapsed time only when fully spanned by the worked window. Regular Hours threshold = 8 (applies to all Day Types). **Night Differential window** = 11 PM–6 AM. Night Differential is counted _separately_ and can overlap with Overtime. Standard JCE shifts (7 AM–4 PM and 8 AM–5 PM) both yield 8 Regular Hours / 0 OT / 0 Night Diff under this engine (Section 4.2).
- ✅ **Payroll multiplier composition is multiplicative (DOLE-standard)** — _corrected in v1.39; the earlier v1.11 additive record is superseded._ JCE combines compound premiums multiplicatively per the DOLE Handbook (e.g., Rest-day OT = 1.30 × 1.30 = 169%). The Timekeeping model records Regular / OT / Night Diff as separate counts; the Payroll engine derives the overlap and applies the compound matrix cell from Settings (Sections 4.2, 5.2.1).
- ✅ **Verification workflow specified** — the timekeeper reviews and verifies each pay-period batch before it ships to Accounting; once verified, the batch is locked. Re-opening is allowed with an audit trail (Section 4.2).
- ✅ **Pay periods and cutoffs confirmed** — three schedules: Monthly (8–22 / 23–7, semi-monthly), Daily (6–20 / 21–5, semi-monthly), Weekly (Sunday–Saturday). Driven by the employee's Salary Rate Category. Weekly entry rhythm aggregates into the right period per employee (Section 4.2).
- ✅ **Multi-project day pattern confirmed** — one shared Time In / Time Out per day, one auto-calculated Manhours Distribution for the day, divided **evenly across N project rows** for cost allocation. Fractional hours allowed when the split doesn't divide cleanly. Future flexibility: timekeeper-overridable split for cases where work was clearly weighted (Section 4.2).
- ✅ **HR Requests workflow model — paper-first, system records.** Confirmed for the Official Business / Travel Form: the employee fills out the paper form, the actual approval signatures are collected sequentially on paper (Approving Officer → Admin and Finance → HR Acknowledges), and HR then enters the form's data into the system and uploads a scanned copy. The system is a digital record-keeping layer, not a digital approval workflow. (Section 4.3, 4.3.1)
- ✅ **Official Business / Travel form fully scoped.** Form-level fields (form number entered by HR, date filed, reasons for leaving, Project Name, Sales Order #, Destination, departure/return date and time, approver names, scanned upload, status) shared across the form; employee list with auto-populated employee details. Form-level UX confirmed: per-row fields collapsed into form-level fields since the team shares the same trip. OB and Timekeeping kept **separate** — no auto-creation of Timekeeping entries. Sales Order # source depends on BDD module being scoped. (Section 4.3.1)
- ✅ **Paper-first model confirmed for all five HR Request forms.** The system records paper forms; signatures happen on paper for every request type. (Section 4.3)
- ✅ **Overtime (OT) Form fully scoped.** Form-level fields (form number entered by HR, Section radio for Servicing/Shop-Office/Project site, Project, Date prepared, Date of OT, Overtime Request and Actual Time ranges, Date received by Timekeeper, Reason, Remarks, OT Type as Pre-approved or After-the-fact); per-row content is just the employee list (with each employee signing their row on paper). Four signers: Requester (from employees on the form), Department Head, Timekeeper (Noted by), HR Head (Approved by). OT and Timekeeping kept **separate** — no auto-creation. (Section 4.3.2)
- ✅ **Leave of Absence Form — Without Pay fully scoped.** Single-employee form (not multi-employee like OB/OT). Fields: system-generated internal ID (no paper form number), date filed, employee (from database), Position (auto-populated), Date of Absence range, No. of Days, Leave Type single-select (Vacation/Sick/Paternity/Maternity/Others — all five usable for Without Pay), Reason, four signers (Requester = the employee, Section Head Approved, Plant Operation Head Noted, HR Acknowledged), scanned upload. **Cross-module: auto-creates Timekeeping rows** for each working day in the range, marked "Leave Without Pay" so Payroll knows not to pay; rows appear in the timekeeper's verification batch. No leave-balance deduction (since unpaid). (Section 4.3.5)
- ✅ **Request for Leave fully scoped — and consolidates the original "LOA With Pay" entry.** Single-employee form with sequential form number (RFL-YY-XXX, entered manually by HR). Fields auto-populate from employee record (Name, Position, Dept, Length of Service). Type of Leave single-select (Vacation / Sick / Others Specify; Paternity/Maternity go through "Others"). **Pay type checkbox: With Pay (default) or Without Pay** — drives both leave-balance behavior and the Timekeeping Leave Status marker. Pre-approved by default; After-the-fact allowed for emergencies (requires proof/evidence attachment). Four signers reach up to President/VP (system picks names from employee DB, not the hard-coded names on the template): Employee (Requester) → Department Head/Supervisor (Approved) → HR Head (Checked) → President/VP (Noted). Cross-module: auto-creates Timekeeping rows with Leave Status = "Leave With Pay" or "Leave Without Pay" depending on the form's Pay type. With Pay deduction will engage once leave-balance fields are added to the employee record (open in 4.1). **HR Requests module (Section 4.3) is now fully scoped — all four distinct forms have subsections.** (Section 4.3.3)
- ✅ **BDD confirmed as a sixth department.** Added as a placeholder in Section 9 (Business Development Department). Sections 10–12 renumbered accordingly (Public Website → 10; Non-Functional Requirements → 11; Appendix → 12). (Section 9)
- ✅ **Compound case "holiday on Sunday" is handled multiplicatively** — _corrected to multiplicative (v1.39)._ When a Holiday Day Type is picked and the row's Date is a Sunday, the system auto-detects Sunday from the Date and applies the Holiday-on-Rest-Day matrix cell (e.g., Regular Holiday on Sunday = 2.0 × 1.3 = 260%). The row still records one Day Type; the Sunday overlap is resolved at payroll time (Sections 4.2, 5.2.1).

**HR Module — still open (small clarifications surfaced by the PIS):**

- **S/N** — is this a true employee identifier (and if so, auto-generated or entered) or only a list index? (Section 4.1)
- **Status** — exact set of allowed values for the employment status (e.g., probationary, regular, on leave, suspended, resigned/terminated). (Section 4.1)
- **Place of Assignment** — free-text field or selected from a list (e.g., the future Project Management module's project list)? (Section 4.1)
- **Insurance** — yes/no indicator, provider/policy name, or coverage detail? (Section 4.1)
- **Vaccinated** — yes/no indicator or a richer status (vaccine type, doses, booster)? (Section 4.1)
- **Expiration Date** — which item does this date refer to (contract, ID, ATM card, etc.)? Whether it is the same date that drives the sub-6-month contract-expiry alert. (Section 4.1)
- **Contract-expiry alert mechanism** — how the alert is delivered (on-screen indicator, notification, email) and who receives it. (Section 4.1)
- The **layout of the PIS form** (helpful for the add/edit screen design; not blocking). (Section 4.1)

**HR Module — Timekeeping (Section 4.2), still open:**

- **Employee Number vs. S/N vs. BIO Nos** — confirm how these three identifiers relate (is "Employee Number" the same as S/N from the PIS, or a separate canonical employee identifier?).
- **"Travel Time (Regular Rate)" definition** — when does travel time get logged here? And does travel time occur on rest days, holidays, etc., or is it by definition an ordinary-day-rate item?
- **Historical handling of compound cases** — nice to confirm with HR for context: how the existing spreadsheet handled cases like "Regular Holiday falling on Rest Day" or "Double Regular Holiday" before this systematization. Mainly useful for data migration if past timesheets are brought into the new model.
- **DOLE multiplier table ownership** — confirm that the multiplier table lives in the future Accounting / Payroll module as configuration (not in Timekeeping), and who is responsible for maintaining it when DOLE revises rates.
- Obtain a **sample of the actual spreadsheet** for visual layout fidelity (column order, grouping, weekly view, totals/formulas). The structure is already received; the sample is for the on-screen design.
- **Salary Rate Category linkage** — confirm that the Salary Rate Category field is the one that drives an employee's pay-period type (Monthly / Daily / Weekly), versus a separate field. (Section 4.1, Section 4.2)
- **Verification re-open permissions and audit trail** — confirm whether re-opening a locked, verified batch requires a role beyond ordinary HR access, and confirm that the verification timestamp + verifier name should be recorded for HR/owner visibility. Proposed default in the draft: timekeeper can re-open with an audited reason; verifier and timestamp are recorded on every batch. (Section 4.2)

**HR Module — HR Requests (Section 4.3), still open:**

- ~~Obtain remaining HR Request forms.~~ ✅ **All four HR Request forms now obtained and scoped** — OB/Travel (4.3.1), OT (4.3.2), Request for Leave (4.3.3, which also covers what was called "Leave with Pay"), LOA Without Pay (4.3.5). The original list of five forms turned out to be four after consolidating Request for Leave with the "LOA With Pay" entry.
- ~~Approval path / lifecycle for remaining forms.~~ ✅ **All four HR Request forms' signer chains confirmed** — each is a sequential 4-step paper-signed chain with its own roles. OB: Requester → Approving Officer → Admin & Finance → HR Acknowledger. OT: Requester → Department Head → Timekeeper (Noted) → HR Head (Approved). Request for Leave: Employee → Dept Head/Supervisor (Approved) → HR Head (Checked) → President/VP (Noted). LOA Without Pay: Employee → Section Head (Approved) → Plant Operation Head (Noted) → HR (Acknowledged).
- ✅ **Leave entitlements — not tracked at JCE.** All leave-type requests are recorded for audit and reporting only; no balance enforcement.
- **OB / Travel — auto-Timekeeping defaults** — exact Time In / Time Out for entries auto-created from approved OB (each employee's normal shift, or a system default like 8 AM–5 PM); and **Sunday handling** in the OB window (skip Sundays, or create a Rest Day entry). (Section 4.3.1)
- **OB / Travel — Pending status** — confirm whether HR ever records an OB before all signatures are collected, in which case a Pending state is needed; otherwise Approved-only. (Section 4.3.1)
- **OB / Travel — notification policy** — confirm whether the system sends any notifications when an OB is recorded, or whether the paper-first model means no system notifications. (Section 4.3.1)
- **BDD module** — confirmed as the sixth department (now Section 9). Detailed scoping is pending its stakeholder interview; the Sales Order # field in OB / Travel depends on the BDD sales-order data being defined.

**HR Module — Access and roles, still open:**

- **Decision needed:** the extent of **employee self-service** — do employees log in to file their own requests and view balances, or does HR enter requests on their behalf? This drives the number of user accounts, the RBAC model, and the request module's design (Sections 2.2, 3.3, 4.3).
- The internal breakdown of **HR roles** — including who can see sensitive compensation, government ID, and ATM fields versus who can only see general employee information (Sections 2.2, 3.3, 4.1).

**Compliance:**

- Confirm any **Data Privacy Act (RA 10173)** obligations beyond standard practice — e.g., consent capture, data subject access requests, retention periods (Section 4.1, Section 12.2).

**Access Control (Section 3):**

- Role definitions and detailed **permission matrices** for the Accounting, Engineering, and Business Development (BDD) departments (HR, Purchasing, Project Management, and Warehouse provide their role inputs in-section).
- A consolidated **permissions matrix** (roles × modules × actions) once all departments are scoped.

**Un-scoped Department Modules (Sections 5–9):**

- Full requirements for **Accounting**, **Project Management**, **Purchasing**, **Engineering**, and **Business Development (BDD)** — pending each department's stakeholder interview. BDD is now confirmed as the sixth department (sales orders, business development activities).

**Public Website (Section 11):**

- Detailed **SEO and GEO goals**, content and structure improvements, branding direction, and supporting assets — pending requirements gathering.

**System-Wide (Sections 2 and 10):**

- **Operating environment** specifics: hosting, supported devices, mobile vs. desktop, offline needs, and integrations with any existing tools.
- Additional **constraints**: data-retention rules, compliance requirements, branding guidelines.
- Specific **non-functional targets**: performance thresholds, security rules, availability/backup expectations, expected growth figures, and accessibility/device requirements.

> As each interview is completed, the relevant placeholder section should be filled in using the same structure established by the HR module (Section 4), and the corresponding items above should be moved out of this pending list and into the body of the document.

### 13.3 Approval & Sign-off

This Software Requirements Specification is submitted for review and approval. By signing, the approver confirms that the scoped sections accurately reflect JC Electrofields' requirements and authorizes the described scope as the basis for design and development. Placeholder sections (Engineering §8, Public Website §11) and items marked _client-input required_ are explicitly excluded from this approval and will be confirmed in subsequent revisions.
